  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic department that owns and administers this prerequisite rule. Determines which department chair has override authority and which curriculum committee reviews rule changes.']
  parent_rule_prerequisite_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent prerequisite rule record in a nested Boolean condition hierarchy. Null for top-level rules. Enables recursive evaluation of complex prerequisite trees.']
  primary_prerequisite_controlling_course_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course that owns this prerequisite rule — the course a student is attempting to register for. This is the \'gating\' course whose enrollment eligibility is governed by this rule.']
  rule_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking multiple prerequisite rule records into a logical group for complex Boolean evaluation (e.g., \'CALC1 AND (TRIG OR PRECALC)\'). Rules sharing the same rule_group_id are evaluated together using their respective boolean_operator values.']
  advisory_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates that this rule is displayed as an academic advising recommendation in degree planning tools but does not generate a hard registration block in the SIS. Distinct from sis_enforcement_flag: a rule may be SIS-enforced as a soft warning but still be advisory.']
  ap_credit_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Advanced Placement (AP) exam credit awarded by the institution can satisfy this prerequisite rule. When True, qualifying AP scores on file in the SIS are evaluated during registration.']
  applicable_term_end varchar [note: 'Banner-format term code (YYYYTT) representing the last academic term in which this prerequisite rule is enforced. Null for rules with no planned end term. Used to sunset rules aligned with curriculum revision cycles.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or institutional username of the faculty member, department chair, or curriculum committee representative who formally approved this prerequisite rule. Supports audit trail for accreditation and compliance reviews.']
  boolean_operator varchar [note: 'Logical operator used to combine this rule with sibling rules within the same rule group. \'AND\' requires all conditions in the group to be met; \'OR\' requires at least one; \'NOT\' excludes students who have completed the referenced course (anti-requisite logic).']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'The academic catalog year (e.g., \'2024-2025\') in which this prerequisite rule was first published. Students are typically held to the prerequisite rules of their catalog year for degree planning and articulation agreement purposes.']
  class_standing_required varchar [note: 'Minimum academic class standing a student must have achieved to enroll in the controlling course. Derived from cumulative credit hours earned per institutional policy (e.g., \'junior\' requires 60+ credit hours).']
  concurrent_enrollment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a student may be simultaneously enrolled in the required course and the controlling course within the same term to satisfy a corequisite rule. True for corequisites; typically False for standard prerequisites.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prerequisite rule record was first created in the system of record (Ellucian Banner or the curriculum management system). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  curriculum_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the institutional curriculum committee formally approved this prerequisite rule. Required for accreditation documentation (AACSB, ABET, HLC) and academic quality assurance records.']
  dfw_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prerequisite rule was established or modified in response to analysis of the DFW (D-grade, F-grade, Withdrawal) rate for the controlling course. Supports institutional research and academic quality assurance reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this prerequisite rule becomes enforceable in the SIS registration system. Rules are applied to all registration attempts on or after this date for the applicable term.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The calendar date after which this prerequisite rule is no longer enforced. Null for rules with no planned end date. Used when a curriculum revision sunsets an existing prerequisite requirement.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this prerequisite rule was most recently reviewed by the curriculum committee or department, regardless of whether any changes were made. Supports periodic review cycles required by accreditation bodies (HLC, SACSCOC, AACSB).']
  minimum_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of credit hours (CR) a student must have earned overall or in a specific subject area prior to enrolling in the controlling course. Used for class-standing prerequisites (e.g., \'Junior standing: 60+ credit hours\').']
  minimum_gpa decimal(4,3) [note: 'Minimum cumulative GPA (Grade Point Average) a student must maintain to satisfy this prerequisite rule. Commonly used for upper-division or honors course prerequisites (e.g., 3.000 GPA required).']
  minimum_grade varchar [note: 'The minimum letter grade a student must have earned in the required course to satisfy this prerequisite. For example, \'C\' means a grade of C or better is required. Null when the rule type is \'test_score\' or when any passing grade is acceptable.']
  minimum_grade_points decimal(3,2) [note: 'Numeric GPA (Grade Point Average) equivalent of the minimum grade threshold (e.g., 2.00 for a C grade on a 4.0 scale). Supports automated SIS enforcement when letter-grade comparison is insufficient for transfer or non-standard grading schemes.']
  minimum_test_score decimal(7,2) [note: 'The minimum score a student must achieve on the specified standardized test (test_type / test_subject_code) to satisfy this prerequisite rule. Scale varies by test (e.g., 550 for SAT Math, 22 for ACT, 3 for AP exam).']
  nesting_level varchar [note: 'Depth of this rule within a nested Boolean condition tree (0 = top-level, 1 = first nested group, 2 = second nested group, etc.). Supports complex prerequisite structures such as \'(A AND B) OR (C AND D)\'.']
  override_permission_level varchar [note: 'Highest authority level permitted to grant a prerequisite override for a student who does not meet this rule. \'none\' means no override is allowed; \'instructor\' allows the course instructor to waive; \'department_chair\' requires chair approval; \'registrar\' requires Registrar office action; \'dean\' requires dean-level approval.']
  override_requires_documentation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prerequisite override request must be accompanied by supporting documentation (e.g., transfer transcript, test score report, instructor approval form) before the SIS will permit enrollment.']
  program_enrollment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student must be actively enrolled in a specific academic program or major to satisfy this prerequisite rule. When True, the program_id field identifies the required program.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Academic justification for why this prerequisite rule exists, as documented during the curriculum approval process. Used in accreditation self-studies (AACSB, ABET, HLC) to demonstrate alignment between course prerequisites and Student Learning Outcomes (SLOs).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Institution-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this prerequisite rule within the SIS (e.g., PREREQ-CALC1-001). Used for cross-system reference in Banner SFAREGS and academic advising tools.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable narrative description of the prerequisite rule as it appears in the course catalog and student-facing degree planning tools (e.g., \'Students must complete MATH 1510 with a grade of C or better, or achieve a score of 550 or higher on the SAT Math section\').']
  rule_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this rule within its rule group, controlling the evaluation sequence for nested Boolean logic. Lower sequence numbers are evaluated first.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the prerequisite rule. \'active\' rules are enforced in real-time SIS registration; \'pending_approval\' rules are awaiting curriculum committee sign-off; \'retired\' rules are preserved for historical audit.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the enrollment eligibility rule. \'prerequisite\' requires prior completion; \'corequisite\' requires concurrent enrollment; \'anti_requisite\' prohibits enrollment if the course was already taken; \'pre_or_corequisite\' allows either; \'test_score\' requires a qualifying standardized test score.']
  sis_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prerequisite rule is actively enforced in real-time by the Student Information System (SIS) during the Banner SFAREGS registration process. When False, the rule is advisory only and does not block enrollment.']
  test_subject_code varchar [note: 'Subject area code for the standardized test component that must meet the minimum score threshold (e.g., \'MATH\' for SAT Math, \'ENG\' for ACT English, \'CALC\' for AP Calculus AB). Used in conjunction with test_type and minimum_test_score.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of standardized test whose score may satisfy this prerequisite rule when rule_type is \'test_score\'. Includes SAT (Scholastic Assessment Test), ACT (American College Testing), AP (Advanced Placement), CLEP, TOEFL (Test of English as a Foreign Language), IELTS (International English Language Testing System), GRE (Graduate Record Examination), GMAT (Graduate Management Admission Test), or institutional PLACEMENT exam. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAT|ACT|AP|CLEP|TOEFL|IELTS|GRE|GMAT|PLACEMENT — promote to reference product]']
  transfer_credit_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an equivalent course completed at another institution and accepted as transfer credit can satisfy this prerequisite rule. When True, the SIS evaluates transfer equivalency records during registration validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this prerequisite rule record. Used for incremental ETL (Extract Transform Load) processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and for audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines prerequisite, corequisite, and anti-requisite relationships for courses, enforcing enrollment eligibility rules during registration. Captures the controlling course (the course requiring the prerequisite), the required course(s) or standardized test scores (SAT, ACT, AP, placement exam), minimum grade threshold (e.g., C or better), concurrent enrollment allowance for corequisites, override permission levels (instructor, department chair, registrar), and complex Boolean logic (AND/OR groupings, nested conditions). Supports real-time registration enforcement in SIS (Banner SFAREGS), academic advising, and degree planning tools.'
}

Table curriculum.slo {
  slo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Student Learning Outcome (SLO) record in the curriculum domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Optional reference to the academic program to which this SLO contributes, enabling curriculum mapping from course-level SLOs up to Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs). Nullable when the SLO is course-scoped only.']
  assessment_enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Student learning outcomes are assessed and tracked through specialized assessment management enterprise applications (Taskstream, Anthology, Watermark). Required for accreditation reporting, program r']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the course with which this Student Learning Outcome (SLO) is associated. Each SLO is scoped to a specific course in the curriculum catalog.']
  superseded_by_slo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLO record that replaces this one when a substantive revision creates a new SLO version. Nullable for current active SLOs. Enables curriculum lineage tracing and ensures historical assessment data can be linked to the correct outcome version.']
  aacsb_standard_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific AACSB International Assurance of Learning (AoL) standard or learning goal to which this SLO is aligned (e.g., \'LG1-LO2: Written Communication\'). Required for business school accreditation self-studies and continuous improvement reporting submitted to AACSB.']
  abet_outcome_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ABET Criterion 3 student outcome to which this SLO is aligned (e.g., \'Outcome 1: Apply knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering\'). Required for engineering and computing program accreditation self-studies submitted to ABET.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLO was formally approved by the curriculum committee or academic governance body. Required for accreditation self-study documentation to demonstrate systematic curriculum review processes.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of this SLO within the institutional curriculum approval workflow. Pending means submitted for review; approved means formally ratified by the curriculum committee; rejected means not approved; revision_required means returned to the originator for changes. Supports governance and QA processes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the curriculum committee, faculty governance body, or academic officer who formally approved this SLO. Provides an audit trail for accreditation documentation and institutional governance records.']
  assessment_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently this SLO is formally assessed and reported. Every_term means assessed each time the course is offered; annual means once per academic year; biennial means every two years; rotating means assessed on a departmentally defined rotation schedule. Drives assessment planning calendars.']
  assessment_level varchar [note: 'Scope at which this SLO is assessed: course-level (within a single course section), program-level (aggregated across multiple course sections within a program), or institutional-level (contributing to institution-wide general education or core competency assessment).']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to assess student attainment of this SLO (e.g., written exam, rubric-scored essay, lab report, oral presentation, portfolio, standardized test, capstone project). Informs Canvas LMS gradebook configuration and accreditation assessment plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written_exam|rubric_essay|lab_report|oral_presentation|portfolio|standardized_test|capstone_project|simulation|case_study|performance_task — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_tool varchar [note: 'Specific instrument or tool used to measure student performance on this SLO (e.g., AAC&U VALUE Rubric, ETS Major Field Test, departmental rubric name, standardized exam code). Provides traceability between the SLO and its measurement instrument for accreditation evidence.']
  banner_outcome_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this SLO as stored in Ellucian Banner\'s academic history and curriculum modules. Used for ETL reconciliation between Banner (system of record) and the Databricks Silver Layer data product.']
  blooms_domain varchar [note: 'Domain of learning as classified by Bloom\'s Taxonomy: cognitive (knowledge and intellectual skills), affective (attitudes, values, and feelings), or psychomotor (physical and procedural skills). Supports comprehensive curriculum design and accreditation reporting.']
  blooms_taxonomy_level varchar [note: 'Cognitive level of the learning outcome as classified by Bloom\'s Revised Taxonomy (Anderson & Krathwohl, 2001). Drives assessment design and curriculum rigor analysis. Values: remember, understand, apply, analyze, evaluate, create — ordered from lower-order to higher-order thinking.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) code associated with the discipline of this SLO, following the NCES taxonomy (e.g., 52.0301 for Accounting). Used for IPEDS reporting, program benchmarking, and cross-institutional curriculum comparisons.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLO record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail entry point for data governance, lineage tracking, and accreditation evidence of when outcomes were formally documented.']
  credit_hour_type varchar [note: 'Type of instructional activity associated with the course section in which this SLO is assessed. Relevant for determining appropriate assessment methods and Carnegie Unit compliance. Values align with federal credit hour definitions under 34 CFR 600.2.']
  curriculum_map_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the level at which this SLO is addressed in the curriculum map for the associated program: introduced (first exposure), developed (reinforced and practiced), or mastered (demonstrated at capstone level). Standard taxonomy used in AACSB and ABET curriculum mapping documentation.']
  delivery_modality varchar [note: 'Instructional delivery modality for which this SLO is applicable. Some SLOs may be modality-specific (e.g., lab-based outcomes only apply to face-to-face sections). Supports modality-disaggregated assessment reporting required by accreditors for distance education programs.']
  effective_term varchar [note: 'The academic term in which this SLO first became effective, expressed as a four-digit year followed by a term code (e.g., 2024FA for Fall 2024, 2025SP for Spring 2025). Supports term-over-term curriculum change tracking and accreditation cycle reporting.']
  faculty_lead varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the faculty member designated as the primary lead for assessing and reporting on this SLO. This individual is responsible for collecting assessment evidence, analyzing results, and submitting improvement plans to the curriculum committee.']
  hlc_criterion_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific HLC (Higher Learning Commission) Criterion for Accreditation to which this SLO contributes evidence (e.g., \'Criterion 4.B: The institution demonstrates a commitment to educational achievement and improvement through ongoing assessment of student learning\'). Supports regional accreditation self-study documentation.']
  is_core_outcome boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLO is designated as a core or primary outcome for the course (True) versus a supplementary or supporting outcome (False). Core outcomes are typically required to be assessed every term; supplementary outcomes may be assessed on a rotating schedule.']
  is_general_education boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLO is designated as a General Education (Gen Ed) outcome (True), meaning it contributes to the institution\'s general education assessment framework and may be reported to regional accreditors as evidence of broad learning competencies.']
  is_stem_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLO is associated with a STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) designated course or program. Relevant for NSF grant reporting, IPEDS STEM program classification, and OPT extension eligibility tracking for international students.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLO was most recently reviewed for currency, rigor, and alignment with program goals. Accreditors require evidence of systematic, ongoing review of learning outcomes. Supports identification of SLOs overdue for review.']
  lms_outcome_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this SLO as recorded in the Canvas LMS Outcomes module. Enables bidirectional traceability between the authoritative curriculum data product and the LMS gradebook alignment, supporting automated assessment data collection from Canvas.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this SLO as part of the institution\'s continuous improvement and assessment cycle. Enables proactive curriculum governance and ensures compliance with accreditor-mandated review frequencies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, clarifications, or implementation guidance related to this SLO. May include notes from curriculum committee reviews, faculty feedback, or accreditor recommendations. Not used for structured data.']
  oer_aligned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary instructional materials used to achieve this SLO are designated as OER (Open Educational Resources), meaning freely available and openly licensed. Supports institutional OER adoption tracking, affordability reporting, and grant compliance for OER initiatives.']
  outcome_statement varchar [note: 'Full narrative statement describing the measurable competency students are expected to demonstrate upon completing the course. Written in observable, measurable terms aligned with Bloom\'s Taxonomy (e.g., \'Students will be able to analyze financial statements using ratio analysis\'). This is the authoritative text used in syllabi, accreditation self-studies, and curriculum maps.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLO record. Active SLOs are in use for the current or upcoming academic term. Draft SLOs are under development. Under_review SLOs are in the curriculum approval workflow. Retired SLOs are no longer assessed but retained for historical reporting.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the academic department responsible for maintaining and assessing this SLO (e.g., \'Department of Accounting\', \'School of Engineering\'). Supports departmental assessment reporting, faculty workload attribution, and accreditation unit-level reporting.']
  performance_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of assessed students expected to meet or exceed the proficiency benchmark for this SLO, as defined in the assessment plan (e.g., 70.00 means 70% of students must demonstrate proficiency). Used to determine whether the SLO has been \'met\' in annual assessment reporting and accreditation self-studies.']
  plo_alignment varchar [note: 'Code or identifier of the Program Learning Outcome (PLO) to which this course-level SLO contributes, enabling curriculum mapping from SLO to PLO. Supports program-level assessment aggregation and accreditation curriculum maps. Distinct from program_id — this references the specific PLO statement, not the program entity.']
  proficiency_benchmark varchar [note: 'Qualitative or quantitative description of the minimum acceptable performance standard for this SLO (e.g., \'Score of 3 or higher on a 4-point rubric\', \'Correctly answers 75% of exam items mapped to this outcome\'). Complements performance_threshold_pct with narrative context for accreditation documentation.']
  retirement_term varchar [note: 'The academic term in which this SLO was retired or superseded. Nullable for active SLOs. Enables historical curriculum mapping and ensures retired outcomes are excluded from current assessment cycles while remaining available for longitudinal analysis.']
  sacscoc_standard_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SACSCOC (Southern Association of Colleges and Schools Commission on Colleges) Principles of Accreditation standard to which this SLO is aligned (e.g., \'Standard 8.2: Student Outcomes: Educational Programs\'). Applicable for institutions in the SACSCOC region.']
  scorm_objective_code varchar [note: 'Identifier mapping this SLO to a SCORM (Sharable Content Object Reference Model) learning objective within a packaged e-learning module deployed in the LMS. Enables automated tracking of objective completion data from SCORM-compliant content packages.']
  slo_code varchar [note: 'Institutionally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this SLO within the course or program context. Used in accreditation reports, curriculum maps, and assessment tracking systems. Example: ACCT301-SLO-001.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLO record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trails, and curriculum change management processes.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Integer version number of this SLO record, incremented each time the outcome statement, assessment method, or benchmark is substantively revised through the curriculum approval workflow. Enables longitudinal tracking of outcome evolution across accreditation cycles.']

  Note: 'Student Learning Outcome (SLO) master record defining the measurable competencies students are expected to demonstrate upon completing a course. Captures outcome statement, Bloom\'s taxonomy level, assessment method, alignment to accreditation standards (AACSB, ABET, HLC), active status, and the owning course or program. Serves as the foundational unit for course-level assessment and curriculum mapping. Distinct from PLO (Program Learning Outcome) which is program-scoped.'
}

Table curriculum.plo {
  plo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Program Learning Outcome (PLO) record in the curriculum data product.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic program (degree or certificate program) to which this PLO belongs. Links PLO to its parent program record.']
  assessment_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member or staff person designated as the primary lead responsible for coordinating assessment of this PLO within the program.']
  prior_version_plo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version of this PLO record if it was revised and a new record created. Enables curriculum change lineage tracking and accreditation historical documentation.']
  accreditation_standard_code varchar [note: 'Specific accreditation standard or criterion code to which this PLO is aligned (e.g., AACSB Standard 4, ABET Criterion 3, SACSCOC 8.2, HLC 4.B). Enables direct traceability from PLO to accreditor requirement in self-study reports.']
  accrediting_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accrediting body whose standards this PLO is designed to satisfy. Supports multi-accreditor programs (e.g., a business school may be subject to both AACSB and HLC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AACSB|ABET|SACSCOC|HLC|MSCHE|NWCCU|NECHE|WSCUC|ABA|LCME|none|other — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this PLO was formally approved through the institution\'s curriculum governance process (e.g., curriculum committee, faculty senate, academic affairs). Required for accreditation documentation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or title of the governance body or individual who formally approved this PLO (e.g., \'Undergraduate Curriculum Committee\', \'Dean of Academic Affairs\'). Provides audit trail for accreditation self-studies.']
  assessment_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this PLO is formally assessed and reported: annual, biennial (every two years), triennial (every three years), continuous, or other. Drives the assessment calendar and accreditation reporting schedule.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to assess student achievement of this PLO (e.g., capstone project, standardized exam, portfolio review, rubric-scored assignment, licensure exam, employer survey). Documented for accreditation assessment plans.']
  assessment_tool_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary instrument or tool used to assess this PLO (e.g., \'Capstone Project Rubric\', \'ETS Major Field Test\', \'NCLEX-RN\', \'CPA Exam\', \'Portfolio Assessment Rubric\'). Supports assessment plan documentation.']
  bloom_taxonomy_level varchar [note: 'Cognitive level of the learning outcome as classified by Bloom\'s Revised Taxonomy (Anderson & Krathwohl, 2001): remember, understand, apply, analyze, evaluate, or create. Used in curriculum design quality assurance and SLO alignment.']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'Academic catalog year in which this PLO was first published (e.g., \'2023-2024\'). Ties the PLO to the official program catalog for a given academic year, supporting student rights under catalog year policies.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code (XX.XXXX format) assigned by the U.S. Department of Education classifying the instructional program discipline to which this PLO belongs. Required for IPEDS reporting and federal program approval.']
  course_count varchar [note: 'Number of courses in the program curriculum that are mapped to this PLO. Provides a quick indicator of instructional coverage breadth. Note: authoritative count is derived from course-PLO mapping associations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PLO record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for curriculum governance and accreditation documentation.']
  degree_level varchar [note: 'Academic credential level of the program to which this PLO applies: certificate, associate, bachelor, master, doctoral, or professional. Determines the rigor and complexity expectations for the outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certificate|associate|bachelor|master|doctoral|professional|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  direct_indirect_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the primary assessment method for this PLO is direct (measures student performance on the outcome directly, e.g., exam, project), indirect (measures student perception or satisfaction, e.g., survey, alumni feedback), or both. Accreditors typically require at least one direct measure per PLO.']
  domain_area varchar [note: 'Disciplinary or thematic domain area to which this PLO belongs within the program (e.g., \'Quantitative Reasoning\', \'Ethical Leadership\', \'Technical Communication\', \'Research Methods\'). Supports curriculum mapping and outcome clustering for assessment reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this PLO became or becomes officially effective for the program. Marks the start of the assessment obligation for this outcome.']
  improvement_action_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting \'closing the loop\' actions taken based on assessment results for this PLO (e.g., curriculum changes, pedagogical adjustments, resource additions). Required by accreditors to demonstrate use of assessment data for improvement.']
  institutional_mission_alignment varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how this PLO aligns to the institution\'s stated mission, strategic plan, or core values. Required for regional accreditation self-studies demonstrating mission-driven curriculum design.']
  is_accreditation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PLO is mandated by a specific accrediting body\'s standards (True) or is an institutionally defined outcome beyond accreditor minimums (False). Supports gap analysis during accreditation self-study preparation.']
  is_core_outcome boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PLO is designated as a core or required outcome for all graduates of the program (True), as opposed to a concentration-specific or elective outcome (False). Core outcomes are typically required for all accreditation reporting.']
  is_general_education boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PLO is also designated as a General Education (Gen Ed) outcome applicable across multiple programs at the institution (True) or is program-specific (False). Supports institutional general education assessment reporting.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this PLO was most recently reviewed by the program faculty or curriculum committee, regardless of whether changes were made. Supports continuous improvement documentation required by accreditors.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date on which this PLO is next scheduled for formal review by the program faculty or curriculum committee. Drives the assessment calendar and ensures timely accreditation compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text administrative notes or contextual information about this PLO not captured in other structured fields. May include rationale for outcome design, cross-program alignment notes, or accreditor feedback.']
  outcome_category varchar [note: 'Broad category classifying the type of learning outcome: knowledge (cognitive understanding), skill (applied ability), disposition (attitude or value), competency (integrated performance), or other. Supports Bloom\'s Taxonomy alignment and accreditation reporting.']
  outcome_statement varchar [note: 'Full narrative statement describing the measurable competency, knowledge, skill, or disposition that graduates of the program are expected to demonstrate upon completion. Written in observable, assessable language per accreditation standards.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PLO: draft (being developed), active (in use for assessment), under_review (undergoing curriculum revision), suspended (temporarily paused), or retired (no longer assessed). Governs whether the PLO appears in active assessment plans.']
  plo_code varchar [note: 'Institutionally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this PLO within the program (e.g., PLO-01, CS-PLO-03). Used in accreditation self-studies and curriculum mapping documents.']
  proficiency_scale_max decimal(7,2) [note: 'Maximum possible score on the assessment instrument used to measure this PLO (e.g., 4.0 for a rubric, 100 for a percentage scale). Provides context for interpreting the proficiency threshold score.']
  proficiency_threshold_score decimal(7,2) [note: 'Minimum score or rubric rating a student must achieve on the designated assessment instrument to be counted as meeting this PLO. Expressed in the scale of the assessment tool (e.g., 3.0 on a 4-point rubric, 70 on a 100-point scale).']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which this PLO was or will be retired and removed from active assessment plans. Null if the PLO is currently active. Supports historical curriculum audit trails.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this PLO within the program\'s ordered list of learning outcomes (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Used for display ordering in accreditation reports, syllabi, and assessment plans.']
  slo_count varchar [note: 'Number of Student Learning Outcomes (SLOs) currently mapped to this PLO through the slo_plo_mapping association. Provides a quick indicator of curriculum coverage depth for this outcome. Note: this is a denormalized convenience field; authoritative count is derived from slo_plo_mapping.']
  strategic_goal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or label of the institutional strategic plan goal or objective to which this PLO contributes (e.g., \'Strategic Goal 2: Student Success\'). Supports institutional effectiveness reporting.']
  target_benchmark_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of assessed students expected to meet or exceed the proficiency threshold for this PLO (e.g., 70.00 means 70% of students must demonstrate proficiency). Used as the success criterion in assessment reporting and accreditation self-studies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PLO record was most recently modified. Supports change detection in ETL pipelines and curriculum governance audit trails.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this PLO statement (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented when the outcome statement or assessment criteria are substantively revised. Supports curriculum change history and accreditation audit trails.']

  Note: 'Program Learning Outcome (PLO) master record defining the measurable competencies graduates of an academic program are expected to demonstrate. Captures outcome statement, associated accreditation standard (AACSB, ABET, SACSCOC), assessment cycle, target performance benchmark, and alignment to institutional mission. PLOs are mapped to SLOs through the slo_plo_mapping association. Supports program-level assessment reporting and accreditation self-studies.'
}

Table curriculum.map {
  map_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each curriculum map record linking a course to a Program Learning Outcome (PLO) within an academic program. Serves as the primary key for this Silver Layer data product.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic degree or certificate program within which this curriculum map entry exists. Establishes the program-level context for PLO alignment.']
  assessment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assessment instrument (e.g., rubric, exam, project, portfolio) used within this course to measure student attainment of the mapped PLO.']
  compliance_accreditation_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_standard. Business justification: Curriculum maps demonstrate how course sequences meet specific accreditor standards. Essential for accreditation self-studies showing standards compliance through curriculum design. Core accreditation']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific course in the course catalog that is mapped to the PLO. Identifies which course contributes to the learning outcome.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member (typically the course coordinator or program director) responsible for maintaining this curriculum map entry and ensuring PLO assessment is conducted. Links to the HR employee record.']
  mapping_enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Curriculum maps are created and maintained in specialized enterprise applications for accreditation compliance and program assessment. Accreditation bodies (AACSB, ABET, HLC) require documented curric']
  plo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Program Learning Outcome (PLO) that this course is mapped to. PLOs define the competencies graduates are expected to demonstrate upon program completion.']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'The academic year in which this curriculum map entry is effective (e.g., \'2024-2025\'). Supports versioning of curriculum maps across catalog years and accreditation review cycles.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The accreditation organization whose standards govern this curriculum map entry (e.g., \'AACSB\', \'ABET\', \'HLC\', \'SACSCOC\', \'LCME\', \'ABA\'). Determines which assurance-of-learning or criterion-based reporting requirements apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AACSB|ABET|HLC|SACSCOC|MSCHE|NWCCU|NECHE|WSCUC|LCME|ABA|AACSB_ABET — promote to reference product]']
  accreditation_standard_ref varchar [note: 'Specific accreditation standard or criterion that this curriculum map entry satisfies (e.g., \'AACSB Standard 8 - Assurance of Learning\', \'ABET Criterion 3 - Student Outcomes\'). Enables direct traceability from curriculum map to accreditation evidence.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment of the PLO in this course uses direct evidence (e.g., exams, projects, rubric-scored artifacts), indirect evidence (e.g., surveys, self-reports), or both. Accreditation bodies require a mix of direct and indirect assessment evidence.']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'The academic catalog year under which this curriculum map entry is published (e.g., \'2024-2025\'). Students follow degree requirements from their catalog year; curriculum maps must be versioned accordingly for compliance and advising.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'The six-digit CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) code assigned to the academic program by the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES). Used for federal reporting (IPEDS), accreditation, and program classification (e.g., \'11.0701\' for Computer Science).']
  closing_loop_action varchar [note: 'Description of the curricular or instructional improvement action taken in response to PLO assessment results for this course-PLO mapping (e.g., \'Added additional case studies to reinforce analytical reasoning PLO\', \'Revised rubric criteria for written communication\'). Core component of AACSB Assurance of Learning closing-the-loop documentation.']
  college_name varchar [note: 'Name of the college or school within the institution that houses the academic program (e.g., \'College of Engineering\', \'School of Business\'). Used for accreditation unit-level reporting (AACSB, ABET) and institutional analytics.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the depth at which the PLO is addressed in this course: I = Introduced (first exposure), R = Reinforced (practiced and developed), M = Mastered (demonstrated at program exit level). This I/R/M taxonomy is the standard framework used in AACSB Assurance of Learning and ABET Criterion 3 curriculum mapping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this curriculum map record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for data governance and accreditation evidence management.']
  credit_hours decimal(4,1) [note: 'Number of credit hours (CR) assigned to the course in this curriculum map entry. Used to weight the course\'s contribution to the program\'s total credit hour requirements and to calculate PLO coverage intensity across the curriculum.']
  curriculum_committee_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the curriculum committee formally approved this curriculum map entry. Establishes the official governance record for accreditation audit trails and catalog publication timelines.']
  degree_level varchar [note: 'The level of the academic credential associated with this program (e.g., \'bachelor\', \'master\', \'doctoral\'). Used to segment curriculum maps by degree tier for accreditation reporting and institutional analytics.']
  delivery_modality varchar [note: 'The instructional delivery format for the course in this curriculum map entry: \'in_person\' = face-to-face; \'online\' = fully asynchronous/synchronous online; \'hybrid\' = blended in-person and online; \'hyflex\' = simultaneous in-person and online; \'mooc\' = Massive Open Online Course (MOOC). Relevant for accreditation distance education reviews.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the academic department responsible for delivering the course in this curriculum map entry (e.g., \'CS\', \'MGMT\', \'ENGR\'). Used for departmental curriculum reporting and accreditation unit-level analysis.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this curriculum map entry ceases to be effective, typically aligned with a catalog year transition or program revision. Null indicates the entry is currently active. Supports historical curriculum map versioning.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this curriculum map entry is effective and applicable to students enrolled under the corresponding catalog year. Supports temporal versioning of curriculum maps across accreditation cycles.']
  gap_analysis_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting identified gaps in PLO coverage, redundancies, or areas requiring curriculum revision as identified during curriculum gap analysis reviews. Supports closing-the-loop actions in Assurance of Learning (AoL) cycles.']
  is_capstone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this course serves as a capstone or culminating experience for the program. Capstone courses are typically the primary site for PLO mastery-level assessment and are highlighted in accreditation self-studies.']
  is_oer_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course uses Open Educational Resources (OER) as the primary instructional materials, eliminating or significantly reducing textbook costs for students. Supports institutional OER adoption tracking and affordability reporting.']
  is_writing_intensive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this course is designated as writing intensive, requiring substantial written work as a primary mode of learning and assessment. Relevant for general education requirements, accreditation, and curriculum gap analysis for written communication PLOs.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this curriculum map entry was most recently reviewed by the program faculty or curriculum committee. Supports accreditation requirements for regular curriculum review cycles (typically annual or per accreditation cycle).']
  map_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this curriculum map entry within the curriculum approval workflow. \'draft\' = under initial development; \'active\' = approved and in use; \'under_review\' = submitted for curriculum committee review; \'approved\' = formally approved by governance body; \'retired\' = no longer in use.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date by which this curriculum map entry is scheduled for its next formal review. Supports proactive curriculum quality assurance and ensures compliance with accreditation body review cycle requirements.']
  performance_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of students expected to meet or exceed the proficiency threshold on the PLO assessment in this course (e.g., 70.00 means 70% of students must achieve the target score). Used in Assurance of Learning (AoL) closing-the-loop analysis.']
  proficiency_threshold_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum score or rubric rating a student must achieve on the assessment instrument to be considered proficient on the mapped PLO (e.g., 3.0 on a 4-point rubric, or 75.00 on a 100-point scale). Defines the benchmark for PLO attainment.']
  slo_alignment varchar [note: 'Description of how the course-level Student Learning Outcome (SLO) aligns with and contributes to the mapped Program Learning Outcome (PLO). Provides the narrative linkage between course-level and program-level assessment for accreditation documentation.']
  term_sequence_position varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the course within the program\'s recommended term sequence (e.g., 1 = first term, 4 = fourth term). Used to visualize the progression of PLO coverage across the curriculum and identify gaps in the learning sequence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this curriculum map record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and identification of stale curriculum map entries requiring review.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Integer version number of this curriculum map entry, incremented each time the mapping is revised through the curriculum approval workflow. Supports audit trail and historical comparison of curriculum map changes across accreditation cycles.']

  Note: 'Structured mapping of courses within an academic program to the program\'s PLOs, showing where each outcome is introduced, reinforced, and mastered across the curriculum sequence. Captures the program, the course, the PLO, the coverage level (I/R/M), the term sequence position, and the assessment instrument used. Supports accreditation reporting (AACSB Assurance of Learning, ABET Criterion 3) and curriculum gap analysis.'
}

Table curriculum.course_proposal {
  course_proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the course proposal record in the curriculum management system. Primary key for this entity.']
  effective_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Course proposals specify the academic term when the new/revised course becomes available for registration. Curriculum approval workflow requires term context for SIS integration, catalog publication, ']
  existing_course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.course. Business justification: When proposal_type is \'modify\' or \'discontinue\', the proposal must reference the existing course being modified or discontinued. This FK enables the workflow to link back to the authoritative course r']
  it_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.service_request. Business justification: New course proposals trigger IT service requests for LMS course shell creation, software license provisioning, classroom technology setup, and specialized lab software installation. Workflow automatio']
  library_resource_bib_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to library.bib_record. Business justification: New course proposals require library review to verify adequate resources exist. Curriculum approval workflow includes library impact statement citing specific holdings. The library_resource_adequate_f']
  primary_course_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic department submitting the course proposal. Links to the organizational unit responsible for the proposed course.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: New course proposals must verify compliance with ADA accessibility standards, copyright law, and accreditation requirements. Required for curriculum approval workflows and accreditation documentation.']
  submitter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty or staff member who formally submitted the course proposal. Typically the course coordinator or department chair.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the specialized accreditation body (e.g., AACSB, ABET, ABA, LCME) whose standards this course is designed to satisfy or contribute toward. Null if the course is not tied to a specialized accreditor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AACSB|ABET|ABA|LCME|ACEN|CAEP|NASM — promote to reference product]']
  banner_course_nmbr varchar [note: 'The internal Banner Student Information System (SIS) course number assigned upon successful integration of the approved proposal into the SCACRSE course master. Null until Banner integration is complete.']
  banner_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of the data integration process that loads the approved course proposal into the Ellucian Banner course master (SCACRSE). Tracks whether the approved course has been successfully activated in the Student Information System (SIS).']
  catalog_description varchar [note: 'Official narrative description of the course content, learning objectives, and instructional approach as it will appear in the institutional academic catalog. Subject to curriculum committee review for clarity and accuracy.']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'Academic catalog year (e.g., 2024-2025) in which the proposed course change will first appear. Used for degree audit catalog locking and student program requirement tracking.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) code assigned to the proposed course per the U.S. Department of Education taxonomy. Required for IPEDS reporting, state funding formulas, and program accreditation alignment.']
  college_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the college-level curriculum committee or dean formally approved the course proposal, advancing it to the institutional curriculum committee.']
  committee_vote_date date [note: 'Date on which the institutional curriculum committee conducted its formal vote on the course proposal. Recorded for governance transparency and accreditation audit trail.']
  committee_vote_outcome varchar [note: 'Formal outcome of the institutional curriculum committee vote on the course proposal. Drives subsequent workflow routing to provost office or back to the submitting department.']
  contact_hours_lab decimal(4,2) [note: 'Weekly scheduled laboratory or studio contact hours for the proposed course. Distinct from lecture hours for workload, scheduling, and accreditation compliance calculations.']
  contact_hours_lecture decimal(4,2) [note: 'Weekly scheduled lecture contact hours for the proposed course. Used to verify compliance with the federal credit hour definition and accreditor seat-time requirements.']
  corequisite_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of courses or conditions that must be taken concurrently with this course. Displayed in the academic catalog and enforced in Banner registration controls.']
  course_level varchar [note: 'Academic level classification of the proposed course indicating the student population for which it is designed. Drives degree audit rules, financial aid eligibility, and accreditation reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the course proposal record was first created in the curriculum management system, including draft saves prior to formal submission.']
  credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of academic credit hours (CR) assigned to the proposed course. Determines student load calculations, financial aid eligibility, degree audit progress, and faculty workload. Aligns with federal credit hour definition (34 CFR 600.2).']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Instructional delivery modality for the proposed course. Impacts room scheduling, technology resource requirements, financial aid eligibility for distance education, and accreditor substantive change review.']
  department_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the department curriculum committee or department chair formally approved the course proposal, advancing it to the college-level review stage.']
  effective_term varchar [note: 'Banner term code (YYYYTT format, e.g., 202501 for Spring 2025) in which the approved course action takes effect in the course master and academic catalog. Populated upon final approval.']
  grading_basis varchar [note: 'Method by which student performance in the proposed course will be evaluated and recorded on the transcript. Affects GPA calculation, Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) determination, and degree audit.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the course proposal record, capturing any field-level change, status transition, or approver action.']
  library_resource_adequate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution\'s library resources (print, digital, Alma/Ex Libris holdings) are deemed adequate to support the proposed course, as confirmed by the library liaison review. Required for regional accreditation compliance.']
  max_enrollment varchar [note: 'Proposed maximum number of students permitted to enroll in a single section of this course. Informs room scheduling, faculty workload planning, and Archibus space utilization.']
  oer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed course is designated to use Open Educational Resources (OER) as the primary instructional materials, eliminating or significantly reducing student textbook costs. Supports institutional affordability reporting.']
  prerequisite_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of prerequisite courses, minimum grade requirements, test scores, or other conditions students must satisfy before enrolling. Displayed in the academic catalog and enforced in Banner registration controls.']
  proposal_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the course proposal upon submission (e.g., CP-2024-00123). Used in curriculum committee agendas, approval letters, and Banner integration.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the course proposal within the institutional curriculum approval workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|withdrawn|deferred — promote to reference product]']
  proposal_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the curriculum action being requested: new course creation, modification of an existing course, deactivation/retirement, reactivation of a previously deactivated course, or experimental/pilot offering.']
  provost_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the Provost or Chief Academic Officer granted final institutional approval for the course proposal, authorizing Banner course master integration.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative justification provided by the proposing department explaining the academic need, student demand, program alignment, or strategic rationale for the proposed course action. Required by curriculum committee for deliberation.']
  repeat_credit_limit decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum total credit hours a student may earn by repeating this course. Applicable only when repeatable_flag is true. Used in degree audit and financial aid SAP calculations.']
  repeatable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students may enroll in this course more than once for additional credit (e.g., independent study, thesis, ensemble). When true, the repeat_credit_limit field governs maximum allowable credit accumulation.']
  resource_impact_statement varchar [note: 'Narrative assessment of the fiscal, staffing, library, technology, and facilities resources required to support the proposed course. Reviewed by college dean and provost office for budget and capacity planning.']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Abbreviated course title (typically 30 characters or fewer) used in Banner transcript display, schedule of classes, and systems with character-length constraints.']
  slo_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the Student Learning Outcomes (SLOs) for the proposed course, describing what students will know, value, or be able to do upon successful completion. Required for accreditation and curriculum quality assurance.']
  stem_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed course is classified as a Science, Technology, Engineering, or Mathematics (STEM) course per DHS/SEVIS STEM CIP code list. Relevant for international student visa compliance and OPT extension eligibility.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the course proposal was formally submitted by the originating department into the curriculum approval workflow. Serves as the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi-level curriculum approval workflow indicating which governance body currently holds the proposal for review or action (department, college, curriculum committee, provost office, or registrar for Banner integration).']
  writing_intensive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed course is designated as writing intensive, requiring substantial written work as a core pedagogical component. Used in general education requirement tracking and accreditation reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal workflow for proposing a new course, modifying an existing course, or deactivating a course through the institutional curriculum approval process. Tracks proposal type (new, modify, deactivate), submitting department, proposed changes, rationale, resource impact statement, workflow stage (department, college, curriculum committee, provost), approver actions, vote outcomes, and effective term. Integrates with Banner course master upon final approval.'
}

Table curriculum.program_proposal {
  program_proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the program proposal record. Primary key for tracking the formal approval workflow to create, modify, suspend, or discontinue an academic program.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the existing academic program if this proposal is a modification, suspension, or discontinuation. Null for new program proposals.']
  department_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic department responsible for delivering the proposed program curriculum and managing faculty resources.']
  it_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.service_request. Business justification: New academic program proposals trigger IT service requests for infrastructure provisioning, application access, network resources, and specialized software. Program launch checklists require IT readin']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the college or school that will house the proposed program and provide administrative oversight.']
  proposal_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member or administrator who is the primary author and champion of the program proposal, responsible for shepherding it through the approval process.']
  proposed_effective_term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term when the proposed program is planned to launch or when modifications will take effect, critical for catalog publication and student recruitment timelines.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: New program proposals require extensive regulatory review including HLC substantive change, state authorization, Title IV eligibility determination. Essential for program approval process and regulato']
  state_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_authorization. Business justification: New programs trigger state authorization applications, especially for online delivery. Links proposal workflow to authorization tracking. Critical for launch planning and regulatory compliance timelin']
  supporting_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to library.collection. Business justification: New program proposals require library impact assessment demonstrating collection depth and breadth. Accreditation standards mandate documented library support before program approval. The library_reso']
  accreditation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized programmatic accreditation is required for the proposed program to operate, grant degrees, or enable graduates to pursue professional licensure.']
  board_of_trustees_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the institutional governing board granted final approval for the program proposal, the ultimate authority for new program authorization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the program proposal record was first created in the curriculum management system, supporting audit trail and workflow tracking.']
  curriculum_committee_vote_date date [note: 'Date when the institutional curriculum committee formally voted on the program proposal, representing the first major governance milestone.']
  curriculum_committee_vote_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the curriculum committee vote on the program proposal, determining whether it advances to the next governance stage.']
  estimated_annual_operating_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected recurring annual expenses to operate the proposed program at steady state, including faculty salaries, staff support, facilities, and supplies.']
  estimated_annual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected annual tuition and fee revenue generated by the proposed program at steady-state enrollment, used for financial viability and return on investment (ROI) analysis.']
  estimated_new_faculty_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total full-time equivalent faculty positions that must be hired to support the proposed program, critical for budget planning and accreditation compliance.']
  estimated_startup_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total one-time costs required to launch the proposed program, including curriculum development, faculty recruitment, equipment purchases, and marketing expenses.']
  external_accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of specialized programmatic accreditor that will review this program (e.g., AACSB for business, ABET for engineering, ABA for law, LCME for medicine), if applicable.']
  facility_resource_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of classroom space, laboratory facilities, specialized equipment, technology infrastructure, and physical resources needed to support the proposed program.']
  faculty_resource_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of new faculty positions, reassigned faculty FTE (Full-Time Equivalent), adjunct needs, and faculty qualifications required to deliver the proposed program.']
  faculty_senate_vote_date date [note: 'Date when the faculty senate or academic council formally voted on the program proposal, representing faculty governance approval.']
  faculty_senate_vote_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the faculty senate vote on the program proposal, critical for shared governance compliance.']
  final_approval_date date [note: 'Date when all required approvals (internal governance, regional accreditation, state authorization, specialized accreditation) were secured and the program is authorized to launch.']
  hlc_approval_date date [note: 'Date when HLC granted approval for the substantive change, if required. Program cannot launch until this approval is received.']
  hlc_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the institution formally notified HLC of the substantive change, required to maintain regional accreditation compliance.']
  hlc_substantive_change_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed program triggers HLC substantive change notification or approval requirements based on degree level, modality, or location criteria.']
  is_stem_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed program qualifies for STEM designation, which affects international student visa extensions (OPT) and federal grant eligibility.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the program proposal record was most recently updated, tracking the evolution of the proposal through the review and revision process.']
  library_resource_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of new library holdings, databases, journals, and information resources needed to support the proposed program curriculum and student research.']
  market_demand_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of labor market demand, employer needs, student interest, and competitive landscape supporting the need for this program. Required for HLC substantive change and state approval processes.']
  projected_enrollment_year_one varchar [note: 'Estimated number of students expected to enroll in the first year of program operation, used for resource planning and financial viability assessment.']
  projected_enrollment_year_three varchar [note: 'Estimated steady-state enrollment by the third year of program operation, used for long-term resource allocation and sustainability analysis.']
  proposal_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the program proposal, typically formatted as department code, year, and sequence number (e.g., ENGR-2024-001).']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program proposal in the approval workflow, tracking progression through institutional governance stages from draft through final board approval or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|curriculum_committee_review|faculty_senate_review|provost_review|board_review|approved|rejected|withdrawn|on_hold — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proposal_type varchar [note: 'Type of program proposal action being requested: new program creation, modification to existing program, temporary suspension, permanent discontinuation, reactivation of suspended program, or change in degree level.']
  proposed_cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code that classifies the proposed program\'s instructional content area for federal reporting (IPEDS) and program taxonomy.']
  proposed_degree_designation varchar [note: 'Specific degree designation or abbreviation for the proposed program (e.g., BS, BA, MS, MBA, PhD, EdD, MD, JD).']
  proposed_degree_level varchar [note: 'Academic level of the proposed program aligned with IPEDS award level classifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certificate|associate|bachelor|post_baccalaureate_certificate|master|post_master_certificate|specialist|doctoral_research|doctoral_professional|post_doctoral_certificate — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proposed_delivery_modality varchar [note: 'Primary instructional delivery format for the proposed program, critical for state authorization and accreditation compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: on_campus|fully_online|hybrid|competency_based|accelerated|evening_weekend|low_residency — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proposed_program_title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the proposed academic program as it will appear in the catalog, marketing materials, and on transcripts.']
  proposed_total_credit_hours varchar [note: 'Total number of credit hours required for completion of the proposed program, used for Title IV financial aid eligibility and program length determination.']
  provost_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the chief academic officer (provost or vice president for academic affairs) approved the program proposal, representing administrative endorsement.']
  state_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed program requires approval from state higher education agencies, particularly critical for online programs under State Authorization Reciprocity Agreement (SARA) rules.']
  state_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of state-level approval process for the proposed program, tracking compliance with state higher education regulatory requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the program proposal was formally submitted to the curriculum governance process, marking the start of the official review timeline.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for the formal approval workflow to create, modify, suspend, or discontinue an academic program. Captures proposal type, degree level, CIP code, market demand justification, resource requirements, external accreditation implications, state authorization requirements, HLC substantive change notification status, curriculum committee votes, board of trustees approval, and effective implementation term. Distinct from course_proposal in scope, governance pathway, and regulatory implications.'
}

Table curriculum.articulation_agreement {
  articulation_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the articulation agreement record. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic program at the receiving institution into which students transfer under this agreement.']
  partner_institution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner institution with which this articulation agreement is established.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Transfer agreements must comply with federal credit hour definitions, state transfer mandates, and accreditation standards for credit acceptance. Required for agreement approval and audit documentatio']
  active_student_count varchar [note: 'Current number of students enrolled under this articulation agreement pathway.']
  agreement_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the articulation agreement, used for external reference and reporting.']
  agreement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or clarifications regarding the articulation agreement.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the articulation agreement.']
  agreement_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the articulation agreement, including partner institution and program pathway.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the articulation agreement structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2+2 pathway|3+1 pathway|course-by-course equivalency|statewide transfer compact|reverse transfer|dual enrollment|concurrent enrollment — promote to reference product]']
  agreement_url varchar [note: 'Web address where the full text of the articulation agreement document is published for student and advisor access.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the articulation agreement was approved by the governing body or curriculum committee.']
  approving_body varchar [note: 'Name of the committee, board, or authority that approved the articulation agreement (e.g., Faculty Senate, Board of Trustees, State Higher Education Commission).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the articulation agreement automatically renews at the end of the renewal term without explicit action.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code identifying the instructional program area covered by this articulation agreement.']
  contact_person_home varchar [note: 'Name and contact information of the primary point of contact at the home institution for questions about this articulation agreement.']
  contact_person_partner varchar [note: 'Name and contact information of the primary point of contact at the partner institution for questions about this articulation agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the articulation agreement record was first created in the system.']
  degree_level_from varchar [note: 'Degree or credential level at the sending institution that qualifies for transfer under this agreement.']
  degree_level_to varchar [note: 'Degree or credential level at the receiving institution into which students transfer.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the articulation agreement expires or is no longer valid. Null indicates open-ended agreement.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the articulation agreement becomes binding and operational for transfer students.']
  governing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the articulation agreement is governed or mandated.']
  guaranteed_admission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students meeting the agreement criteria are guaranteed admission to the receiving institution and program.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the articulation agreement was last reviewed for currency and compliance.']
  min_gpa_required decimal(3,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative GPA a student must maintain at the partner institution to qualify for transfer under this agreement.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the articulation agreement.']
  partner_fice_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit FICE code identifying the partner institution in federal education databases.']
  partner_institution_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the partner institution as registered with accreditation agencies.']
  partner_institution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner institution by Carnegie classification or state designation.']
  partner_opeid varchar [note: 'Eight-digit OPEID number assigned by the U.S. Department of Education to the partner institution for Title IV financial aid administration.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after which the articulation agreement must be reviewed and renewed.']
  residency_requirement_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of credit hours that must be completed at the receiving institution to satisfy residency requirements under this agreement.']
  scholarship_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students transferring under this articulation agreement are eligible for special transfer scholarships or financial aid.']
  sending_program_code varchar [note: 'Program code or credential designation at the partner (sending) institution that qualifies for this articulation pathway.']
  signatory_authority_home varchar [note: 'Name and title of the authorized signatory from the home institution who executed the agreement.']
  signatory_authority_partner varchar [note: 'Name and title of the authorized signatory from the partner institution who executed the agreement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the articulation agreement was formally signed by both parties.']
  state_mandate_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the state-level transfer mandate or legislation governing this agreement, if applicable.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the articulation agreement was formally terminated or discontinued, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the articulation agreement was terminated (e.g., program discontinuation, partner accreditation loss, state policy change).']
  total_credit_hours_transferable decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum number of credit hours that can transfer under this articulation agreement.']
  total_students_transferred varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of students who have successfully transferred under this articulation agreement since inception.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the articulation agreement record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for formal articulation agreements between the institution and partner colleges, universities, or systems, defining how courses or credentials transfer and satisfy requirements. Captures partner institution (FICE/OPEID code), agreement type (2+2 pathway, 3+1, course-by-course equivalency, statewide transfer compact, reverse transfer), effective date range, renewal terms, signatory authorities, governing state mandate (if applicable), total credit hours transferable, and agreement status (draft, active, expired, terminated). Supports transfer student advising, state articulation compliance reporting (e.g., state transfer module requirements), and strategic enrollment partnership management.'
}

Table curriculum.transfer_equivalency {
  transfer_equivalency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transfer equivalency mapping record. Primary key for the transfer equivalency entity.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific academic program if this equivalency mapping is restricted to students in that program. Null if the mapping applies broadly across programs.']
  articulation_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent articulation agreement under which this course equivalency is defined. Links this equivalency to the formal agreement between sending and receiving institutions.']
  effective_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Transfer equivalencies become effective at specific term start dates for transfer credit evaluation. Registrar operations require term-specific equivalency activation for articulation agreement implem']
  primary_transfer_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically registrar, department chair, or curriculum committee member) who approved this transfer equivalency mapping.']
  receiving_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific course at the receiving institution that the sending course maps to. Null if the equivalency is to elective credit or a requirement group rather than a specific course.']
  receiving_institution_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the institution receiving the transfer student. The institution that will award equivalent credit based on this mapping.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Transfer credit evaluations must comply with federal credit hour regulations, accreditation standards for credit acceptance, and state transfer policies. Required for financial aid eligibility and acc']
  sending_institution_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the institution from which the student is transferring. The originating institution whose course is being evaluated for equivalency.']
  applies_to_degree_level varchar [note: 'The degree level(s) to which this equivalency mapping applies. Restricts the use of the equivalency to specific academic levels.']
