  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this transfer equivalency mapping was officially approved for use in transfer credit evaluations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this equivalency mapping. Tracks whether the mapping has been reviewed and approved by the appropriate academic authority.']
  counts_toward_residency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credit awarded through this equivalency counts toward the receiving institutions residency credit hour requirement for degree completion. False if the credit is transfer credit that does not count toward residency.']
  course_content_match_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of how closely the sending course content aligns with the receiving course content. Exact: nearly identical. Substantial: 75%+ overlap. Partial: 50-74% overlap. Minimal: less than 50% overlap but still transferable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this transfer equivalency record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_hours_awarded decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of credit hours (CR) awarded by the receiving institution for successful completion of the sending course. May differ from the sending course credit hours based on institutional policy.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this transfer equivalency mapping expires or is no longer valid for new evaluations. Null if the mapping is open-ended. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this transfer equivalency mapping becomes active and can be applied to transfer credit evaluations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  equivalency_type varchar [note: 'The type of equivalency mapping. Direct: one-to-one course match. Block: multiple courses map to a block of credit. Elective: maps to elective credit hours. General Education: satisfies a general education requirement. Major Requirement: satisfies a major-specific requirement. No Credit: course does not transfer for credit.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the rationale for the equivalency mapping, special conditions, or instructions for transfer credit evaluators. May include syllabus comparison details or accreditation considerations.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this equivalency mapping in the transfer credit evaluation workflow. Active: currently in use. Inactive: temporarily disabled. Pending Review: awaiting approval. Superseded: replaced by newer mapping. Expired: past effective end date.']
  external_equivalency_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this equivalency mapping in a third-party transfer credit system or statewide articulation database (e.g., state transfer portal, ASSIST system in California).']
  is_reciprocal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equivalency mapping is reciprocal (bidirectional). True if the receiving institution course also maps back to the sending institution course in reverse transfer scenarios.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this transfer equivalency record was most recently updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this equivalency mapping was last reviewed for accuracy and currency. Used to track periodic review cycles required by accreditation or institutional policy. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  learning_outcome_alignment varchar [note: 'Assessment of how well the sending course learning outcomes align with the receiving course Student Learning Outcomes (SLO). Used for accreditation and quality assurance purposes.']
  max_credit_applicable decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum number of credit hours (CR) that can be awarded through this equivalency mapping, even if the sending course carries more credit. Used to cap transfer credit in certain scenarios.']
  min_grade_required varchar [note: 'The minimum grade the student must earn in the sending course for the equivalency to apply and credit to be awarded (e.g., C, C-, D). Null if no minimum grade is specified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this equivalency mapping. Ensures mappings remain current with curriculum changes. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  prerequisite_waived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion of the sending course waives prerequisite requirements for subsequent courses at the receiving institution. True if the equivalency satisfies prerequisite chains.']
  requirement_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the degree requirement group or category that this equivalency satisfies (e.g., CORE-MATH, GEN-ED-SCI, MAJOR-ELEC). Used when the equivalency maps to a requirement rather than a specific course.']
  sending_course_code varchar [note: 'The course code or number at the sending institution that is being mapped for transfer credit. Example: MATH 101, ENG 1301.']
  sending_course_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of credit hours (CR) assigned to the course at the sending institution. Used to determine how much credit should be awarded at the receiving institution.']
  sending_course_subject varchar [note: 'The academic subject or discipline area of the sending course (e.g., Mathematics, English, Biology). Used for classification and mapping purposes.']
  sending_course_title varchar [note: 'The official title of the course at the sending institution as it appears in their course catalog.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system or module where this equivalency mapping was originally created (e.g., Banner SHATRCE, TES, custom articulation system). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']

  Note: 'Defines the course-level equivalency mappings within an articulation agreement, specifying how a specific course at a sending institution maps to a course or requirement at the receiving institution. Captures sending institution course, receiving institution course or requirement group, equivalency type (direct, block, elective credit), credit hours awarded, effective term range, and evaluation status. Supports transfer credit evaluation workflows and degree audit processing for transfer students.'
}

Table curriculum.program_accreditation {
  program_accreditation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the program accreditation record. Primary key for tracking specialized accreditation status and review cycles.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic program subject to specialized accreditation review.']
  primary_program_director_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member serving as the academic program director responsible for accreditation compliance and reporting.']
  tertiary_program_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last updated this accreditation record.']
  accreditation_decision_date date [note: 'The date when the accrediting body officially communicated its accreditation decision following the review.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the current accreditation status expires and renewal or reaffirmation is required.']
  accreditation_fees_annual decimal(12,2) [note: 'The annual membership or maintenance fees charged by the accrediting body for maintaining accredited status.']
  accreditation_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level at which accreditation is granted: program (specific degree program), department (academic unit), college (school within university), or institutional (entire institution).']
  accreditation_standards_version varchar [note: 'The version or edition of the accrediting body\'s standards under which the program is being reviewed (e.g., ABET 2023-2024 Criteria, AACSB 2020 Standards).']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation standing of the program: accredited (full approval), candidate (pre-accreditation status), probation (conditional approval with concerns), withdrawn (voluntarily removed), denied (application rejected), expired (lapsed without renewal).']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'The type of accreditation review being conducted: initial (first-time accreditation), continuing (renewal), reaffirmation (periodic review), specialized (discipline-specific), or conditional (provisional status).']
  accreditation_url varchar [note: 'Web address where the program\'s accreditation status and related documentation are publicly disclosed.']
  accrediting_body varchar [note: 'The specialized accreditation agency responsible for program review. Examples include AACSB (Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business), ABET (Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology), ABA (American Bar Association), LCME (Liaison Committee on Medical Education). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AACSB|ABET|ABA|LCME|ACPE|CCNE|NAAB|NASM|CEPH|CSWE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  annual_report_due_date date [note: 'The recurring deadline for submission of annual reports to the accrediting body, if required.']
  annual_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accrediting body mandates submission of annual data or progress reports between comprehensive reviews.']
  appeal_decision_date date [note: 'The date when the accrediting body issued a final decision on the institution\'s appeal, if applicable.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution has filed a formal appeal of an adverse accreditation decision.']
  commendations varchar [note: 'Areas of strength or exemplary practices recognized by the accrediting body during the review process.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with accreditation standards: compliant (meeting all standards), non-compliant (deficiencies identified), monitoring (under observation), remediation (corrective action in progress).']
  conditions_cited varchar [note: 'Specific conditions, deficiencies, or areas of concern identified by the accrediting body that require remediation or monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this accreditation record was first created in the system.']
  current_accreditation_effective_date date [note: 'The effective start date of the current accreditation status or most recent reaffirmation.']
  initial_accreditation_date date [note: 'The date when the program first received accreditation from this accrediting body.']
  interim_report_due_date date [note: 'The deadline for submission of any required interim or progress report to the accrediting body.']
  interim_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accrediting body has mandated submission of an interim progress report between comprehensive reviews.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent comprehensive accreditation review or site visit conducted by the accrediting body.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next comprehensive accreditation review or reaffirmation cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional contextual information, internal observations, or supplementary details regarding the accreditation status and review process.']
  public_disclosure_statement varchar [note: 'The official public statement regarding the program\'s accreditation status as required for transparency and consumer information purposes.']
  review_cycle_years varchar [note: 'The number of years between comprehensive accreditation reviews as specified by the accrediting body (typically 5, 7, or 10 years).']
  review_fees_estimated decimal(12,2) [note: 'The estimated total cost of the comprehensive accreditation review process, including application fees, site visit expenses, and evaluation costs.']
  self_study_due_date date [note: 'The deadline for submission of the self-study report to the accrediting body as part of the review process.']
  site_visit_date date [note: 'The date when the accrediting body conducted or is scheduled to conduct an on-site evaluation visit.']
  substantive_change_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accrediting body requires prior approval for substantive changes to the program (e.g., new delivery modality, significant curriculum revision, new location).']
  teach_out_plan_approval_date date [note: 'The date when the teach-out plan was approved by the accrediting body, if applicable.']
  teach_out_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a teach-out plan is required due to program closure, loss of accreditation, or institutional closure to ensure current students can complete their degrees.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this accreditation record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Tracks the accreditation status and review cycle for each academic program subject to specialized accreditation (AACSB, ABET, ABA, LCME, AACSB). Captures accrediting body, accreditation type (initial, continuing, specialized), current status (accredited, candidate, probation, withdrawn), last review date, next review date, self-study due date, conditions or concerns cited, and institutional liaison. Supports compliance reporting and accreditation preparation workflows.'
}

Table curriculum.assessment_cycle {
  assessment_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the assessment cycle record. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic program being assessed in this cycle.']
  assessment_enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Assessment cycles are executed and documented through enterprise assessment management systems. Assessment coordinators use these applications to collect data, analyze results, and generate accreditat']
  assessment_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty or staff member responsible for leading this assessment cycle.']
  compliance_accreditation_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_standard. Business justification: Assessment cycles demonstrate compliance with specific accreditor standards (AACSB, ABET, HLC criteria). Essential for accreditation self-studies and continuous improvement documentation. Core accredi']
  plo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific PLO being evaluated in this assessment cycle.']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'The academic year during which this assessment cycle is conducted (e.g., 2023-2024).']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The accrediting agency for which this assessment cycle provides evidence (e.g., AACSB, ABET, HLC, SACSCOC).']
  accreditation_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Specific accreditation standard or criterion addressed by this assessment cycle (e.g., AACSB Standard 8, ABET Criterion 3, HLC Criterion 4.B).']
  action_plan_approved_date date [note: 'The date when the improvement action plan was formally approved by the curriculum committee or program leadership.']
  actual_mean_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The mean score achieved by students on the assessment instrument in this cycle.']
  actual_proficiency_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual percentage of students who met or exceeded the proficiency threshold in this cycle.']
  analysis_completed_date date [note: 'The date when the analysis of assessment data was completed.']
  assessment_method_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific assessment method used (e.g., embedded case analysis rubric in capstone course, exit survey, portfolio review).']
  assessment_method_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment method as direct (embedded exam, rubric-scored artifact, capstone evaluation) or indirect (survey, focus group, self-report).']
  benchmark_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target benchmark was achieved (True) or not (False) in this cycle.']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'The catalog year of the program curriculum being assessed, ensuring alignment with published program requirements.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'The CIP code of the program being assessed, used for federal reporting and program classification.']
  closure_evidence varchar [note: 'Documentation that improvement actions were enacted and their impact evaluated, demonstrating closure of the assess-analyze-act-improve loop required by accreditors.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assessment cycle record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_closed_date date [note: 'The date when the assessment cycle was formally closed, indicating that improvement actions were implemented and closure-of-loop evidence was documented.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the assessment cycle, typically combining program code, PLO code, and academic year (e.g., MBA-PLO1-2023).']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the assessment cycle, summarizing the program, outcome, and period under review.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment cycle within the assess-analyze-act-improve loop.']
  data_collection_end_date date [note: 'The date when data collection for this assessment cycle was completed.']
  data_collection_instrument varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the tool, rubric, survey, or exam used to collect assessment data (e.g., AAC&U VALUE Rubric, custom capstone rubric, Qualtrics exit survey).']
  data_collection_start_date date [note: 'The date when data collection for this assessment cycle began.']
  degree_level varchar [note: 'The level of the degree program being assessed (e.g., bachelor, master, doctoral).']
  gap_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis of the gap between target and actual performance, identifying specific areas where students did not meet expectations and potential root causes.']
  implementation_timeline varchar [note: 'Planned timeline for implementing the identified improvement actions, including milestones and responsible parties.']
  improvement_actions varchar [note: 'Specific improvement actions identified to address performance gaps, such as curriculum revisions, pedagogical changes, or resource enhancements.']
  instrument_version varchar [note: 'Version number or date of the data collection instrument to track changes over time.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assessment cycle record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or contextual information about the assessment cycle.']
  proficiency_scale_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum possible score on the assessment instrument scale (e.g., 4.00 for a 4-point rubric, 100.00 for a percentage scale).']
  proficiency_threshold_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum score on the assessment instrument that represents proficiency (e.g., 3.00 on a 4-point rubric).']
  results_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the assessment results, including key findings, trends, and observations from the data analysis.']
  sample_size_actual varchar [note: 'Actual number of students or artifacts assessed in this cycle.']
  sample_size_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of students or artifacts to be assessed in this cycle.']
  target_benchmark_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target percentage of students expected to meet or exceed the proficiency threshold (e.g., 80.00 means 80% of students should score at proficiency or above).']

  Note: 'Defines a scheduled assessment cycle for evaluating PLO attainment within an academic program, representing one complete iteration of the assess-analyze-act-improve loop required by accreditors. Captures the program, PLO(s) under review, academic year, assessment method (direct: embedded exam, rubric-scored artifact, capstone evaluation; indirect: survey, focus group), data collection instrument, sample size, target performance benchmark, actual results summary, gap analysis, improvement actions identified, implementation timeline, and closure-of-loop evidence documenting that improvements were enacted. Supports AACSB Assurance of Learning, ABET continuous improvement, and HLC Criteria 4.B documentation.'
}

Table curriculum.cip_code {
  cip_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CIP code record. Primary key for the CIP code reference taxonomy.']
  action_code varchar [note: 'The type of change applied to this CIP code in the current edition relative to the prior edition. New indicates a newly created code. Revised indicates definition or title changes. Moved indicates reclassification to a different series. Deleted indicates retirement. No change indicates the code is unchanged from the prior edition.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit hierarchical CIP code in format XX.XXXX where the first two digits represent the broad field (series), the middle two represent the narrow field (intermediate grouping), and the last two represent the specific program. This is the authoritative NCES classification code.']
  cip_definition varchar [note: 'Official NCES definition and scope statement for the CIP code. Describes the instructional content, typical courses, and program characteristics that define this classification.']
  cip_edition_year varchar [note: 'The year of the CIP taxonomy edition in which this code was published or last updated (e.g., 2010, 2020). NCES updates the CIP taxonomy biennially.']
  cip_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CIP code. Active codes are valid for current use in IPEDS reporting and program classification. Inactive codes have been retired or superseded. Moved codes have been reclassified to a different CIP code. Deleted codes are no longer valid.']
  cip_title varchar [note: 'Official NCES title for the CIP code. This is the authoritative program name as published by the National Center for Education Statistics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this CIP code record was first created in the institutional system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cross_reference_cip_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related or similar CIP codes that institutions should consider when classifying programs. Used for disambiguation and classification guidance.']
  discontinue_date date [note: 'The date on which this CIP code was retired, superseded, or otherwise discontinued for use in program classification. Null for active codes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this CIP code became valid for use in institutional program classification and IPEDS reporting.']
  examples varchar [note: 'Illustrative examples of specific degree programs, majors, or concentrations that would be classified under this CIP code. Provided by NCES for classification guidance.']
  four_digit_series varchar [note: 'The first four digits of the CIP code (XX.XX format) representing the intermediate or narrow instructional field within the broad series (e.g., 11.01 = Computer and Information Sciences, General).']
  four_digit_series_title varchar [note: 'The official NCES title for the four-digit narrow field series (e.g., Computer and Information Sciences, General).']
  ipeds_reporting_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this CIP code is valid for use in IPEDS completions reporting. Only active CIP codes from the current edition are IPEDS-eligible.']
  is_stem_designated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this CIP code is designated as a STEM program per the Department of Homeland Security STEM Designated Degree Program List. STEM designation affects international student Optional Practical Training (OPT) eligibility and STEM OPT extension eligibility.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this CIP code record was last updated in the institutional system. Used for data lineage and change tracking.']
  nces_url varchar [note: 'The official NCES web URL for this CIP code definition and detailed classification guidance.']
  opt_stem_extension_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether international students who complete a degree program in this CIP code are eligible for the 24-month STEM OPT extension beyond the standard 12-month OPT period.']
  prior_cip_code_2010 varchar [note: 'The equivalent CIP code from the 2010 edition, if applicable. Used for historical crosswalk mapping and longitudinal analysis of program completions data.']
  title_iv_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether programs classified under this CIP code are eligible for Title IV federal financial aid. Certain CIP codes (e.g., recreational or avocational programs) may be ineligible for federal aid.']
  two_digit_series varchar [note: 'The first two digits of the CIP code representing the broad instructional field or discipline area (e.g., 11 = Computer and Information Sciences, 14 = Engineering, 51 = Health Professions).']
  two_digit_series_title varchar [note: 'The official NCES title for the two-digit broad field series (e.g., Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services, Engineering, Health Professions and Related Programs).']

  Note: 'Reference taxonomy record for the Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) maintained by the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES), updated biennially. Captures CIP code (6-digit hierarchical: XX.XXXX), title, definition, two-digit series (broad field), four-digit series (narrow field), six-digit series (specific program), active/inactive status, STEM designation flag (DHS STEM Designated Degree Program List), and crosswalk mappings to prior CIP editions (2010→2020). Serves as the authoritative classification for IPEDS completions reporting, state authorization applications, OPT/STEM OPT visa eligibility determination, Title IV federal financial aid program eligibility, and institutional research analytics. Updated centrally when NCES publishes new editions; institutional programs reference but do not modify CIP definitions.'
}

Table curriculum.concentration {
  concentration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the concentration, track, specialization, emphasis, or minor within an academic program. Primary key for the concentration entity.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent academic program under which this concentration is offered. Links to curriculum.academic_program.']
  cip_code_id varchar [note: 'FK to curriculum.cip_code']
  concentration_college_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the college or school that houses the concentration. Typically aligns with the parent program college but may differ for cross-college concentrations.']
  concentration_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the academic department that administers the concentration. May differ from the parent program department for interdisciplinary concentrations.']
  director_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the faculty member serving as the concentration director or coordinator, responsible for curriculum oversight, student advising, and assessment.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the specialized accrediting agency that reviews this concentration if distinct from the parent program accreditation (e.g., AACSB for a Finance concentration within a Business program, ABET for an Engineering concentration).']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the concentration with the specialized accrediting body. Not applicable if the concentration does not require separate accreditation.']
  admission_requirements varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any special admission requirements for the concentration, including prerequisite courses, minimum GPA, application materials, auditions, portfolios, or faculty approval processes.']
  admission_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether admission to the concentration requires a separate application, faculty approval, portfolio review, or other selective criteria beyond enrollment in the parent program.']
  appears_on_diploma boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration title is printed on the official diploma. Varies by institutional policy; some institutions print only the major degree, others include concentrations.']
  appears_on_transcript boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration title is printed on the official academic transcript. True for most concentrations; false for internal tracking-only designations.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the concentration was formally approved by the curriculum committee, faculty senate, or governing board, authorizing its inclusion in the academic catalog.']
  banner_area_code varchar [note: 'Ellucian Banner SMRPRLE area code used in degree audit processing (DegreeWorks) to identify and evaluate concentration requirements. Maps directly to Banner curriculum rules.']
  capstone_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of a capstone course, project, thesis, or comprehensive examination is required as part of the concentration requirements.']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'Academic catalog year in which this concentration version became effective, formatted as YYYY-YYYY (e.g., 2023-2024). Students are typically held to the catalog year in effect at the time of their initial enrollment.']
  concentration_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the concentration within the institution. Used in student information systems, degree audits, and transcript processing. Maps to Banner SMRPRLE area code.']
  concentration_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the concentration, including its academic focus, career outcomes, unique features, and relationship to the parent program. Published in the academic catalog.']
  concentration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concentration indicating whether it is available for student enrollment and degree audit processing.']
  concentration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sub-plan type: concentration (focused study area within major), track (pathway variant), specialization (advanced focus), emphasis (thematic grouping), minor (secondary credential), or certificate (standalone credential).']
  core_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours that must be fulfilled through required core courses specific to the concentration, with no substitution allowed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this concentration record was first created in the student information system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this concentration was discontinued or superseded by a new version. Null for currently active concentrations. Students enrolled before this date may continue under teach-out provisions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this concentration became available for student enrollment and degree audit processing. Aligns with the start of the catalog year or mid-year curriculum changes.']
  elective_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours that must be fulfilled through elective courses within the concentration, allowing student choice within defined parameters.']
  honors_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students in this concentration are eligible to pursue honors designation through additional coursework, thesis, or research requirements.']
  internship_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of an internship, practicum, clinical placement, or field experience is required as part of the concentration requirements.']
  ipeds_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completions of this concentration must be reported separately to IPEDS as a distinct award. True when the concentration has a unique CIP code and meets IPEDS reporting thresholds.']
  last_curriculum_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent comprehensive curriculum review for the concentration, typically conducted on a 3-7 year cycle as part of program assessment and continuous improvement.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this concentration record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  max_transfer_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum number of transfer credit hours that may be applied toward concentration requirements. Null if no transfer credit is allowed or if parent program limits apply.']
  min_gpa_required decimal(3,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative or major GPA required for admission to or continuation in the concentration. Null if no GPA threshold applies.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next comprehensive curriculum review of the concentration, ensuring ongoing alignment with industry standards, accreditation requirements, and student learning outcomes.']
  oer_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration has been designated as using primarily Open Educational Resources, reducing textbook costs for students and supporting affordability initiatives.']
  required_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of credit hours required to complete the concentration, including both required and elective courses within the concentration plan.']
  residency_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of credit hours within the concentration that must be completed in residence at the degree-granting institution, not through transfer or test credit.']
  stem_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration is designated as a STEM program for purposes of Optional Practical Training (OPT) extension eligibility for international students on F-1 visas.']
  thesis_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of a formal thesis or dissertation is required as part of the concentration requirements, typically for graduate-level concentrations.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official full title of the concentration, track, specialization, emphasis, or minor as it appears in the academic catalog and on official transcripts.']
  total_enrolled_students varchar [note: 'Current count of students actively enrolled in this concentration. Used for capacity planning, resource allocation, and viability assessment.']
  url varchar [note: 'Web address of the official concentration page on the institutional website, providing prospective and current students with detailed information, course requirements, and contact information.']
  writing_intensive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concentration includes a significant writing component across multiple courses, meeting institutional writing-intensive program criteria.']

  Note: 'Master record for concentrations, tracks, specializations, emphases, and minors embedded within an academic program that represent a structured sub-plan of study. Captures concentration title, parent academic program, required credit hours, elective credit hours, specific course requirements, CIP sub-code if distinct from parent program, active status, effective catalog year range, admission requirements (if restricted), whether the concentration appears on the official transcript and/or diploma, and Banner SMRPRLE area code. Supports degree audit processing (DegreeWorks/Banner), program-level IPEDS completions reporting at the concentration level, and student academic planning.'
}

Table curriculum.curriculum_enrollment {
  curriculum_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for curriculum_enrollment']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the course master record in which the student athlete is enrolled']
  enrollment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the enrollment record. Primary key for the enrollment association.']
  student_athlete_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the student athlete who is enrolled in the course']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'The academic year of the enrollment (e.g., 2023 for 2023-2024 academic year). Used for eligibility year calculations and compliance reporting.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date when the student athlete completed the course (grade posted). Used for progress-toward-degree calculations and eligibility certification timing.']
  credit_hours_earned decimal(4,2) [note: 'The number of credit hours earned by the student athlete for this specific enrollment. May differ from the course catalog credit hours if the student enrolled for variable credit or received partial credit.']
  degree_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credits from this enrollment count toward the student athletes degree progress for NCAA eligibility purposes. Not all enrolled courses count toward the percentage-of-degree requirement.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The date when the student athlete enrolled in this course. Used to determine if enrollment occurred before census date for eligibility credit.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the enrollment. Tracks whether the student athlete is actively enrolled, has dropped, withdrawn, or completed the course. Critical for real-time eligibility monitoring.']
  grade varchar [note: 'The final grade earned by the student athlete in this course. Used to calculate term GPA and determine satisfactory academic progress for eligibility.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'The academic term in which the enrollment occurred (format: YYYYTT where TT is term type). Critical for eligibility tracking as student athletes must maintain full-time status each term.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment event between a course and a student athlete. It captures the term-specific registration of a student athlete in a course, including enrollment status, academic performance, credit hours earned, and whether the credits count toward degree progress for NCAA eligibility certification. Each record links one course to one student athlete for a specific term with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship.. Existence Justification: In higher education operations, student athletes enroll in multiple courses each term to maintain full-time status (minimum 12 credit hours for NCAA eligibility), and each course section has multiple student athletes enrolled. Compliance officers actively manage and monitor these enrollments to verify eligibility requirements: full-time enrollment status, term GPA calculation, percentage-of-degree progress, and whether credits are degree-applicable. Enrollment is a recognized business entity with term-specific data (grades, status, dates) that belongs to neither the course catalog nor the student athlete profile alone.'
}

Table curriculum.program_affiliation {
  program_affiliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the program affiliation record. Primary key for the association.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the academic program to which the instructor is affiliated']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instructor who holds the program affiliation']
  affiliation_type varchar [note: 'Type of affiliation the instructor holds with the program. Primary indicates main program responsibility, secondary indicates supporting role, joint indicates shared appointment across programs, courtesy indicates non-compensated affiliation, affiliate indicates external/adjunct relationship, graduate_faculty indicates authorization to teach graduate courses and chair dissertations. Critical for accreditation faculty sufficiency calculations.']
  appointment_term varchar [note: 'Academic term or contract period for this affiliation (e.g., Fall 2024, AY 2024-2025, Multi-year). Supports term-based appointment tracking and renewal processes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this program affiliation ended or will end. Null for current active affiliations. Supports historical tracking and transition planning.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this program affiliation became effective. Supports historical tracking of faculty affiliations for accreditation self-studies and workload history.']
  graduate_faculty_status varchar [note: 'Graduate faculty status of the instructor within this program. Full indicates authorization to teach graduate courses, chair dissertations, and serve on comprehensive exam committees. Associate indicates authorization to teach graduate courses and serve on committees but not chair. None indicates no graduate faculty privileges. Critical for graduate program accreditation and governance.']
  is_primary_affiliation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the instructor\'s primary program affiliation. Each instructor should have exactly one primary affiliation at any time. Used for reporting and organizational hierarchy.']
  research_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the instructor\'s research workload allocated to this program. Relevant for research-intensive programs and graduate programs where faculty research productivity contributes to program quality metrics.']
  service_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the instructor\'s service workload (committees, advising, governance) allocated to this program. Used for workload planning and faculty contribution assessment.']
  teaching_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the instructor\'s teaching workload allocated to this program. Used for faculty sufficiency calculations in accreditation reporting. Sum across all program affiliations for an instructor should equal 100% of their teaching FTE.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal appointment or affiliation relationship between an instructor and an academic program. It captures faculty sufficiency for accreditation, workload allocation across programs, graduate faculty status, and appointment terms. Each record links one instructor to one academic program with attributes that exist only in the context of this affiliation relationship, supporting accreditation reporting (AACSB, ABET faculty sufficiency requirements), workload planning, and academic governance.. Existence Justification: In higher education, instructors routinely hold formal affiliations with multiple academic programs (joint appointments, cross-disciplinary teaching, graduate faculty appointments), and programs require multiple affiliated faculty to meet accreditation standards for faculty sufficiency. The affiliation relationship itself carries critical operational data including workload allocation percentages, graduate faculty status, and appointment terms that belong to neither the instructor nor the program alone but to the specific instructor-program pairing.'
}

Table curriculum.section {
  section_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the section association']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the course master record that defines the academic content, credit hours, and catalog attributes for this section']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instructor assigned to teach this section']
  delivery_modality varchar [note: 'The instructional delivery modality for this specific section. May differ from the course catalog default based on instructor preference or term-specific constraints.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last day of instruction for this section. Used for grade submission deadlines and faculty load calculations.']
  enrollment_count varchar [note: 'The current number of students enrolled in this section. Used for capacity management and faculty load reporting.']
  meeting_pattern varchar [note: 'The scheduled meeting days and times for this section (e.g., MWF 10:00-10:50, TR 14:00-15:15). Used for room scheduling and student schedule conflict detection.']
  section_number varchar [note: 'The section designator within the term (e.g., 001, 002, W01 for web sections). Distinguishes multiple offerings of the same course in the same term.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first day of instruction for this section. May differ from term start date for accelerated or late-start sections.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'The academic term in which this section is offered (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024). Part of the natural key for section identity.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the specific offering of a course taught by an instructor in a particular term. It captures the operational reality of course delivery: scheduling, enrollment capacity, delivery modality, and meeting patterns. Each record links one course definition to one instructor for one term and section, with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific teaching assignment.. Existence Justification: In higher education operations, instructors teach multiple courses across different terms and sections, and courses are taught by different instructors depending on term, section, and faculty availability. The business actively manages course sections as operational entities with scheduling, enrollment capacity, delivery modality, and meeting patterns. Section is a recognized business concept in academic operations, distinct from both the course catalog definition and the instructor master record.'
}

Table curriculum.teaching_assignment {
  teaching_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the teaching assignment record']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the course master record being taught']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the non-faculty staff employee assigned as instructor']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date this teaching assignment ended, typically the last day of the term or when the instructor was reassigned.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date this teaching assignment became effective, typically the first day of the term or section start date.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the teaching assignment (Active, Completed, Cancelled, Pending).']
  credit_hour_allocation decimal(4,2) [note: 'The number of credit hours allocated to this instructor for this course section, used for workload calculation. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  enrollment_count varchar [note: 'The number of students enrolled in this course section at the time of assignment or as of census date. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  instructional_method varchar [note: 'The instructional delivery method for this specific teaching assignment (e.g., lecture, lab, online, hybrid). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  primary_instructor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employee is the instructor-of-record (primary instructor) for this course section. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  section_number varchar [note: 'The specific section number of the course being taught (e.g., 001, 002, LAB). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'The academic term code (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024) during which this teaching assignment is active. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  workload_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of teaching responsibility this instructor has for this course section (e.g., 100.00 for sole instructor, 50.00 for team-teaching). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the teaching assignment relationship between courses and instructors (non-faculty employees). It captures the operational assignment of staff instructors to course sections, including workload distribution, instructional role, and section-specific teaching responsibilities. Each record links one course to one employee with attributes that exist only in the context of this teaching relationship.. Existence Justification: In higher education operations, courses are routinely taught by multiple instructors through team-teaching arrangements, lab coordinators, teaching assistants, and co-instructors across different sections and terms. Simultaneously, instructors teach multiple courses per term as part of their workload. The registrar actively manages these teaching assignments as operational records, tracking instructor-of-record status, workload distribution, credit hour allocation, and section-specific responsibilities.'
}

Table curriculum.course_training_requirement {
  course_training_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this course-training requirement association record']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the course master record that has associated compliance training requirements']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance training program required for teaching the associated course']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which this training requirement was established for this course, typically driven by regulatory changes, accreditation standards updates, or institutional policy revisions']
  completion_deadline date [note: 'Absolute date by which instructors teaching this course must complete the associated training. May be set based on term start dates, regulatory effective dates, or institutional policy timelines.']
  completion_required_for_teaching boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this training is mandatory before an instructor can be assigned to teach this course. True means instructor cannot teach without current certification; False means training is recommended but not blocking.']
  course_training_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this course-training requirement. Active requirements are enforced for instructor assignments; Inactive requirements are historical; Pending requirements are scheduled for future enforcement; Superseded requirements have been replaced by updated training programs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this training requirement is no longer applicable for this course. Null indicates the requirement is currently active. Used when regulatory requirements change or courses are modified to no longer require specific training.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this training requirement is effective for this course. Used to track when requirements were added or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, exceptions, or additional context for this specific course-training requirement pairing']
  requirement_basis varchar [note: 'The regulatory, accreditation, or policy basis that mandates this training for this course. Examples: \'Title IX - health sciences content\', \'FERPA - student record access\', \'ADA - online course delivery\', \'IRB - human subjects research lab\'']
  waiver_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any waiver request for this training requirement for this course. Tracks whether an exception has been requested or granted due to equivalent prior training, subject matter expertise, or other institutional considerations.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance training requirements mandated for faculty teaching specific courses. It captures which compliance training programs must be completed before or during instruction of particular courses, driven by regulatory requirements (Title IX, FERPA, accessibility standards, research ethics) and institutional policy. Each record links one course to one required training program with completion tracking attributes that exist only in the context of this teaching requirement relationship.. Existence Justification: In higher education compliance operations, courses require multiple compliance training programs based on their content and delivery characteristics (e.g., health courses require Title IX training, online courses require accessibility training, lab courses require research ethics training), and each compliance training program applies to multiple courses across different departments. Compliance officers actively manage these requirement mappings, tracking which trainings are mandatory for teaching specific courses, setting course-specific completion deadlines, managing waiver requests, and auditing instructor compliance before course assignments.'
}

Table curriculum.course_asset_requirement {
  course_asset_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the course-asset requirement record']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the course master record that requires the IT asset']
  it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the IT asset master record required by the course']
  asset_requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requirement level indicating whether the asset is mandatory for course delivery, preferred but substitutable, or optional enhancement']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this asset requirement becomes active for the course']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset requirement is no longer active, supporting historical tracking of changing technology requirements']
  quantity_required varchar [note: 'Number of units of this asset type required to support the course (e.g., 25 lab workstations for a computer science lab course)']
  scheduling_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used by registrar scheduling systems to resolve resource conflicts when multiple courses require the same asset during overlapping time periods']
  section_number varchar [note: 'Specific course section identifier when asset requirements vary by section (e.g., Section 001 uses Mac lab, Section 002 uses PC lab)']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term identifier indicating when this asset requirement is active (e.g., Fall 2024, Spring 2025), allowing requirements to vary by term']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational requirement relationship between courses and IT assets. It captures the specific technology assets required to deliver a course, including lab computers, specialized software, projectors, and equipment. Each record links one course to one IT asset with scheduling and quantity attributes that exist only in the context of this requirement. This supports registrar scheduling, facilities management, and resource conflict resolution across academic terms.. Existence Justification: In higher education operations, courses require multiple IT assets (lab computers, projectors, specialized software, equipment) to support delivery, and individual assets are shared across multiple courses throughout academic terms. The registrar and facilities management actively manage these requirements to schedule sections, avoid resource conflicts, and ensure availability. This is an operational relationship with specific attributes (quantity, priority, term) that belong to neither the course catalog nor the asset inventory alone.'
}

Table curriculum.institution {
  institution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for institution']
  parent_institution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent institution if this institution is a branch campus, satellite location, or subsidiary of a larger system.']
  accreditation_date date [note: 'Date when the institution received its current accreditation status from the primary accreditor.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current accreditation status expires and requires renewal or reaffirmation.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current institutional accreditation standing with the primary regional or national accrediting body.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the institution\'s main campus or administrative headquarters.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or department information.']
  carnegie_classification varchar [note: 'Carnegie Classification framework category describing the institution\'s mission, degree offerings, and research activity level.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the institution\'s main campus or administrative headquarters is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the institution ceased operations or closed permanently.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the institution is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this institution record was first created in the system.']
  email_domain varchar [note: 'Primary email domain used by the institution for official communications and student/faculty email accounts.']
  enrollment_size_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the institution based on total student enrollment headcount ranges.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the institution\'s main administrative office.']
  fice_code varchar [note: 'Federal identification code used for Title IV financial aid eligibility and federal reporting.']
  founded_year varchar [note: 'Year the institution was originally established or chartered.']
  hispanic_serving_institution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution meets the federal definition of a Hispanic-Serving Institution with at least 25% Hispanic enrollment.']
  historically_black_college boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is designated as a Historically Black College or University under federal law.']
  institution_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the institution for external identification and reporting purposes.']
  institution_level varchar [note: 'Primary level of instruction offered by the institution based on degree programs.']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the higher education institution.']
  institution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the institution based on control and ownership structure.']
  ipeds_unit_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit identifier assigned by the National Center for Education Statistics for federal reporting.']
  land_grant_institution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is designated as a land-grant institution under the Morrill Acts.']
  locale_classification varchar [note: 'Geographic setting classification describing the institution\'s location as city, suburb, town, or rural.']
  ope_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit identifier assigned by the US Department of Education Office of Postsecondary Education for Title IV program participation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the institution indicating whether it is actively enrolling students and offering programs.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the institution\'s main administrative office.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the institution\'s main address.']
  primary_accreditor varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regional or national accrediting agency responsible for institutional accreditation.']
  religious_affiliation varchar [note: 'Religious denomination or affiliation of the institution, if applicable.']
  research_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the institution\'s research activity level and doctoral program offerings.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the institution is located, using standard postal abbreviations.']
  system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the higher education system or consortium to which the institution belongs, if applicable.']
  title_iv_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is eligible to participate in federal Title IV student financial aid programs.']
  tribal_college boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is a Tribal College or University chartered by a federally recognized tribe.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this institution record was last modified.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the institution.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for institution. Referenced by partner_institution_id.'
}

Table curriculum.assessment_instrument {
  assessment_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assessment_instrument']
  parent_assessment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on assessment_instrument (parent_assessment_instrument_id)']
  rubric_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grading rubric used to evaluate student performance on this assessment instrument.']
  accessibility_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment instrument meets accessibility standards such as WCAG 2.1 AA for students with disabilities.']
  accommodations_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment instrument allows for student accommodations such as extended time or assistive technology.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accreditation body that requires or recognizes this assessment instrument (e.g., AACSB, ABET, HLC).']
  accreditation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment instrument is required for program accreditation purposes by bodies such as AACSB or ABET.']
  alignment_to_plo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment instrument is formally aligned to one or more Program Learning Outcomes.']
  alignment_to_slo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment instrument is formally aligned to one or more Student Learning Outcomes.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment instrument was formally approved by the curriculum committee or academic authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the academic authority or committee that approved this assessment instrument.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Pedagogical method or approach used by the assessment instrument to measure learning outcomes.']
  bloom_taxonomy_level varchar [note: 'Highest cognitive level from Bloom\'s Taxonomy that this assessment instrument is designed to measure.']
  collaboration_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students are permitted to collaborate or work in groups on this assessment instrument.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment instrument record was first created in the curriculum management system.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Mode through which the assessment instrument is administered to students.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the assessment instrument including its purpose, scope, and methodology.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Allocated time in minutes for students to complete the assessment instrument.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this assessment instrument is no longer active or available for use in courses.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this assessment instrument becomes active and available for use in courses.']
  feedback_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students receive automated or instructor feedback upon completion of the assessment instrument.']
  feedback_timing varchar [note: 'Timing at which feedback is released to students relative to assessment submission and grading.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the assessment instrument used in catalogs and external references.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment instrument by its primary format and delivery method.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who most recently modified this assessment instrument record.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment instrument record was most recently modified.']
  maximum_retakes varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a student may retake or resubmit this assessment instrument if retakes are allowed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or instructions related to the assessment instrument for faculty or administrative reference.']
  passing_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum score or points required to achieve a passing grade on this assessment instrument.']
  proctoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment instrument requires proctoring or invigilation during administration.']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions, items, or components included in this assessment instrument.']
  randomization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether question order or answer choices are randomized to reduce academic dishonesty.']
  retake_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students are permitted to retake or resubmit this assessment instrument for a revised grade.']
  retired_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment instrument was formally retired from active curriculum use.']
  retired_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for why the assessment instrument was retired from active use.']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or shortened title of the assessment instrument for display in constrained spaces.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment instrument in the curriculum management system.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the assessment instrument as it appears in curriculum documentation.']
  total_points decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum possible points or score that can be earned on this assessment instrument.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the assessment instrument to track revisions and updates over time.']
  weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight of this assessment instrument in the overall course grade calculation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the faculty member or instructional designer who created this assessment instrument.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for assessment_instrument. Referenced by assessment_instrument_id.'
}

Table curriculum.prerequisite_group {
  prerequisite_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for prerequisite_group']
  academic_term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term when this prerequisite group was established or last revised.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic department responsible for maintaining and enforcing this prerequisite group.']
  parent_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent prerequisite group when this group is nested within a hierarchical prerequisite structure. Null for top-level groups.']
  parent_prerequisite_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on prerequisite_group (parent_prerequisite_group_id)']
  applies_to_transfer_students boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prerequisite group applies to transfer students or only to native students. Used for articulation agreement handling.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this prerequisite group was approved by the curriculum committee or authorized governance body.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or committee that approved this prerequisite group.']
  catalog_display_text varchar [note: 'Formatted text describing this prerequisite group as it should appear in the course catalog and student-facing documentation.']
  competency_framework varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the competency framework used to define prerequisite competencies in this group. Applicable when group_type is competency.']
  concurrent_enrollment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students may enroll in the target course concurrently with prerequisites in this group (true) or must complete them beforehand (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prerequisite group record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the prerequisite group explaining the requirements, rationale, and any special conditions or notes for students and advisors.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this prerequisite group becomes effective and begins to be enforced for course enrollment.']
  enforcement_level varchar [note: 'Level of enforcement for this prerequisite group: hard_block prevents enrollment, soft_warning allows enrollment with notification, advisory_only provides guidance without restriction.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this prerequisite group expires and is no longer enforced. Null indicates no expiration.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the prerequisite group used in course catalogs and curriculum documentation.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the prerequisite group describing the collection of prerequisite requirements.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prerequisite group indicating the nature of requirements (course-based, competency-based, test-based, corequisite, concurrent enrollment, or advisory).']
  logic_operator varchar [note: 'Boolean logic operator defining how prerequisites within this group must be satisfied (AND requires all, OR requires any one, XOR requires exactly one).']
  minimum_courses_required varchar [note: 'Minimum number of courses from this prerequisite group that must be completed to satisfy the requirement. Used for flexible prerequisite structures.']
  minimum_credits_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of credit hours from courses in this prerequisite group required to satisfy the prerequisite. Supports credit-based prerequisite rules.']
  minimum_gpa_required decimal(3,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative or prerequisite-specific GPA required for courses in this group, on a 4.0 scale.']
  minimum_grade_required varchar [note: 'Minimum grade that must be achieved in prerequisite courses within this group (e.g., C-, B, P for Pass, S for Satisfactory).']
  minimum_test_score varchar [note: 'Minimum score on the specified test required to satisfy this prerequisite group. Applicable when group_type is placement_test.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prerequisite group record was last modified.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number tracking revisions to this prerequisite group over time. Increments with each approved change.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this prerequisite group when multiple groups apply to a course, controlling display and evaluation sequence.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prerequisite group indicating whether it is actively enforced, inactive, awaiting curriculum committee approval, or archived.']
  test_score_type varchar [note: 'Type of standardized or placement test that can satisfy this prerequisite group (e.g., SAT, ACT, AP, CLEP, institutional placement test). Applicable when group_type is placement_test.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prerequisite group can be waived by authorized personnel (department chair, advisor, registrar).']
  waiver_authority_role varchar [note: 'Role or position authorized to grant waivers for this prerequisite group when waiver_allowed is true.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for prerequisite_group. Referenced by prerequisite_group_id.'
}

Table curriculum.competency_framework {
  competency_framework_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competency_framework']
  parent_competency_framework_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competency_framework (parent_competency_framework_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accrediting agency or quality assurance body associated with this framework (e.g., AACSB, ABET, SACSCOC, HLC). Used for compliance tracking and program review.']
  accreditation_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Specific accreditation standard or criterion document reference that this framework addresses (e.g., AACSB Standard 9: Curriculum Content, ABET Criterion 3: Student Outcomes).']
  alignment_standard varchar [note: 'External standard or framework to which this competency framework is aligned or mapped (e.g., Degree Qualifications Profile, Lumina Foundation DQP, European Qualifications Framework). Supports articulation and transfer.']
  applicable_degree_levels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of degree levels to which this framework applies (e.g., undergraduate, graduate, doctoral, certificate, associate). Supports filtering and applicability rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: undergraduate|graduate|doctoral|certificate|associate|professional|post_doctoral — promote to reference product if more granular tracking needed]']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the committee, board, or individual with authority to approve adoption or revision of this competency framework (e.g., Faculty Senate, Curriculum Committee, Dean of Academic Affairs).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the competency framework was formally approved for use by the designated approval authority.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code identifying the instructional program area aligned with this competency framework (e.g., 52.0201 for Business Administration). Supports federal reporting and program classification.']
  competency_domains varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct competency domains or categories defined in this framework (e.g., cognitive, affective, psychomotor; or technical, professional, interpersonal).']
  competency_levels varchar [note: 'Total number of hierarchical competency levels defined in this framework (e.g., 3 for beginner/intermediate/advanced, 6 for Bloom\'s cognitive levels). Used for competency progression mapping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this competency framework record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the competency framework, including its purpose, scope, intended audience, and key competency domains covered.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Primary academic discipline or field of study to which this competency framework applies (e.g., Business Administration, Engineering, Computer Science, Liberal Arts). May be null for cross-disciplinary frameworks.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official documentation, guidelines, or detailed specification of the competency framework. Provides access to full framework details for faculty and administrators.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the competency framework became or will become active and applicable for curriculum mapping and assessment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the competency framework will be or was superseded or retired. Null for frameworks with no planned end date.']
  framework_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the competency framework (e.g., AACSB-2020, ABET-EAC, QAA-FHEQ). Used for cross-institutional references and accreditation mapping.']
  framework_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the competency framework (e.g., Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business Assurance of Learning Standards, Framework for Higher Education Qualifications).']
  framework_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competency framework by scope and authority: accreditation (AACSB, ABET), institutional (university-specific), disciplinary (field-specific), professional (industry certification), national (country-level qualifications), or international (global standards).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary geographic region or country for which this framework is designed (e.g., USA, GBR, AUS). Use \'INT\' for international frameworks.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adoption of this competency framework is mandatory (true) or optional (false) for programs within its scope. Used for compliance enforcement.']
  issuing_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, accrediting body, or institution that published and maintains the competency framework (e.g., AACSB International, ABET, Quality Assurance Agency for Higher Education).']
  issuing_organization_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the issuing organization where the framework documentation and updates can be accessed.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags for search and discovery of the competency framework (e.g., critical thinking, quantitative reasoning, communication, ethics, leadership).']
  language varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the primary language in which the competency framework is published (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish, FRA for French).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review or revision of the competency framework by the issuing organization or institutional committee.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this competency framework record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this competency framework record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review or revision of the competency framework. Used for governance planning and compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, implementation guidance, or contextual information about the competency framework. Used for special instructions or historical context.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Standard interval in months for periodic review and update of the competency framework (e.g., 36 for three-year review cycle, 60 for five-year cycle). Supports governance and quality assurance.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Level of application for the competency framework: program (degree/certificate program level), course (individual course level), institutional (university-wide), discipline (academic field), or industry (professional sector).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competency framework: draft (under development), active (in use), under_review (being revised), deprecated (phased out but still referenced), archived (historical record only).']
  taxonomy_type varchar [note: 'Educational taxonomy model used to structure competency levels within this framework: blooms (Bloom\'s Taxonomy), solo (Structure of Observed Learning Outcomes), fink (Fink\'s Taxonomy of Significant Learning), custom (institution-defined), or other.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the competency framework (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Supports tracking of framework revisions and historical mappings.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this competency framework record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for competency_framework. Referenced by competency_framework_id.'
}

Table enrollment.prospect {
  prospect_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the prospective student record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the prospect master record sourced from Slate CRM.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the admissions counselor or recruiter assigned to manage this prospect\'s recruitment relationship. Supports workload balancing, counselor performance analytics, and personalized outreach tracking.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.identity_account. Business justification: Prospects who engage digitally (event registration, portal access, inquiry forms) receive guest/prospect identity accounts for authentication and activity tracking. Essential for recruitment CRM integ']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.principal_investigator.
