  hold_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for the hold, resolution steps required, or additional context provided by the originating office. Displayed to students and advisors.']
  hold_notes varchar [note: 'Internal staff notes providing additional context about the hold, resolution history, or special circumstances. Not displayed to students. Supports case management and inter-office communication.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for this hold record as assigned by the originating office or Banner. Used for student-facing communications and staff lookup.']
  hold_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating how strictly the hold is enforced. \'hard\' blocks the restricted action entirely; \'soft\' generates a warning but allows the action to proceed; \'informational\' is advisory only with no system enforcement.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold. \'active\' means the hold is currently enforced; \'released\' means it has been cleared; \'expired\' means it lapsed past its end date without manual release; \'pending\' means it is queued but not yet enforced; \'suspended\' means temporarily paused.']
  hold_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the category of hold (e.g., FI=Financial, AC=Academic, AD=Advising, IM=Immunization, LB=Library, JD=Judicial, HO=Housing, IN=Insurance). Drives enforcement rules and routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FI|AC|AD|IM|LB|JD|HO|IN|OT — promote to reference product]']
  hold_type_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the hold type code (e.g., \'Financial Hold\', \'Academic Hold\', \'Advising Hold\'). Sourced from the Banner STVHOLD validation table.']
  is_auto_release_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold is eligible for automated release when the triggering condition is resolved (e.g., balance paid, immunization record received). Drives automated release workflow processing.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold was placed by an automated batch process (True) or manually by a staff member (False). Supports workflow auditing and distinguishes system-generated from human-initiated holds.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the student was notified of the hold via email, student portal, or other communication channel.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification has been sent to the student informing them of the hold placement and required resolution steps.']
  originating_office_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the institutional office or department that placed the hold (e.g., BURSAR, REGISTRAR, ADVISING, LIBRARY, HEALTH, JUDICIAL). Determines which office must authorize release.']
  originating_office_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the institutional office or department that placed the hold (e.g., \'Bursar Office\', \'Office of the Registrar\', \'Academic Advising Center\').']
  outstanding_balance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the outstanding financial obligation that triggered or is associated with a financial hold. Applicable primarily to bursar/financial holds. Null for non-financial hold types.']
  placed_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the hold was placed on the student account. Represents the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the hold was placed, including timezone offset. Used for audit trails and batch processing sequencing.']
  release_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the hold was released (e.g., PAID=Balance paid, ADVMEET=Advising meeting completed, IMMCOMP=Immunization records complete, APPEAL=Appeal approved, AUTO=Automated batch release). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PAID|ADVMEET|IMMCOMP|APPEAL|AUTO|WAIVER|ADMIN — promote to reference product]']
  release_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explaining the specific circumstances under which the hold was released. Complements the release reason code with additional context.']
  released_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the hold was manually or automatically released. Null if the hold is still active.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the hold was released, including timezone offset. Supports audit trail and SLA measurement for hold resolution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or process that created this hold record (e.g., Banner for SIS-generated holds, Batch for automated nightly processes, Manual for staff-entered holds, API for integration-sourced holds).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Banner SPRHOLD sequence key). Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the system of record.']
  student_acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has acknowledged receipt and awareness of the hold through the student self-service portal or other acknowledgment mechanism.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration hold record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture.']

  Note: 'Records holds placed on a student\'s account that restrict registration activity. Captures hold type (financial, academic, advising, immunization, library, judicial), hold description, originating office, date placed, date released, release reason, hold severity, and restriction scope (blocks registration, transcript, graduation, or combination). Sourced from Banner SPRHOLD. Critical for enforcing prerequisite compliance, financial clearance, and advising mandates before registration opens. Supports batch hold processing and automated release workflows.'
}

Table enrollment.registration_override {
  registration_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the registration override record. Primary key.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section for which the override is granted.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or faculty who granted the override.']
  enrollment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.registration. Business justification: Registration override is granted and then used during a specific registration transaction. The used_date attribute indicates when the override was applied. This FK links the override to the registrati']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student receiving the registration override.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term in which the override applies.']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'Academic year in which the override applies (e.g., 2023-2024).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the override was officially approved by the authorizing party.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the override approval was recorded in the system.']
  capacity_override_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats being added beyond the section enrollment capacity limit.']
  condition_description varchar [note: 'Description of any conditions or requirements the student must meet to use the override.']
  corequisite_course_code varchar [note: 'Course code of the corequisite requirement being waived, if applicable.']
  course_number varchar [note: 'Catalog course number within the subject (e.g., 101, 2050, 4999).']
  course_reference_number varchar [note: 'Banner Course Reference Number uniquely identifying the course section within the term.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the override record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when an override request is denied.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the override is no longer valid and cannot be used for registration.']
  granting_authority_type varchar [note: 'Role or position type of the individual authorized to grant this override.']
  granting_department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the academic department or administrative unit that authorized the override.']
  granting_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member or faculty who granted the override.']
  is_conditional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the override is subject to additional conditions or requirements.']
  is_one_time_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the override can only be used once or is reusable within the validity period.']
  justification_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation or rationale for granting the registration override.']
  level_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Code of the student classification or level restriction being waived (e.g., freshman, sophomore, graduate).']
  major_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Code of the major or program restriction being waived.']
  override_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable reference number assigned to the override for tracking and audit purposes.']
  override_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the override request or authorization.']
  override_type varchar [note: 'Category of registration restriction being overridden. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prerequisite|corequisite|capacity|time_conflict|major_restriction|level_restriction|college_restriction|department_permission|instructor_permission|dean_permission|campus_restriction|classification_restriction|cohort_restriction — promote to reference product]']
  prerequisite_course_code varchar [note: 'Course code of the prerequisite requirement being waived, if applicable.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the student or advisor initiated the override request.']
  restriction_code varchar [note: 'System code identifying the specific registration restriction being overridden.']
  restriction_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the registration restriction that was overridden.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when an approved override is subsequently revoked.']
  revoked_date date [note: 'Date the override was revoked or withdrawn before use.']
  section_number varchar [note: 'Section identifier for the course offering (e.g., 001, A, LAB).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or module where the override was originally created (e.g., Banner Student, Slate, Self-Service).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the override record in the source system for traceability.']
  student_banner_code varchar [note: 'Banner system identifier for the student (PIDM or student ID number).']
  subject_code varchar [note: 'Academic subject or discipline code for the course (e.g., MATH, ENGL, BIOL).']
  time_conflict_course_code varchar [note: 'Course code of the conflicting section for which the time conflict is being overridden.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the override record was last modified.']
  used_date date [note: 'Date the student successfully used the override to complete registration.']

  Note: 'Documents authorized exceptions granted to allow a student to bypass a registration restriction such as a prerequisite, co-requisite, time conflict, capacity limit, or major restriction. Captures override type, granting authority (advisor/department chair/registrar), override date, justification notes, expiration date, and approval status. Provides an auditable trail of all registration exceptions for compliance and academic integrity purposes.'
}

Table enrollment.restriction {
  restriction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the enrollment restriction record. Primary key.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the course section to which this restriction applies.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the staff member who created this restriction record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  restriction_corequisite_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a corequisite course that must be taken concurrently for enrollment eligibility. Used when restriction enforces corequisite enrollment.']
  restriction_prerequisite_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a prerequisite course that must be completed for enrollment eligibility. Used when restriction enforces prerequisite completion.']
  updated_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the staff member who last updated this restriction record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  applies_to_waitlist boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction also applies to waitlist enrollment. If false, ineligible students may join the waitlist even if they cannot register directly.']
  attribute_code varchar [note: 'Student attribute code when restriction_type is \'attribute\'. References institutional student attribute classifications.']
  class_level_minimum varchar [note: 'Minimum class standing required for enrollment when restriction_type is \'class_level\'. Values: FR (Freshman), SO (Sophomore), JR (Junior), SR (Senior), GR (Graduate).']
  cohort_code varchar [note: 'Cohort identifier when restriction_type is \'cohort\'. Used for cohort-based programs and special populations.']
  college_code varchar [note: 'College or school code when restriction_type is \'college\'. Identifies the academic college unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this restriction record was created in the system.']
  credit_hours_minimum decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative credit hours earned required for enrollment eligibility. Used for progression-based restrictions.']
  degree_code varchar [note: 'Degree type code when restriction_type is \'degree\' (e.g., BA, BS, MS, MBA, PHD).']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Academic department code when restriction_type is \'department\'. Identifies the departmental unit.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the restriction expires and no longer controls enrollment eligibility. Null indicates no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the restriction becomes active and begins to control enrollment eligibility.']
  gpa_minimum decimal(3,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative GPA required for enrollment eligibility. Used for academic performance-based restrictions.']
  major_code varchar [note: 'Four-character major code when restriction_type is \'major\'. References institutional major classification.']
  message varchar [note: 'User-facing message displayed when a student attempts to register but does not meet the restriction criteria. Provides guidance on eligibility requirements.']
  override_authority_level varchar [note: 'Minimum authority level required to override this restriction. Null or \'none\' if override_eligible is false.']
  override_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restriction can be overridden by authorized personnel (registrar, advisor, department chair) to allow enrollment of otherwise ineligible students.']
  permission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit instructor or department permission is required for enrollment in addition to meeting restriction criteria.']
  population_code varchar [note: 'Special population code when restriction_type is \'population\' (e.g., HONORS, ATHLETE, VETERAN, INTERNATIONAL).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank defining the order in which multiple restrictions are evaluated for a course section. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Academic program code when restriction_type is \'program\'. Identifies specific degree program.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when reserved seats are released to the general population if not filled by eligible students. Used for time-based seat release strategies.']
  reserved_seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats reserved for the specified population when restriction_scope is \'reserved\'. Null for include/exclude restrictions.']
  reserved_seat_used_count varchar [note: 'Number of reserved seats currently occupied by eligible students. Tracks utilization of reserved capacity.']
  restriction_code varchar [note: 'Institutional code identifying the specific restriction rule (e.g., MAJOR, LEVEL, COLLEGE, COHORT, HONORS).']
  restriction_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the restriction rule and its purpose. Displayed to students and advisors during registration.']
  restriction_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether the restriction includes only specified populations (include), excludes specified populations (exclude), or reserves seats for specified populations (reserved).']
  restriction_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the restriction rule.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Category of restriction applied to the course section enrollment. Defines the dimension along which enrollment is controlled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: major|minor|college|department|class_level|degree|program|cohort|population|attribute|reserved_seating — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restriction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific value that defines the restriction criteria (e.g., major code \'BIOL\', class level \'JR\', college code \'ENGR\', cohort identifier \'HONORS2024\').']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this restriction (BANNER for SIS-managed restrictions, SLATE for admissions-driven restrictions, MANUAL for ad-hoc restrictions, API for external integrations).']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'Natural key or unique identifier from the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit term code identifying the academic term for which this restriction is effective (format: YYYYMM where MM represents term type).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this restriction record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Defines rules that restrict or control enrollment in a course section, including major restrictions, class-level restrictions (sophomore and above), college restrictions, degree restrictions, cohort restrictions, and population-based reserved seating (e.g., honors program, athletes, veterans). Captures restriction type, restriction value, restriction scope, effective term, and override eligibility. Sourced from Banner SSRRESV and SSRRSTS tables.'
}

Table enrollment.registration_period {
  registration_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the registration period. Primary key.']
  primary_registration_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the administrative user who created this registration period record.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term for which this registration period applies.']
  academic_level varchar [note: 'Academic level of students eligible for this registration period.']
  add_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date students can add courses during this registration period without special permission.']
  allows_waitlist boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students can add themselves to course waitlists during this registration period.']
  allows_web_registration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students can register online through the web portal during this period.']
  banner_period_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from Ellucian Banner system for cross-system reconciliation.']
  campus_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the campus to which this registration period applies (e.g., MAIN, BRANCH, ONLINE).']
  college_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the college or school to which this registration period applies (e.g., ARTS, ENGR, BUS, MED).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this registration period record was created in the system.']
  drop_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date students can drop courses during this registration period without academic penalty.']
  enrollment_status_restriction varchar [note: 'Enrollment status restriction for this registration period, if applicable.']
  gpa_minimum decimal(4,3) [note: 'Minimum cumulative GPA required for students to register during this period, if applicable.']
  max_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum number of credit hours a student is allowed to register for during this period without special permission or overload approval.']
  min_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of credit hours a student must register for during this period to maintain enrollment status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this registration period for administrative reference.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify students about this registration period.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date when notification about this registration period was sent to eligible students.']
  overload_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Credit hour threshold above which a student is considered to have an overload and may require advisor or dean approval.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the registration period (e.g., EARLY_REG, GENERAL_REG, LATE_REG, ADD_DROP).']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the registration period (e.g., Early Registration for Seniors, General Registration, Late Registration).']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the registration period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the registration period indicating the phase of the registration cycle.']
  priority_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the student population eligible for this registration period (e.g., SENIOR, GRAD, HONORS, ATHLETE, VETERAN, DISABLED).']
  priority_group_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the student population eligible for this registration period (e.g., Senior Students, Graduate Students, Honors Program, Student Athletes).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the academic program to which this registration period applies, if restricted to specific programs.']
  registration_close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when registration closes for this period. No further registrations are accepted after this time.']
  registration_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when registration opens for this period. Students in the eligible population can begin registering at this time.']
  requires_advisor_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students must obtain advisor approval before registering during this period.']
  requires_hold_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students must clear all registration holds before being allowed to register during this period.']
  residency_restriction varchar [note: 'Residency classification restriction for this registration period, if applicable.']
  sap_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students must meet Satisfactory Academic Progress standards to register during this period.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence indicating the order of this registration period within the term registration cycle.']
  student_classification varchar [note: 'Student classification level eligible for this registration period based on credit hours completed.']
  time_ticket_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students must have an assigned time ticket to register during this period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this registration period record was last updated in the system.']
  withdrawal_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date students can withdraw from courses during this registration period. Withdrawals after this date may require special approval.']

  Note: 'Defines the registration windows and priority scheduling periods for a given term, including early registration for specific populations (seniors, athletes, honors, graduate students), general registration open dates, late registration period, and add/drop period boundaries. Captures population code, priority group, registration open datetime, registration close datetime, and maximum credit hour load allowed during each period. Supports Banner registration time-ticketing and priority enrollment management.'
}

Table enrollment.cross_list_group {
  cross_list_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cross-listed course section group. Primary key. This identifier links multiple course sections that share combined enrollment and meet together.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member or staff user authorized to approve enrollment requests for the cross-list group. Null if approval is not required.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff user who created the cross-list group record in the student information system. Used for audit and accountability purposes.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member designated as the instructor of record for the entire cross-list group. This instructor is responsible for grading and course outcomes across all sections.']
  primary_cross_course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary or lead section within the cross-list group. The primary section typically determines the course title, instructor of record, and grading schema for all linked sections.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shared classroom or meeting space where all sections in the cross-list group meet together. Null for online or asynchronous courses.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term in which this cross-list group is active. Cross-list groups are term-specific and must be re-established each term.']
  updated_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff user who last modified the cross-list group record. Used for audit and change tracking purposes.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether departmental or instructor approval is required for students to enroll in any section of the cross-list group. True for restricted courses, capstones, or research sections.']
  available_seats varchar [note: 'Number of available seats remaining in the cross-list group, calculated as max_enrollment minus current_enrollment. Used by registration systems to determine if students can enroll.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cross-list group record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours awarded for successful completion of the course. Typically consistent across all sections in the cross-list group, though variable credit arrangements are possible.']
  cross_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cross-list arrangement. Departmental: sections from different departments; interdisciplinary: sections spanning multiple colleges; level: undergraduate and graduate sections combined; honors: honors and regular sections; special topics: variable content courses; dual-listed: same course with multiple catalog numbers.']
  current_enrollment varchar [note: 'Current total number of students enrolled across all sections in the cross-list group. Used to calculate available seats and prevent over-enrollment. Updated in real-time during registration.']
  current_waitlist_count varchar [note: 'Current number of students on the waitlist for the cross-list group. Updated as students are added to or removed from the waitlist.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cross-list group is deactivated and no longer available for enrollment. Typically aligns with the term end date or course completion date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cross-list group becomes active and available for student enrollment. Typically aligns with the term registration open date.']
  fte_divisor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Divisor used to calculate student FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) for enrollment reporting. Typically 12 for undergraduate, 9 for graduate. Used to prevent double-counting students enrolled in cross-listed sections.']
  grading_mode varchar [note: 'Grading schema applied to the cross-list group. Standard: letter grades (A-F); pass_fail: binary outcome; audit: no grade assigned; honors: enhanced grading for honors sections; satisfactory_unsatisfactory: binary outcome for certain professional programs.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the cross-list group for external reference and reporting. Used in student information systems and course catalogs.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the cross-list group, typically combining the primary section identifier with participating sections for clarity in registration systems.']
  group_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cross-list group. Active groups allow enrollment; inactive groups are suspended; pending groups await approval; cancelled groups are terminated before activation; closed groups have completed their term.']
  max_enrollment varchar [note: 'Maximum combined enrollment capacity across all sections in the cross-list group. This is the total seat capacity shared by all participating sections and determines when the group is full.']
  meeting_pattern_shared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all sections in the cross-list group meet at the same time and location. True for most cross-list groups; false for asynchronous or hybrid arrangements where sections may have different meeting times.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for administrative notes, special instructions, or comments about the cross-list group. May include information about enrollment restrictions, prerequisite waivers, or coordination details.']
  prevent_duplicate_enrollment boolean [note: 'System flag indicating whether students are prevented from enrolling in multiple sections within the same cross-list group. True for most cross-list groups to prevent duplicate credit.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'Date when student registration closes for the cross-list group. Typically aligns with the add/drop deadline for the term.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'Date when student registration opens for the cross-list group. May differ from effective_start_date if early registration is permitted.']
  section_count varchar [note: 'Total number of course sections participating in this cross-list group. Typically ranges from 2 to 5 sections, though larger groups are possible for interdisciplinary courses.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this cross-list group data originated. Typically Ellucian Banner, Colleague, PeopleSoft Campus Solutions, or Workday Student.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Primary key or unique identifier of the cross-list group record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with source systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cross-list group record was last modified. Updated whenever enrollment counts, status, or configuration changes occur.']
  waitlist_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of students allowed on the combined waitlist for the cross-list group when all seats are filled. Null if waitlist is not enabled.']

  Note: 'Manages cross-listed course sections where multiple sections (often from different departments or academic levels) share a combined enrollment pool and meet together in the same room at the same time. Captures cross-list group identifier, maximum combined enrollment, current combined enrollment, primary section designator, and participating section references. Ensures accurate seat availability reporting across linked sections and prevents double-counting in official enrollment headcounts and FTE calculations.'
}

Table enrollment.concurrent_enrollment {
  concurrent_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the concurrent enrollment record. Primary key.']
  aid_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.aid_application. Business justification: Consortium agreements require aid coordination between home/host institutions. Links concurrent enrollment records to aid applications for Title IV eligibility determination, combined enrollment statu']
  home_institution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student\'s primary home institution where they are degree-seeking.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic advisor at the home institution who oversees the student\'s concurrent enrollment plan and ensures courses align with degree requirements.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student enrolled concurrently at multiple institutions.']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Concurrent enrollment agreements specify tuition payment responsibility (home vs host institution). Student account link required to track which institution bills, apply consortium agreements, coordin']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term during which concurrent enrollment is active.']
  academic_standing varchar [note: 'Student\'s academic standing status at the home institution during concurrent enrollment. Good standing indicates satisfactory progress; probation indicates below-standard performance; suspension indicates temporary removal; dismissal indicates permanent removal; warning indicates at-risk status.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the formal consortium or cross-institutional agreement governing this enrollment. Required for Title IV financial aid coordination.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Type of formal agreement between institutions. Consortium agreements coordinate financial aid; articulation agreements facilitate credit transfer; dual credit covers high school partnerships; exchange covers student exchange programs; reciprocal allows mutual course access; visiting covers temporary enrollment arrangements.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the concurrent enrollment arrangement was officially approved by both institutions. Required for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  approved_by_home varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the home institution official who approved the concurrent enrollment arrangement. Typically registrar or academic advisor.']
  approved_by_host varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the host institution official who approved the concurrent enrollment arrangement. Typically registrar or admissions officer.']
  combined_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total credit hours across both home and host institutions. Critical for Title IV financial aid eligibility determination and enrollment intensity classification.']
  combined_fte decimal(4,3) [note: 'Combined Full-Time Equivalent enrollment status calculated across both institutions. Used for financial aid eligibility, enrollment reporting, and satisfactory academic progress (SAP) determination. Typically 1.0 = full-time, 0.5 = half-time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this concurrent enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_transfer_status varchar [note: 'Status of credit transfer from host institution back to home institution. Automatic transfer occurs under articulation agreements; evaluation required indicates manual review needed; pre-approved indicates prior authorization; pending awaits decision; denied indicates credits not accepted; completed indicates transfer finalized.']
  degree_applicability varchar [note: 'Classification of how host institution credits apply to the student\'s degree program at the home institution. Degree requirement indicates required coursework; elective counts toward degree but not specific requirement; non-degree does not count toward degree; enrichment provides supplemental learning; remedial addresses deficiencies; prerequisite satisfies entry requirements.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the concurrent enrollment arrangement expires or terminates. May be null for open-ended arrangements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the concurrent enrollment arrangement becomes effective and the student may begin taking courses at the host institution.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concurrent enrollment arrangement. Active indicates currently enrolled; pending awaits approval; approved indicates authorization granted; denied indicates request rejected; withdrawn indicates student-initiated cancellation; completed indicates term finished; expired indicates agreement lapsed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|approved|denied|withdrawn|completed|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the concurrent enrollment arrangement. Dual enrollment refers to high school students taking college courses; consortium covers financial aid coordination across institutions; visiting student indicates temporary enrollment; cross-registration allows course-sharing between partner institutions; transient student indicates temporary enrollment for specific courses; exchange covers formal student exchange programs.']
  ferpa_consent_date date [note: 'Date when the student provided FERPA consent for cross-institutional data sharing. Null if consent not provided or not required.']
  ferpa_consent_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has provided FERPA consent authorizing cross-institutional data sharing between home and host institutions. Required for transcript exchange and academic progress reporting.']
  financial_aid_home_institution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the home institution is the degree-granting institution responsible for administering Title IV financial aid. True when home institution manages aid; false when host institution manages aid.']
  high_school_ceeb_code varchar [note: 'College Board CEEB code for the high school partner institution in dual enrollment arrangements. Null for non-high-school concurrent enrollments.']
  high_school_partnership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concurrent enrollment is part of a dual enrollment or early college program with a high school partner. True for high school students taking college courses.']
  home_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours the student is enrolled in at their home institution during the concurrent enrollment period. Used for combined Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) calculation.']
  host_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours the student is enrolled in at the host institution during the concurrent enrollment period. Used for combined Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) calculation.']
  host_institution_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the host institution where the student is taking concurrent courses.']
  ipeds_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concurrent enrollment must be included in IPEDS enrollment reporting. Dual enrollment high school students may be excluded from headcount depending on institutional policy and IPEDS guidance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, conditions, restrictions, or administrative details related to the concurrent enrollment arrangement.']
  nsc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concurrent enrollment must be reported to the National Student Clearinghouse for enrollment verification and degree verification services. Required for accurate multi-institutional enrollment tracking.']
  residency_classification varchar [note: 'Student\'s residency classification at the host institution for tuition and fee purposes. In-state qualifies for resident rates; out-of-state pays non-resident rates; international indicates foreign student status; reciprocity indicates special agreement rates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this concurrent enrollment record originated. Typically Banner, PeopleSoft, or other Student Information System (SIS).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this concurrent enrollment record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  title_iv_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concurrent enrollment arrangement qualifies for federal Title IV financial aid eligibility. Must meet consortium agreement requirements and combined enrollment intensity thresholds.']
  tuition_payment_responsibility varchar [note: 'Identifies which party is responsible for paying tuition to the host institution. Home institution pays under consortium agreements; host institution waives under reciprocal agreements; student pays directly; shared indicates split responsibility; third party covers external sponsor payments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this concurrent enrollment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  visa_type varchar [note: 'Type of visa held by international students in concurrent enrollment. Common values include F-1 (academic student), J-1 (exchange visitor), M-1 (vocational student). Null for domestic students. Critical for SEVIS reporting compliance.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the student withdrew from the concurrent enrollment arrangement. Null if enrollment remains active or was completed normally.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for withdrawal from concurrent enrollment. Common reasons include academic difficulty, financial constraints, schedule conflicts, transfer to single institution, personal circumstances, or completion of required coursework.']

  Note: 'Records students enrolled simultaneously at this institution and another institution under formal agreements. Covers dual enrollment (high school students taking college courses), consortium agreements (financial aid coordination across institutions), and visiting student arrangements. Captures home/host institution, enrollment type, credit hours at each institution, combined FTE for financial aid purposes, consortium agreement reference, and National Student Clearinghouse reporting flags. Supports Title IV financial aid eligibility determination and FERPA-compliant cross-institutional data sharing.'
}

Table enrollment.repeat_course_record {
  repeat_course_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the repeat course record. Primary key.']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course that was repeated. Links to the course catalog.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty or staff member who approved the repeat, if approval was required.']
  primary_repeat_enrollment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the repeat course enrollment attempt. Links to the enrollment record for the repeat attempt.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student who repeated the course. Links to the student master record.']
  repeat_enrollment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original course enrollment attempt. Links to the enrollment record for the first attempt.']
  academic_standing_impact varchar [note: 'The impact of the repeat on the student\'s academic standing: improved (standing improved), no_change (standing unchanged), declined (standing worsened).']
  course_title varchar [note: 'The title of the course that was repeated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this repeat course record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of credit hours associated with the repeated course.']
  degree_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repeat course credits are applicable toward degree completion (True/False).']
  financial_aid_countable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repeat course counts toward financial aid eligibility and Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) under federal Title IV regulations (True/False).']
  gpa_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate GPA impact: use_repeat_only (only repeat grade counts), use_both (both grades count), use_highest (highest grade counts), use_average (average of both grades).']
  grade_replacement_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether grade replacement was actually applied to this repeat (True/False). May differ from eligibility if student opted out or policy limits were reached.']
  grade_replacement_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repeat is eligible for grade replacement under institutional policy (True/False).']
  institutional_repeat_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times a course may be repeated under institutional policy.']
  ipeds_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repeat is included in IPEDS reporting calculations (True/False).']
  original_gpa_impact decimal(5,3) [note: 'The impact of the original grade on cumulative GPA calculation after repeat policy is applied. May be zero if grade replacement removes original grade from GPA.']
  original_grade varchar [note: 'The grade earned in the original course attempt (e.g., D, F, W).']
  original_grade_points decimal(4,2) [note: 'The grade points earned per credit hour in the original attempt, based on institutional grading scale.']
  original_quality_points decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total quality points earned in the original attempt (credit hours × grade points).']
  original_term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term code when the course was originally attempted (e.g., 202301 for Spring 2023).']
  repeat_approval_date date [note: 'The date when repeat approval was granted, if required.']
  repeat_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether institutional approval was required for this repeat (True/False). Some institutions require advisor or dean approval for course repeats.']
  repeat_approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status for this repeat: approved, denied, pending, not_required.']
  repeat_count varchar [note: 'The sequential number of times this course has been repeated by the student (1 for first repeat, 2 for second repeat, etc.).']
  repeat_gpa_impact decimal(5,3) [note: 'The impact of the repeat grade on cumulative GPA calculation.']
  repeat_grade varchar [note: 'The grade earned in the repeat course attempt (e.g., A, B, C).']
  repeat_grade_points decimal(4,2) [note: 'The grade points earned per credit hour in the repeat attempt, based on institutional grading scale.']
  repeat_limit_exceeded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repeat exceeds the institutional repeat limit (True/False).']
  repeat_quality_points decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total quality points earned in the repeat attempt (credit hours × grade points).']
  repeat_reason varchar [note: 'The reason the student repeated the course: grade_improvement, failed_course, withdrawn_course, degree_requirement, gpa_improvement, other.']
  repeat_term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term code when the course was repeated (e.g., 202308 for Fall 2023).']
  repeat_type varchar [note: 'The type of repeat policy applied: grade_replacement (new grade replaces old), grade_averaging (both grades averaged), grade_forgiveness (original grade excluded from GPA but remains on transcript), no_replacement (both grades count separately).']
  sap_repeat_limit_exceeded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repeat exceeds the federal financial aid repeat limit (students may repeat a previously passed course once for financial aid purposes) (True/False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record where this repeat course record originated (e.g., Banner, PeopleSoft, Colleague).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this record in the source system.']
  subject_code varchar [note: 'The academic subject or discipline code for the repeated course (e.g., MATH, ENGL, BIOL).']
  transcript_notation varchar [note: 'Special notation that appears on the academic transcript for this repeat (e.g., \'Repeated\', \'Grade Replaced\', \'Excluded from GPA\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this repeat course record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Tracks instances where a student repeats a previously attempted course, capturing original attempt term, repeat attempt term, original grade, repeat grade, grade replacement eligibility, grade replacement applied flag, GPA impact (both grades counted vs. replacement), repeat count, and institutional repeat policy applied. Supports GPA recalculation workflows, financial aid SAP repeat-course rules, and academic standing determinations per institutional repeat policy.'
}

Table enrollment.athletic_recruitment {
  athletic_recruitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this sport-specific recruitment relationship record. Primary key.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the prospective student record in the enrollment domain']
  recruiting_coach_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the coach (assistant or head coach) assigned as the primary recruiter for this prospect in this sport.']
  sport_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sport program record in the athletics domain']
  commitment_date date [note: 'Date when the prospect committed to this sport program. Null if not yet committed.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'The prospect\'s commitment status for this specific sport program. Tracks whether they have committed to compete for this sport.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sport-specific recruitment record was created in the system.']
  evaluation_rating varchar [note: 'Coaching staff\'s athletic evaluation rating for this prospect in this specific sport. Used to prioritize recruitment efforts and scholarship allocation.']
  first_contact_date date [note: 'Date of first contact between coaching staff and this prospect regarding this sport program. Used for NCAA compliance and recruitment timeline tracking.']
  interest_level varchar [note: 'The prospect\'s expressed level of interest in competing for this specific sport program. Assessed and updated by coaching staff during recruitment.']
  official_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the prospect\'s official visit to campus for this sport program. Null if no official visit has occurred.']
  primary_position varchar [note: 'The primary playing position or event specialty for which this prospect is being recruited in this sport (e.g., point guard, midfielder, 100m sprinter).']
  recruitment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from coaching staff regarding this prospect\'s recruitment for this specific sport. Captures evaluation details, contact history, and recruitment strategy.']
  recruitment_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of this prospect in the sport-specific recruitment pipeline. Tracks progression from initial inquiry through commitment for this particular sport.']
  scholarship_offer_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The scholarship equivalency amount offered to this prospect for this sport (e.g., 0.5 for half scholarship). Null if no offer extended.']
  scholarship_offer_date date [note: 'Date when the scholarship offer was extended to this prospect for this sport. Used for compliance tracking and offer timeline management.']
  scholarship_offer_extended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a scholarship offer has been extended to this prospect for this sport program. Critical for NCAA compliance and scholarship budget tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this sport-specific recruitment record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the recruitment relationship between a prospective student and a sport program. It captures sport-specific recruitment pipeline data including interest level, position evaluation, scholarship offers, and commitment status. Each record links one prospect to one sport with attributes that exist only in the context of this sport-specific recruitment process.. Existence Justification: In higher education athletics, prospective students who are multi-sport athletes are actively recruited by multiple sport programs simultaneously. Each sport program maintains its own independent recruitment pipeline for the same prospect, with sport-specific evaluations, scholarship offers, and commitment tracking. Coaches manage these recruitment relationships as distinct operational entities, and a prospect can commit to one sport while declining others.'
}

Table enrollment.review_committee {
  review_committee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for review_committee']
  academic_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic unit (college, school, department) that this committee serves or reports to.']
  chair_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee serving as the chair or lead of the review committee.']
  institution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the institution that established and governs this review committee.']
  parent_review_committee_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_committee (parent_review_committee_id)']
  secretary_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee serving as the secretary responsible for committee documentation and minutes.']
  vice_chair_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee serving as the vice chair or co-lead of the review committee.']
  appeal_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of this committee in the appeals process, where higher numbers indicate higher levels of appeal authority.']
  average_review_duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical number of days required for the committee to complete a review from submission to decision.']
  charter_document_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the committee charter document that defines its purpose, authority, and operating procedures.']
  committee_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the review committee used in external communications and reporting.']
  committee_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the review committee as recognized by the institution.']
  committee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the committee based on its primary review function within the enrollment lifecycle.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the confidentiality requirements for committee proceedings and decisions.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address for inquiries and communications directed to the review committee.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting the review committee office or administrative support.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this committee record was first created in the system.']
  decision_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of decision-making authority granted to the committee indicating whether decisions are recommendations or binding.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the committee\'s purpose, responsibilities, and scope of review authority.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the review committee ceased operations or was dissolved. Null for currently active committees.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the review committee was established and began operations.']
  external_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee includes external members from outside the institution.']
  faculty_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee includes faculty members as part of its composition.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this committee record was most recently updated in the system.']
  meeting_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency at which the committee convenes to conduct reviews.']
  meeting_location varchar [note: 'Primary physical or virtual location where the committee conducts its review meetings.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current total number of active members serving on the review committee.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or special instructions related to the committee\'s operations or unique characteristics.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Physical office location or building where the committee administrative staff is located.']
  quorum_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum number of committee members required to be present for official review decisions.']
  review_criteria_document_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the official document defining the review criteria and decision-making guidelines for this committee.']
  review_scope varchar [note: 'Academic level scope that defines which student populations this committee reviews.']
  staff_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee includes administrative staff members as part of its composition.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the review committee indicating whether it is actively conducting reviews.']
  student_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee includes student members as part of its composition.']
  term_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee operates on a term-by-term basis or continuously throughout the academic year.']
  voting_member_count varchar [note: 'Number of committee members with voting privileges on review decisions.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_committee. Referenced by committee_id.'
}

Table enrollment.institution {
  institution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for institution']
  parent_institution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent institution if this institution is a branch campus or subsidiary of a larger system.']
  accreditation_date date [note: 'Date when the institution received its current accreditation status.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation standing of the institution with its primary regional or national accrediting body.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the institution\'s main campus or administrative headquarters.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or department information.']
  campus_housing_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution provides on-campus housing facilities for students.']
  campus_setting varchar [note: 'Geographic setting classification of the institution\'s primary campus location.']
  carnegie_classification varchar [note: 'Carnegie Classification framework category describing the institution\'s mission, degree offerings, and research activity level.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the institution\'s main campus is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the institution is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this institution record was first created in the system.']
  degree_granting_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is authorized to grant degrees (true) or only offers certificates and non-degree programs (false).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for general institutional inquiries and correspondence.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the institution\'s main administrative office.']
  federal_aid_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is eligible to participate in federal student financial aid programs under Title IV.']
  fice_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit code formerly used by the federal government to identify postsecondary institutions, now largely replaced by OPE ID.']
  founded_date date [note: 'Date when the institution was originally established or chartered.']
  graduate_offering boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution offers graduate programs (master or doctoral degrees).']
  highest_degree_offered varchar [note: 'The highest level of degree or credential that the institution is authorized to confer.']
  hispanic_serving_institution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is designated as a Hispanic Serving Institution based on enrollment demographics.']
  historically_black_college boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is designated as a Historically Black College or University.']
  institution_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the institution for external identification and reporting purposes.']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the higher education institution.']
  institution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the institution based on control and ownership structure.']
  ipeds_unit_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit unique identifier assigned by IPEDS for federal reporting and data collection.']
  land_grant_institution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is designated as a land-grant institution under the Morrill Acts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this institution record was last updated.']
  medical_degree_granting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution grants medical degrees (MD, DO) through an accredited medical school.']
  next_accreditation_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next comprehensive accreditation review or reaffirmation.']
  online_programs_offered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution offers fully online degree or certificate programs.']
  ope_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit identifier assigned by the U.S. Department of Education Office of Postsecondary Education for Title IV eligibility.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the institution indicating whether it is actively enrolling and teaching students.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the institution\'s main administrative office.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the institution\'s main address.']
  primary_accreditor varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regional or national accrediting agency responsible for institutional accreditation.']
  religious_affiliation varchar [note: 'Religious denomination or affiliation of the institution, if applicable.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or territory where the institution is located.']
  system_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is part of a larger multi-campus system or consortium.']
  tribal_college boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is designated as a Tribal College or University.']
  undergraduate_offering boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution offers undergraduate programs (associate or bachelor degrees).']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the institution.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for institution. Referenced by home_institution_id.'
}

  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic department that is hosting or sponsoring this session.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or employee responsible for coordinating and managing this recruitment event session.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or staff member who created this recruitment event session record.']

  recruitment_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent recruitment event under which this session is organized.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location or facility where the in-person or hybrid session is held.']
  updated_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or staff member who last updated this recruitment event session record.']
  attendance_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of prospective students or attendees who participated in or checked into this session.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason provided when a recruitment event session is cancelled or postponed.']
  capacity_available varchar [note: 'Current number of open seats or slots remaining for registration in this session.']
  capacity_total varchar [note: 'Maximum number of prospective students or attendees that can be accommodated in this session.']
  check_in_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendees must check in upon arrival or at the start of the session for attendance tracking.']
  confirmation_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether confirmation emails have been sent to registered attendees for this session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recruitment event session record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which the recruitment event session is delivered to prospective students.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the recruitment event session measured in minutes.']
  meeting_access_code varchar [note: 'Password or access code required to join the virtual session.']
  presenter_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names of faculty, staff, or student ambassadors who will present or facilitate this session.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'Date when registration for this session closes and no further sign-ups are accepted.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'Date when registration for this session becomes available to prospective students.']
  registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prospective students must pre-register to attend this session.']
  reminder_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reminder emails have been sent to registered attendees prior to the session start.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the recruitment event session, used for registration and communication purposes.']
  session_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the recruitment event session, including agenda, topics covered, and what prospective students can expect.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recruitment event session is scheduled to conclude.']
  session_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the recruitment event session (e.g., \'Engineering Open House Morning Session\', \'Virtual Campus Tour - Fall 2024\').']
  session_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments about the session for staff reference, including special instructions or post-event observations.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recruitment event session is scheduled to begin.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recruitment event session indicating its operational state.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recruitment event session by format and purpose.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary audience segment that this recruitment event session is designed to serve.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this recruitment event session record was last modified or updated.']
  virtual_meeting_url varchar [note: 'Web link or URL for accessing the virtual recruitment event session.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the virtual meeting or webinar platform used for virtual or hybrid sessions (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Webex).']
  waitlist_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waitlist is available for prospective students when the session reaches full capacity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for recruitment_event_session. Referenced by event_session_id.'
}

Table aid.aid_application {
  aid_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial aid application record. Primary key for the aid application entity.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Aid applications require program context for eligibility determination—program-specific aid rules (STEM grants, nursing scholarships), COA variations by program, and enrollment intensity validation al']
  aid_sap_evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.sap_evaluation. Business justification: Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) evaluation is performed at checkpoints and affects aid eligibility. The aid_application should reference the SAP evaluation record that determined eligibility for ']
  award_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.award_package. Business justification: An aid application results in the creation of an award package. This is a 1:1 or 1:0..1 relationship (application may not result in package if student is ineligible). The aid_application should refere']
  award_year_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic award year for which the student is applying for financial aid (e.g., 2023-2024). Links to the award year reference table.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial aid staff member or system user who last updated the application record. Used for audit and accountability purposes.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Financial aid offices use specific enterprise applications (Banner Financial Aid, PowerFAIDS, CampusLogic) to process applications. Tracking which system processed each application is essential for da']
  isir_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.isir_record. Business justification: The aid_application is based on a specific ISIR (Institutional Student Information Record) received from CPS. Currently aid_application has isir_transaction_number as a STRING, but should FK to the is']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student submitting the financial aid application. Links to the student master record.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Title IV applications must track governing federal regulations (HEA sections, 34 CFR parts) that define eligibility criteria, EFC calculation methodology, and verification requirements. Essential for ']
  residence_building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Financial aid COA budgets vary by housing type (on-campus residence hall vs off-campus). Aid applications capture housing_plan_code but need actual residence building for verification, COA calculation']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Aid applications must reference student billing accounts to verify enrollment charges exist, coordinate aid packaging with account balances, and ensure disbursements post correctly. Core operational d']
  verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.verification. Business justification: Students selected for federal verification have a verification record that tracks the process. The aid_application should FK to the verification record. Columns verification_selection_flag, verificati']
  aggregate_loan_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has reached or exceeded federal aggregate loan limits for subsidized and unsubsidized Direct Loans. When true, student cannot borrow additional federal loans.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the financial aid application, often used for external reference and student communication.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the financial aid application in the institutional workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_review|verified|packaged|awarded|cancelled|incomplete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  c_code_flag varchar [note: 'Federal comment codes from the ISIR indicating issues that must be resolved before aid can be disbursed (e.g., conflicting information, missing signatures, data mismatches).']
  citizenship_status varchar [note: 'Federal citizenship or eligible noncitizen status. Determines eligibility for federal Title IV financial aid programs.']
  coa_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total Cost of Attendance budget assigned to this application. Includes tuition, fees, room, board, books, supplies, transportation, and personal expenses. Used to calculate financial need.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the aid application record was first created in the Student Information System.']
  default_overpayment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is in default on a federal student loan or owes an overpayment on a federal grant. Students with this flag are ineligible for Title IV aid until resolved.']
  direct_loan_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is eligible for Federal Direct Loans (subsidized and/or unsubsidized) based on enrollment, citizenship, and loan limit checks.']
  drug_conviction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has a federal or state drug conviction that affects Title IV aid eligibility during the period of enrollment.']
  enrollment_intensity varchar [note: 'Expected enrollment intensity for the award year. Determines eligibility for certain aid programs and affects disbursement amounts.']
  fafsa_receipt_date date [note: 'Date the institution received the students FAFSA data from the federal processor. Critical for priority deadline determination and aid packaging sequencing.']
  financial_need_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated financial need for the application. Computed as Cost of Attendance minus Expected Family Contribution (or Student Aid Index). Determines eligibility for need-based aid.']
  housing_plan_code varchar [note: 'Students planned housing arrangement for the award year. Affects Cost of Attendance (COA) calculation as different housing plans have different budget allowances.']
  institutional_methodology_efc decimal(12,2) [note: 'Expected Family Contribution calculated using the institutions own methodology (e.g., CSS Profile). Used for awarding institutional need-based aid, separate from federal methodology.']
  pell_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is eligible for a Federal Pell Grant based on EFC/SAI and enrollment status.']
  pell_leu_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the students lifetime Pell Grant eligibility used. Students are limited to 600% (equivalent to six years of full-time enrollment). Tracked cumulatively across all institutions.']
  professional_judgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial aid administrator exercised professional judgment to adjust the students EFC/SAI or dependency status based on special circumstances.']
  professional_judgment_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for professional judgment adjustment, such as loss of employment, medical expenses, or dependency override.']
  selective_service_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether male students aged 18-25 have registered with Selective Service as required for Title IV aid eligibility.']
  state_aid_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is eligible for state-funded financial aid programs based on residency and state-specific requirements.']
  submitted_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the student initially submitted the financial aid application to the institution.']
  unusual_enrollment_history_flag boolean [note: 'Federal flag indicating the student has an unusual enrollment history pattern (attending multiple institutions in a short period). Requires additional documentation before aid can be disbursed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the aid application record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  veteran_benefits_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is receiving or eligible for veteran education benefits (e.g., GI Bill, Yellow Ribbon Program). Affects coordination of benefits and COA adjustments.']

  Note: 'Master record of a student\'s financial aid application for a specific award year. Captures FAFSA receipt date, dependency status, verification selection flag, EFC/SAI, housing plans, enrollment intensity, citizenship status, SAP status, and application processing stage. Includes Title IV eligibility indicators (selective service, default/overpayment clearance, aggregate loan limits, Pell LEU). SSOT for a student\'s aid eligibility determination and federal eligibility gating for a given award year.'
}

Table aid.award_package {
  award_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial aid award package. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Award packaging methodology varies by program—professional programs have different COA structures, program-specific scholarship pools require program identification, and packaging rules often differen']
  award_year_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the award year for which this package is assembled.']
  coa_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the COA budget used for this award package, defining the total allowable cost for the student.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial aid counselor who assembled this award package.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student receiving this award package.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Award packages must comply with federal methodology requirements (34 CFR 668.2 for COA, Pell LEU limits, aggregate loan caps). Tracking regulatory basis ensures packaging decisions are defensible duri']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Award packages reduce billing obligations and appear as anticipated aid on statements. Financial aid officers review account status during packaging; billing systems need award totals to calculate net']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this award package record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this package (typically USD for U.S. institutions).']
  default_status_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is in default on any federal student loans, affecting eligibility for additional aid.']
  demonstrated_need_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated financial need for the student, typically COA minus EFC, representing the gap to be filled by aid.']
  dependency_status varchar [note: 'Student dependency status as determined by FAFSA, affecting eligibility for certain aid types.']
  efc_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Expected Family Contribution calculated from FAFSA data, representing the amount the family is expected to contribute toward educational costs.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Student enrollment intensity at the time of packaging, affecting aid eligibility and amounts.']
  housing_status varchar [note: 'Student housing arrangement used to determine the appropriate COA budget.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this award package record was last modified.']
  last_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision to this award package.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this award package, used for internal documentation and communication among financial aid staff.']
  offer_letter_sent_date date [note: 'Date on which the award offer letter was sent to the student.']
  overaward_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount by which total awarded aid exceeds the COA or regulatory limits, requiring resolution to maintain Title IV compliance.']
  overaward_detected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an overaward condition has been detected for this package.']
