  event_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this faculty event participation record. Primary key for the association.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the advancement event in which the instructor is participating']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instructor participating in the advancement event']
  honoree_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the faculty member is being honored or recognized at this event (e.g., distinguished professor award, endowed chair announcement). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  invitation_date date [note: 'Date when the faculty member was invited to participate in the advancement event. Used for tracking response time and planning timelines.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about the faculty member\'s participation including special requirements, dietary restrictions, AV needs, or post-event feedback.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the faculty member\'s participation in the event. Tracks progression from invitation through attendance. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  presentation_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Scheduled duration in minutes for the faculty member\'s presentation or remarks. Used for event agenda planning.']
  presentation_title varchar [note: 'Title of the presentation, speech, or remarks delivered by the faculty member at the event. Null for non-speaking roles. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  response_date date [note: 'Date when the faculty member responded to the participation invitation. Used for event planning and follow-up.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'The role in which the faculty member participates in the advancement event. Determines expectations, compensation, and recognition. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  service_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of service hours credited to the faculty member for this event participation. Used for faculty service tracking and promotion/tenure documentation.']
  speaker_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation paid to the faculty member for their participation in the event. May be zero for voluntary participation. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  travel_reimbursed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution reimburses travel expenses for the faculty member to attend this event. Relevant for regional or off-campus events. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between instructors and advancement events. It captures faculty involvement in donor cultivation and stewardship activities including speaking engagements, honoree recognition, and hosting duties. Each record links one instructor to one advancement_event with attributes that describe the nature, compensation, and logistics of their participation.. Existence Justification: Faculty members participate in multiple advancement events throughout their careers (galas, donor dinners, campaign launches, named lecture series), and each advancement event involves multiple faculty members in various capacities (speakers, honorees, hosts, panelists). The advancement office actively manages faculty rosters for each event, tracking invitation status, roles, compensation, and service credit. This is an operational business process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table faculty.resource_evaluation {
  resource_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this evaluation record. Primary key.']
  electronic_resource_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the electronic resource being evaluated']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instructor who evaluated the resource']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this evaluation record was created in the library system.']
  evaluation_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments from the instructor regarding resource quality, usability, content coverage, and value proposition.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date when the instructor submitted their evaluation feedback to the library.']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'Instructors recommendation regarding whether the institution should acquire or subscribe to this electronic resource.']
  subject_relevance_rating varchar [note: 'Instructors rating of how relevant the resource is to their discipline and teaching/research needs. Scale 1-5 where 5 is highly relevant.']
  trial_evaluation_role varchar [note: 'Role in which the instructor evaluated the resource. Indicates whether they were solicited as primary evaluator, department representative, or provided unsolicited feedback.']
  usage_commitment varchar [note: 'Instructors statement of how they intend to use the resource (e.g., required reading for HIST 301, supplemental research for graduate seminars, reference for thesis advising).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the evaluation event between instructor and electronic_resource during trial periods. It captures faculty feedback solicited by the library to inform collection development and acquisition decisions. Each record links one instructor to one electronic_resource trial with evaluation metadata including role, recommendation, and subject relevance assessment.. Existence Justification: In higher education library operations, electronic resource trial evaluations are a formal collection development process where the library solicits feedback from multiple faculty across relevant departments before making acquisition decisions. One instructor evaluates multiple database trials across their disciplines, and each resource trial is evaluated by multiple faculty to gather diverse perspectives on relevance, usability, and value. The evaluation relationship is an operational business entity that library staff actively create, track, and aggregate to inform renewal decisions and cost-per-use analysis.'
}

Table faculty.project_participation {
  project_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this faculty project participation record. Primary key.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instructor participating in the IT project']
  it_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the IT project in which the instructor is participating']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the instructor was formally assigned to participate in this IT project by governance or project management. May precede actual start_date.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this faculty participation is billable to the project budget (true) or contributed as in-kind effort (false). Used for project cost accounting and faculty compensation tracking.']
  effort_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the instructor\'s time allocated to this IT project participation. Used for workload planning and effort tracking across concurrent project engagements.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the instructor\'s participation in this IT project ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing active participation.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the instructor\'s participation in this IT project. Active indicates ongoing engagement, completed indicates role fulfilled, on_hold indicates temporary suspension, withdrawn indicates early termination.']
  role_on_project varchar [note: 'The specific role or capacity in which the instructor is participating in the IT project. Determines expectations and responsibilities for this engagement.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the instructor\'s participation in this IT project began. Marks the start of their engagement in the specified role.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between faculty instructors and institutional IT projects. It captures faculty involvement as subject matter experts, pilot users, governance representatives, or advisory committee members across technology initiatives. Each record links one instructor to one IT project with role, effort allocation, and participation timeline attributes that exist only in the context of this specific project engagement.. Existence Justification: In higher education IT governance, faculty members routinely participate in multiple concurrent IT projects (LMS upgrades, research computing initiatives, ERP implementations) in various capacities such as subject matter experts, pilot users, or governance representatives. Simultaneously, each IT project requires participation from multiple faculty members across different departments to ensure stakeholder representation, domain expertise, and user acceptance. Universities actively manage these participation assignments to balance faculty workload, ensure adequate project staffing, and track effort allocation for budget and compensation purposes.'
}

Table faculty.faculty_application_access {
  faculty_application_access_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for faculty_application_access']
  access_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this application access record. Primary key for the application_access association.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enterprise application for which access has been provisioned']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instructor who has been provisioned access to the enterprise application']
  access_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this access grant expires and requires renewal or review. Null for permanent access grants. Used for periodic access recertification.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access granted to the instructor for this application, indicating scope and permissions (e.g., full, restricted, read_only, department_scoped).']
  access_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the instructor\'s access to this application (active, suspended, expired, pending_approval, revoked). Used for access governance and security management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved this application access request. Used for compliance and audit trail.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center or department code to which this application access license cost is allocated. Used for chargeback and budget management.']
  deprovisioning_date date [note: 'Date when access to this application was revoked or deprovisioned for the instructor. Null for active access. Used for access lifecycle tracking.']
  deprovisioning_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for access revocation (e.g., termination, role_change, license_optimization, security_incident). Used for audit and analysis.']
  last_login_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the instructor\'s most recent login to this application. Used for license optimization, inactive account identification, and usage analytics.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Type of license assigned to the instructor for this application (e.g., named_user, concurrent, site_license). Used for license cost allocation and optimization.']
  provisioning_date date [note: 'Date when access to this application was initially provisioned for the instructor. Used for access lifecycle tracking and compliance auditing.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person (typically department chair or IT staff) who requested this application access for the instructor. Used for audit trail.']
  training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the instructor has completed required training for this application. Used for compliance tracking and support planning.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the instructor completed required training for this application. Null if training not yet completed.']
  user_role varchar [note: 'Role assigned to the instructor within this specific application, determining functional permissions and capabilities (e.g., admin, instructor, researcher, standard_user).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the access provisioning relationship between instructors and enterprise applications. It captures user access management, role assignments, and usage tracking for faculty members across the institutional application portfolio. Each record links one instructor to one enterprise application with attributes that exist only in the context of this access relationship, including role, access level, provisioning dates, and training status.. Existence Justification: In higher education IT operations, instructors require access to multiple enterprise applications (Canvas LMS, Banner, research administration systems, library systems, HR self-service portals) to perform their teaching, research, and administrative duties. Each application has hundreds or thousands of faculty users with varying roles and access levels. The university actively manages this many-to-many relationship through IT provisioning processes, tracking access grants, roles, training completion, last login dates, and license assignments for compliance, security auditing, and cost optimization.'
}

Table instruction.course_section {
  course_section_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the course section. Primary key for this operational instance of a course offering.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Course scheduling systems must validate building availability, accessibility compliance, and campus location for registration display and facility coordination. Building reference required for room sc']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Course sections are delivered by academic departments that are cost centers. Essential for departmental budget reports, cost allocation, financial planning, and IPEDS finance surveys that aggregate in']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent course catalog definition from the curriculum domain. Links this operational section to its canonical course definition.']
  llc_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.llc_program. Business justification: Living-Learning Community programs require specific course enrollments (honors seminars, themed first-year experiences). Registrar systems restrict section access to LLC participants, enforce curricul']
  lms_course_shell_id varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this section to its Canvas LMS course shell. Used for integration and analytics.']
  primary_instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member designated as the primary instructor of record for this section. Responsible for grading and course management.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Primary meeting location for course sections drives registration systems, student schedules, and facility utilization reporting. Core scheduling relationship required for room conflict detection and c']
  statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_statement. Business justification: Billing statements itemize charges by enrolled course section. Registrar and bursar offices use this link for enrollment verification, statement accuracy audits, and processing drop/add adjustments th']
  student_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.student_org. Business justification: Student organizations sponsor course sections (Greek life leadership seminars, honor society colloquia). Registrar systems track org-affiliated sections for co-curricular transcript notation, restrict']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term in which this section is offered (e.g., Fall 2024, Spring 2025).']
  tuition_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tuition_charge. Business justification: Course sections generate tuition charges upon enrollment. Bursar offices require this link for drop/add refund calculations, enrollment verification on billing statements, and Title IV return-of-funds']
  campus_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the campus where this section is primarily offered. Used for multi-campus institutions.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code classifying the subject matter of this section for federal IPEDS reporting. Format: XX.XXXX.']
  college_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the college or school offering this section. Higher-level organizational unit above department.']
  contact_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of instructional contact hours per week for this section. Used for workload calculation and accreditation reporting.']
  course_level varchar [note: 'Academic level of the course section: undergraduate, graduate, professional (law, medicine), or continuing education.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this course section record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of academic credit hours awarded upon successful completion of this section. May differ from catalog default for variable-credit courses.']
  crn varchar [note: 'Five-digit Banner-specific course reference number that uniquely identifies this section within a term. The externally-known business identifier used by students and faculty for registration.']
  cross_listed_sections varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CRNs for sections cross-listed with this section. Students in cross-listed sections attend the same class but receive credit under different course numbers.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which instruction is delivered: in-person (traditional classroom), online (fully remote), hybrid (mix of in-person and online), HyFlex (students choose attendance mode per session), or blended.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when instruction concludes for this section. May differ from term end date for sub-term or module-based courses.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Time when the class session ends in 24-hour format (HH:MM). Null for asynchronous online sections.']
  enrollment_actual varchar [note: 'Current number of students enrolled in this section. Updated in real-time during registration periods.']
  enrollment_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of students who can enroll in this section. Determined by room capacity, pedagogical considerations, or accreditation requirements.']
  fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional course-specific fees in US dollars (e.g., lab fees, materials fees, technology fees) charged for this section.']
  grade_mode varchar [note: 'Grading scheme used for this section: standard letter grades, pass/fail, audit (no grade), or honors designation.']
  instructional_method varchar [note: 'Pedagogical approach used for instruction (e.g., lecture, laboratory, seminar, studio, independent study, practicum). Distinct from delivery mode.']
  linked_sections varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CRNs for sections that must be taken concurrently (e.g., lecture and lab). Students must register for all linked sections together.']
  meeting_days varchar [note: 'Days of the week when the section meets using standard abbreviations: M=Monday, T=Tuesday, W=Wednesday, R=Thursday, F=Friday, S=Saturday, U=Sunday.']
  meeting_pattern varchar [note: 'Days and times when the section meets (e.g., MWF 10:00-10:50, TR 14:00-15:15). Encoded string representing the schedule.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this course section record was last updated. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  registration_end_date date [note: 'Date when student registration closes for this section. Typically the add/drop deadline.']
  registration_start_date date [note: 'Date when student registration opens for this section. May vary by student population or priority group.']
  section_number varchar [note: 'Section identifier within the course and term (e.g., 001, 002, A, B, W01). Distinguishes multiple offerings of the same course in the same term.']
  section_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the course section indicating whether it is available for enrollment, has been cancelled, is in progress, or has concluded.']
  section_title varchar [note: 'Optional section-specific title that may differ from the catalog course title. Used when a section focuses on a particular topic or theme.']
  special_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students need instructor, advisor, or departmental permission to register for this section.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when instruction begins for this section. May differ from term start date for sub-term or module-based courses.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Time when the class session begins in 24-hour format (HH:MM). Null for asynchronous online sections.']
  tuition_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base tuition charge for this section in US dollars. May vary by student residency, program, or fee structure.']
  waitlist_actual varchar [note: 'Current number of students on the waitlist for this section.']
  waitlist_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of students allowed on the waitlist when the section reaches enrollment capacity. Zero indicates no waitlist is available.']
  withdrawal_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date students can withdraw from this section without academic penalty. After this date, withdrawals may result in a W grade.']

  Note: 'Operational instance of a course offering for a specific term, representing the scheduled class that students enroll in. Captures section number, delivery mode (in-person, online, hybrid, HyFlex), meeting pattern, enrollment capacity, waitlist capacity, section status, credit hours, instructional method, CIP code, Canvas course shell reference, and Banner CRN. This is the primary operational unit of instruction delivery and the anchor entity for all instructional activity — faculty assignments, meetings, grades, and assessments all reference this product. Distinct from the curriculum domain\'s course catalog definition.'
}

Table instruction.section_meeting {
  section_meeting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the section meeting record. Primary key.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'FK to facility.building']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent course section for which this meeting pattern is scheduled. A single section may have multiple meeting records (e.g., lecture MWF plus lab T).']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary instructor assigned to this meeting. A meeting may have multiple instructors; this captures the primary instructor.']
  lms_course_shell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Canvas LMS course shell associated with this section meeting. Used for integration between SIS and LMS.']
  scorm_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of SCORM-compliant learning content associated with this meeting. Used for tracking standardized e-learning modules.']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of accessibility features available in the assigned room (e.g., wheelchair accessible, hearing loop, adjustable desks). Supports ADA compliance.']
  begin_time timestamp [note: 'Time at which the meeting begins, in 24-hour HH:MM format. Null for arranged or online asynchronous meetings.']
  building_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the building where the meeting is held. Null for online or arranged meetings.']
  campus_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the campus where the meeting is held (e.g., MAIN, NORTH, ONLINE).']
  conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting has a detected scheduling conflict (room double-booked or instructor double-booked). Used for schedule validation.']
  contact_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of contact hours per week for this meeting pattern. Critical for accreditation reporting and faculty workload calculation.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meeting record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours associated with this meeting. Denormalized from section for reporting convenience.']
  crn varchar [note: 'Five-digit unique identifier for the course section within a term. Denormalized from section for reporting convenience.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last date on which this meeting pattern is effective. May differ from term end date for modules or sub-term courses.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Time at which the meeting ends, in 24-hour HH:MM format. Null for arranged or online asynchronous meetings.']
  friday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Fridays.']
  meeting_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying this meeting within the section. Used to distinguish multiple meeting patterns for the same section (e.g., 1 for lecture, 2 for lab).']
  meeting_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting follows a fixed schedule, is arranged by instructor, is fully online, or is to be announced (TBA).']
  meeting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the meeting pattern. Active meetings are in effect; cancelled meetings have been removed from the schedule; rescheduled meetings have been moved; completed meetings have concluded.']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Type of instructional meeting. Distinguishes between lecture, lab, recitation, and other instructional modalities. Critical for contact hour calculation and accreditation reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lecture|lab|recitation|seminar|studio|clinical|practicum|independent_study|online_synchronous|online_asynchronous|hybrid — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system process that last modified this meeting record.']
  modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meeting record was last modified.']
  monday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Mondays.']
  online_meeting_url varchar [note: 'URL for synchronous online meetings (e.g., Zoom, Teams). Populated for online synchronous and hybrid meeting types.']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting has been published to the public class schedule. Unpublished meetings are visible only to administrators.']
  room_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum seating capacity of the assigned room. Used for enrollment cap validation and space utilization analysis.']
  room_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific room within the building. Null for online or arranged meetings.']
  room_setup_type varchar [note: 'Physical configuration of the room (e.g., lecture hall, seminar table, lab benches). Used for room assignment validation and space planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lecture|seminar|lab|computer_lab|studio|auditorium|conference — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  saturday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Saturdays.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which this meeting data originated (Banner for section scheduling, Archibus for room assignments, or manual entry).']
  start_date date [note: 'First date on which this meeting pattern is effective. May differ from term start date for modules or sub-term courses.']
  sunday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Sundays.']
  technology_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of technology features available in the assigned room (e.g., projector, smartboard, video conferencing, recording capability).']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit code identifying the academic term (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024). Denormalized from section for reporting convenience.']
  thursday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Thursdays.']
  tuesday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Tuesdays.']
  wednesday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meeting occurs on Wednesdays.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system process that created this meeting record.']

  Note: 'Scheduled meeting pattern for a course section, capturing the specific days, times, start and end dates, building, room, and meeting type (lecture, lab, recitation, online synchronous). A single course section may have multiple meeting records (e.g., lecture MWF + lab T). Sourced from Banner SSRMEET and Archibus room scheduling. Supports class schedule publication, room conflict detection, and accreditation contact-hour reporting.'
}

Table instruction.faculty_assignment {
  faculty_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the faculty assignment record. Primary key for the faculty assignment entity.']
  campus_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.campus_event. Business justification: Faculty receive workload credit for event facilitation (symposia, guest lectures, orientation sessions). HR systems track compensable event assignments for contract compliance, overload pay calculatio']
  compliance_training_completion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_completion. Business justification: Faculty must complete mandatory compliance training (Title IX, FERPA, Clery, research ethics) before teaching assignments are finalized. HR and academic departments verify training completion as prere']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section to which the faculty member is assigned. Links to the course section entity in the instruction domain.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty member or instructor assigned to the course section. Links to the faculty master data entity in the faculty domain.']
  instructor_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.identity_account. Business justification: Faculty assignments trigger access provisioning to LMS, email, and campus systems. Business needs this link for automated role-based access control, entitlement assignment, and timely deprovisioning w']
  office_building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Faculty office location assignment drives space allocation, office hour scheduling, and HR-facilities coordination. Real process: institutions track faculty office buildings for workload planning, stu']
  office_room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Faculty office room assignment required for space allocation systems, office hour publishing, student advising logistics, and facilities space utilization reporting. Real process: HR and facilities jo']
  primary_faculty_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (typically department chair or dean) who approved this faculty assignment. Links to the user or employee entity.']
  primary_faculty_instruction_assignment_id varchar [note: 'The native assignment identifier from Ellucian Banner Student Information System. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking.']
  primary_faculty_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic department responsible for this faculty assignment. May differ from the faculty member\'s home department in cases of cross-departmental teaching.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term (semester, quarter, session) during which the faculty assignment is active. Links to the term entity.']
  tertiary_faculty_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this faculty assignment record. Links to the user or employee entity for audit purposes.']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'The type of faculty appointment held by the instructor at the time of assignment. Reflects the employment classification and tenure status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tenure_track|tenured|adjunct|visiting|emeritus|clinical|research|lecturer — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the faculty assignment was officially approved by the department chair or authorized administrator.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the faculty assignment concludes. Typically aligns with the term end date but may differ for early departures or extended grading periods.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this faculty assignment. May include special arrangements, accommodations, or administrative remarks.']
  assignment_role varchar [note: 'The instructional role the faculty member fulfills for this course section. Defines the type of teaching responsibility.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the faculty assignment becomes effective. Typically aligns with the term start date but may differ for late assignments or mid-term changes.']
  compensation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary compensation amount for this specific assignment, if applicable. Null for assignments covered under base salary. Stored in institutional base currency (USD).']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the faculty member is compensated for this assignment. Indicates whether the assignment is part of base salary, overload pay, or other compensation arrangement.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'The contract or agreement number associated with this faculty assignment, if applicable. Relevant for adjunct and visiting faculty with specific contractual arrangements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this faculty assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  fte_contribution decimal(5,4) [note: 'The portion of a full-time equivalent position this assignment represents. Used for FTE reporting to IPEDS and internal resource planning. Values typically range from 0.0000 to 1.0000.']
  grant_account_code varchar [note: 'The financial account or grant code to which this faculty assignment\'s costs should be charged, if grant-funded. Null if not applicable.']
  grant_funded_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this faculty assignment\'s compensation funded by external grants or sponsored research. Values range from 0.00 to 100.00. Null if not grant-funded.']
  ipeds_reporting_category varchar [note: 'The IPEDS functional expense category to which this faculty assignment\'s effort should be allocated for federal reporting purposes.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this faculty assignment record is currently active in the system. True if active, False if logically deleted or archived.']
  is_grading_authority boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this faculty member has authority to submit final grades for the course section. True if authorized, False otherwise.']
  is_primary_instructor boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this faculty member is the primary instructor of record for the course section. True if primary instructor, False otherwise.']
  is_team_taught boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this course section is team-taught by multiple instructors. True if team-taught, False if single instructor.']
  lms_instructor_role varchar [note: 'The role assigned to the faculty member in the Canvas Learning Management System course shell. Determines LMS permissions and capabilities.']
  office_hours_required decimal(4,1) [note: 'Number of office hours per week the faculty member is required to hold for students in this course section. Measured in hours per week.']
  percent_responsibility decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of instructional responsibility allocated to this faculty member for the course section. Used when multiple instructors share teaching duties. Values range from 0.00 to 100.00.']
  teaching_modality varchar [note: 'The instructional delivery mode for this faculty assignment. Indicates whether teaching is conducted in-person, online, or in a hybrid format.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this faculty assignment record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  workload_credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours counted toward the faculty member\'s teaching load for this assignment. May differ from course credit hours based on institutional workload policies and percent responsibility.']

  Note: 'Assignment of a faculty member or instructor to a course section for a given term, capturing role (primary instructor, co-instructor, teaching assistant, guest lecturer), percent responsibility, assignment status, workload credit hours, and Banner/Workday identifiers. Represents the instructional staffing record for a section. Supports teaching load calculation, FTE reporting, and IPEDS faculty activity reporting. References faculty master data owned by the faculty domain.'
}

Table instruction.lms_course_shell {
  lms_course_shell_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the LMS course shell record. Primary key for the Canvas course shell entity representing the digital instructional container.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Hybrid and HyFlex courses require building reference in LMS for in-person session locations, campus resource access instructions, and student wayfinding. Real process: LMS displays building location f']
  enrollment_term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term in which this course shell is active (e.g., Fall 2024, Spring 2025). Links to the term master data.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: LMS platforms (Canvas, Blackboard, Moodle) are enterprise applications. Business needs this link for vendor management, license tracking, support ticket routing, integration planning, and contract ren']
  it_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_service. Business justification: LMS course shells are hosted on IT services (Canvas service, Blackboard service). Business needs this link for SLA monitoring, incident impact analysis, capacity planning, service outage notifications']
  primary_lms_canvas_course_id varchar [note: 'Native Canvas system identifier for the course shell. This is the internal Canvas ID used in API calls and Canvas URLs.']
  tertiary_lms_blueprint_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent blueprint course if this course shell is associated with a Canvas blueprint. Blueprint courses serve as templates that push content and settings to associated child courses. Null if not a blueprint-associated course.']
  allow_student_discussion_editing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students can edit their own discussion posts after submission. True if editing is allowed; false if posts are locked after creation.']
  allow_student_discussion_topics boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students are permitted to create their own discussion topics in the course. True if students can create discussions; false if only instructors can create discussions.']
  allow_student_forum_attachments boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students can attach files to discussion posts. True if attachments are allowed; false if disabled.']
  apply_assignment_group_weights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assignment group weights are used in final grade calculation. True if weighted grading is enabled (e.g., exams 40%, homework 30%, participation 30%); false if all assignments are equally weighted.']
  concluded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the course shell transitioned to completed workflow state. Marks the official conclusion of the course and archival of active instruction.']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the course in Canvas, typically derived from the SIS course code (e.g., MATH-101-01-FALL2024). Displayed in Canvas UI and used for sorting and searching.']
  course_format varchar [note: 'Instructional delivery format for the course. On-campus indicates traditional face-to-face instruction; online indicates fully remote delivery; blended indicates hybrid instruction combining both modalities.']
  course_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the course shell as displayed to students and faculty in Canvas (e.g., Introduction to Calculus - Section 01).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the course shell record was first created in Canvas. Represents the initial provisioning of the digital instructional container.']
  default_view varchar [note: 'The landing page students see when entering the course shell. Options include activity feed, wiki front page, modules list, syllabus, or assignments list.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the course shell concludes and transitions to completed state. Typically aligns with the last day of the academic term or course section end date.']
  grading_standard_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the grading standard (grading scheme) applied to this course shell. Defines letter grade thresholds and GPA calculation rules. Null if using default institutional grading standard.']
  hide_final_grades boolean [note: 'Indicates whether final course grades are hidden from students in the Canvas gradebook. True if final grades are hidden; false if visible to students.']
  homeroom_course boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this course shell is designated as a homeroom course for K-12 or advisory purposes. True if homeroom; false if regular academic course.']
  integration_ref varchar [note: 'External integration identifier used for third-party system synchronization. Supports integration with external tools, SIS systems, or other enterprise applications.']
  is_blueprint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this course shell itself is a blueprint master course. True if this is a blueprint template; false if it is a regular course or blueprint-associated course.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course shell is publicly visible to unauthenticated users. True if the course is open to public view; false if restricted to enrolled users only.']
  is_public_to_auth_users boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course shell is visible to all authenticated institution users regardless of enrollment. True if any logged-in user can view; false if restricted to enrolled users only.']
  last_sis_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization with the Student Information System (Banner). Used for monitoring integration health and data freshness.']
  license varchar [note: 'Intellectual property license applied to course content. Defines usage rights for course materials. Common values include Creative Commons licenses or institutional copyright policies.']
  lms_account_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the Canvas account (organizational unit) that owns this course shell. Accounts represent colleges, schools, departments, or other organizational hierarchies within the institution.']
  lms_root_account_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the top-level Canvas account in the organizational hierarchy. Typically represents the institution itself. Used for multi-tenant Canvas instances.']
  public_syllabus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course syllabus is publicly accessible without authentication. True if syllabus is public; false if restricted to enrolled users.']
  public_syllabus_to_auth boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course syllabus is visible to all authenticated institution users. True if any logged-in user can view syllabus; false if restricted to enrolled users.']
  restrict_enrollments_to_dates boolean [note: 'Indicates whether student access is restricted to the start and end date range. True if access is date-restricted; false if students can access the course outside the defined date range.']
  sis_import_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the SIS import batch that created or last updated this course shell. Used for tracking data lineage and synchronization history with Banner.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the course shell becomes active and accessible to enrolled students. Typically aligns with the first day of the academic term or course section start date.']
  storage_quota_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum storage space allocated to this course shell for file uploads, measured in megabytes. Instructors and students cannot upload files exceeding this quota.']
  storage_used_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current storage space consumed by files uploaded to this course shell, measured in megabytes. Used for monitoring quota utilization and capacity planning.']
  syllabus_body varchar [note: 'HTML content of the course syllabus. Contains course description, learning outcomes, grading policies, schedule, and other syllabus information as authored by the instructor.']
  template_applied varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the course template that was applied during course shell creation. Templates provide standardized structure, content, and settings for new courses. Null if no template was used.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone setting for the course shell, used for displaying due dates and scheduling. Typically inherits from the institution or can be customized per course (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the course shell record was last modified in Canvas. Tracks the most recent change to course settings, content, or metadata.']
  workflow_state varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the course shell in Canvas. Unpublished courses are not visible to students; available courses are active; completed courses are archived; deleted courses are soft-deleted.']

  Note: 'Canvas LMS course shell record corresponding to a course section or standalone learning environment. Captures Canvas course ID, SIS course ID, enrollment term, course state (unpublished, available, completed, deleted), visibility settings, blueprint course linkage, template applied, storage quota, and integration sync status with Banner. Represents the digital instructional container where content, assignments, discussions, and gradebook live. Owned by the instruction domain as the operational LMS record.'
}

Table instruction.instruction_assignment {
  instruction_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the assignment record. Primary key for the assignment entity.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the course section in which this assignment is created. Links assignment to the specific section offering where students will complete the work.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty or staff member who originally created the assignment. Used for accountability and support workflows.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external tool configuration in the LMS. Used for LTI launches and grade passback from external assessment platforms.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty or staff member who last modified the assignment. Tracks change history and supports collaborative course design.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member who serves as the moderator and selects the final grade when moderated grading is enabled. Null if not applicable.']
  map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.curriculum_map. Business justification: Curriculum maps specify which assignments assess which PLOs/SLOs in which courses. Linking assignment to curriculum_map enables verification that planned assessment instruments are deployed, supports ']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Proctored exams, lab practicals, and place-based assessments require specific room assignments. Real process: exam scheduling systems coordinate room bookings for final exams and lab assignments requi']
  rubric_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grading rubric associated with this assignment. Rubrics provide structured criteria and performance levels for consistent assessment.']
  slo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.slo. Business justification: Instructors map assignments to specific SLOs for direct outcomes assessment. This link enables SLO attainment reporting, accreditation evidence collection (AACSB, ABET, HLC), and curriculum effectiven']
  tuition_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tuition_charge. Business justification: Some institutions assess assignment-specific fees (lab fees, proctoring fees, course material charges). This link supports itemized billing for assignment-level charges and enables refund processing w']
  wellness_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.wellness_program. Business justification: Wellness programs offered for academic credit assign coursework (stress management journals, nutrition logs). LMS assignment records must link to wellness program participation for grade calculation, ']
  allowed_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of times a student may submit or resubmit the assignment. Null or -1 indicates unlimited attempts. Used for quiz retakes and iterative assignments.']
  allowed_file_extensions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of file extensions that students are permitted to upload for this assignment. Examples: pdf,docx,xlsx. Null indicates no restriction.']
  assignment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions, requirements, and expectations for the assignment. May include rich text, HTML formatting, embedded media, and rubric details.']
  assignment_name varchar [note: 'The title or name of the assignment as displayed to students and faculty. Should clearly communicate the assignment purpose and topic.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assignment. Draft assignments are not visible to students; published assignments are active and available.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment by pedagogical format and delivery method. Determines workflow, submission requirements, and assessment approach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homework|quiz|exam|project|discussion|lab_report|presentation|case_study|essay|problem_set|group_work — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  available_from_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the assignment becomes visible and accessible to students. Controls release timing for scaffolded learning and pacing.']
  canvas_assignment_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier from the Canvas LMS system. Used for integration and synchronization with the source system of record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and assignment lifecycle tracking.']
  due_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time by which students must submit the assignment. Used for late penalty calculation and student planning. Aligns with academic calendar and course schedule.']
  external_tool_url varchar [note: 'URL of the external Learning Tools Interoperability (LTI) tool used for this assignment. Applicable when submission_type is external_tool. Enables integration with third-party assessment platforms.']
  grade_group_students_individually boolean [note: 'For group assignments, indicates whether each group member receives an individual grade rather than a shared group grade. Supports differentiated assessment within groups.']
  grader_count varchar [note: 'Number of independent graders assigned to assess each submission when moderated grading is enabled. Null if moderated grading is not used.']
  grading_type varchar [note: 'The method by which student performance on this assignment will be evaluated and recorded. Determines gradebook display and transcript reporting format.']
  group_category_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the group category that defines which student groups will collaborate on this assignment. Null if not a group assignment.']
  group_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the assignment group (category) for grade weighting purposes. Examples include Homework, Exams, Projects. Used for weighted final grade calculation.']
  is_anonymous_grading boolean [note: 'Indicates whether student identities are hidden from graders during assessment. Reduces grading bias and supports equitable evaluation practices.']
  is_anonymous_peer_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether peer reviewers identities are hidden from submission authors. Reduces bias and encourages honest feedback.']
  is_group_assignment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment is completed collaboratively by student groups rather than individually. Affects submission workflow and grade distribution.']
  is_omit_from_final_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment should be excluded from final grade calculations. Used for practice assignments, extra credit, or formative assessments.']
  is_only_visible_to_overrides boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is visible only to students with specific assignment overrides (differentiated due dates or access). Supports accommodations and differentiated instruction.']
  is_peer_review_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students will evaluate and provide feedback on each others submissions. Supports collaborative learning and critical thinking development.']
  is_published boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is currently visible and accessible to students. Unpublished assignments are visible only to instructors.']
  is_rubric_for_grading boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rubric is used to calculate the assignment grade or is informational only. When true, rubric scores determine the final grade.']
  lock_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time after which students can no longer submit the assignment. Enforces hard deadlines and prevents late submissions beyond instructor policy.']
  moderated_grading_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multiple graders will independently assess submissions with a moderator reconciling scores. Used for high-stakes assessments and inter-rater reliability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the assignment record was last updated. Tracks changes to assignment settings, dates, or content.']
  peer_review_count varchar [note: 'Number of peer submissions each student must review. Null if peer review is not enabled. Ensures balanced participation in peer assessment.']
  peer_review_due_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time by which students must complete their peer reviews. Typically set after the assignment submission due date.']
  points_possible decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum number of points a student can earn on this assignment. Used for grading scale calculation and weighted grade computation in the gradebook.']
  position varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this assignment within its assignment group or course module. Controls display order in the gradebook and course navigation.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which students will submit their completed work. Determines workflow, file handling, and integration requirements with external tools. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online_upload|online_text_entry|online_url|on_paper|external_tool|media_recording|student_annotation|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  turnitin_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether submissions will be checked for plagiarism using Turnitin or similar originality detection service. Supports academic integrity enforcement.']
  vericite_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether submissions will be checked for plagiarism using VeriCite originality detection service. Alternative to Turnitin for academic integrity.']

  Note: 'Academic assignment or assessment task created within a course section, capturing assignment name, type (homework, quiz, exam, project, discussion, lab report, presentation), due date, available date, lock date, points possible, grading type (points, percentage, letter grade, pass/fail, not graded), submission type (online, on-paper, external tool), allowed attempts, group assignment flag, peer review flag, and Canvas assignment ID. Represents the instructional assessment unit within a course shell.'
}

Table instruction.instruction_submission {
  instruction_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the student submission record. Primary key for the submission entity.']
  ada_accommodation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ada_accommodation. Business justification: Students with ADA accommodations receive modified submission requirements (extended deadlines, alternative formats). Instructors and LMS systems must track which accommodations apply to each submissio']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the course section in which this submission was made. Links to the course section entity in the instruction domain.']
  instruction_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assignment for which this submission was made. Links to the assignment entity in the instruction domain.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member or teaching assistant who graded this submission. Links to the faculty entity in the faculty domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student who made this submission. Links to the student entity in the student domain.']
  wellness_participation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.wellness_participation. Business justification: Students submit wellness program artifacts (reflection essays, activity logs) through LMS. Tracking submission compliance is required for mandatory wellness requirements tied to conduct sanctions, ath']
  anonymous_ref varchar [note: 'An anonymized identifier used for anonymous grading. Masks the student identity during the grading process to reduce bias.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'The sequential attempt number for this submission. Indicates which attempt this represents if multiple submissions are allowed for the assignment.']
  body varchar [note: 'The text content of the submission for online text entry submissions. Contains the student\'s written response or essay.']
  cached_due_date timestamp [note: 'The due date for the assignment at the time of submission, cached for historical accuracy. Accounts for differentiated due dates and extensions.']
  canvas_submission_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier from the Canvas Learning Management System (LMS) for this submission. Used for integration and reconciliation with the source system.']
  comments_count varchar [note: 'The number of comments associated with this submission. Used for engagement analytics and feedback tracking.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the submission record was first created in the system. Represents the initial capture of the submission entity.']
  entered_grade varchar [note: 'The grade as originally entered by the instructor before any late penalties or adjustments were applied.']
  entered_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'The numeric score as originally entered by the instructor before any late penalties or adjustments were applied.']
  extra_attempts varchar [note: 'The number of additional attempts granted to the student beyond the standard allowed attempts for the assignment. Used for accommodations and special circumstances.']
  grade varchar [note: 'The grade assigned to this submission by the instructor. May be a letter grade, percentage, points, or pass/fail depending on the grading scheme.']
  graded_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the submission was graded by the instructor. Represents a distinct lifecycle event in the submission workflow.']
  grading_period_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the grading period during which this submission was made. Used for term-based grade calculations and academic reporting.']
  group_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the student group if this is a group assignment submission. Links to the group entity in the instruction domain.']
  history_count varchar [note: 'The total number of submission versions in the history for this assignment. Tracks the number of times the student has submitted or resubmitted work.']
  is_anonymous_grading boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this submission is being graded anonymously. When true, the grader does not see the student\'s identity.']
  is_excused boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the submission has been excused by the instructor. Excused submissions are not counted against the student in grade calculations.']
  is_late boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the submission was submitted after the assignment due date. Used for late policy enforcement and academic analytics.']
  is_missing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the submission is missing (not submitted by the student). Used for DFW (D-grade, F-grade, Withdrawal) early warning analytics and student success interventions.']
  last_activity_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the last student activity related to this submission. Used for engagement tracking and Title IV last-date-of-attendance determination for financial aid compliance.']
  late_policy_status varchar [note: 'The status of the submission with respect to the late policy. Indicates whether no late policy applies, the submission is late, the submission is missing, or the student has been granted an extension.']
  originality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The originality or similarity score from plagiarism detection tools, expressed as a percentage. Used for academic integrity review and flagging potential violations.']
  points_deducted decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of points deducted from the submission score due to late submission penalties. Used in conjunction with late policy enforcement.']
  posted_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the grade was posted and made visible to the student. Represents the point at which the student can view their grade.']
  preview_url varchar [note: 'The URL for previewing the submission content within the Canvas LMS interface. Used for inline viewing of submission materials.']
  redo_request boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the instructor has requested that the student redo or resubmit the assignment. Used for feedback loops and iterative learning.']
  rubric_assessment_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the rubric assessment used to grade this submission. Links to the rubric assessment entity for detailed criterion-based grading.']
  score decimal(10,2) [note: 'The numeric score assigned to this submission. Represents the points earned by the student on this assignment.']
  seconds_late varchar [note: 'The number of seconds the submission was late, calculated as the difference between the submission timestamp and the assignment due date. Used for late penalty calculations and policy enforcement.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the student submitted their work. Indicates whether the submission was a text entry, file upload, URL link, media recording, annotation, or external tool submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online_text_entry|online_url|online_upload|media_recording|student_annotation|basic_lti_launch|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submitted_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the student submitted their work. This is the principal business event timestamp for the submission transaction. Critical for late policy enforcement and Title IV last-date-of-attendance determination for financial aid purposes.']
  turnitin_data varchar [note: 'JSON or structured data containing plagiarism detection results from Turnitin or similar academic integrity tools. Supports academic integrity monitoring and policy enforcement.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the submission record was last modified. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  url varchar [note: 'The URL submitted by the student for online URL submissions. Contains the web link to external content submitted as part of the assignment.']
  workflow_state varchar [note: 'Current state of the submission in the grading workflow. Indicates whether the submission has been submitted, is awaiting grading, has been graded, or is pending review.']

  Note: 'Student submission record for an assignment, capturing submission timestamp, submission type (file upload, text entry, URL, media recording, annotation), attempt number, late flag, missing flag, excused flag, workflow state, and Canvas submission ID. Represents the transactional event of a student submitting work for evaluation. Supports late policy enforcement, academic integrity monitoring, DFW early warning analytics, and Title IV last-date-of-attendance determination for financial aid purposes.'
}

Table instruction.grade_entry {
  grade_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grade entry record. Primary key for the grade entry entity.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section in which this grade was awarded. Links to the course section offering.']
  instruction_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific assignment or assessment for which this grade was awarded. Links to the assignment definition.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty member, teaching assistant, or automated system that assigned this grade. Links to the faculty or system user record.']
  modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Links grade modifications to specific employees for registrar audit trails, grade appeal investigations, and FERPA compliance. Current modified_by is text; need FK to employee for accountability. Role']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student who received this grade. Links to the student master record.']
  rubric_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rubric used to grade this assignment. Links to the rubric definition with criteria and scoring levels.']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'Academic year during which this grade was awarded (e.g., 2023-2024). Used for longitudinal analysis and annual reporting.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which attempt this grade represents if multiple submissions are allowed. First submission is attempt 1.']
  canvas_grade_object_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier from Canvas LMS for this grade entry. Used for synchronization and reconciliation with the source LMS system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this grade entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  due_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assignment was originally due. Used to calculate late submissions and apply late penalties.']
  extra_credit_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this assignment is designated as extra credit. Extra credit assignments may be treated differently in grade calculation.']
  grade_appeal_status varchar [note: 'Status of any grade appeal filed by the student for this assignment. Tracks the lifecycle of grade dispute resolution.']
  grade_entry_source varchar [note: 'System or method through which this grade was entered. Indicates whether the grade originated from Canvas LMS, Banner SIS, manual entry, SCORM content, or external integration.']
  grade_letter varchar [note: 'Letter grade assigned to the student for this assignment (e.g., A, B+, C-, F). May include pass/fail, withdrawal, incomplete, or audit designations.']
  grade_override_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this grade was manually overridden by an instructor or administrator. True if the grade differs from the calculated or original grade.']
  grade_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage grade calculated from the score and maximum score. Represents the student\'s performance as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  graded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grade was assigned or last modified by the grader. Represents the authoritative grading event timestamp.']
  grader_comments varchar [note: 'Textual feedback provided by the grader to the student. Contains qualitative assessment, suggestions for improvement, and explanations of the grade.']
  grading_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the grading process for this assignment submission. Indicates whether the grade is final, pending review, or not yet assigned.']
  is_excused boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the student was excused from this assignment. Excused assignments do not count toward the final grade calculation.']
  is_final_attempt boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the final graded attempt for the assignment. True if no further submissions are allowed or if this is the last attempt used for grade calculation.']
  is_late boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the assignment was submitted after the due date. True if submission timestamp exceeds due date.']
  is_missing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the student failed to submit the assignment. Missing assignments may receive a zero or be excluded from grade calculation based on policy.']
  late_penalty_applied decimal(10,2) [note: 'Point deduction applied to the score due to late submission. Represents the numeric penalty subtracted from the raw score.']
  max_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum possible score for this assignment. Used to calculate percentage and relative performance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this grade entry record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the grade was overridden. Provides audit trail and justification for manual grade adjustments.']
  plo_alignment varchar [note: 'Mapping of this grade entry to specific Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs) assessed by this assignment. Used for program-level assessment and accreditation reporting.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grade was released to the student. Null if the grade has not yet been posted.']
  posted_to_student boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the grade has been released and is visible to the student. False if the grade is being held for batch release or pending review.']
  rubric_score_breakdown varchar [note: 'Detailed breakdown of scores by rubric criteria. Stored as a structured text representation (e.g., JSON or delimited format) showing points earned for each rubric dimension.']
  score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric score awarded to the student for this assignment. Represents the raw points earned before any scaling or weighting.']
  slo_alignment varchar [note: 'Mapping of this grade entry to specific Student Learning Outcomes (SLOs) assessed by this assignment. Stored as a structured text representation linking to SLO identifiers.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student submitted the assignment. Used to determine late penalties and compliance with due dates.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term code during which this grade was awarded. Used for temporal analysis and reporting by semester or quarter.']
  weight_in_final_grade decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight of this assignment in the final course grade calculation. Represents the contribution of this grade to the overall course grade (0.00 to 100.00).']

  Note: 'Gradebook entry recording the score or grade awarded to a student for a specific assignment or assessment, capturing score, grade (letter or percentage), graded timestamp, grader identifier, grading status (not_graded, graded, pending_review), rubric score breakdown, late penalty applied, and Canvas grade object ID. Represents the authoritative transactional record of academic performance at the assignment level. Feeds into final grade calculation and SLO assessment.'
}

Table instruction.final_grade {
  final_grade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the final grade record. Primary key for this entity.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific course section in which this grade was awarded. Links to the course section master record.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the primary instructor of record who assigned this final grade. Links to the faculty master record.']
  lms_course_shell_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the corresponding course shell in the Canvas Learning Management System. Used to link Banner grades with LMS gradebook and analytics.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the student who received this final grade. Links to the student master record.']
  studentlife_housing_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.housing_assignment. Business justification: Housing eligibility is tied to academic standing (minimum GPA requirements). Residential life systems check final grades for contract renewal decisions, scholarship housing continuation, and LLC parti']
  tuition_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tuition_charge. Business justification: Grade-dependent refund policies (no refund if credit earned) and SAP billing adjustments require linking final grades to original tuition charges. Financial aid offices use this for satisfactory acade']
  academic_standing_impact varchar [note: 'Impact of this grade on the student\'s academic standing for the term. Values include good standing, probation, suspension, dismissal, dean\'s list, honors, or no impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: good_standing|probation|suspension|dismissal|deans_list|honors|no_impact — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  course_number varchar [note: 'Three or four digit numeric code identifying the specific course within a subject, optionally followed by a letter suffix (e.g., 101, 2050, 301A).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this final grade record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.']
  credit_hours_attempted decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours the student attempted for this course. Used in GPA calculation and academic progress tracking.']
  credit_hours_earned decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours the student successfully earned for this course. Zero for failing grades, withdrawals, or incomplete grades.']
  crn varchar [note: 'Five-digit Course Reference Number uniquely identifying the course section within a term. Banner-specific identifier.']
  degree_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this course and grade apply toward degree requirements. False for developmental courses, audit enrollments, or courses taken for personal enrichment.']
  ferpa_protected_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this grade record is subject to enhanced FERPA privacy protection due to student directory information suppression request or other privacy hold.']
  grade_change_date date [note: 'Date on which the grade was changed from its original value. Null if no grade change has occurred.']
  grade_change_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this grade has been changed after initial posting. True if the grade was modified from its original value.']
  grade_change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for a grade change. Required when grade_change_flag is true. Values include calculation error, incomplete resolved, grade appeal, administrative correction, instructor error, or data entry error.']
  grade_mode varchar [note: 'Grading mode under which the student enrolled in the course. Standard uses letter grades A-F; pass/fail uses P/NP; audit uses AU; honors may use H suffix; satisfactory/unsatisfactory uses S/U.']
  grade_point_value decimal(3,2) [note: 'Grade point value assigned to the letter grade on a 4.0 scale (e.g., A=4.0, B=3.0, C=2.0, D=1.0, F=0.0). Null for non-graded modes.']
  grade_posted_date date [note: 'Date on which the final grade was officially posted to the student\'s academic record by the instructor or registrar.']
  grade_posting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the grade in the Banner grade posting workflow. Not posted indicates grade not yet submitted; posted indicates grade submitted by instructor; verified indicates grade reviewed by registrar; locked indicates grade finalized and cannot be changed without special approval; pending approval indicates grade change awaiting authorization.']
  grade_source_system varchar [note: 'System or process from which this final grade originated. Values include Banner (direct entry), Canvas LMS (imported from learning management system), manual entry, transfer evaluation, or prior learning assessment.']
  grade_submission_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the instructor submitted the final grade. Values include web entry (Banner self-service), LMS import (Canvas gradebook sync), batch upload (spreadsheet), API integration, or scantron (optical scan form).']
  grade_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the grade was verified and approved by the registrar\'s office as part of the grade finalization process.']
  incomplete_deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which the student must complete outstanding coursework to resolve an incomplete grade. After this date, the incomplete typically converts to a failing grade per institutional policy.']
  incomplete_extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether an extension beyond the standard incomplete deadline has been granted by the instructor or academic dean.']
  incomplete_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this grade is an incomplete (I grade). True if the student has not completed all course requirements and has been granted an extension.']
  last_attendance_date date [note: 'Last date the student attended or participated in an academically-related activity for this course. Required for Title IV financial aid return calculations and academic engagement tracking.']
  letter_grade varchar [note: 'Official letter grade awarded to the student. Standard values include A through F with plus/minus modifiers, P (Pass), NP (No Pass), W (Withdrawal), I (Incomplete), IP (In Progress), AU (Audit), S (Satisfactory), U (Unsatisfactory), NG (No Grade Submitted).']
  numeric_grade decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric representation of the final grade on a 0-100 scale. May be null for pass/fail, audit, or incomplete grades.']
  original_letter_grade varchar [note: 'The original letter grade that was posted before any grade change occurred. Null if no grade change has occurred. Retained for audit trail purposes.']
  quality_points decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total quality points earned for this course, calculated as grade point value multiplied by credit hours attempted. Used in GPA (Grade Point Average) calculation.']
  repeat_course_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this course enrollment is a repeat of a previously taken course. Used in repeat course policy enforcement and GPA calculation.']
  repeat_course_include_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this repeated course grade should be included in GPA calculation. Depends on institutional repeat course policy (e.g., replace previous grade, average grades, or include both).']
  sap_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this course grade counts toward Satisfactory Academic Progress evaluation for federal financial aid eligibility. Some grades (e.g., audit, withdrawn after census) do not count.']
  section_number varchar [note: 'Two or three character alphanumeric code identifying the specific section of a course (e.g., 01, 02, A1, W01 for web sections).']
