  subject_code varchar [note: 'Two to four character alphabetic code representing the academic subject or discipline (e.g., MATH, ENGL, BIOL, CS).']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit code identifying the academic term in which the grade was awarded (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024). Format: YYYYTT where TT is term sequence.']
  transfer_credit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this grade represents transfer credit accepted from another institution. Transfer grades typically do not calculate into institutional GPA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this final grade record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes. Part of standard audit trail.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the student officially withdrew from the course. Populated only when letter_grade is W (Withdrawal).']

  Note: 'Official final grade awarded to a student for a course section at the end of a term, capturing letter grade, numeric grade, quality points, credit hours attempted, credit hours earned, grade mode (standard, pass/fail, audit, incomplete), incomplete deadline date, grade change flag, grade change reason, Banner grade posting status, and FERPA-protected access flag. This is the authoritative academic record grade that feeds GPA calculation and academic standing. Sourced from Banner SHRTCKN.'
}

Table instruction.attendance_record {
  attendance_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the attendance record. Primary key for the attendance tracking system.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section for which attendance is being recorded. Links to the course section master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (faculty, staff, or system) who last modified the attendance record. Links to the user master record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty member or instructor who recorded the attendance. Links to the faculty master record.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student whose attendance is being recorded. Links to the student master record.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical room or virtual space where the class meeting occurred. Links to the facilities room master record. Used for space utilization analytics and attendance verification.']
  section_meeting_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific class meeting or session for which attendance is recorded. Links to the scheduled meeting instance.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term in which the attendance was recorded. Links to the term master record. Used for term-level attendance reporting and SAP evaluation.']
  academic_activity_type varchar [note: 'The type of academic activity for which attendance is being recorded. Values: lecture, lab, discussion, exam, quiz, presentation, field_trip, online_activity, hybrid. Used to differentiate attendance requirements across different instructional modalities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lecture|lab|discussion|exam|quiz|presentation|field_trip|online_activity|hybrid — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  attendance_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the class meeting occurred and attendance was taken. Used for Title IV last-date-of-attendance determination and Return of Title IV Funds (R2T4) calculations.']
  attendance_percentage_to_date decimal(5,2) [note: 'The cumulative attendance percentage for the student in this course section as of this record. Calculated as (total minutes present / total scheduled minutes) * 100. Used for early alert thresholds and SAP monitoring.']
  attendance_policy_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attendance record represents a violation of the institutional or course-specific attendance policy (e.g., exceeded maximum allowed absences). True if violation occurred, False otherwise.']
  attendance_status varchar [note: 'The attendance status of the student for this meeting. Values: present (student attended), absent (student did not attend), tardy (student arrived late), excused (absence approved by instructor or policy), late_excused (tardy with excuse), early_departure (left before end). Required for Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) compliance and Title IV attendance verification.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or annotations related to the attendance record. May include administrative notes, student explanations, or system-generated messages.']
  consecutive_absences_count varchar [note: 'The number of consecutive class meetings the student has been absent as of this record. Used to trigger early alert interventions and administrative withdrawal processes.']
  early_alert_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when an early alert was triggered based on this attendance record. Used for tracking intervention timeliness and student success analytics.']
  early_alert_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attendance record triggered an early alert intervention flag based on institutional thresholds (e.g., three consecutive absences, cumulative absence percentage). True if alert was triggered, False otherwise.']
  excuse_documentation_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student provided supporting documentation for an excused absence (e.g., medical note, court summons, travel itinerary). True if documentation was provided, False otherwise.']
  excuse_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for an excused absence or tardy, such as illness, family emergency, university-sanctioned activity, religious observance, or military duty. Required for compliance with institutional attendance policies and NCAA eligibility tracking.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate of the students location at the time of attendance check-in via mobile app or card swipe. Used for location-based attendance verification.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate of the students location at the time of attendance check-in via mobile app or card swipe. Used for location-based attendance verification.']
  instructor_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the instructor regarding the attendance record. May include reasons for absence, behavioral observations, or other contextual information relevant to early alert intervention.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the student accessed the LMS or checked in via mobile app. Used for location verification and fraud detection in online attendance tracking.']
  last_date_of_attendance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the last documented date of attendance for the student in this course section. True if this is the last attendance, False otherwise. Critical for Return of Title IV Funds (R2T4) calculations when a student withdraws.']
  lms_activity_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the attendance record was derived from or corroborated by student activity in the Canvas LMS (e.g., assignment submission, discussion post, quiz attempt). True if LMS activity was detected, False otherwise.']
  lms_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent LMS activity that contributed to this attendance record. Used for online and hybrid course attendance verification.']
  minutes_absent varchar [note: 'The number of minutes the student was absent during the class meeting. Used for calculating total absence time for SAP and early alert triggers.']
  minutes_present varchar [note: 'The number of minutes the student was present during the class meeting. Used for partial attendance tracking and FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) verification for state funding.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the attendance record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data quality monitoring.']
  recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the attendance record was captured in the system. Used for audit trail and to determine timeliness of attendance reporting for Title IV compliance.']
  recording_method varchar [note: 'The method by which attendance was captured. Values: manual (instructor manually recorded), lms_activity (derived from LMS activity in Canvas), clicker (student response system), card_swipe (physical card reader), biometric (fingerprint or facial recognition), mobile_app (student check-in via mobile application).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which the attendance data originated. Values: banner (Ellucian Banner SIS), canvas (Canvas LMS), slate (Slate CRM), manual (instructor entry), mobile_app (student check-in app), card_reader (physical access system), clicker (student response system). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: banner|canvas|slate|manual|mobile_app|card_reader|clicker — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_record_ref varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this attendance record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation between systems.']
  title_iv_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is receiving Title IV federal financial aid for this course section, making attendance tracking mandatory for compliance. True if Title IV eligible, False otherwise.']
  total_absences_count varchar [note: 'The total number of absences (excused and unexcused) the student has accumulated in this course section as of this record. Used for attendance policy enforcement and grade impact calculations.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'The verification status of the attendance record. Values: unverified (not yet reviewed), verified (confirmed accurate by instructor), disputed (student contested the record), corrected (record was amended after review). Used for data quality assurance and FERPA compliance.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the attendance record was verified or corrected. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Attendance tracking record for a student in a course section meeting, capturing attendance status (present, absent, tardy, excused), recorded timestamp, recording method (manual, LMS activity, clicker, card swipe), and instructor notes. Required for Title IV financial aid attendance verification, Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) compliance, last-date-of-attendance determination for Return of Title IV Funds (R2T4) calculations, early alert intervention triggers, and state funding FTE verification.'
}

Table instruction.rubric {
  rubric_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grading rubric. Primary key.']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course in which this rubric was originally created or is primarily used. Links the rubric to its originating academic context.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty member or administrator who originally created this rubric. Used for attribution and ownership tracking.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who most recently modified this rubric. Supports audit trail and change management.']
  slo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.slo. Business justification: Rubric criteria operationalize SLO assessment by defining performance levels. This link supports outcomes assessment workflows, accreditation reporting (mapping rubric rows to SLO statements), and clo']
  alignment_outcome_group_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the outcome group (collection of Student Learning Outcomes or Program Learning Outcomes) that this rubric is aligned to. Used for outcomes-based assessment and accreditation reporting.']
  archived_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rubric was archived or retired from active use. Null if the rubric is still active.']
  assessment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of times this rubric has been used to assess student work. Indicates the rubric\'s usage frequency and adoption.']
  canvas_rubric_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier for the rubric in the Canvas Learning Management System (LMS). Used for integration and synchronization with the source system.']
  context_code varchar [note: 'A composite code identifying the specific context instance (e.g., course code, account code) where this rubric is defined. Used for cross-referencing and reporting.']
  context_type varchar [note: 'The organizational context in which this rubric is defined. Course indicates the rubric is specific to a single course, Account indicates it is shared at the department or college level, and Program indicates it is used across an academic program.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rubric was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  criteria_count varchar [note: 'The total number of criteria (rows) defined in this rubric. Each criterion represents a distinct dimension of performance being evaluated.']
  free_form_criterion_comments boolean [note: 'Indicates whether instructors can add free-form text comments to individual rubric criteria when grading. True enables qualitative feedback alongside quantitative scores.']
  hide_points boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual criterion point values are hidden from students. True hides point values for each performance level, focusing student attention on qualitative descriptors rather than numeric scores.']
  hide_score_total boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the total score is hidden from students when viewing rubric feedback. True hides the numeric total, emphasizing qualitative feedback over quantitative scores. Used for formative assessment and developmental feedback.']
  lms_account_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the institutional account (department, college, or institution-level) that owns or governs this rubric. Used for rubric sharing and governance at the organizational level.']
  public boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rubric is publicly visible and shareable across the institution. True allows other faculty to discover and adopt the rubric for their own courses.']
  published_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rubric was published and made available for use in grading. Null if the rubric is still in draft status.']
  read_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rubric is locked from further editing. True prevents modifications to the rubric structure or criteria, ensuring consistency after grading has begun.']
  reusable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rubric is marked as reusable across multiple assignments, courses, or terms. True allows the rubric to be shared and applied to multiple assessment contexts, promoting consistency in grading standards.']
  rubric_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the rubric\'s purpose, scope, and intended use. May include instructions for faculty on how to apply the rubric to student work.']
  rubric_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rubric. Draft indicates the rubric is under development, active indicates it is published and available for use, archived indicates it is retired but retained for historical reference, and deleted indicates it has been soft-deleted.']
  rubric_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rubric scoring methodology. Points-based rubrics assign numeric scores, percentage-based rubrics use percentages, outcome-aligned rubrics map to Student Learning Outcomes (SLO) or Program Learning Outcomes (PLO), holistic rubrics provide a single overall score, analytic rubrics break down performance into multiple criteria, and checklist rubrics use binary yes/no indicators.']
  scoring_method varchar [note: 'The method used to calculate and display scores from the rubric. Determines whether the rubric outputs numeric points, percentages, letter grades, or pass/fail indicators.']
  title varchar [note: 'The name or title of the rubric as displayed to faculty and students. Provides a concise identifier for the rubric\'s purpose.']
  total_points_possible decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum number of points that can be earned on this rubric. Calculated as the sum of maximum points across all criteria. Used for grade normalization and weighting.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rubric was last modified. Supports change tracking and version control.']
  use_range boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rubric uses point ranges for each performance level rather than fixed point values. True allows instructors to assign any value within a defined range for each criterion level.']
  workflow_state varchar [note: 'The current workflow state of the rubric in the Canvas LMS system. Tracks the rubric through its lifecycle from creation to deletion.']

  Note: 'Grading rubric defining the criteria and performance levels used to evaluate student work for an assignment or course. Captures rubric title, description, rubric type (points, percentage, outcome-aligned), free-form ratings flag, hide score total flag, reusable flag, and Canvas rubric ID. Each rubric contains multiple criteria rows with performance level descriptors and point values. Supports consistent, transparent grading and SLO/PLO assessment alignment.'
}

Table instruction.slo_assessment {
  slo_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the student learning outcome assessment record.']
  compliance_accreditation_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_standard. Business justification: SLO assessment results provide direct evidence of compliance with accreditation standards. Programs map learning outcomes to specific standards and aggregate assessment data for self-studies, interim ']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the course section in which this SLO assessment was conducted.']
  instruction_assignment_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for the Canvas LMS assignment or activity associated with this SLO assessment.']
  map_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the curriculum map entry linking this SLO to the course and program structure.']
  plo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program learning outcome to which this SLO maps, supporting program-level assessment rollup.']
  primary_slo_instructor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member or evaluator who conducted the SLO assessment.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student whose performance is being assessed. Null if this is a cohort-level assessment.']
  rubric_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assessment rubric used to evaluate this SLO, if applicable.']
  service_learning_placement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.service_learning_placement. Business justification: SLO assessments measure learning outcomes achieved through service placements. Accreditation bodies (AACSB, ABET) require evidence linking community engagement experiences to academic achievement for ']
  slo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific student learning outcome being assessed.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The accreditation agency for which this assessment data is being collected (e.g., HLC, SACSCOC, ABET, AACSB). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HLC|SACSCOC|MSCHE|NWCCU|NECHE|WSCUC|ABET|AACSB|ABA|LCME|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assessment_cycle varchar [note: 'Academic year cycle for continuous improvement reporting (e.g., 2023-2024), used for accreditation reporting.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the SLO assessment was conducted or the student performance was evaluated.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'The method or instrument used to assess the student learning outcome (e.g., exam, project, presentation, portfolio, rubric-based evaluation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exam|project|presentation|portfolio|rubric|capstone|lab_practical|case_study|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the assessor regarding the student performance, context, or assessment conditions.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the assessment record (draft, submitted, reviewed, approved, archived).']
  cohort_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment record represents a cohort-level aggregate assessment (true) or an individual student assessment (false).']
  cohort_size varchar [note: 'Number of students included in the cohort assessment, if this is a cohort-level record. Null for individual assessments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SLO assessment record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_artifact_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI to the evidence artifact supporting this assessment (e.g., document ID, LMS assignment link, portfolio entry).']
  improvement_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the improvement action planned or taken in response to this assessment result.']
  improvement_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment result triggered a need for curriculum or instructional improvement action.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SLO assessment record was last modified.']
  performance_level varchar [note: 'The level of achievement demonstrated by the student or cohort against the SLO (exemplary, proficient, developing, unsatisfactory, not assessed).']
  proficiency_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to achieve proficiency level for this SLO assessment.']
  reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assessment record was reviewed and approved.']
  score_maximum decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum possible score for the assessment, providing context for the numeric score.']
  score_numeric decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved on the assessment, if applicable. Scale and interpretation depend on the assessment method.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assessment record was submitted for review or finalized.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit term code identifying the academic term in which the assessment occurred (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024).']

  Note: 'Student Learning Outcome (SLO) assessment record capturing the measured performance of a student or cohort against a defined SLO for a course section in a given term. Captures SLO identifier, assessment method, performance level achieved (exemplary, proficient, developing, unsatisfactory), evidence artifact reference, assessment date, and assessor. Supports program-level PLO rollup, accreditation continuous improvement cycles, and curriculum mapping validation.'
}

Table instruction.scorm_package {
  scorm_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SCORM package. Primary key for the scorm_package product.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Canvas LMS (Learning Management System) course section where this SCORM package is deployed. Links the package to the instructional delivery context.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (faculty, instructional designer, or administrator) who uploaded or created the SCORM package record. Used for accountability and support routing.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: SCORM packages are delivered via enterprise learning platforms (separate from or integrated with LMS). Business needs this link for content licensing, vendor support, platform migration planning, and ']
  finance_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.vendor. Business justification: SCORM packages are purchased digital content from vendors. Required for procurement spend analysis, vendor performance evaluation, license renewal tracking, and educational technology budget reporting']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the SCORM package record. Used for change management and audit purposes.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.purchase_order. Business justification: SCORM packages are procured assets requiring PO tracking for spend accountability, contract compliance verification, asset lifecycle management, and procurement audits. Essential for IT procurement re']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or textual description of accessibility features included in the SCORM package such as closed captions, screen reader compatibility, keyboard navigation, and alternative text. Used for ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) compliance and student accommodation.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assessment embedded within the SCORM package. Indicates whether the package includes quizzes, exams, or other evaluation mechanisms and their pedagogical purpose.']
  average_completion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average percentage of students who complete the SCORM package across all deployments. Used for content effectiveness evaluation and instructional design improvement.']
  average_mastery_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average score achieved by students on assessments within the SCORM package. Used for content difficulty calibration and SLO (Student Learning Outcome) assessment.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) code identifying the instructional program area to which this SCORM package belongs. Used for IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System) reporting and federal compliance.']
  completion_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of content that a student must complete for the SCORM package to be marked as complete. Typically ranges from 0 to 100 percent. Used for SLO (Student Learning Outcome) tracking and satisfactory academic progress.']
  cost_per_student decimal(10,2) [note: 'Licensing or usage cost per student for proprietary SCORM packages. Zero for OER (Open Educational Resources). Used for COA (Cost of Attendance) calculations and budget planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SCORM package record was first created in the institutional data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes for a typical student to complete the SCORM package. Used for course planning, workload estimation, and credit hour compliance.']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total file size of the SCORM package in megabytes. Used for storage planning, bandwidth estimation, and student download time considerations.']
  is_mobile_compatible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SCORM package is optimized for mobile devices (smartphones and tablets). Used for student accessibility and BYOD (Bring Your Own Device) support.']
  is_oer boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SCORM package is an OER (Open Educational Resource) available under an open license. Used for cost-of-attendance reduction initiatives and institutional OER (Open Educational Resources) reporting.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country code) indicating the primary language of instruction for the SCORM package. Used for accessibility and international student support.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the SCORM package was last reviewed for quality, accuracy, and alignment with current curriculum standards. Used for content lifecycle management and QA (Quality Assurance) tracking.']
  launch_url varchar [note: 'The URL endpoint used to launch the SCORM package within the LMS (Learning Management System). This is the entry point for student interaction with the content.']
  learning_objectives varchar [note: 'Detailed list or description of SLO (Student Learning Outcomes) that students are expected to achieve upon completion of the SCORM package. Used for assessment mapping and accreditation reporting.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'The intellectual property license under which the SCORM package is distributed. Determines usage rights, redistribution permissions, and attribution requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: proprietary|CC BY|CC BY-SA|CC BY-NC|CC BY-NC-SA|CC0|public domain — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mastery_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score a student must achieve on assessments within the SCORM package to be considered to have mastered the content. Expressed as a percentage or raw score depending on package configuration. Used for competency-based education and SLO (Student Learning Outcome) assessment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SCORM package record was last modified in the institutional data system. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next quality assurance review of the SCORM package. Used for proactive content maintenance and compliance with institutional review cycles.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, implementation guidance, known issues, or special instructions related to the SCORM package. Used by instructional designers and faculty for operational context.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the SCORM package content, learning objectives, and instructional approach. Used for faculty selection and student preview.']
  package_format varchar [note: 'File format or compression method used for the SCORM package distribution. Common formats include ZIP archives and IMS Common Cartridge (imscc).']
  package_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the SCORM package, often assigned by the content provider or institutional catalog system. Used for inventory tracking and content management.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SCORM package. Indicates whether the package is available for deployment, retired, or undergoing quality assurance review.']
  package_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the SCORM package as displayed in the LMS (Learning Management System). This is the primary identifier for instructors and students.']
  package_version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier assigned by the content provider to track updates and revisions to the SCORM package. Used for change management and quality assurance.']
  plugin_name varchar [note: 'Name and version of the browser plugin required to run the SCORM package, if applicable. Used for student technical guidance and IT support.']
  prerequisite_knowledge varchar [note: 'Textual description of prior knowledge, skills, or coursework that students should have before engaging with this SCORM package. Used for advising and course sequencing.']
  requires_plugin boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SCORM package requires browser plugins (e.g., Flash, Java) to function. Used for technical support planning and student device readiness assessment.']
  standard_version varchar [note: 'The technical standard and version that this package conforms to. Determines compatibility with LMS (Learning Management System) and tracking capabilities.']
  subject_area varchar [note: 'Academic discipline or subject area that the SCORM package covers. May align with CIP (Classification of Instructional Programs) codes or institutional taxonomy. Used for content discovery and curriculum mapping.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Intended student or learner population for the SCORM package. Used for content recommendation and catalog filtering.']
  upload_date date [note: 'Date when the SCORM package was uploaded to the Canvas LMS (Learning Management System) or institutional content repository. Used for version control and audit purposes.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the SCORM package has been deployed or launched across all course sections. Used for ROI (Return on Investment) analysis and content popularity tracking.']

  Note: 'SCORM (Sharable Content Object Reference Model) or xAPI/LTI content package deployed within a Canvas LMS course shell. Captures package title, standard version (SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, xAPI/Tin Can, cmi5), content provider, upload date, completion threshold, mastery score threshold, launch URL, package status, and OER (Open Educational Resource) flag. Represents the reusable digital learning object inventory available for instructional deployment.'
}

Table instruction.grade_change_log {
  grade_change_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grade change audit record. Primary key for the grade change log entity.']
  appeal_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the formal grade appeal case if this change resulted from a student grade appeal process. Null if not appeal-related.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Grade changes are frequent subjects of internal and external audits. Audit findings identify improper changes, missing approvals, or policy violations. Linking findings to specific grade changes enabl']
  conduct_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the academic integrity investigation or hearing if this grade change resulted from an academic misconduct case resolution. Null if not integrity-related.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section in which the grade was changed. Links to the specific class offering where the student was enrolled.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the registrar, dean, department chair, or other authorized administrator who approved the grade change. Links to the employee or faculty master record.']
  instruction_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific assignment or assessment whose grade was changed. Null if the change applies to the final course grade rather than an individual assignment.']
  original_change_log_grade_change_log_id varchar [note: 'If this record is a reversal, references the grade_change_log_id of the original change being reversed. Null if not a reversal.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student whose grade was changed. Links to the student master record.']
  requesting_instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty member who initiated the grade change request. Links to the faculty master record.']
  academic_standing_impact varchar [note: 'Describes the impact of this grade change on the student\'s academic standing status, if any. Indicates transitions between good standing, probation, suspension, or dismissal.']
  accreditation_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade change must be included in accreditation reporting or audit documentation for regional or programmatic accreditation bodies (HLC, SACSCOC, ABET, AACSB, etc.). True if reportable.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grade change was officially approved by the authorized administrator. Represents the point at which the change became effective.']
  approving_authority_role varchar [note: 'The organizational role or title of the person who approved the grade change, indicating their level of authority in the approval hierarchy.']
  banner_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier generated by Ellucian Banner SIS when the grade change was processed in the source system. Enables traceability to the system of record.']
  change_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation provided by the requesting party describing the specific circumstances and rationale for the grade change. Required for audit trail and compliance purposes.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the category of reason for the grade change: clerical error, calculation error, late submission accepted, grade appeal outcome, incomplete grade conversion, academic integrity resolution, instructor discretion, or administrative adjustment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clerical_error|calculation_error|late_submission|grade_appeal|incomplete_conversion|academic_integrity|instructor_discretion|administrative_adjustment — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the grade change request: pending approval, approved and applied, rejected, reversed (undone), or under review by committee.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this audit record was first created in the data warehouse. Represents when the grade change event was captured in the analytics system.']
  degree_audit_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade change affects the student\'s degree audit or progress toward graduation requirements. True if degree audit must be re-evaluated.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the grade change officially takes effect for transcript and GPA calculation purposes. May differ from approval timestamp for retroactive changes.']
  gpa_recalculation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade change requires recalculation of the student\'s cumulative, term, or major GPA. True if recalculation is needed.']
  grade_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grade being changed: final course grade, midterm grade, individual assignment, quiz, exam, project, or participation grade. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: final|midterm|assignment|quiz|exam|project|participation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lms_gradebook_sync_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grade change has been synchronized back to the Canvas LMS gradebook. True if synced, False if pending or not applicable.']
  lms_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grade change was synchronized to the Canvas LMS gradebook. Null if not yet synced or not applicable.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this audit record was last updated in the data warehouse. Tracks any subsequent modifications to the grade change record.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has been officially notified of the grade change via email or student portal. True if notification has been sent.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student notification about the grade change was sent. Null if notification has not been sent.']
  original_grade varchar [note: 'The grade value before the change was applied. May be letter grade (A, B+, C), numeric score, or special code (I for Incomplete, W for Withdrawal, P for Pass).']
  original_grade_points decimal(5,2) [note: 'The numeric grade point value associated with the original grade on the institution\'s GPA (Grade Point Average) scale, typically 0.00 to 4.00.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grade change request was submitted by the instructor. Represents the initiation of the grade change workflow.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a reversal of a previous grade change. True if this is a reversal transaction.']
  revised_grade varchar [note: 'The new grade value after the change was applied. May be letter grade, numeric score, or special code.']
  revised_grade_points decimal(5,2) [note: 'The numeric grade point value associated with the revised grade on the institution\'s GPA scale.']
  sap_recalculation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grade change requires recalculation of the student\'s Satisfactory Academic Progress status for Title IV financial aid eligibility. True if SAP recalculation is needed.']
  supporting_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to supporting documentation for the grade change (e.g., scanned forms, email correspondence, appeal decision letters) stored in the document management system.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit academic term code identifying the semester or session in which the grade change occurred (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024).']
  transcript_notation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a special notation must be added to the student\'s official transcript to document this grade change. True if notation is required per institutional policy.']

  Note: 'Audit record of every change made to a student\'s final or assignment grade, capturing original grade, revised grade, change reason code, change justification narrative, requesting instructor, approving registrar or dean, approval timestamp, and Banner grade change transaction ID. Supports FERPA compliance, academic integrity investigations, and accreditation audit trails. Required for Title IV SAP recalculations when grades are retroactively changed.'
}

Table instruction.incomplete_contract {
  incomplete_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the incomplete contract agreement. Primary key for the incomplete contract entity.']
  access_room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Incomplete contracts for lab/studio courses require continued access to specific rooms for work completion. Real process: facilities must grant card access and schedule availability for students compl']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the course section in which the incomplete grade was assigned.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the academic authority (department chair, dean, registrar) who approved the extension or contract modification.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the system user who created this incomplete contract record.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the faculty member who assigned the incomplete grade and is party to this contract.']
  modified_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the system user who last modified this incomplete contract record.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Incomplete grade contracts must comply with institutional academic policies governing eligibility criteria, deadline extensions, default grades, and approval workflows. Registrars and academic affairs']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the student who received the incomplete grade and entered into this contract.']
  tuition_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tuition_charge. Business justification: Incomplete contracts trigger conditional billing holds and affect refund eligibility. Linking contracts to original tuition charges enables automated hold placement, refund processing upon contract re']
  advisor_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the academic advisor was notified of the incomplete contract.']
  advisor_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student\'s academic advisor has been notified of the incomplete contract.']
  completion_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which the student must complete all required work to resolve the incomplete grade.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions documented in the incomplete contract agreement.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the incomplete contract (format: IC-YYYY-NNNNNN).']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'Date when both the student and instructor signed the incomplete contract agreement.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incomplete contract agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incomplete contract record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours associated with the course section for which the incomplete was assigned.']
  default_grade varchar [note: 'The grade that will be automatically assigned if the student fails to complete the required work by the deadline.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the academic department offering the course section for which the incomplete was assigned.']
  extended_deadline date [note: 'New completion deadline if an extension was granted.']
  extension_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the extension request was approved.']
  extension_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the extension request was approved by the instructor or academic authority.']
  extension_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason or justification provided by the student for requesting a deadline extension.']
  extension_request_date date [note: 'Date when the student submitted a request for deadline extension.']
  extension_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has requested an extension to the original completion deadline.']
  financial_aid_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial aid hold has been placed on the student\'s account due to this unresolved incomplete.']
  graduation_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unresolved incomplete prevents the student from graduating.']
  instructor_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the instructor electronically or physically signed the incomplete contract.']
  lms_assignment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to specific Canvas LMS assignments or coursework that must be completed to resolve the incomplete.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incomplete contract record was last modified.']
  original_grade varchar [note: 'The original incomplete grade assigned (typically I for Incomplete or IP for In Progress).']
  original_grade_date date [note: 'Date when the incomplete grade was originally assigned by the instructor.']
  registration_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a registration hold prevents the student from enrolling in future terms until the incomplete is resolved.']
  required_work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the coursework, assignments, exams, or projects the student must complete to resolve the incomplete grade.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the incomplete contract was resolved and the final grade was posted.']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the incomplete contract was resolved.']
  resolved_grade varchar [note: 'The final grade assigned upon resolution of the incomplete contract, either through completion of work or expiration of deadline.']
  sap_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incomplete contract impacts the student\'s Satisfactory Academic Progress status for financial aid eligibility.']
  sis_contract_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier for this incomplete contract in the source Student Information System (e.g., Ellucian Banner).']
  student_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the student electronically or physically signed the incomplete contract.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit code identifying the academic term in which the incomplete grade was originally assigned (format: YYYYTT where TT is term type).']

  Note: 'Formal agreement between a student and instructor documenting the conditions under which an Incomplete (I) grade will be resolved, capturing original incomplete grade date, completion deadline, required work description, default grade if not completed, extension request history, resolution status, resolved grade, and resolution date. Supports academic standing monitoring, financial aid SAP compliance, and registrar grade finalization workflows.'
}

Business justification: Early alerts for financial distress or non-payment often result in account holds restricting registration. Student success offices use this link to track intervention outcomes, coordinate payment plan']
  canvas_course_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for the course in Canvas LMS where the alert was triggered.']
  conduct_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.conduct_case. Business justification: Early alert systems trigger conduct investigations when behavioral concerns are flagged. Academic intervention platforms routinely reference conduct records to coordinate holistic student support and ']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section in which the alert was raised.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Early alert systems (Starfish, Navigate, EAB) are distinct enterprise applications. Business needs this link for vendor support routing, data integration troubleshooting, reporting consolidation, and ']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advisor assigned to follow up on the alert.']
  primary_instruction_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (instructor, advisor, or system) who raised the alert.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student for whom the early alert was raised.']
  title_ix_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.title_ix_case. Business justification: Early alerts for behavioral concerns, safety issues, or interpersonal conflicts often relate to active Title IX cases. Faculty and advisors need visibility to coordinate supportive measures and avoid ']
  absences_count varchar [note: 'Number of absences recorded at the time the alert was raised.']
  alert_date date [note: 'Date when the early alert was raised.']
  alert_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the concern or issue that prompted the alert.']
  alert_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the early alert was created in the system.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Category of the early alert indicating the nature of the concern (attendance, academic performance, engagement, behavioral, financial, personal).']
  attendance_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert is related to attendance issues.']
  banner_alert_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier for the alert in Ellucian Banner SIS if the alert originated there.']
  behavioral_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert is related to behavioral or conduct issues.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Method used to contact the student regarding the alert.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the early alert record was first created in the system.']

  engagement_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert is related to low engagement in course activities.']
  final_course_grade varchar [note: 'Final grade the student received in the course after the alert and intervention.']
  first_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the first contact or intervention attempt with the student.']
  grade_concern_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert is related to academic performance or grade concerns.']
  intervention_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Description of intervention actions taken in response to the alert (e.g., advisor meeting, tutoring referral, counseling referral).']
  intervention_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the intervention process for the alert.']
  lms_activity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Engagement score from the LMS based on logins, assignment submissions, and participation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the early alert record was last modified.']
  referral_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student completed the referral to the support service.']
  referral_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of support service to which the student was referred (e.g., tutoring, counseling, financial aid, disability services, career services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tutoring|counseling|financial_aid|disability_services|career_services|academic_advising|health_services — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the alert was resolved or closed.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing the resolution or outcome of the alert and intervention.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Final resolution status of the alert indicating the outcome of the intervention.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity level of the alert indicating urgency of intervention (low, moderate, high, critical).']
  student_response_date date [note: 'Date when the student responded to the intervention outreach.']
  student_response_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student responded to the intervention outreach.']
  student_retained_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student was retained in the course or institution following the intervention.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term code in which the alert was raised (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024).']
  title_iii_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this alert is reportable under Title III grant requirements for at-risk student interventions.']
  title_iv_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this alert is reportable under Title IV financial aid satisfactory academic progress (SAP) requirements.']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'Source that triggered the alert (manual by instructor, manual by advisor, automated LMS threshold, automated SIS rule, DFW risk model, attendance threshold, grade threshold). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manual_instructor|manual_advisor|automated_lms|automated_sis|dfw_risk_model|attendance_threshold|grade_threshold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Early alert or academic intervention flag raised by an instructor or advisor for a student in a course section, capturing alert type (attendance, academic performance, engagement, behavioral), severity level, trigger source (manual, automated LMS threshold, DFW risk model), alert date, assigned advisor, intervention actions taken, resolution status, and resolution date. Supports student success initiatives, retention programs, and Title III/IV reporting on at-risk student interventions.'
}

Table instruction.faculty_training_requirement {
  faculty_training_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this faculty training requirement record. Primary key.']
  faculty_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the faculty assignment record that is subject to this training requirement']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance training program that is required for this faculty assignment']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date on which this training requirement was assigned to this faculty assignment. Typically aligns with faculty assignment start date or the date a new training program becomes mandatory.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'The business or regulatory reason why this specific training program is required for this faculty assignment. Examples: role_based (all instructors), course_content (FERPA for courses with student records access), department_policy (college-specific requirement), accreditation (required by accreditor), regulatory (federal mandate), new_hire (onboarding requirement), annual_refresh (recertification).']
  attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of attempts the faculty member has made to complete this training requirement. Some training programs limit the number of attempts or require remediation after multiple failures.']
  certificate_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which a completion certificate was issued for this training requirement. Null if training does not issue certificates or if not yet completed.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number issued upon successful completion of this training requirement. Used for verification and audit purposes. Null if no certificate issued.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the faculty member successfully completed this training program for this assignment. Null if not yet completed. Used for compliance reporting and recertification scheduling.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this training requirement for this faculty assignment. Values: not_started (assigned but not accessed), in_progress (started but not completed), completed (successfully finished with passing score), overdue (past due date without completion), waived (requirement waived by authorized personnel), exempt (faculty member exempt from this requirement).']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which this training must be completed for this specific faculty assignment. Calculated from assignment start date plus training program completion deadline, or set manually by compliance office for specific circumstances.']
  last_reminder_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent reminder notification sent for this training requirement. Used to schedule next reminder and avoid over-communication.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding this specific training requirement. May include special circumstances, accommodations provided, technical issues encountered, or follow-up actions required.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date on which the initial notification email was sent to the faculty member informing them of this training requirement. Used to track communication and calculate reminder schedules.']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which this training must be retaken to maintain compliance, calculated from completion date plus training program recertification frequency. Null if training does not require recertification or not yet completed.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of reminder notifications sent to the faculty member regarding this incomplete training requirement. Used to track escalation and compliance follow-up efforts.']
  score_achieved decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage score achieved by the faculty member on the training assessment for this requirement. Null if not yet completed or if training does not include an assessment. Must meet or exceed training program passing score.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the waiver was officially approved. Null if no waiver granted.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the administrator who approved the waiver for this training requirement. Null if no waiver granted. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a waiver has been granted for this training requirement. True if the requirement has been waived by an authorized administrator (e.g., dean, compliance officer), false otherwise.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why a waiver was granted for this training requirement. Required when waiver_flag is true. Examples: prior completion at another institution, equivalent professional certification, subject matter expert exemption.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance training requirement between a faculty assignment and a mandatory training program. It captures the assignment of specific compliance training obligations to faculty members in their instructional roles, tracking completion status, deadlines, waivers, and the regulatory or policy basis for the requirement. Each record links one faculty assignment to one training program with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance obligation.. Existence Justification: In higher education compliance operations, faculty assignments require completion of multiple mandatory training programs (Title IX, FERPA, Clery Act, research ethics, LMS training) based on role, course content, and regulatory requirements. Each training program is required across multiple faculty assignments spanning departments, colleges, and terms. HR and academic affairs actively manage these training requirements as operational records, tracking completion status, due dates, waivers, and generating compliance reports for accreditation and federal audits.'
}

Table instruction.grade_appeal_case {
  grade_appeal_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for grade_appeal_case']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the course section for which the grade is being appealed.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or committee responsible for reviewing and deciding the appeal (department chair, dean, appeal committee).']
  escalated_grade_appeal_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on grade_appeal_case (escalated_grade_appeal_case_id)']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the instructor who assigned the disputed grade.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student who initiated the grade appeal.']
  related_academic_integrity_case_conduct_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related academic integrity case if the grade appeal is connected to or resulted from an academic misconduct finding.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term in which the disputed grade was assigned.']
  appeal_case_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the grade appeal case, typically formatted with prefix and sequential number for external reference and tracking.']
  appeal_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged to the student for filing the grade appeal, if applicable per institutional policy. Amount in institutional currency.']
  appeal_fee_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required appeal fee has been paid by the student.']
  appeal_fee_waived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal fee was waived due to financial hardship or other institutional policy considerations.']
  appeal_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level at which the appeal is currently being reviewed: instructor level (informal resolution), department level, college/school level, university level, or external review.']
  appeal_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the student describing the grounds for the grade appeal and the specific circumstances or errors being contested.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grade appeal case indicating its position in the review and decision workflow.']
  appeal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grade appeal based on the nature of the dispute: grade calculation error, grading policy violation, procedural irregularity, alleged bias or discrimination, extenuating circumstances, or other grounds.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the grade appeal case was officially closed after final decision and all administrative actions were completed.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Level of confidentiality required for this appeal case based on the nature of the issues involved (standard, elevated for sensitive matters, restricted for cases involving discrimination or harassment allegations).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this grade appeal case record was first created in the system.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the appeal: approved (grade changed as requested), partially approved (grade changed but not to requested level), denied (original grade upheld), remanded (sent back for additional review), or withdrawn (student withdrew appeal).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reasoning behind the appeal decision, including findings of fact and application of grading policies.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final decision on the grade appeal was rendered by the reviewing authority or appeal committee.']
  department_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommendation provided by the academic department regarding the appeal, typically from the department chair or designated review committee.']
  escalation_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the appeal has been escalated to a higher review level after an unfavorable decision at a lower level.']
  final_grade varchar [note: 'The final grade determined after the appeal process is complete. May differ from both original and requested grades based on appeal committee decision.']
  grade_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the grade change becomes effective in the student\'s academic record, if the appeal is approved.']
  hearing_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the appeal hearing will be conducted (room number, building name, video conference link).']
  hearing_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date on which a formal hearing or review meeting is scheduled for the grade appeal case, if applicable based on appeal type and institutional policy.']
  initial_review_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the initial administrative review of the appeal was completed to determine if it meets procedural requirements.']
  instructor_response varchar [note: 'Written response from the instructor addressing the student\'s appeal claims and providing their perspective on the grading decision.']
  instructor_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the instructor submitted their response to the grade appeal.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this grade appeal case record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or administrative observations related to the appeal case processing and review.']
  original_grade varchar [note: 'The grade originally assigned by the instructor that is being appealed. Typically letter grade (A through F with optional plus/minus) or special codes (I=Incomplete, W=Withdrawal, P=Pass, N=No Pass, C=Credit).']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appeal case requires expedited review due to time-sensitive circumstances such as graduation eligibility, financial aid status, or academic standing.']
  requested_grade varchar [note: 'The grade the student is requesting through the appeal process. May be null if student is requesting grade review without specifying desired outcome.']
  student_notified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student was officially notified of the appeal decision.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'The deadline date by which the grade appeal must be submitted according to institutional policy, typically calculated from grade posting date.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student formally submitted the grade appeal case for review.']
  supporting_documentation_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has submitted supporting documentation (assignments, exams, correspondence, medical records, etc.) to substantiate the appeal.']
  transcript_updated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student\'s official transcript has been updated to reflect the grade change resulting from a successful appeal.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for grade_appeal_case. Referenced by appeal_case_id.'
}

Table billing.student_account {
  student_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the student account record within the bursar/student accounts office. Primary key for the student account entity. Serves as the parent identifier for all charges, payments, refunds, holds, and billing statements.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Alumni maintain billing accounts for continuing education, lifelong learning programs, transcript requests, and outstanding balance resolution. Critical for alumni services billing, transcript hold ma']
  finance_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external collections agency assigned to this account if in external collections. Nullable if not in external collections.']
  patron_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to library.patron. Business justification: Students with billing accounts are library patrons. Unified identity enables integrated reporting of student financial obligations (tuition + library fines), consolidated holds (billing + library bloc']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active payment plan record if the student is enrolled in an installment plan. Nullable if not enrolled in a payment plan.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student who owns this financial account. Links to the student master record in the Student domain.']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party sponsor or employer organization that is responsible for paying charges on this account. Nullable if no third-party billing arrangement exists.']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'Date when the student account was closed. Nullable for active accounts. Closed when student graduates, withdraws, or account is otherwise terminated.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique account number assigned to the student for billing and payment purposes. Used on billing statements, payment portals, and correspondence.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'Date when the student account was first established in the student accounts system. Typically corresponds to the students initial enrollment or admission acceptance.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the student account. Active accounts are in good standing; suspended accounts have restrictions; closed accounts are no longer active; collections accounts are in debt recovery.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the student account based on the students enrollment category. Determines applicable tuition rates, fee structures, and billing policies.']
  anticipated_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected account balance after all pending charges and pending financial aid are applied. Used for payment planning and financial counseling.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address where statements and correspondence are sent. Typically contains street number and name.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address for apartment, suite, or unit information. Nullable if not applicable.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the billing address where statements and correspondence are sent.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 standard.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address where statements and correspondence are sent.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the billing address. Uses standard postal abbreviations.']
  collections_placement_date date [note: 'Date when the account was placed with an external collections agency. Nullable if never placed in external collections.']
  collections_status varchar [note: 'Current status of debt collection efforts for delinquent accounts. Tracks progression from internal collections through external agency placement and legal action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this student account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cumulative_adjustments decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sum of all adjustments applied to the student account since inception. Includes waivers, write-offs, refunds, reversals, and other balance modifications.']
  cumulative_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sum of all charges posted to the student account since inception. Includes tuition, fees, housing, meal plans, fines, and other assessments.']
  cumulative_payments decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sum of all payments received and applied to the student account since inception. Includes cash, check, credit card, electronic payments, and financial aid disbursements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts on this account. Uses ISO 4217 standard. Typically USD for U.S. institutions.']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance on the student account. Positive values indicate amounts owed by the student; negative values indicate credit balances owed to the student. Calculated as cumulative charges minus cumulative payments and adjustments.']
  has_graduation_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student account has an active hold that prevents graduation or diploma issuance. True if graduation is blocked; false otherwise.']
  has_registration_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student account has an active hold that prevents course registration. True if registration is blocked; false otherwise.']
  has_transcript_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student account has an active hold that prevents transcript release. True if transcript release is blocked; false otherwise.']
  hold_count varchar [note: 'Number of active holds currently placed on the student account. Holds may prevent registration, transcript release, or graduation based on hold type.']
  last_1098t_tax_year varchar [note: 'Most recent tax year for which a 1098-T form was generated for this student account. Nullable if no 1098-T has been issued.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this student account record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment received and posted to the student account. Nullable if no payments have been received.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received and posted to the student account. Nullable if no payments have been received.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent billing statement was generated for this student account. Used to track billing cycle history.']
  next_statement_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next billing statement to be generated for this student account. Used for billing cycle planning and communication.']
  payment_plan_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is currently enrolled in an installment payment plan. True if enrolled; false otherwise.']
  pending_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of charges that have been assessed but not yet posted to the account. Includes anticipated tuition and fees for upcoming terms that are in pre-billing status.']
  pending_financial_aid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of financial aid awards that have been packaged but not yet disbursed to the student account. Includes anticipated grants, loans, scholarships, and work-study.']
  tax_form_1098t_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student account is eligible for IRS Form 1098-T reporting of qualified tuition and related expenses. True if eligible; false otherwise.']
  third_party_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether charges on this account are billed to a third-party sponsor or employer rather than directly to the student. True if third-party billing is active; false otherwise.']

  Note: 'Master record for each student\'s financial account within the bursar/student accounts office. Tracks account status, current balance, cumulative charges, cumulative payments, billing address, account type (undergraduate, graduate, professional), and overall financial standing. Serves as the parent entity for all charges, payments, refunds, holds, and billing statements. SSOT for student financial obligations and payment history in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.tuition_charge {
  tuition_charge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tuition charge record. Primary key for the tuition charge entity.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the academic program in which the student is enrolled. Used to determine program-specific tuition rates and fees.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Tuition charges for alumni taking non-degree courses, certificates, professional development, or lifelong learning programs. Essential for alumni program revenue tracking and continuing education bill']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the college or school within the institution to which the charge is attributed. Used for revenue allocation and reporting.']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific course for which a course-specific fee is charged. Applicable for lab fees, course fees, or other course-related charges.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the person or automated process that assessed the charge. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.fee_schedule. Business justification: Tuition charges are assessed based on fee schedules. The fee_schedule defines the rates and rules; tuition_charge is the application of those rules to a specific student. This is critical for rate tra']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to faculty.instructor. Business justification: Enables course-level revenue attribution and instructional cost analysis for program review, accreditation reporting (AACSB, ABET require faculty workload costing), and academic department budget plan']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Tuition charges post to general ledger accounts for financial reporting and reconciliation. This FK links billing.tuition_charge to the finance ledger_account master, enabling unified financial report']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the academic department to which the charge is attributed. Used for revenue allocation and departmental reporting.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student to whom this tuition charge is assessed. Links to the student master record.']
  statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_statement. Business justification: Tuition charges appear ON billing statements. A charge may be assessed in one period and appear on the next statement. This link tracks which statement a charge was included in for billing and reconci']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Tuition charges are posted TO a student account. Currently tuition_charge links to profile and term, but not to the student_account master record. This is the authoritative account for charge posting ']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the academic term for which this tuition charge applies. Links to the term master record.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of any adjustment applied to the original charge. Positive for additional charges, negative for reductions or credits.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why an adjustment was made to the original charge. Provides context for billing office and audit purposes.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the charge was assessed to the student account. Represents the business event date for billing purposes.']
  billing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate and assess the tuition charge. Determines whether per-credit-hour rates or flat fees are applied.']
  charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of the charge assessed to the student account. Calculated based on credit hours, rate, or flat fee depending on billing model.']
  charge_code varchar [note: 'System-generated or manually assigned code identifying the specific type of charge. Used for billing categorization and financial reporting.']
  charge_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the charge providing context for the student and billing office. May include course title, fee purpose, or program name.']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the charge indicating whether it has been assessed, posted to the student account, adjusted, reversed, waived, or cancelled.']
