  charge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge indicating whether it is tuition or a specific mandatory or course-related fee. Determines billing rules and revenue allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tuition|technology_fee|health_fee|activity_fee|lab_fee|course_fee|program_fee|registration_fee — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tuition charge record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours for which the student is enrolled and upon which tuition is calculated. Used for per-credit-hour billing models.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the charge is denominated. Typically USD for U.S. institutions.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the charge must be paid to avoid late fees or account holds. Determined by institutional billing policy and term calendar.']
  flat_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount charged for tuition when a flat-rate billing model is applied (e.g., full-time students above a credit hour threshold).']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the charge is eligible for refund if the student drops the course or withdraws from the term. Determined by institutional refund policy and timing.']
  is_reportable_1098t boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the charge is reportable on the IRS Form 1098-T for qualified tuition and related expenses. Required for federal tax reporting compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tuition charge record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Final dollar amount of the charge after all adjustments have been applied. Represents the amount owed by the student.']
  posted_date date [note: 'Date on which the charge was officially posted to the student account and became due. May differ from assessment date due to billing cycle timing.']
  program_level varchar [note: 'Academic level of the program for which the student is enrolled and charged. Determines applicable tuition rate and fee structure.']
  rate_per_credit_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount charged per credit hour for tuition. Rate varies by program level, residency classification, and student type.']
  refund_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the charge that is refundable based on the date of withdrawal or drop. Decreases over time according to institutional refund schedule.']
  residency_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the student\'s residency status for tuition purposes. In-state residents typically receive lower tuition rates than out-of-state or international students.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the revenue type for financial reporting purposes. Aligns with institutional chart of accounts and GASB/FASB standards.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the charge was reversed. Required for audit trail and financial reconciliation.']
  student_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the student\'s enrollment type. Affects billing model, fee assessment, and eligibility for certain charges.']
  waiver_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the waiver applied to the charge. Reduces the net amount owed by the student.']
  waiver_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the type of waiver applied to reduce or eliminate the charge. Examples include employee tuition waiver, athletic scholarship waiver, or graduate assistant waiver.']

  Note: 'Individual tuition and mandatory fee charges assessed to a student account for a given term. Captures charge type (tuition, technology fee, health fee, activity fee), credit hour basis, rate applied, assessed amount, term, program level, residency classification (in-state/out-of-state), and charge status. Generated during automated fee assessment based on enrollment and fee schedule rules.'
}

Table billing.statement {
  statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing statement. Primary key.']
  finance_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an authorized third-party payer (parent, guardian, sponsor) who has permission to view and pay this statement.']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an active payment plan arrangement if the student is enrolled in an installment payment program.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student who is the primary account holder for this billing statement.']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Billing statements are generated FOR a student account. This is the master account-to-statement relationship. Currently billing_statement links to profile and term, but not to the student_account mast']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term for which this billing statement was generated.']
  account_hold_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an account hold has been placed due to unpaid balance, preventing registration or transcript release.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total adjustments applied to this statement including billing corrections, charge reversals, or administrative credits.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address to which paper statements are mailed or used for payment processing.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address for apartment, suite, or additional address details.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'The billing cycle period for which this statement was generated (e.g., fall semester, spring semester, monthly).']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code component of the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing statement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this statement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the billing statement was delivered to the student or authorized payer.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing statement was delivered or made available to the student.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due to avoid late fees or account holds.']
  fee_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total mandatory and optional fees assessed including technology fees, student activity fees, lab fees, and course-specific fees.']
  financial_aid_disbursed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total financial aid disbursements applied to this statement including federal, state, and institutional aid.']
  grant_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total grant awards applied as credits including federal Pell grants, state grants, and institutional grants.']
  housing_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total charges for on-campus housing or university-managed residential facilities.']
  is_1098t_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether charges and payments on this statement are eligible for inclusion in IRS Form 1098-T tuition statement reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing statement record was last updated or modified.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Late payment fees assessed if payment was not received by the due date.']
  loan_disbursement decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total student loan disbursements applied to this statement including federal direct loans, PLUS loans, and private loans.']
  meal_plan_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total charges for dining services and meal plan subscriptions.']
  minimum_payment_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum payment amount required by the due date to avoid late fees or account holds, applicable when payment plans are in effect.']
  net_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount due calculated as total charges minus total credits and payments. Positive indicates amount owed; negative indicates credit balance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or messages included on the statement for student communication regarding special circumstances, payment instructions, or account alerts.']
  previous_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Balance carried forward from the previous billing statement, if applicable.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total refunds issued to the student for overpayment or credit balance, typically disbursed after financial aid exceeds charges.']
  scholarship_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total scholarship awards applied as credits to this statement from institutional, external, or donor-funded sources.']
  statement_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing statement was generated and issued to the student or authorized payer.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique statement number used for reference in communications and payment processing.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing statement indicating payment and processing state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|paid|partially_paid|overdue|cancelled|adjusted — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which charges and payments on this statement apply for IRS Form 1098-T reporting purposes.']
  third_party_sponsor_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total payments or credits from third-party sponsors such as employers, government agencies, or external organizations.']
  total_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all charges assessed on this statement including tuition, fees, housing, meal plans, and other institutional charges.']
  total_credits decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all credits applied to this statement including financial aid disbursements, scholarships, grants, refunds, and adjustments.']
  total_payments decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all payments received and applied to this statement from student, sponsor, or third-party sources.']
  tuition_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tuition charges assessed for enrolled courses during this billing cycle.']

  Note: 'Periodic billing statement issued to a student or authorized payer summarizing charges, credits, payments, financial aid disbursements, and balance due for a billing cycle. Tracks statement date, due date, total charges, total credits, net balance, delivery method (email, portal, paper), and statement status. Generated during scheduled billing cycle processing.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment transaction. Primary key for the payment record.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Alumni payments for transcripts, parking permits, facility rentals, continuing education, and other services. Required for comprehensive alumni transaction history and correlation with donor giving pa']
  cashier_session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cashier or staff member who processed the payment, if applicable. Used for audit trail and accountability in manual payment processing.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment batch or deposit group to which this payment belongs. Used for reconciliation of daily deposits and batch processing.']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment plan to which this payment is applied, if the payment is part of an installment or deferred payment arrangement.']
  primary_reversed_by_payment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reversal payment transaction that reversed this payment. Creates an audit trail linking original payments to their reversals.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student whose account this payment is applied to. Links payment to the student master record.']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student account to which this payment is applied. Links payment to the billing account.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term to which this payment is applied. Used for term-specific payment allocation and reporting.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount of the payment received, in the institution\'s base currency. Represents the gross payment before any adjustments or allocations.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'The authorization code provided by the credit card issuer or payment processor confirming approval of the transaction. Applicable to credit and debit card payments.']
  bank_account_last_four varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the bank account number used for the payment. Stored for payment verification while maintaining PCI compliance by not storing full account numbers.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit ABA routing number of the bank from which the payment originated. Applicable to ACH and check payments. Used for payment verification and reconciliation.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The payment amount converted to the institution\'s base currency using the exchange rate. Used for financial reporting and account reconciliation.']
  card_last_four varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the credit or debit card number used for the payment. Stored for payment verification while maintaining PCI compliance.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'The brand or type of credit or debit card used for the payment. Applicable only to card-based payments.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The interface or channel through which the payment was submitted. Indicates where the payer initiated the transaction (e.g., web portal, mobile app, in-person at cashier, mail, phone).']
  check_number varchar [note: 'The check number from the physical check or money order used for payment. Used for tracking and reconciliation of check payments.']
  cleared_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment cleared the bank or payment processor and funds were confirmed as available. Applicable to checks, ACH, and credit card payments.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible confirmation or receipt number provided to the payer upon successful payment processing. Used for payment lookup and reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the payment was made. Supports international student payments in foreign currencies.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was deposited to the institution\'s bank account. Used for cash flow management and bank reconciliation.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert the payment from the original currency to the institution\'s base currency. Applicable only for foreign currency payments.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'The sequential installment number if this payment is part of a payment plan. Indicates which installment this payment satisfies (e.g., 1 of 4, 2 of 4).']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is eligible for refund based on institutional policy and payment timing. Used to enforce refund eligibility rules.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net amount received by the institution after deducting processing fees. Represents the actual funds available for application to the student account.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the payment transaction. Used to capture special instructions, payment purpose, or other contextual information.']
  payer_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the payer. Used for sending payment confirmations, receipts, and communication regarding the payment transaction.']
  payer_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the individual or organization making the payment. Used for payment reconciliation and third-party billing verification.']
  payer_phone varchar [note: 'The contact phone number of the payer. Used for payment verification and follow-up communication if issues arise with the payment.']
  payer_type varchar [note: 'The category or relationship of the party making the payment. Indicates whether the payment is from the student, parent, sponsor, employer, or other third party.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was received or initiated by the payer. This is the business event date for the payment transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to make the payment. Indicates how the funds were transferred (e.g., check, ACH, credit card, wire transfer, cash). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: check|ach|credit_card|debit_card|wire_transfer|cash|money_order — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Indicates whether the payment has been received, cleared by the bank, posted to the student account, or reversed.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment transaction was received or processed by the institution. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  posted_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was officially posted to the student account ledger. May differ from payment_date due to processing delays or batch posting cycles.']
  processing_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged by the payment processor or merchant service provider for processing the payment. May be absorbed by the institution or passed to the payer.']
  processor_reference varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier or reference number assigned by the payment processor or gateway. Used for reconciliation with processor settlement reports.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for reversing or voiding the payment, if applicable. Includes reasons such as insufficient funds, duplicate payment, student withdrawal, or payer request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Record of a payment received against a student account. Captures payment method (check, ACH, credit card, wire transfer, cash), payment amount, payment date, payer type (student, parent, sponsor, employer), confirmation number, processor reference, applied term, and payment status (pending, cleared, reversed). SSOT for all inbound monetary receipts in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.payment_plan {
  payment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment plan agreement. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the financial aid or student accounts staff member who approved the payment plan enrollment.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Payment plans are offered under institutional policies defining eligibility criteria, enrollment fees, installment terms, interest rates, and default consequences. Policy reference is required for pla']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student who enrolled in this payment plan.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the academic term for which this payment plan applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan was officially approved by the institution.']
  auto_pay_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this payment plan (True if auto-pay is active, False otherwise).']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan was cancelled by the student or institution. Null if the plan has not been cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the payment plan was cancelled (e.g., student withdrawal, full payment received, financial hardship, administrative error).']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when all scheduled installment payments were successfully received and the payment plan obligations were fully satisfied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment plan record was first created in the system.']
  default_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan was declared in default due to missed payments exceeding the grace period. Null if the plan has not defaulted.']
  down_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial payment amount required at the time of payment plan enrollment, paid before the first installment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan obligations are scheduled to be fully satisfied and the agreement concludes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the payment plan obligations and installment schedule officially begin.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the student enrolled in and the payment plan agreement became effective.']
  enrollment_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Non-refundable administrative fee charged to the student for enrolling in the payment plan.']
  final_installment_due_date date [note: 'Date when the final scheduled installment payment is due, marking the completion of the payment plan.']
  first_installment_due_date date [note: 'Date when the first scheduled installment payment is due.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the installment due date before late fees are assessed or the plan is considered in default.']
  installment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard amount due for each scheduled installment payment. May vary for the final installment if the total does not divide evenly.']
  installment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which installment payments are scheduled (monthly, biweekly, weekly, or custom schedule).']
  installments_missed_count varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled installment payments that were not received by their due date.']
  installments_paid_count varchar [note: 'Number of installment payments that have been successfully received and applied to the payment plan.']
  interest_rate_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'Annual interest rate percentage applied to the payment plan balance, if applicable. Zero for interest-free plans.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment plan record was most recently updated in the system.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard late fee amount charged when an installment payment is not received by the due date.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special terms, or administrative notes related to the payment plan agreement.']
  number_of_installments varchar [note: 'Total count of scheduled installment payments in the payment plan agreement.']
  outstanding_balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the payment plan, calculated as total plan amount minus total paid amount plus any accrued fees or interest.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument type used for installment payments (ACH bank transfer, credit card, debit card, check, cash, wire transfer).']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the payment plan agreement, typically formatted as PP followed by 8 digits.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment plan: active (in good standing), completed (all payments made), defaulted (missed payments beyond grace period), cancelled (terminated by student or institution), suspended (temporarily on hold), pending (awaiting approval or first payment).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment plan based on institutional offerings (standard multi-month plan, extended plan with more installments, custom negotiated plan, emergency short-term plan, deferred payment plan).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this payment plan record originated (e.g., Ellucian Banner Student Accounts, Nelnet Payment Plans, Cashnet).']
  total_paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all payments received against this payment plan to date, including down payment and installments.']
  total_plan_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of tuition, fees, and other charges covered by this payment plan agreement, excluding enrollment fees and interest.']

  Note: 'Installment payment plan agreement established for a student to spread tuition and fee obligations across multiple scheduled payments within a term. Tracks plan type, enrollment date, total plan amount, number of installments, enrollment fee, down payment amount, plan status (active, completed, defaulted, cancelled), and the administering term.'
}

Table billing.payment_plan_installment {
  payment_plan_installment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment plan installment. Primary key for this entity.']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent payment plan under which this installment is scheduled. Links to the payment_plan product for plan-level terms and conditions.']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the installment was actually paid by the student or sponsor. Null if the installment has not yet been paid.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of any manual adjustment applied to this installment after initial scheduling. Positive values increase the installment amount, negative values decrease it. Used for institutional corrections or student-specific modifications.']
  adjustment_authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the institutional staff member who authorized the adjustment to this installment. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  adjustment_date date [note: 'The date on which the adjustment to this installment was authorized and applied. Used for audit trail and financial reconciliation.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for any adjustment applied to the installment amount. Examples include billing error correction, financial aid disbursement, or institutional credit.']
  amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual dollar amount paid toward this installment. May differ from scheduled_amount if partial payments or overpayments occur. Null if unpaid.']
  auto_pay_attempt_date date [note: 'The date on which an automatic payment attempt was made for this installment. Populated only if auto_pay_enabled is True and an attempt has been processed.']
  auto_pay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this installment. True if the installment is scheduled for automatic deduction from a stored payment method, False otherwise.']
  auto_pay_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or error message describing why an automatic payment attempt failed. Examples include insufficient funds, expired card, or payment method declined. Populated only when auto_pay_status is failed.']
  auto_pay_status varchar [note: 'Status of the automatic payment attempt for this installment. Pending: scheduled but not yet processed. Successful: payment processed successfully. Failed: payment attempt declined or failed. Not Attempted: auto pay not enabled or not yet due.']
  collections_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the external collections agency to which this overdue installment was referred. Populated only if collections_referral_date is not null.']
  collections_referral_date date [note: 'The date on which this overdue installment was referred to a collections agency or internal collections process. Indicates escalation of delinquent payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this installment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_due_date date [note: 'The actual date by which payment must be received to avoid late fees, calculated as scheduled_due_date plus grace_period_days. Used for late fee assessment logic.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the scheduled due date during which payment can be made without incurring a late fee. Typically set at the plan level but may be overridden at the installment level.']
  hold_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an account hold has been applied to the student account due to this installment being overdue. True if hold is active, False otherwise. Holds may prevent registration or transcript release.']
  hold_applied_date date [note: 'The date on which an account hold was applied due to this overdue installment. Populated only when hold_applied is True.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this installment within the parent payment plan (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Determines the order of scheduled payments.']
  installment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the installment. Scheduled: not yet due or paid. Paid: fully satisfied. Overdue: past due date and unpaid. Partial: partially paid but not fully satisfied. Waived: obligation removed by institutional action. Cancelled: installment voided due to plan cancellation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this installment record was last updated. Captures any change to status, amounts, or other attributes. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of late fee assessed on this installment if payment was received after the due date. Null or zero if no late fee applies.']
  late_fee_assessed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a late fee has been assessed on this installment due to payment past the scheduled due date. True if late fee applied, False otherwise.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that last modified this installment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the student of the upcoming or overdue installment. Examples include email, SMS text, postal mail, student portal alert, or phone call.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which a payment reminder or due date notification was sent to the student for this installment. Used to track communication and compliance with notification policies.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method used to satisfy this installment. Examples include credit card, ACH bank transfer, check, cash, wire transfer, or third-party sponsor payment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ach|check|cash|wire_transfer|third_party — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or transaction identifier from the payment processing system that fulfilled this installment. Links to payment records when installment is paid.']
  remaining_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'The outstanding balance remaining on this installment after applying payments. Calculated as scheduled_amount plus any late fees minus amount_paid.']
  scheduled_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The dollar amount scheduled to be paid for this installment. Represents the planned payment obligation before any adjustments or late fees.']
  scheduled_due_date date [note: 'The date on which this installment payment is scheduled to be due. Established at the time the payment plan is created or modified.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'The academic term or semester to which this installment applies. Examples include Fall 2024, Spring 2025. Used for term-specific billing and financial aid coordination.']
  waiver_authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the institutional staff member who authorized the waiver of this installment. Populated only when installment_status is waived.']
  waiver_date date [note: 'The date on which the installment waiver was authorized and applied. Populated only when installment_status is waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for waiving this installment obligation. Populated only when installment_status is waived. Examples include financial hardship, institutional error, or administrative adjustment.']

  Note: 'Individual scheduled installment within a student payment plan. Captures installment sequence number, scheduled due date, scheduled amount, actual payment date, amount paid, remaining balance, late fee assessed flag, and installment status (scheduled, paid, overdue, waived). Links to the parent payment_plan for plan-level terms and references payment records when fulfilled via payment application.'
}

Table billing.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the refund record.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Refunds to alumni for overpayments, cancelled continuing education programs, or service fee reversals. Necessary for alumni account reconciliation and financial relationship tracking in advancement sy']
  financial_aid_award_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial aid award that generated the refund, if the refund resulted from financial aid disbursement exceeding charges.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student receiving the refund. Links to the student master record.']
  refund_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the staff member who approved the refund for processing.']
  refund_processed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the staff member who processed the refund disbursement.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Title IV refunds must comply with federal Return of Title IV Funds (R2T4) regulations. Tracking the specific regulatory requirement enables audit trails, ensures correct calculation methodology, and s']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: When research-funded students receive financial aid exceeding charges, refunds are issued from award funds. Universities track which awards generate refunds for sponsor reporting, award budget reconci']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student account from which the refund originates. Links to the student account billing record.']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'Academic year during which the refund was issued, in YYYY-YYYY format (e.g., 2023-2024).']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount of the refund issued to the student or payer, in US dollars.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the refund was approved by authorized personnel or automated business rules.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for ACH direct deposit refunds. Encrypted in storage.']
  bank_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account for ACH direct deposit refunds (checking or savings).']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing number for ACH direct deposit refunds.']
  check_cleared_date date [note: 'Date on which the refund check was cashed or cleared through the banking system.']
  check_issue_date date [note: 'Date printed on the paper check for refund disbursement.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Sequential check number assigned to paper check refunds for tracking and reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund record was first created in the system.']
  eft_trace_number varchar [note: 'Unique 15-digit trace number assigned by the ACH system for tracking electronic refund transactions.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Fund accounting code identifying the institutional fund from which the refund is disbursed.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the refund transaction is posted for financial reporting.']
  is_reportable_1098t boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this refund must be reported on the student\'s IRS Form 1098-T as a reduction in qualified tuition and related expenses.']
  is_title_iv_refund boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this refund is subject to Title IV federal financial aid return of funds regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund record was most recently updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the refund transaction, used for internal documentation and audit trail.']
  originating_credit_source varchar [note: 'The source of the credit balance or funds that generated the refund, identifying where the excess payment originated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tuition_payment|financial_aid|scholarship|grant|loan|sponsor_payment|third_party|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payee_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address where the refund check or correspondence is sent.']
  payee_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address (apartment, suite, building number) for refund delivery.']
  payee_city varchar [note: 'City name of the payee mailing address for refund delivery.']
  payee_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the payee mailing address for refund delivery.']
  payee_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or entity receiving the refund payment.']
  payee_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the payee mailing address for refund delivery.']
  payee_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code of the payee mailing address for refund delivery.']
  processed_date date [note: 'The date on which the refund was fully processed and sent to the payment processor or disbursement system.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason for the refund issuance, used for reporting and compliance tracking.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of the reason for the refund, providing context beyond the reason code.']
  refund_date date [note: 'The date on which the refund was officially issued or disbursed to the student or payer.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism used to disburse the refund to the recipient.']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the refund transaction, typically displayed on student statements and correspondence.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the refund in its lifecycle workflow.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund based on the originating business event or reason for issuance.']
  requested_date date [note: 'The date on which the refund was initially requested or triggered in the system.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this refund is reportable on IRS Form 1098-T for tax purposes.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term code for which the refund was issued, typically in YYYYMM format where MM represents the term (01=Spring, 05=Summer, 09=Fall).']
  title_iv_return_calculation_date date [note: 'Date on which the Title IV return of funds calculation was performed for withdrawal refunds.']

  Note: 'Record of a refund issued to a student or payer resulting from credit balances, financial aid disbursements exceeding charges, dropped courses, or withdrawal. Captures refund amount, refund method (ACH direct deposit, check, original payment method), refund date, reason code, originating credit source, processing status, and EFT trace number.'
}

Table billing.third_party_contract {
  third_party_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the third-party billing contract record. Primary key for the third-party contract entity.']
  corporate_sponsorship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.corporate_sponsorship. Business justification: Corporate sponsors often have both billing contracts (employee tuition benefits) and advancement sponsorships (naming rights, program support). Critical for unified corporate partnership management an']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the institutional staff member responsible for managing this third-party billing contract.']
  finance_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external sponsor organization (employer, government agency, tribal organization, vocational rehabilitation agency) that is party to this billing contract.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the system user who created this contract record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the system user who last modified this contract record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Third-party billing contracts must align with institutional policies on sponsor agreements, data sharing, FERPA compliance, and payment terms. Policy reference is required during contract approval wor']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Contracts with federal agencies (VA, DoD tuition assistance, workforce grants) must reference specific regulatory requirements. This link supports compliance verification, federal audit trails, and en']
  auto_apply_to_eligible_students boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract should automatically apply to all students meeting eligibility criteria, or whether manual assignment is required.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the sponsor organization billing address where invoices should be sent.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the sponsor organization billing address (suite, department, building number). Optional field.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the sponsor organization billing address.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the billing contact at the sponsor organization. Used for invoice delivery and payment communications.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary billing contact person at the sponsor organization responsible for invoice receipt and payment processing.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the billing contact at the sponsor organization for invoice and payment inquiries.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the sponsor organization billing address.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated and sent to the sponsor organization under this contract.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the sponsor organization billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the sponsor organization billing address. Use standard two-letter state abbreviations for US addresses.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the third-party billing contract for easy identification and reporting purposes.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique contract number or agreement identifier assigned by the institution or sponsor organization. Used for reference in communications and invoicing.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the third-party billing contract. Determines whether the contract is available for student enrollment and billing.']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount the sponsor will pay per student per term or academic year under this contract. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  coverage_limit_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which the coverage limit amount applies. Defines the billing cycle for cap enforcement.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Defines what charges the sponsor agrees to pay under this contract. Determines which student account charges are eligible for third-party billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this third-party contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  default_purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Standing purchase order number provided by the sponsor for use on all invoices under this contract. Nullable if PO numbers vary by invoice.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the third-party billing contract becomes active and eligible for student enrollment and billing. Contract terms apply from this date forward.']
  eligible_student_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules or criteria defining which students are eligible for billing under this contract. May include employment status, program enrollment, veteran status, or other sponsor-specific requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the third-party billing contract expires and is no longer valid for new enrollments or billing. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  federal_contract_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a federal government contract subject to federal regulations and reporting requirements (VA, DoD, etc.).']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which invoices are delivered to the sponsor organization.']
  invoice_format varchar [note: 'Preferred invoice format specified by the sponsor organization. Determines level of detail included in billing statements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this third-party contract record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contract terms, special instructions, billing notes, or administrative comments relevant to contract management and billing operations.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which the sponsor is expected to remit payment. Standard net payment terms.']
  purchase_order_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor requires a purchase order number on all invoices for payment processing.']
  renewal_notification_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before contract expiration that renewal notification should be sent to the sponsor and contract administrator.']
  requires_enrollment_verification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor requires enrollment verification or certification before payment. Common for VA, DoD, and vocational rehabilitation contracts.']
  requires_student_authorization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual student authorization or consent is required before billing the sponsor under this contract. Important for FERPA compliance.']
  sponsor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsor organization type. Determines applicable regulations and billing procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: employer|government_agency|vocational_rehabilitation|tribal_organization|military|consortium|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was terminated prior to its natural expiration date. Nullable if contract was not terminated early.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for early contract termination. Nullable if contract was not terminated early.']

  Note: 'Master agreement record for third-party billing arrangements with sponsors, employers, government agencies (VA, DoD, vocational rehabilitation), or tribal organizations that pay tuition and fees on behalf of enrolled students. Captures sponsor organization, contract number, billing contact, authorized coverage scope (tuition only, tuition and fees, capped dollar amount), eligible student criteria, billing frequency, contract effective dates, and status. Governs sponsor invoice generation.'
}

Table billing.sponsor_invoice {
  sponsor_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sponsor invoice record. Primary key.']
  academic_term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic term for which tuition and fees are being invoiced to the sponsor.']
  donor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.donor. Business justification: Third-party sponsors who also make philanthropic gifts require unified relationship tracking. Essential for comprehensive corporate giving analysis, stewardship coordination, and avoiding duplicate so']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Sponsor invoices must post to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition and financial reporting. New FK column needed because gl_account_code is a string code, not a proper FK. Replaces denormalize']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party sponsor organization responsible for payment of tuition and fees on behalf of sponsored students.']
  third_party_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master sponsorship agreement or contract governing the terms of this billing relationship.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total adjustments applied to the invoice for corrections, credits, or billing modifications after initial generation.']
  amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by the sponsor organization to date against this invoice. May be partial or full payment.']
  balance_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the sponsor invoice calculated as total invoiced amount minus amount paid.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the sponsor organization billing address to which the invoice is sent.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the sponsor organization billing address for suite, department, or additional location details.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name of the sponsor organization billing address.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the billing contact at the sponsor organization for invoice delivery and payment correspondence.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the sponsor organization responsible for invoice processing and payment.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the billing contact at the sponsor organization for invoice inquiries and payment coordination.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the sponsor organization billing address.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the academic term or billing period covered by this sponsor invoice.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the academic term or billing period covered by this sponsor invoice.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the sponsor organization billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code of the sponsor organization billing address.']
  board_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total meal plan or board charges included in this sponsor invoice if covered by the sponsorship agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sponsor invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the sponsor invoice is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount or negotiated rate reduction applied to the sponsor invoice per the sponsorship agreement terms.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason provided by the sponsor organization if the invoice status is disputed. Null if not disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected from the sponsor organization per the sponsorship agreement terms.']
  fees_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total mandatory and course-specific fees included in this sponsor invoice for all covered students during the billing period.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor invoice was generated and issued to the sponsor organization.']
  invoice_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to this sponsor invoice for internal reference or sponsor communication.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number assigned to this sponsor invoice for tracking and reference purposes.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sponsor invoice indicating its position in the billing and payment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|sent|partially_paid|paid|disputed|written_off|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sponsor invoice record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  other_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total of miscellaneous charges such as books, supplies, or special program fees included in this sponsor invoice.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the sponsor organization remits payment for this invoice.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date on which full or partial payment was received from the sponsor organization. Null if no payment has been received.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Description of payment terms and conditions applicable to this sponsor invoice as defined in the sponsorship agreement.']
  room_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total housing or room charges included in this sponsor invoice if covered by the sponsorship agreement.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sponsor invoice was sent or transmitted to the sponsor organization. Null if not yet sent.']
  student_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sponsored students whose charges are included in this invoice.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all charge components before adjustments, discounts, or taxes are applied.']
  total_invoiced_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final total amount invoiced to the sponsor organization after all charges, discounts, and adjustments have been applied.']
  tuition_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tuition charges included in this sponsor invoice for all covered students during the billing period.']
  write_off_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for writing off the invoice balance if the invoice status is written off. Null if not written off.']

  Note: 'Invoice generated and sent to a third-party sponsor organization for tuition and fees owed on behalf of sponsored students. Captures invoice number, sponsor organization, billing period, total invoiced amount, list of covered students, invoice date, due date, payment received date, and invoice status (draft, sent, partially paid, paid, disputed, written off).'
}

Table billing.account_hold {
  account_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account hold record. Primary key.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Alumni account holds (unpaid balances blocking transcript release, diploma access) require advancement visibility for donor prospect qualification and stewardship impact assessment. Critical business ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Account holds are placed based on institutional policies defining payment deadlines, minimum payment thresholds, and hold release criteria. Policy reference is essential for student appeals, hold just']
  primary_account_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the staff member or automated process that placed the hold.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student whose account has the hold placed. Links to the student master record.']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Account holds are placed ON a student account. Currently account_hold only links to profile, but the hold is actually placed on the account entity itself. This is critical for hold management and acco']
  tertiary_account_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the staff member or automated process that last modified the hold record.']
  academic_year varchar [note: 'Academic year associated with the hold in YYYY-YYYY format (e.g., 2023-2024). Null for holds not tied to a specific academic year.']
  collections_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold is associated with an account that has been referred to collections for outstanding debt recovery.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the originating office that the student should contact to resolve the hold.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the originating office that the student should contact to resolve the hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hold record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Applicable for financial holds.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the hold becomes active and begins restricting student services. May differ from placed_date for scheduled holds.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the hold automatically expires if not released earlier. Null indicates no automatic expiration.']
  hold_code varchar [note: 'Institution-defined code representing the type of hold (e.g., BURSAR, LIBRARY, PARKING, HEALTH, ACADEMIC). Used for system processing and reporting.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the hold was placed on the student account (e.g., outstanding tuition balance, unreturned library materials, missing immunization records, unpaid parking fines).']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold indicating whether it is currently in effect or has been resolved.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the hold indicating the functional area responsible for the hold.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hold record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum payment amount required to release the hold, applicable for financial holds. Null for non-financial holds or holds with no minimum payment option.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, context, or special circumstances related to the hold.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date when the hold notification was sent to the student. Null if no notification has been sent.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification has been sent to the student informing them of the hold placement.']
  originating_office varchar [note: 'Name or code of the department or office that initiated the hold (e.g., Bursar Office, Registrar, Library, Health Services, Parking Services).']
  placed_date date [note: 'Date when the hold was originally placed on the student account.']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the hold was placed on the student account, including time zone information.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of the hold, used when multiple holds exist on an account to determine processing order. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the hold was released or removed from the student account. Null if hold is still active.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the hold was released (e.g., balance paid in full, materials returned, documentation submitted, administrative waiver granted). Null if hold is still active.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the hold was released, including time zone information. Null if hold is still active.']
  resolution_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions provided to the student on how to resolve the hold and have it released.']
  restricts_diploma boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold prevents the conferral or release of the diploma to the student.']
  restricts_financial_aid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold prevents the disbursement of financial aid funds to the student account.']
  restricts_grades boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold prevents the student from viewing their grades online or receiving grade reports.']
  restricts_registration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold prevents the student from registering for courses.']
  restricts_transcript boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold prevents the release of official transcripts to the student or third parties.']
  returned_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold was triggered by a returned or dishonored payment (e.g., NSF check, declined credit card).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Categorical severity classification indicating the urgency and impact of the hold on student services.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Academic term code associated with the hold, if the hold is term-specific (e.g., 202401 for Spring 2024). Null for holds that span multiple terms.']
  waiver_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold is eligible for administrative waiver under institutional policies.']

  Note: 'Financial hold placed on a student account that restricts registration, transcript release, diploma conferral, or other services due to outstanding balances, returned payments, or collections activity. Captures hold type, hold reason, originating office, date placed, release date, release reason, minimum payment required for release, and hold status.'
}

Table billing.collections_case {
  collections_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the collections case record. Primary key.']
  account_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account_hold. Business justification: Collections cases often result in account holds being placed. This link tracks which hold (if any) was placed as a result of the collections case. This is a business process relationship for collectio']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Alumni in collections must be flagged in advancement systems for donor prospect disqualification and stewardship strategy adjustment. Essential for preventing solicitation of alumni with unresolved de']
  collections_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Collections cases follow institutional policies on referral thresholds, payment plan options before referral, write-off criteria, and legal action authorization. Policy reference is required for case ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal staff member or external agency representative assigned to work this collections case.']
  finance_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external collections agency assigned to this case, if applicable. Null for internal collections.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified this collections case record.']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment plan established for this collections case, if applicable.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student whose account is in collections. Links to the student master record.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Collections must comply with FDCPA, state collection laws, and accreditation standards on debt collection practices. Tracking the regulatory requirement per case supports audit trails, ensures lawful ']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student account that is delinquent and referred to collections.']
  aging_bucket_at_referral varchar [note: 'Aging category of the delinquent balance at the time of collections referral. Indicates how long the debt was overdue before collections action.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique case number assigned to the collections case for tracking and reference purposes.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the collections case was closed, either due to full payment, write-off, settlement, or other resolution.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the collections case was closed. Supports bad debt reporting and receivables management analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid_in_full|payment_plan_completed|settled|written_off|bankruptcy|deceased|uncollectible|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  collections_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the collections case in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from internal review through external placement, legal action, resolution, or write-off. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_review|external_placement|legal_action|payment_plan_active|resolved|written_off|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  collections_type varchar [note: 'Type of collections activity: internal collections by institutional staff, external collections agency placement, or legal action.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total commission amount paid or owed to the external collections agency based on recovered amounts. Null for internal collections.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate (percentage) charged by the external collections agency for recovery services. Null for internal collections.']
  contact_attempts_count varchar [note: 'Total number of contact attempts made to reach the student regarding this collections case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collections case record was first created in the system.']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance on the collections case after any payments, adjustments, or write-offs. Updated as recovery activity occurs.']
  days_delinquent_at_referral varchar [note: 'Number of days the account was past due at the time of collections referral.']
  delinquent_balance_at_referral decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance owed by the student at the time the account was referred to collections. Represents the principal amount subject to recovery efforts.']
  judgment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the legal judgment obtained against the student, including principal, interest, and legal fees.']
  judgment_date date [note: 'Date when a legal judgment was obtained, if applicable.']
  judgment_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a legal judgment has been obtained against the student for the debt.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contact attempt or communication with the student regarding this collections case.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collections case record was last updated or modified.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment received on this collections case.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received on this collections case.']
  legal_action_date date [note: 'Date when legal action was initiated for this collections case, if applicable.']
  legal_action_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal action (lawsuit, judgment, wage garnishment) has been initiated to recover the debt.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding collections activities, student communications, special circumstances, or case-specific details.']
  payment_plan_established boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a payment plan or payment arrangement has been established with the student as part of collections resolution efforts.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date when the delinquent account was referred to collections (internal or external).']
  referral_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the collections case was created and the account was referred to collections.']
  settlement_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount agreed upon in a settlement arrangement with the student, if the debt was settled for less than the full balance.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when a settlement agreement was reached with the student, if applicable.']
  total_recovered_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount recovered from the student through collections efforts, including payments, offsets, and other recovery mechanisms.']
  write_off_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount written off as bad debt for this collections case. Used for bad debt expense reporting and financial statement preparation.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date when the outstanding balance was written off as uncollectible bad debt, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Record of a delinquent student account referred to internal collections or an external collections agency for recovery. Captures referral date, delinquent balance at referral, aging bucket at referral, collections agency assigned, collection status (internal review, external placement, legal action, resolved, written off), payment arrangements, recovery amounts, and closure reason. Supports receivables management, bad debt reporting, and state attorney general reporting requirements.'
}

Table billing.tax_form_1098t {
  tax_form_1098t_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IRS Form 1098-T record. Primary key for the tax form entity.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: 1098-T forms issued to alumni taking post-graduation courses (continuing education, certificates) require alumni record linkage for IRS compliance reporting and comprehensive alumni education history ']
  original_form_tax_form_1098t_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original 1098-T form ID that this corrected form is replacing. Populated only when correction_indicator is true.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student for whom this 1098-T form is generated. Links to the student master record.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: 1098-T forms are mandated by IRS regulations under IRC Section 6050S. Each form must reference the specific regulatory requirement for audit verification, IRS compliance reviews, and to document the l']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: 1098-T forms are generated based on student account transaction history. Currently tax_form_1098t only links to profile, but the form is actually generated from the student_account charge and payment ']
  box10_insurance_contract_reimbursement decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amounts billed for qualified tuition and related expenses that were reimbursed or refunded under an insurance contract. Reported in Box 10. Rare field, typically zero.']
  box1_payments_received decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total payments received during the tax year for qualified tuition and related expenses. Reported in Box 1 of Form 1098-T. Represents amounts actually paid by or on behalf of the student.']
  box2_amounts_billed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amounts billed during the tax year for qualified tuition and related expenses. Reported in Box 2 of Form 1098-T. Used by institutions that report amounts billed rather than amounts received. Mutually exclusive with Box 1 reporting method.']
  box3_change_reporting_method boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution changed its reporting method from prior year amounts billed (Box 2) to amounts received (Box 1) or vice versa. Checked if true.']
  box4_adjustments_prior_year decimal(15,2) [note: 'Adjustments to qualified tuition and related expenses reported on a prior year 1098-T. May be positive (additional charges) or negative (refunds/reductions). Reported in Box 4.']
  box5_scholarships_grants decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of scholarships and grants administered and processed by the institution during the tax year. Reported in Box 5. Includes Title IV federal aid, state grants, and institutional scholarships.']
  box6_adjustments_scholarships_prior_year decimal(15,2) [note: 'Adjustments to scholarships or grants reported on a prior year 1098-T. May be positive or negative. Reported in Box 6.']
  box7_checked_amount_includes_expenses boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amounts reported in Box 1 or Box 2 include expenses for an academic period that begins in the first three months of the following calendar year. Checked if true.']
  box8_half_time_student boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student was enrolled at least half-time for at least one academic period that began during the tax year. Checked if true. Used to determine eligibility for certain education tax credits.']
  box9_graduate_student boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student was enrolled in a graduate-level program during the tax year. Checked if true. Affects eligibility for certain education tax benefits.']
  correction_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this 1098-T is a corrected form replacing a previously filed form for the same student and tax year. True if this is a correction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this 1098-T form record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the 1098-T form is delivered to the student: electronic (via secure portal), paper (mailed), or both. Institutions must obtain student consent for electronic delivery.']
  electronic_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the student provided consent to receive the 1098-T form electronically. Required if delivery_method is electronic. Must be obtained prior to electronic delivery.']
  eligibility_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the student is eligible (or not eligible) for a 1098-T form. Students must be enrolled in credit courses and have qualifying transactions to receive a form.']
  filer_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the educational institution filing the 1098-T form.']
  filer_city varchar [note: 'City name for the educational institution\'s address.']
  filer_ein varchar [note: 'The institution\'s federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) as registered with the IRS. Appears on all 1098-T forms filed by the institution.']
  filer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the educational institution filing the 1098-T form. Must match the name on file with the IRS for the filer EIN.']
  filer_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the educational institution\'s bursar or student accounts office. Used for student inquiries regarding the 1098-T form.']
  filer_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code for the educational institution\'s address.']
  filer_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code for the educational institution\'s address.']
  form_number varchar [note: 'Unique form number assigned to this 1098-T instance for tracking and reference purposes. Typically includes tax year and student identifier components.']
  form_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the 1098-T form. Tracks progression from draft through IRS filing and any subsequent corrections or voids.']
  irs_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation number received from the IRS FIRE system upon successful submission of the 1098-T form. Serves as proof of filing.']
  irs_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the 1098-T form was electronically filed with the IRS via the FIRE (Filing Information Returns Electronically) system. Required for Title IV compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special circumstances related to this 1098-T form. Used for internal documentation and audit trail purposes.']
  reporting_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the institution reports qualified tuition and related expenses using the payments received method (Box 1) or the amounts billed method (Box 2). Institutions must use one method consistently.']
  student_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the student\'s mailing address as of the tax year end. Required for IRS Form 1098-T mailing.']
  student_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (apartment, suite, unit number) for the student\'s mailing address. Optional field.']
  student_city varchar [note: 'City name for the student\'s mailing address as of the tax year end.']
  student_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the student\'s mailing address. Defaults to USA for domestic students.']
  student_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the 1098-T form was delivered to the student (electronically posted or mailed). Must be on or before January 31 following the tax year.']
  student_legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the student as it appears on their tax records and must match IRS records. Used for 1098-T reporting.']
  student_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the student\'s mailing address. Five-digit or nine-digit format.']
  student_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or territory code for the student\'s mailing address (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Uses USPS standard abbreviations.']
  student_tin varchar [note: 'The student\'s Social Security Number (SSN) or Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) as reported to the IRS. Required for IRS filing.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which qualified tuition and related expenses (QTRE) and scholarships/grants are being reported to the IRS. Four-digit year (e.g., 2023).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this 1098-T form record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Part of audit trail.']

  Note: 'IRS Form 1098-T record generated annually for each eligible student reporting qualified tuition and related expenses (QTRE) and scholarships/grants for federal tax purposes. Captures tax year, Box 1 (payments received), Box 5 (scholarships/grants), Box 8 (at least half-time enrollment), Box 9 (graduate student), TIN, delivery consent (electronic/paper), and IRS submission status. Required for Title IV compliance.'
}

Table billing.charge_adjustment {
  charge_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the charge adjustment record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who approved the adjustment. Links to the employee or user record for audit purposes.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch process that created this adjustment if it was part of a bulk operation. Null for individual transactions.']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Charge adjustments must post to specific GL accounts for financial reporting and audit compliance. New FK column needed because gl_account_code is a string code, not a proper FK. Replaces denormalized']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Charge adjustments (tuition waivers, retroactive drops, appeals) must follow institutional policies defining eligibility, approval authority, and documentation requirements. Policy reference is requir']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student whose account is being adjusted. Links to the student master record.']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Charge adjustments occur when research award funding changes mid-term (budget modifications, award reductions). Linking adjustments to awards enables accurate financial aid reconciliation, sponsor inv']
