  home_building_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary building or facility where the crew is based.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facilities department to which this crew is assigned.']
  parent_crew_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on crew (parent_crew_id)']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  active_work_order_count varchar [note: 'Current number of open work orders assigned to the crew, used for workload balancing and capacity planning.']
  ada_compliance_trained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether crew members have received training on ADA accessibility requirements and compliance standards for facility modifications and maintenance.']
  average_work_order_completion_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean number of days the crew takes to complete assigned work orders, used as a key performance indicator (KPI) for crew efficiency.']
  certification_requirements varchar [note: 'List of required certifications, licenses, or training credentials that crew members must hold (e.g., \'EPA 608 Universal\', \'Licensed Electrician\', \'Forklift Operator\', \'Asbestos Awareness\').']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for crew communications and work order notifications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number to reach the crew or crew supervisor during work hours.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting cost center code to which crew labor and expenses are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew record was first created in the system.']
  crew_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the crew for identification in work orders and scheduling systems.']
  crew_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the crew (e.g., \'HVAC Maintenance Team A\', \'Electrical Services Crew 3\').']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Number of personnel assigned to the crew.']
  crew_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the crew in its lifecycle.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the crew based on primary function: maintenance (HVAC, electrical, plumbing), custodial (cleaning, sanitation), grounds (landscaping, snow removal), utilities (energy systems), construction (capital projects), or emergency response (safety, incident management).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the crew was disbanded or deactivated, if applicable. Null for active crews.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the crew was officially formed and became operational.']
  emergency_response_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the crew is trained and equipped to respond to emergency situations (e.g., power outages, water leaks, HVAC failures, safety incidents).']
  equipment_assigned varchar [note: 'Major tools, vehicles, or equipment permanently assigned to the crew (e.g., \'Truck #45\', \'Scissor Lift\', \'Welding Kit\').']
  hourly_labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard blended hourly labor rate for the crew used in work order costing and Facilities and Administrative (F&A) cost calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew record was most recently updated.']
  last_safety_training_date date [note: 'Date when the crew last completed mandatory safety training, used to track compliance with OSHA and institutional safety requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information about the crew, including special capabilities, restrictions, or operational notes.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether crew members are eligible for overtime compensation under Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) regulations.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance evaluation rating for the crew based on work quality, timeliness, safety record, and customer satisfaction.']
  radio_channel varchar [note: 'Two-way radio channel or frequency assigned to the crew for field communications.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of recordable safety incidents involving this crew, tracked for OSHA reporting and safety performance metrics.']
  service_area varchar [note: 'Geographic or functional area of campus that the crew is responsible for servicing (e.g., \'North Campus\', \'Medical Center Complex\', \'Athletic Facilities\').']
  shift_end_time varchar [note: 'Standard end time for the crew\'s work shift in HH:MM format (24-hour clock).']
  shift_schedule varchar [note: 'Primary work shift pattern for the crew.']
  shift_start_time varchar [note: 'Standard start time for the crew\'s work shift in HH:MM format (24-hour clock).']
  specialization varchar [note: 'Specific technical specialization or trade expertise of the crew (e.g., \'HVAC Systems\', \'Electrical Distribution\', \'Plumbing and Piping\', \'Carpentry\', \'Locksmith Services\').']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of labor union representing crew members, if applicable (e.g., \'International Union of Operating Engineers\', \'Service Employees International Union\').']

  Note: 'Master reference table for crew. Referenced by assigned_crew_id.'
}

Table library.bib_record {
  bib_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bibliographic record in the library catalog. Primary key for the bibliographic master record representing a distinct intellectual work.']
  access_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any restrictions on access to the resource, including licensing limitations, embargo periods, or institutional access requirements.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the first item associated with this bibliographic record was acquired by the library. Used for collection age analysis and accreditation reporting.']
  author varchar [note: 'Primary author, creator, or corporate body responsible for the intellectual content. Includes personal names, corporate entities, or conference names.']
  bib_record_description varchar [note: 'Physical characteristics of the resource including pagination, dimensions, illustrations, and accompanying materials. Example: \'324 pages : illustrations ; 24 cm\'.']
  catalog_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bibliographic record in the catalog. Determines visibility in discovery systems and availability for circulation.']
  cataloging_level varchar [note: 'Level of completeness and detail in the bibliographic description. Examples include full-level, core-level, minimal-level, or provisional cataloging.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Taxonomic code classifying the subject matter for academic program alignment. Used for collection development and accreditation reporting.']
  circulation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times items associated with this bibliographic record have been checked out. Used for collection usage analysis and weeding decisions.']
  copyright_year varchar [note: 'Year of copyright as stated in the resource. Used for intellectual property tracking and rights management.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bibliographic record was first created in the library catalog system. Used for audit trails and collection development analysis.']
  dewey_call_number varchar [note: 'Dewey Decimal Classification number for subject-based organization. Alternative classification system used in some academic and public libraries.']
  doi varchar [note: 'Persistent identifier for digital scholarly resources. Used for electronic resource linking, citation tracking, and scholarly communication.']
  edition varchar [note: 'Edition information indicating version, revision, or issue of the work. Examples include \'Second edition\', \'Revised\', \'Director\'s cut\'.']
  encoding_level varchar [note: 'Code indicating the level of bibliographic control and completeness of the MARC record. Used for quality assessment and record enhancement workflows.']
  format_type varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the physical or digital format of the resource. Determines circulation policies, shelving, and discovery facets.']
  holdings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical or electronic holdings attached to this bibliographic record. Used for collection size reporting and availability display.']
  isbn varchar [note: 'Unique commercial book identifier for monographs. May include ISBN-10 or ISBN-13 format. Used for acquisitions, cataloging, and discovery.']
  issn varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for serial publications including journals, magazines, and continuing resources. Eight-digit code used for periodical management and discovery.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code representing the primary language of the intellectual content. Used for discovery filtering and collection analysis.']
  last_circulation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent checkout of any item associated with this bibliographic record. Used for collection evaluation and retention decisions.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the cataloger or system process that last updated the bibliographic record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bibliographic record. Used for change tracking, synchronization, and data quality monitoring.']
  lc_call_number varchar [note: 'Library of Congress classification number used for subject-based shelving and retrieval. Primary call number for academic libraries.']
  lccn varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the Library of Congress for cataloging and bibliographic control. Used for authoritative cataloging and national bibliography.']
  marc_record varchar [note: 'Full MARC 21 bibliographic record in ISO 2709 or MARCXML format. Contains complete structured metadata for interoperability and data exchange.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Specific material or media type describing the physical or digital carrier. Examples include \'print book\', \'DVD\', \'streaming video\', \'e-book\', \'microfilm\'.']
  mms_code varchar [note: 'Alma-specific unique identifier for the bibliographic record in the MARC Management System. Used for system-level record tracking and API integration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional descriptive information not captured in other fields. May include bibliographic history, contents notes, summary, or restrictions on access.']
  oclc_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by OCLC WorldCat for shared cataloging and interlibrary loan. Enables global resource discovery and bibliographic data sharing across institutions.']
  oer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resource is an Open Educational Resource freely available for educational use. Supports OER tracking and affordability initiatives.']
  peer_reviewed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resource has undergone peer review process. Critical for scholarly research and academic quality assessment.']
  publication_place varchar [note: 'Geographic location where the resource was published or distributed. Includes city and country information.']
  publication_year varchar [note: 'Year the resource was published, issued, or released. Used for chronological sorting, collection development, and discovery filtering.']
  publisher varchar [note: 'Name of the publisher, distributor, or issuing body responsible for making the resource available. Used for acquisitions and cataloging.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the bibliographic record, such as original cataloging, OCLC import, vendor-supplied metadata, or batch load. Used for quality control and provenance tracking.']
  series_statement varchar [note: 'Information about the series to which the resource belongs, including series title and numbering. Used for collection grouping and discovery.']
  subject_headings varchar [note: 'Controlled vocabulary subject terms describing the intellectual content. Includes Library of Congress Subject Headings (LCSH) or Medical Subject Headings (MeSH). Supports subject-based discovery.']
  suppressed_from_discovery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bibliographic record is hidden from public discovery interfaces while remaining in the catalog. Used for records in process or with access restrictions.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full title of the intellectual work including main title, subtitle, and statement of responsibility. Primary discovery field for catalog searches.']
  url varchar [note: 'Web address providing direct access to electronic resources. Used for e-books, streaming media, and digital collections.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the cataloger or system process that created the bibliographic record. Used for workflow tracking and quality control.']

  Note: 'Bibliographic master record representing a distinct intellectual work in the library catalog. Stores MARC-based metadata including title, author, edition, publication year, ISBN/ISSN, OCLC number, CIP classification, subject headings, language, format type (monograph, serial, electronic, AV), and discovery metadata. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris catalog module. Serves as the authoritative catalog entry for all physical and digital holdings.'
}

Table library.holding {
  holding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the holding record. Primary key. Represents the library\'s ownership or access rights to a specific manifestation of a bibliographic work.']
  bib_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the bibliographic record that this holding is linked to. Represents the intellectual work that this holding manifests.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to library.collection. Business justification: holding has collection_code (STRING) and collection_name (STRING) that reference collection. Business: holdings belong to library collections (named groupings of materials managed as units). This is a']
  access_policy varchar [note: 'The policy governing who may access this holding and under what conditions. Common policies include open access, restricted access, staff only, special permission required, or course reserves.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'The date on which the library acquired ownership or access rights to this holding. Critical for tracking collection growth and retention policies.']
  acquisition_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the library acquired this holding. Common methods include purchase, gift, exchange, deposit, license agreement, or subscription.']
  call_number varchar [note: 'The classification number and location code used to organize and retrieve the item on library shelves. Typically follows Library of Congress (LC) or Dewey Decimal Classification (DDC) systems.']
  call_number_type varchar [note: 'The classification system used for the call number. Common types include Library of Congress (LC), Dewey Decimal, Superintendent of Documents (SuDoc), or local custom schemes.']
  copy_statement varchar [note: 'A textual statement describing the copies held by the library. Used for serials and multi-volume sets to indicate which volumes, issues, or parts are held.']
  copyright_status varchar [note: 'The copyright status of this holding. Indicates whether the work is protected by copyright, in the public domain, has unknown status, or is covered by a specific license agreement.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this holding record was first created in the library system. Used for audit trail and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price amount. Examples include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD.']
  digitization_status varchar [note: 'The status of digitization efforts for this holding. Tracks whether the item has been digitized, is scheduled for digitization, or is not applicable for digital conversion.']
  electronic_access_url varchar [note: 'The URL providing electronic access to this holding. Applicable for electronic resources, digital collections, and online subscriptions.']
  enumeration_chronology varchar [note: 'Structured data describing the enumeration (volume, issue, part numbers) and chronology (dates) of serial holdings. Critical for tracking journal and serial completeness.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the budget fund used to acquire this holding. Critical for financial tracking and collection development budget management.']
  holding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the holding based on its format and manifestation. Distinguishes between physical items, electronic resources, digital collections, microforms, serials, and monographs.']
  holdings_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this holding record. Indicates whether the holding is active and available, inactive, withdrawn from the collection, lost, missing, or in processing.']
  internal_note varchar [note: 'A note visible only to library staff. May include information about processing status, preservation concerns, or administrative details.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual items (copies) associated with this holding record. For physical holdings, this represents the number of physical copies; for electronic holdings, this may represent the number of concurrent users or licenses.']
  last_inventory_date date [note: 'The date when this holding was last physically inventoried or verified. Used for collection management and loss prevention.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this holding record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  library_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the library branch or unit that owns or manages this holding. Used in multi-campus or multi-branch library systems.']
  library_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the library branch or unit that owns or manages this holding.']
  license_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the license agreement governing access to this electronic holding. Links to detailed license terms, usage rights, and restrictions.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'The physical or digital format of the holding. Examples include book, journal, DVD, e-book, streaming video, database, manuscript, map, or dataset.']
  mms_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by Alma/Ex Libris for this holding record in the Metadata Management System. Used for system-level tracking and integration.']
  oer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holding is designated as an Open Educational Resource. True if the resource is freely accessible and openly licensed for educational use; false otherwise.']
  permanent_location varchar [note: 'The permanent home location for this holding. Distinct from temporary location, which may change due to circulation, reserves, or special exhibits.']
  preservation_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned for preservation and conservation activities. High priority items may require special handling, climate control, or digitization.']
  price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The purchase price or subscription cost paid for this holding. Used for financial reporting and cost-per-use analysis.']
  proxy_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remote access to this electronic holding is enabled through the library\'s proxy server or authentication system. True if proxy access is enabled; false otherwise.']
  public_note varchar [note: 'A note visible to library patrons in the public catalog. May include information about access restrictions, special handling, or usage instructions.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'The status of serial or continuing resource receipt. Indicates whether the library is actively receiving issues, has completed the subscription, or has ceased receiving.']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'The policy governing how long the library will retain this holding. May specify permanent retention, time-based retention, or conditions for weeding and deaccessioning.']
  shelving_location varchar [note: 'The specific physical or virtual location where the holding is stored or accessed. Examples include stacks, reference, reserves, special collections, or electronic resource platform.']
  statistical_code varchar [note: 'A code used for statistical reporting and analysis of library holdings. May be used to track collection development priorities, funding sources, or reporting categories.']
  suppressed_from_discovery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holding is suppressed from public discovery interfaces. True if the holding should not appear in the library catalog or discovery services; false if it is publicly discoverable.']
  temporary_location varchar [note: 'The current temporary location for this holding, if different from the permanent location. Used when items are on course reserves, special display, or temporary storage.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the vendor or supplier from whom this holding was acquired. Used for acquisitions tracking and vendor performance analysis.']

  Note: 'Physical or electronic holding record linked to a bibliographic record, representing the library\'s ownership or access rights to a specific manifestation of a work. Tracks call number, shelving location, library branch, collection code, acquisition date, retention policy, and item count. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris holdings management module. Distinct from the bib_record (what exists) and item (individual copy).'
}

Table library.item {
  item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the library item. Primary key representing the individual physical copy or digital instance of a library holding.']
  holding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent holding record that groups items of the same bibliographic resource at a specific location.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or estimated value of the item at time of acquisition in local currency.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the item was acquired by the library through purchase, donation, or other means.']
  acquisition_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the item was acquired (purchase, gift, exchange, deposit, or transfer).']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode identifier affixed to the physical item for circulation scanning and tracking. The externally-known unique code for this resource.']
  call_number varchar [note: 'Classification number used to organize and locate the item on library shelves. May follow Library of Congress (LC), Dewey Decimal, or local classification schemes.']
  call_number_type varchar [note: 'Classification scheme used for the call number (Library of Congress, Dewey Decimal, SuDoc, local, or other).']
  cataloging_date date [note: 'Date when the item record was created or cataloged in the library system.']
  chronology varchar [note: 'Date or time period designation for serial items (e.g., \'January 2023\' or \'Spring 2022\'). Complements enumeration for periodicals.']
  condition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific damage, wear, or preservation concerns for the item. Documents condition details beyond the physical_condition field.']
  copy_number varchar [note: 'Sequential copy identifier when multiple identical items exist for the same holding. Distinguishes duplicate copies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  due_date date [note: 'Date when the currently loaned item is scheduled to be returned. Null if item is not currently on loan.']
  enumeration varchar [note: 'Volume, issue, or part designation for serial items (e.g., \'Vol. 12, No. 3\' or \'Part 2\'). Used for multi-part works and periodicals.']
  internal_note varchar [note: 'Staff-only note for internal library operations and communication. Not visible to patrons.']
  inventory_date date [note: 'Date when the item was last physically verified during inventory or shelf-reading activities.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent physical inventory check indicating whether the item was found and in expected condition.']
  is_fragile boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the item requires special handling due to fragility, age, or preservation concerns.']
  is_magnetic_media boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the item contains magnetic media requiring special security gate handling (e.g., CDs, DVDs, videotapes).']
  is_suppressed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the item is hidden from public catalog display. Used for items in processing, restricted materials, or withdrawn items.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the item indicating availability for circulation and current operational state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|on_loan|in_transit|on_hold_shelf|missing|lost|withdrawn|in_process — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_loan_date date [note: 'Date when the item was most recently checked out to a patron. Used for collection assessment and weeding decisions.']
  last_return_date date [note: 'Date when the item was most recently returned by a patron.']
  loan_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this item has been loaned to patrons since acquisition. Tracks circulation activity and usage.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the current physical or virtual location of the item within the library system (e.g., main stacks, reference, special collections, branch library).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the current location where the item is shelved or stored.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Physical or digital format classification of the item. Categorical field that segments the resource population by format. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: book|journal|dvd|cd|microfilm|map|manuscript|thesis|electronic_resource — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the library staff member who last modified the item record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item record was last updated in the system.']
  physical_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the item\'s physical state and preservation needs. Used to prioritize conservation and replacement decisions.']
  policy varchar [note: 'Circulation policy governing loan rules, loan period, renewals, and holds for this item. Determines borrowing privileges and restrictions.']
  process_type varchar [note: 'Current workflow or processing status indicating the item\'s stage in library operations (acquisition, cataloging, preservation, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition|cataloging|binding|preservation|digitization|weeding|missing|lost — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  provenance_notes varchar [note: 'Historical ownership and custodial information for the item. Particularly important for special collections, rare books, and archival materials.']
  public_note varchar [note: 'Note visible to library patrons in the catalog, providing additional information about the item (e.g., \'Includes supplementary materials\', \'Latest issue available at reference desk\').']
  receiving_date date [note: 'Date when the item was physically received by the library from the vendor or donor.']
  statistical_code varchar [note: 'Local code used for collection analysis, reporting, and statistical tracking. May represent fund codes, collection codes, or other local classifications.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the item was removed from the active collection through weeding, disposal, or transfer.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for removing the item from the collection. Used for collection development analysis and reporting.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the library staff member who created the item record.']

  Note: 'Individual physical copy or digital instance of a library holding. Tracks barcode, item policy, material type, process type, current location, due date, number of loans, last loan date, condition, and provenance notes. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris item management. Represents the loanable unit for circulation transactions.'
}

Table library.patron {
  patron_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the library patron record. Primary key for the patron entity.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.identity_account. Business justification: Every library patron requires a university NetID/identity account for authentication to library catalog, electronic resources, ILL systems, and self-service portals. Essential for SSO integration, acc']
  accessibility_needs varchar [note: 'Description of any accessibility accommodations or assistive technology requirements the patron has registered with the library.']
  alternate_email_address varchar [note: 'Secondary email address for patron communications when primary institutional email is unavailable.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Physical library card barcode number used for circulation transactions and self-service kiosks.']
  block_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the patron account is blocked, including specific policy violations or outstanding obligations.']
  blocked_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron\'s borrowing privileges are currently blocked due to excessive fines, overdue materials, or policy violations.']
  borrower_category varchar [note: 'Granular borrowing classification that defines specific circulation policies, loan limits, and resource access levels.']
  campus_location varchar [note: 'Primary campus or branch location associated with the patron, determining default pickup library and service location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this patron record was first created in the library system.']
  directory_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron has opted out of having their information included in public directory services or patron lists.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary institutional email address for library communications, overdue notices, and hold notifications.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the patron\'s borrowing privileges expire, typically aligned with enrollment or employment end dates.']
  external_system_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent synchronization with external systems such as Ellucian Banner SIS or Workday HCM.']
  ferpa_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron has provided consent for disclosure of library records under FERPA regulations.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the patron as registered in the institutional system.']
  interlibrary_loan_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron is authorized to request materials through interlibrary loan services.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'The patron\'s preferred language for library communications and interface display, using ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ENG|SPA|FRA|DEU|CHI|JPN|ARA|RUS|POR|ITA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'The most recent date the patron engaged in any library transaction (checkout, return, login, request).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this patron record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes maintained by library staff regarding special circumstances, service history, or account-specific information.']
  patron_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patron based on their institutional affiliation, determining borrowing privileges and loan periods.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for urgent library notifications and account recovery.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the patron prefers to be addressed by, which may differ from their legal name.']
  preferred_notification_channel varchar [note: 'The patron\'s preferred method for receiving library communications including overdue notices, hold notifications, and service announcements.']
  primary_affiliation varchar [note: 'The patron\'s primary organizational unit or department within the institution (e.g., College of Engineering, Department of History).']
  proxy_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron has authorized proxy borrowers to check out materials on their behalf.']
  registration_date date [note: 'The date when the patron account was first created in the library system.']
  research_support_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron is eligible for specialized research support services including subject librarian consultations and advanced resource access.']
  special_collections_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron has been granted access to restricted special collections, archives, and rare materials.']
  statistical_category varchar [note: 'Classification code used for library usage reporting and analytics, often aligned with institutional reporting categories or IPEDS classifications.']
  total_charges_outstanding decimal(10,2) [note: 'The cumulative monetary amount of unpaid charges including replacement costs, processing fees, and service charges.']
  total_fines_outstanding decimal(10,2) [note: 'The cumulative monetary amount of unpaid fines and fees owed by the patron for overdue materials, lost items, and other library charges.']

  Note: 'Library patron master record representing any individual authorized to use library services — students, faculty, staff, and community borrowers. Stores patron type, borrower category, primary institutional affiliation, contact information, preferred notification channel, registration date, expiration date, total fines outstanding, blocked status, and FERPA consent flags. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris patron management, synchronized with Ellucian Banner SIS. Distinct from the student domain\'s student master — this is the library-specific borrower identity that aggregates all library privileges and history.'
}

Table library.loan {
  loan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the circulation loan transaction. Primary key for the loan record.']
  checkout_desk_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the circulation desk or service point where the item was checked out.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library item (book, journal, media, equipment) that was checked out.']
  loan_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library staff member who processed the checkout transaction.']
  loan_return_operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library staff member who processed the return transaction. Null if not yet returned.']
  patron_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library patron (student, faculty, staff, or community member) who checked out the item.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loan policy applied to this transaction, which determines loan period, renewal limits, and fine rules.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Physical barcode identifier affixed to the item and scanned during checkout and return transactions.']
  call_number varchar [note: 'Library classification call number assigned to the item for shelving and retrieval (e.g., Library of Congress or Dewey Decimal classification).']
  checkout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item was checked out to the patron. Principal business event timestamp for the loan transaction.']
  claimed_returned_date date [note: 'Date when the patron claimed to have returned the item. Null if no claim has been made.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this loan record was first created in the library system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the patron must return the item to avoid overdue fines.']
  fine_amount_accrued decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total amount of fines and fees accrued on this loan transaction in the institution\'s base currency (typically USD for U.S. institutions).']
  fine_amount_paid decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of fines and fees paid by the patron for this loan transaction in the institution\'s base currency.']
  fine_amount_waived decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of fines and fees waived by library staff for this loan transaction in the institution\'s base currency.']
  fine_collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of fine collection efforts for this loan (open, paid in full, waived, sent to external collections agency, or written off).']
  fine_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the patron paid fines associated with this loan. Null if no payment has been made.']
  fine_type varchar [note: 'Category of fine or fee associated with this loan transaction (overdue, lost item, damaged item, service charge, replacement fee, or processing fee).']
  fine_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for waiving fines on this loan, such as system error, patron appeal, or staff discretion.']
  is_claimed_returned boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the patron has claimed to have returned the item but the library has no record of receiving it.']
  is_lost boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the item has been declared lost by the patron or the library.']
  is_overdue boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the loan is currently overdue (past the due date without being returned).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this loan record was last updated in the library system.']
  loan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the loan transaction, often displayed to patrons and staff for reference and tracking.']
  loan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loan transaction indicating whether the item is still checked out, returned, overdue, or in another state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|returned|overdue|lost|claimed_returned|renewed|recalled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the loan transaction by lending category (regular circulation, course reserve, interlibrary loan, equipment loan, or digital resource loan).']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the physical or virtual location of the item at the time of checkout (e.g., main stacks, reference, media center).']
  lost_date date [note: 'Date when the item was declared lost. Null if the item has not been declared lost.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Type of library material checked out (e.g., book, journal, DVD, laptop, tablet, microfilm) which may affect loan policies and fine structures.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by library staff regarding special circumstances, patron requests, or issues related to this loan transaction.']
  overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the item is overdue. Null or zero if not overdue.']
  patron_group varchar [note: 'Classification of the patron at the time of checkout (e.g., undergraduate, graduate, faculty, staff, community) which may affect loan policies.']
  recall_due_date date [note: 'Revised due date if the item has been recalled. Null if no recall is active.']
  recall_requested boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether another patron has requested this item be recalled, requiring the current borrower to return it early.']
  renewal_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the patron has renewed this loan. Zero indicates no renewals have occurred.']
  return_desk_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the circulation desk or service point where the item was returned. Null if not yet returned.']
  return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item was returned by the patron. Null if the item has not yet been returned.']

  Note: 'Circulation loan transaction recording the checkout of a library item to a patron, including associated fines and fees. Captures checkout date/time, due date, return date, renewal count, loan policy applied, overdue status, fine type (overdue, lost, damaged, service charge), fine amount accrued, amount paid, amount waived, waiver reason, payment date, collection status, and the desk/operator that processed the transaction. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris circulation and fines modules. Core transactional record for all physical and in-library digital lending activity and its financial consequences.'
}

Table library.hold_request {
  hold_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hold request record. Primary key.']
  bib_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bibliographic title being requested. Used when hold is placed at title level rather than specific item level.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library branch or service desk where the patron will collect the item once it becomes available.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific library item being requested. Links to the item master record.']
  patron_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the patron who placed the hold request. Links to the patron master record in the library system.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Physical barcode identifier of the specific item being requested, used for scanning and tracking during fulfillment process.']
  call_number varchar [note: 'Library classification call number of the requested item, used for shelving location and retrieval by staff.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the hold request was cancelled, either by the patron, staff, or system due to expiration.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the hold request was cancelled. Patron request indicates patron-initiated cancellation, item unavailable indicates the item was lost or withdrawn, expired indicates the pickup window passed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this hold request record was first created in the library management system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  estimated_availability_date date [note: 'Projected date when the item is expected to become available for this hold request, based on current checkout due dates and queue position.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the hold request will expire if not fulfilled. After this date, the request is automatically cancelled and the item is made available to the next patron in queue.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date when the patron picked up the item and the hold request was marked as fulfilled.']
  hold_shelf_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the item will be removed from the hold shelf if not picked up by the patron. Typically 7-14 days after the ready-for-pickup notification.']
  isbn varchar [note: 'International Standard Book Number of the requested title, used for bibliographic identification and interlibrary loan coordination.']
  issn varchar [note: 'International Standard Serial Number for journal or serial publications being requested.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the staff member or system process that last modified this hold request record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this hold request record was last updated. Tracks any status changes, updates to pickup location, or other modifications.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Type of library material being requested. Affects processing workflow and pickup procedures, especially for special collections or equipment items.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify the patron about the hold request status. Email is most common, SMS is used for urgent notifications.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date when the ready-for-pickup notification was sent to the patron via email, SMS, or other communication channel.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification has been sent to the patron informing them that the item is ready for pickup.']
  owning_library_code varchar [note: 'Code of the library branch or collection that owns the requested item. Used for routing and transfer logistics in multi-campus systems.']
  patron_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or special instructions provided by the patron when placing the hold request, such as preferred pickup times or accessibility needs.']
  patron_group varchar [note: 'Classification of the patron who placed the request, used to determine loan periods, priority levels, and access privileges.']
  pickup_location_code varchar [note: 'Short code representing the pickup location, used for operational routing and display purposes.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the hold request. Faculty and reserve requests typically receive higher priority than standard patron requests.']
  queue_position varchar [note: 'Current position of this request in the hold queue for the title or item. Position 1 indicates next in line for fulfillment.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the patron placed the hold request. Used to determine queue position and priority.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the hold request, typically displayed to patrons and staff for tracking purposes.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'System or interface through which the hold request was submitted. OPAC (Online Public Access Catalog) is the traditional web catalog, discovery service includes third-party discovery tools.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold request. Pending indicates awaiting item availability, in transit indicates item is being transferred to pickup location, ready for pickup indicates item is available for patron collection, expired indicates pickup window has passed, cancelled indicates patron or staff cancelled the request, fulfilled indicates patron has picked up the item.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the hold request was submitted by the patron. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of request placed by the patron. Hold is for unavailable items, recall is for expedited return of checked-out items, booking is for scheduled use, digitization is for scan-on-demand services.']
  staff_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes added by library staff regarding the hold request processing, special handling requirements, or issues encountered.']
  transit_arrival_date date [note: 'Date when the item arrived at the pickup location and was placed on the hold shelf for patron collection.']
  transit_start_date date [note: 'Date when the item was sent in transit from the owning library to the pickup location.']

  Note: 'Patron-initiated hold or recall request placed on a library item or title that is currently unavailable. Tracks request date, expiration date, pickup location, queue position, request status (pending, in transit, ready for pickup, expired, cancelled), notification sent flag, and fulfillment date. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris fulfillment module. Distinct from ILL requests which involve external libraries.'
}

Table library.ill_request {
  ill_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interlibrary loan request record. Primary key.']
  bib_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to library.bib_record. Business justification: ill_request contains bibliographic metadata (title, author, isbn, issn, oclc_number, publication_year, publisher, edition) that duplicates bib_record. Business: interlibrary loan requests may be for m']
  patron_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the patron (student, faculty, or staff member) who submitted the interlibrary loan request.']
  article_title varchar [note: 'Title of the specific article or chapter being requested, for copy requests from journals or books.']
  borrowing_institution_code varchar [note: 'OCLC symbol or institutional code identifying the library requesting the material (typically the home institution for borrowing requests).']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the interlibrary loan request was cancelled.']
  cancelled_date date [note: 'Date when the interlibrary loan request was cancelled by the patron or library staff.']
  copyright_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interlibrary loan request complies with copyright law and institutional policies (e.g., CONTU guidelines for periodical copying).']
  cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the lending institution for providing the interlibrary loan service, including lending fees, shipping costs, and copyright fees.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interlibrary loan request record was first created in the library system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the borrowed material must be returned to the lending institution.']
  issue varchar [note: 'Issue number of the journal or serial publication containing the requested article.']
  journal_title varchar [note: 'Title of the journal or periodical containing the requested article, for copy requests.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interlibrary loan request record was last updated or modified.']
  lending_institution_code varchar [note: 'OCLC symbol or institutional code identifying the external library or institution providing the requested material.']
  lending_institution_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external library or institution providing the requested material.']
  needed_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the patron requires the requested material, used to prioritize request processing.']
  oclc_ill_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by OCLC WorldShare ILL system for tracking the request across the library network.']
  pages varchar [note: 'Page range of the requested article or chapter within the source publication.']
  patron_charged_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount charged to the patron for the interlibrary loan service, which may differ from the institutional cost.']
  pickup_location varchar [note: 'Library branch or service desk where the patron will pick up the requested material.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date when the requesting library received the material from the lending institution.']
  renewal_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the due date for the borrowed material has been extended or renewed.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the patron submitted the interlibrary loan request. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the interlibrary loan request, typically generated by the library system.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interlibrary loan request tracking its progress from submission through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|pending|in_process|shipped|received|completed|returned|cancelled|unfilled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of interlibrary loan transaction: borrowing (requesting from another institution), lending (providing to another institution), copy request (article/chapter copy), or loan request (physical item loan).']
  returned_date date [note: 'Date when the borrowed material was returned to the lending institution.']
  shipped_date date [note: 'Date when the lending institution shipped or transmitted the requested material.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the requested material: physical mail, courier service, electronic delivery, Ariel document transmission, DocLine, email, or fax. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physical_mail|courier|electronic_delivery|ariel|docline|email|fax — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  staff_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes added by library staff for tracking, troubleshooting, or communication regarding the interlibrary loan request.']
  volume varchar [note: 'Volume number of the journal or serial publication containing the requested article.']

  Note: 'Interlibrary loan request record capturing borrowing and lending transactions with external institutions. Tracks request type (borrowing/lending), patron requester, requested title/OCLC number, lending institution, request date, due date, shipping method, status (submitted, in process, received, returned, cancelled), cost, and OCLC ILL transaction number. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris ILL module. Supports resource sharing across the academic library network.'
}

Table library.fine {
  fine_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the library fine or fee record. Primary key.']
  bib_record_id varchar [note: 'FK to library.bib_record']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library staff member who authorized the waiver. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library item (book, journal, media) associated with this fine. Applicable for overdue, lost, or damaged item fines.']
  loan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the circulation loan transaction that originated this fine, if applicable. Null for non-loan-related fees such as service charges or lost item replacement fees.']
  patron_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the library patron who incurred the fine or fee. Links to the patron master record in the library system.']
  revenue_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Fine revenue posts to specific GL revenue accounts for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and reconciliation. Each fine type (overdue, lost item, processing) maps to distinct revenue accounts. ']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Library fines post to student billing accounts for consolidated billing statements, unified collections processes, and integrated account holds. Real business process: bursar offices require library f']
  amount_paid decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by the patron toward this fine or fee. Cumulative sum of all payment transactions applied to this fine.']
  amount_waived decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total amount waived or forgiven by library staff. Represents the portion of the fine that was administratively cancelled and is no longer owed.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the fine or fee was assessed and recorded in the library system. Represents the business event date when the charge was created.']
  balance_due decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the fine. Calculated as original amount minus amount paid minus amount waived. Represents the current outstanding liability.']
  billing_export_date date [note: 'Date on which the fine was exported to the student billing system. Null if not yet exported or if billing integration is not enabled.']
  billing_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fine has been exported to the student billing system (e.g., Banner Student Accounts) for inclusion in the student\'s account statement. True if exported, false otherwise.']
  collections_date date [note: 'Date on which the fine was sent to collections or the bursar office. Null if the fine has not been escalated.']
  collections_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the fine has been escalated to external collections or the institution\'s bursar office. Tracks the collections lifecycle stage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fine record was first created in the library system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fine amounts. Typically the institution\'s base currency (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  daily_fine_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Per-day fine rate applied for overdue items. Used to calculate the total overdue fine based on the number of overdue days. Null for non-overdue fine types.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date on which the patron filed a dispute or appeal for this fine. Null if no dispute has been filed.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patron has disputed or appealed this fine. True if a dispute has been filed, false otherwise.']
  dispute_resolution varchar [note: 'Outcome of the patron\'s dispute or appeal. Indicates whether the dispute is pending review, approved (fine waived), denied (fine upheld), or withdrawn by the patron.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the fine or fee is expected to be paid. Used for aging analysis and collections escalation.']
  fine_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the fine record. Used for patron communication and reference in billing statements.']
  fine_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fine. Tracks whether the fine is open (unpaid), paid in full, waived, partially paid, sent to collections, or cancelled.']
  fine_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fine or fee. Indicates whether the charge is for overdue materials, lost items, damaged items, replacement costs, processing fees, or other service charges.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (library staff or system process) who last modified this fine record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fine record. Tracks changes to status, payments, waivers, or other attributes.']
  library_location varchar [note: 'Physical library branch or location where the fine originated. Used for multi-branch reporting and budget allocation.']
  maximum_fine_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cap or ceiling on the total fine amount for this fine type. Prevents overdue fines from exceeding the replacement cost of the item. Null if no cap is configured.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments added by library staff regarding the fine. May include patron communication history, dispute details, or special circumstances.']
  original_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Initial amount of the fine or fee assessed in the institution\'s base currency. Represents the gross charge before any payments, waivers, or adjustments.']
  overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the item was overdue at the time the fine was assessed. Applicable only for overdue fines; null for other fine types.']
  patron_group varchar [note: 'Category or affiliation of the patron who incurred the fine (e.g., undergraduate, graduate, faculty, staff, community). Denormalized from the patron record for segmentation analysis.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment applied to this fine. Null if no payment has been received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or mechanism used for the most recent payment. Indicates whether the patron paid via cash, check, credit card, debit card, online portal, student account billing integration, or wire transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|check|credit_card|debit_card|online|student_account|wire_transfer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waiver_date date [note: 'Date on which the fine waiver was approved and applied. Null if no waiver has been granted.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or code indicating why the fine was waived. Examples include patron appeal, system error, first-time offense, or administrative discretion.']

  Note: 'Patron fine or fee record generated from overdue items, lost materials, damaged items, or service charges. Tracks fine type, originating loan or item, amount assessed, amount paid, amount waived, waiver reason, payment date, and collection status. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris fines and fees module. Feeds into the billing domain for student account integration.'
}

Table library.acquisition_order {
  acquisition_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the library acquisition order record. Primary key for the acquisition order entity.']
  bib_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to library.bib_record. Business justification: acquisition_order contains bibliographic metadata (title, author, publisher, publication_year, isbn, issn, edition) that duplicates bib_record. Business: acquisition orders are for materials that will']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Acquisition orders are charged to departmental cost centers for budget accountability. Departmental spending reports, cost allocation, and budget manager oversight require this link. Standard practice']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the librarian or subject specialist responsible for selecting and approving this acquisition order as part of collection development.']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Acquisition orders encumber specific GL accounts. Encumbrance reporting, budget-to-actual analysis, year-end accruals, and financial controls require this link. Each order must reference the GL accoun']
  library_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the library fund or budget allocation code used to finance this acquisition order.']
  library_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier from whom the library materials are being acquired.']
  patron_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member, librarian, or staff member who requested the acquisition of this library material.']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'Actual date when the ordered library materials were physically received and logged into the library system.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the acquisition order was cancelled, if applicable (e.g., out of print, budget constraints, duplicate order, vendor unable to supply).']
  collection_code varchar [note: 'Library collection code indicating the specific collection to which the acquired material will be added (e.g., reference, circulating, special collections, reserves).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the acquisition order record was first created in the library system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the acquisition order is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary discount amount applied to the list price, calculated from the discount percentage or negotiated as a fixed reduction.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the list price by the vendor, typically negotiated through library consortia or volume agreements.']
  expected_receipt_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the ordered library materials are expected to be received from the vendor.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor issued the invoice for this acquisition order.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-issued invoice number associated with this acquisition order for payment processing and financial reconciliation.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor invoice associated with this acquisition order: pending (awaiting review), approved (authorized for payment), paid (payment completed), disputed (discrepancy identified), or cancelled (invoice voided).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the acquisition order record was most recently updated or modified in the library system.']
  list_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Published or catalog price of the library material as listed by the vendor or publisher before any discounts or adjustments.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Library location or branch code where the acquired material will be housed and made available to patrons.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the library material being acquired: physical book, serial/periodical, electronic resource (e-book, database, e-journal), audiovisual material (DVD, CD), manuscript, or map.']
  net_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final price paid for the library material after applying all discounts and adjustments to the list price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the acquisition order, including special instructions, vendor communications, or internal processing notes.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the acquisition order was created and placed with the vendor.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique order number assigned to this acquisition order for tracking and reference purposes.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the acquisition order: pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized for purchase), sent (transmitted to vendor), received (materials delivered), cancelled (order voided), or closed (order completed and finalized).']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the acquisition order type indicating the procurement method: firm order (one-time purchase), approval plan (vendor-selected materials), standing order (recurring series), subscription (periodical), gift (donated materials), or exchange (reciprocal agreement).']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when payment was issued to the vendor for this acquisition order.']
  quantity_ordered varchar [note: 'Number of copies or units of the library material ordered from the vendor.']
  quantity_received varchar [note: 'Number of copies or units of the library material actually received from the vendor, which may differ from quantity ordered due to partial shipments or vendor shortages.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating the receipt progress of the ordered materials: not received (no items delivered), partially received (some items delivered), fully received (all items delivered), or backordered (items delayed by vendor).']
  rush_order_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this acquisition order is marked as a rush or expedited order requiring priority processing and faster delivery.']
  shipping_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of shipping and handling charged by the vendor for delivery of the library materials.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sales tax or value-added tax (VAT) amount applied to the acquisition order based on institutional tax status and vendor location.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of the acquisition order including net price, shipping cost, and tax amount. Represents the final amount charged to the library fund.']

  Note: 'Purchase order record for acquiring new library materials — physical books, serials, electronic resources, and AV materials. Tracks vendor, fund code, order type (firm, approval, standing order, subscription), order date, expected receipt date, list price, discount, currency, invoice status, and receiving status. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris acquisitions module. Drives the library collection development workflow.'
}

Table library.library_vendor {
  library_vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the library vendor record. Primary key.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The library\'s account number or customer identifier assigned by the vendor for order tracking and invoicing.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendor\'s business mailing address, typically street address or PO Box.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendor\'s business mailing address, typically suite, floor, or building number.']
  approval_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the library has an approval plan arrangement with this vendor, where materials matching a profile are automatically sent for review. True if approval plan is active; False otherwise.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the vendor\'s business address.']
  claiming_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after expected delivery date before the library initiates a claim for missing or delayed materials. Used for automated claiming workflows.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the library\'s current contract or agreement with the vendor expires. Null if no formal contract exists or if the agreement is open-ended.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the library\'s current contract or agreement with the vendor became effective. Null if no formal contract exists.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the vendor\'s business address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was first created in the library system.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage offered by the vendor on list prices for library materials. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10% discount).']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor supports Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) for automated order transmission, invoicing, and claiming. True if EDI is available; False otherwise.']
  edi_vendor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the vendor in the library\'s EDI system for electronic order and invoice exchange. Populated only if EDI is enabled.']
  firm_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is approved for firm (title-specific) orders. True if firm orders are allowed; False otherwise.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order placed with this vendor. Used to identify inactive vendor relationships.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about the vendor relationship, special instructions, service issues, or account-specific details for library staff reference.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the vendor, such as Net 30, Net 60, or prepayment required. Defines the payment due date relative to invoice date.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the vendor\'s business address.']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the vendor\'s preferred invoicing and payment currency (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary vendor contact for order placement, invoicing inquiries, and account support.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the vendor organization for library acquisitions and account management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary vendor contact for urgent order or delivery issues.']
  return_policy varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s policy for returning damaged, incorrect, or unwanted materials, including time limits and restocking fees.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Default shipping method used by the vendor for delivering library materials (e.g., ground, expedited, freight, electronic delivery).']
  standing_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is approved for standing orders (automatic shipment of series or continuations). True if standing orders are allowed; False otherwise.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the vendor\'s business address.']
  subscription_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is approved for journal or database subscriptions. True if subscriptions are allowed; False otherwise.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s tax identification number (e.g., Employer Identification Number in the USA, VAT number in the EU) for tax reporting and compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was last modified in the library system.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the vendor in the library acquisitions system. Used for ordering, invoicing, and reporting. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris vendor management.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trading name of the library materials vendor, subscription agent, publisher, or database provider.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor relationship. Active vendors are approved for ordering; inactive vendors are no longer used; suspended vendors have temporary holds; pending approval vendors are under review.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor by the type of library materials or services they provide. Book jobbers supply monographs, subscription agents manage journal subscriptions, publishers provide direct content, and database providers license electronic resources.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the vendor for catalog browsing, account access, and support resources.']

  Note: 'Library materials vendor and subscription agent master record. Stores vendor name, vendor code, vendor type (book jobber, subscription agent, publisher, database provider), contact information, EDI capability flag, preferred currency, payment terms, and account status. Sourced from Alma/Ex Libris vendor management. Scoped to library acquisitions — distinct from institutional procurement vendors in the finance domain.'
}

Table library.library_fund {
  library_fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the library fund record. Primary key for the library fund entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Library funds are allocated to academic departments/cost centers. Budget variance analysis, financial consolidation, and departmental accountability reporting require linking library spending to insti']
  donor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or benefactor if this fund originates from a philanthropic gift or endowment. Null for institutionally-funded library funds.']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Library fund expenditures post to institutional general ledger accounts for financial statement preparation, month-end close, and audit trails. Every library fund must map to GL account(s) for GASB/FA']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.employee']
  parent_fund_library_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fund in a hierarchical fund structure. Enables roll-up reporting from sub-funds to master funds. Null for top-level funds.']
  primary_library_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the faculty member or subject librarian responsible for managing this fund and making acquisition decisions. Typically a liaison librarian for a specific academic discipline.']
  allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total budget amount allocated to this fund for the fiscal year. Represents the initial funding available before any commitments or expenditures.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchases from this fund require additional administrative approval beyond the fund owner. True if approval workflow is required; False if fund owner has full discretion.']
  approval_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary threshold above which purchases from this fund require additional approval. Null if no threshold applies or if approval_required_flag is False.']
  available_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'The remaining unencumbered and unexpended balance available for new commitments. Calculated as allocated_amount minus encumbered_amount minus expended_amount.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The institutional cost center or budget unit code for financial tracking and reporting. Links library fund to broader university budget structure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fund record was first created in the library system. Audit trail for fund establishment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this fund (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency library budgets.']
  encumbered_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount committed to purchase orders and subscriptions but not yet expended. Represents funds reserved for pending acquisitions.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date this fund expires or is scheduled to close. Null for perpetual funds such as endowments. For annual funds, typically the end of the fiscal year.']
  expended_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount actually spent from this fund to date. Represents invoices paid and completed transactions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this fund allocation applies, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024). Aligns with institutional fiscal calendar.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this fund within the library budget structure. Used for acquisitions tracking and financial reporting.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable descriptive name of the library fund (e.g., Science Monographs Fund, Humanities Serials Fund).']
  fund_source varchar [note: 'The origin of the funding for this library fund. Institutional funds come from general university budget; endowment funds are from invested principal; grant funds are from external research or program grants; donation/gift funds are from philanthropic contributions; state/federal funds are from government appropriations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: institutional|endowment|grant|donation|gift|state|federal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the fund in its lifecycle. Active funds are available for use; inactive funds are temporarily unavailable; closed funds are permanently retired; frozen funds are locked pending review; pending funds are awaiting approval.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fund based on its financial status and usage restrictions. Allocated funds are budgeted but not yet committed; encumbered funds are committed to orders; expended funds are fully spent; restricted funds have donor or grant limitations; unrestricted funds have no usage constraints; endowment funds are perpetual with spending rules.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the library staff member who most recently modified this fund record. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fund record was most recently updated. Tracks the currency of fund data.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'The type of library materials this fund is designated to purchase. Books include monographs and textbooks; serials include journals and periodicals; electronic resources include e-books and e-journals; databases include subscription research platforms; multimedia includes audio/video materials; archives include special collections; all indicates no material type restriction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: books|serials|electronic_resources|databases|multimedia|archives|all — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special instructions, or historical context about this fund. Used by librarians for fund management documentation.']
  oer_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fund may be used to support Open Educational Resources initiatives, including OER textbook adoption incentives and open access publishing fees. True if OER expenditures are permitted; False otherwise.']
  restriction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing any donor-imposed or institutional restrictions on how this fund may be used (e.g., \'Must be used for rare book acquisitions in American History\', \'Supports STEM Open Educational Resources only\').']
