  cancellation_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to cancel the subscription before auto-renewal.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of content available on the platform.']
  counter_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the platform provides COUNTER-compliant usage statistics.']
  counter_release_version varchar [note: 'Version of the COUNTER Code of Practice supported by the platform (e.g., Release 5, Release 4).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the platform record was first created in the library system.']
  knowledge_base_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this platform to the library\'s knowledge base for electronic resource management.']
  marc_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix code used in MARC records to identify resources from this platform.']
  mobile_optimized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the platform is optimized for mobile device access.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special instructions, or internal notes about the platform.']
  openurl_base_url varchar [note: 'Base URL for constructing OpenURL links to resources on this platform.']
  openurl_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the platform supports OpenURL linking for context-sensitive reference linking.']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or abbreviation used to identify the platform in library systems and reports.']
  platform_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the platform (e.g., JSTOR, ProQuest, EBSCO, SpringerLink, IEEE Xplore).']
  platform_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the platform within the library system.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the platform based on its primary function and content delivery model.']
  platform_url varchar [note: 'Primary web address for accessing the platform.']
  proxy_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether off-campus access to this platform is enabled via proxy server (e.g., EZproxy).']
  proxy_prefix varchar [note: 'URL prefix added by the proxy server to enable off-campus access to platform resources.']
  simultaneous_user_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of users who can access the platform concurrently, as defined by license terms. Null indicates unlimited access.']
  subject_coverage varchar [note: 'Broad subject areas or disciplines covered by the platform\'s content (e.g., STEM, Humanities, Social Sciences, Multidisciplinary).']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription period for the platform ends.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription period for the platform began.']
  support_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for technical support and platform inquiries.']
  support_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting platform technical support.']
  sushi_api_key varchar [note: 'API key or token used to authenticate SUSHI requests to the platform.']
  sushi_requestor_code varchar [note: 'Requestor identifier used for SUSHI authentication, often representing the library or consortium.']
  sushi_service_url varchar [note: 'API endpoint for automated retrieval of COUNTER usage reports via SUSHI protocol.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date when a trial subscription to the platform ends or ended.']
  trial_start_date date [note: 'Date when a trial subscription to the platform began.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the library staff member who last updated the platform record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the platform record was last modified in the library system.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor or publisher that provides the platform.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for platform. Referenced by platform_id.'
}

Table library.publisher {
  publisher_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for publisher']
  parent_publisher_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent publisher if this publisher is an imprint or subsidiary of a larger publishing house.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when this publisher was acquired by another entity, if applicable.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the publisher headquarters or main office.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, or building information.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the publisher is headquartered or primarily operates.']
  copyright_policy_url varchar [note: 'URL to the publishers copyright and permissions policy documentation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary country of publication or publisher headquarters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this publisher record was first created in the library system.']
  defunct_year varchar [note: 'Year the publisher ceased operations, if applicable.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage negotiated with the publisher for institutional purchases.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the publisher organization.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the publisher, if applicable.']
  founding_year varchar [note: 'Year the publisher was established or founded.']
  imprint varchar [note: 'Imprint or brand name under which the publisher releases materials. An imprint is a trade name under which a publisher or group of publishers publishes a work.']
  isbn_prefix varchar [note: 'The ISBN prefix assigned to this publisher by the International ISBN Agency. Format: GS1 prefix (978 or 979) followed by registration group and registrant elements.']
  issn_prefix varchar [note: 'The ISSN prefix or range assigned to this publisher for serial publications.']
  licensing_terms varchar [note: 'Standard licensing terms or Creative Commons license types typically used by the publisher.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trade name of the publisher organization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the publisher for internal library use.']
  open_access_policy varchar [note: 'The publishers policy regarding open access publishing and availability of scholarly content.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the publisher (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, prepayment required).']
  peer_review_policy varchar [note: 'The type of peer review process employed by the publisher for scholarly publications.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the publisher organization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the publisher address.']
  predatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the publisher has been identified as predatory or questionable by recognized watchdog organizations.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this publisher is designated as a preferred vendor for acquisitions.']
  publisher_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or abbreviation used to identify the publisher in library systems and cataloging records.']
  publisher_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the publisher by organizational type and business model.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the publisher is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the publisher in the library system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this publisher record was last modified in the library system.']
  vendor_account_number varchar [note: 'Account or customer number assigned by the publisher for institutional purchasing and licensing.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the publisher.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for publisher. Referenced by publisher_id.'
}

Table library.library {
  library_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for library']
  parent_library_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on library (parent_library_id)']
  accessibility_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the library facility meets ADA accessibility standards for patrons with disabilities including wheelchair access, assistive technology, and accessible workstations.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the library as evaluated by relevant library accreditation bodies or as part of institutional accreditation reviews.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the library facility.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building number.']
  annual_circulation_volume varchar [note: 'Total number of items circulated (checked out) in the most recent fiscal year. Key performance metric for library usage and collection relevance.']
  annual_gate_count varchar [note: 'Total number of patron visits to the library facility in the most recent fiscal year, typically measured by electronic gate counters.']
  building_name varchar [note: 'Name of the building or facility that houses the library.']
  campus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific campus where this library is physically located. Null for digital-only libraries.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the library is located.']
  collection_size_digital varchar [note: 'Total number of digital items and electronic resources accessible through the library including e-books, e-journals, databases, and digital archives.']
  collection_size_physical varchar [note: 'Total number of physical items in the library collection including books, journals, media, and other tangible materials. Reported to IPEDS and accreditation bodies.']
  computer_workstation_count varchar [note: 'Number of public computer workstations available for patron use.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the library location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this library record was first created in the system.']
  director_name varchar [note: 'Name of the current library director or dean of libraries responsible for overall library operations and strategic direction.']
  discovery_service_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the discovery service or search platform used for patron resource discovery. Examples include Primo, Summon, EBSCO Discovery Service, WorldCat Discovery.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for library inquiries and correspondence.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the library was officially established or opened for service.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the library, if applicable.']
  floor_location varchar [note: 'Specific floor or floors within the building where library services and collections are located. May include multiple floors or specific room numbers.']
  ils_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the integrated library system or library services platform used by this library. Common systems include Alma (Ex Libris), Sierra (Innovative), Koha (open source), FOLIO (open source), Symphony (SirsiDynix), and Voyager (Ex Libris legacy).']
  institution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent educational institution that owns and operates this library.']
  last_accreditation_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent accreditation review or assessment of library services and resources.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this library record was most recently updated.']
  library_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify the library in external systems and catalogs. Often used in interlibrary loan systems and discovery services.']
  library_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the library as recognized by the institution and external library networks.']
  library_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the library based on its function and scope within the institution. Main libraries serve as central hubs, branch libraries serve specific campuses, departmental libraries support specific academic departments, special collections house rare materials, digital libraries provide electronic resources, and archives preserve institutional records.']
  oclc_symbol varchar [note: 'Unique OCLC institution symbol used for interlibrary loan, cataloging, and resource sharing through WorldCat and other OCLC services.']
  operating_hours_weekly decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the library is open to patrons during a typical week during the academic term. Used for service level reporting and staffing planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the library. Active libraries are fully operational, inactive libraries are permanently closed, temporarily closed libraries are closed for a defined period, under renovation libraries are undergoing facility improvements, and planned libraries are in pre-opening phase.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the library.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the library address.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of patron seats available in the library including study tables, carrels, lounge seating, and computer workstations.']
  staff_count_fte decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of library staff measured in full-time equivalents, including librarians, support staff, and student workers. Reported to IPEDS.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the library is located. Use standard two-letter abbreviations for US states.']
  study_room_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual or group study rooms available for patron reservation and use.']
  supports_interlibrary_loan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library participates in interlibrary loan services, allowing patrons to borrow materials from other libraries and lending materials to external institutions.']
  supports_oer_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library tracks and promotes open educational resources as part of its collection and services to reduce textbook costs for students.']
  total_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total physical space of the library facility measured in square feet. Used for space planning, capacity analysis, and reporting to accreditation bodies.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'URL of the library\'s public website providing information about services, hours, and resources.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for library. Referenced by library_id.'
}

Table advancement.alumnus {
  alumnus_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumnus record. Primary key for the alumnus entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key that uniquely identifies each individual who has graduated from or attended the institution.']
  cip_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.cip_code. Business justification: Alumni profile stores primary degree CIP for segmentation, STEM alumni tracking, and targeted solicitation campaigns. Proper FK enables accurate STEM designation queries for NSF alumni surveys and OPT']
  cocurricular_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.cocurricular_record. Business justification: Gift officers leverage cocurricular achievements for donor storytelling, impact reporting, and leadership volunteer identification. Service hours, leadership roles, and awards inform recognition socie']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the original student identifier from the Student Information System (SIS). Links the alumnus record back to their student record for historical academic data retrieval. This is the business key that was assigned during enrollment.']
  alternate_email varchar [note: 'A secondary email address for the alumnus. Used as a backup contact method when the primary email is undeliverable or for work vs personal email segmentation.']
  alumni_status varchar [note: 'The current engagement status of the alumnus record. Active indicates the alumnus is reachable and engaged; Deceased indicates confirmed death; Lost indicates contact information is outdated; Do Not Contact indicates explicit request to cease communications.']
  cumulative_gpa decimal(4,3) [note: 'The cumulative GPA earned by the alumnus at the time of graduation. Used for honors society identification, scholarship donor reporting, and academic achievement recognition. Typically on a 4.0 scale.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'The name of the organization where the alumnus is currently employed. Used for career outcomes tracking, employer engagement, and prospect research for major gift fundraising.']
  current_job_title varchar [note: 'The current professional title or position of the alumnus. Used for career outcomes reporting, networking events, and wealth screening for fundraising.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The date of birth of the alumnus. Used for age-based segmentation, reunion year calculations, and identity verification. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  deceased_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus passed away, if known. Used for memorial records and in memoriam recognition. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  deceased_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the alumnus is deceased. True if confirmed deceased, False otherwise. Used to suppress communications and update alumni records appropriately.']
  department_name varchar [note: 'The academic department that administered the alumnus primary degree program. Used for department-level alumni engagement and faculty-alumni networking events.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating the alumnus has requested no contact from the institution. True if do-not-contact request is active, False otherwise. Suppresses all outreach including fundraising, events, and communications.']
  do_not_solicit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating the alumnus has requested no fundraising solicitations. True if do-not-solicit request is active, False otherwise. Suppresses fundraising appeals but allows other alumni communications.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'The self-reported ethnicity or race of the alumnus. Used for diversity reporting, IPEDS compliance, and targeted outreach to underrepresented alumni populations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Hispanic or Latino|American Indian or Alaska Native|Asian|Black or African American|Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander|White|Two or More Races|Unknown — promote to reference product]']
  ferpa_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the alumnus has provided consent to share directory information under FERPA regulations. True if consent given, False if restricted. Controls publication of alumni information in directories and public listings.']
  gender varchar [note: 'The self-identified gender of the alumnus. Used for demographic reporting and diversity analytics. Supports inclusive gender identity options.']
  graduation_date date [note: 'The exact date on which the alumnus degree was conferred. Used for anniversary-based engagement and precise tenure calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  graduation_term_code varchar [note: 'The specific academic term code in which the alumnus graduated (e.g., 202305 for Spring 2023). Provides more granular graduation timing than year alone.']
  graduation_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the alumnus graduated or completed their primary degree. Used for reunion planning, class-year segmentation, and alumni participation rate calculations.']
  honors_designation varchar [note: 'The Latin honors or other academic distinction awarded at graduation based on GPA or other criteria. Used for recognition programs and donor prospect research. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Summa Cum Laude|Magna Cum Laude|Cum Laude|Honors|High Honors|Highest Honors|None — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'The legal first name of the alumnus as recorded in official institutional records and on the degree conferred. Used for official transcripts, diplomas, and legal correspondence.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'The legal last name (surname/family name) of the alumnus as recorded in official institutional records and on the degree conferred. Used for official transcripts, diplomas, and legal correspondence.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'The legal middle name or initial of the alumnus as recorded in official institutional records. May be null if no middle name was provided.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'The URL to the alumnus LinkedIn profile. Used for professional networking, career outcomes verification, and prospect research.']
  maiden_name varchar [note: 'The birth surname of the alumnus, typically used to track alumni who have changed their last name after graduation. Critical for alumni search and reunion planning.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the alumnus mailing address (street number and name, apartment/unit number). Used for direct mail campaigns, alumni magazine distribution, and event invitations.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the alumnus mailing address (additional address details such as suite, building, or care-of information). Optional field.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'The city of the alumnus mailing address. Used for geographic segmentation of alumni populations and regional event planning.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the alumnus mailing address. Used for international alumni segmentation and global engagement strategies.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code or ZIP code of the alumnus mailing address. Used for mail sorting, geographic analysis, and targeted regional campaigns.']
  mailing_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the alumnus mailing address. Used for regional alumni chapter organization and state-level reporting.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'The mobile/cell phone number of the alumnus. Used for SMS communications, text-to-give campaigns, and mobile-first engagement strategies.']
  preferred_first_name varchar [note: 'The first name the alumnus prefers to be called in informal communications, alumni events, and publications. May differ from legal name. Used for personalized engagement and respecting individual identity preferences.']
  preferred_last_name varchar [note: 'The last name the alumnus prefers to be used in informal communications and alumni engagement activities. Often reflects name changes due to marriage or personal preference.']
  primary_degree_level varchar [note: 'The highest degree level earned by the alumnus from the institution (e.g., Bachelor, Master, Doctoral, Certificate, Associate). Used for segmentation in fundraising appeals and alumni engagement strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Certificate|Associate|Bachelor|Master|Doctoral|Professional|Post-Doctoral — promote to reference product]']
  primary_degree_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the primary degree earned (e.g., Bachelor of Science, Master of Business Administration, Doctor of Philosophy). Used for alumni directory listings and credential verification.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for contacting the alumnus. Used for alumni communications, event invitations, fundraising campaigns, and institutional updates. This is the preferred digital contact channel.']
  primary_major varchar [note: 'The primary field of study or major for the alumnus highest degree. Used for academic affinity segmentation and school/college-based fundraising campaigns.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'The primary telephone number for contacting the alumnus. Used for phonathon campaigns, event reminders, and urgent communications.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this alumnus record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'The system or process that created or last significantly updated this alumnus record. Used for data lineage tracking and data quality auditing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SIS|CRM|Manual Entry|Data Import|Alumni Survey|Third Party|Merge — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this alumnus record was last modified. Used for audit trail, data freshness assessment, and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  school_college_name varchar [note: 'The name of the academic school, college, or division from which the alumnus graduated. Used for school-based fundraising campaigns and dean-level engagement strategies.']

  Note: 'Master record for every individual who has graduated from or attended the institution. Serves as the SSOT for alumni identity, capturing legal name, preferred name, graduation year, degree(s) earned, primary major/CIP code, student ID cross-reference, demographic data, FERPA consent flags, deceased indicator, and record source. This is the foundational entity for all alumni relations, advancement, and career services activities. Supports IPEDS graduation rate reporting and alumni participation rate calculations.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_contact {
  alumni_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni contact record. Primary key.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus this contact record belongs to. Links to the alumni master record.']
  address_verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been verified through USPS or other address validation services.']
  contact_frequency_preference varchar [note: 'How often the alumnus wishes to receive communications from the institution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this contact record was first created in the system.']
  do_not_call_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of telephone communications.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Global flag indicating the alumnus has requested no contact from the institution across all channels.']
  do_not_email_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of email communications.']
  do_not_mail_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of postal mail communications.']
  do_not_sms_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of SMS text message communications.']
  email_consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which email consent was obtained.']
  email_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus provided consent for email communications.']
  home_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the alumnus home mailing address (street number and name).']
  home_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the alumnus home mailing address (apartment, suite, unit number).']
  home_city varchar [note: 'The city of the alumnus home address.']
  home_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the alumnus home address.']
  home_phone varchar [note: 'The alumnus home telephone number for voice contact.']
  home_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the alumnus home address.']
  home_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the alumnus home address.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this contact record was last updated.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'The URL to the alumnus LinkedIn profile for professional networking and career tracking.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the alumnus preferred mailing address if different from home address.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'The city of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'The alumnus mobile telephone number for voice and SMS contact.']
  ncoa_update_date date [note: 'The date when the address was last updated through the USPS National Change of Address database.']
  opt_in_career_news boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive career-related news and opportunities.']
  opt_in_event_invitations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive invitations to alumni events.']
  opt_in_fundraising_appeals boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive fundraising and donation appeals.']
  opt_in_institutional_news boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive general institutional news and updates.']
  phone_consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which phone consent was obtained.']
  phone_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus provided consent for phone communications.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'The alumnus preferred method for receiving communications from the institution.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'The alumnus preferred language for communications using ISO 639-2 three-letter codes (ENG=English, SPA=Spanish, FRA=French, DEU=German, ZHO=Chinese, JPN=Japanese).']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the alumnus prefers to be addressed by in communications and events.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for alumni communications. Used as the default contact channel for digital outreach.']
  seasonal_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of a seasonal or temporary address (e.g., winter residence).']
  seasonal_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of a seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_city varchar [note: 'The city of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_end_date date [note: 'The date when the seasonal address becomes inactive each year.']
  seasonal_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_start_date date [note: 'The date when the seasonal address becomes active each year.']
  seasonal_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  secondary_email varchar [note: 'An alternate email address for the alumnus, often a professional or work email.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'The alumnus work or business telephone number.']

  Note: 'Stores all contact information and explicit communication preferences for each alumnus. Contact data includes addresses (home, mailing, seasonal, international), email addresses (personal, professional), phone numbers, LinkedIn profile URL, address verification status, and NCOA update dates. Communication preferences include preferred language, preferred contact method (email, postal mail, phone, SMS), frequency preferences (weekly, monthly, annual), topic opt-ins (career news, event invitations, fundraising appeals, institutional news), global do-not-contact flag, opt-in/opt-out flags per channel, consent timestamps, and consent source (web form, phone, event). Supports multi-address scenarios, FERPA-compliant outreach, CAN-SPAM/GDPR compliance, and international alumni communications.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_communication_preference {
  alumni_communication_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each alumni communication preference record. Primary key for the alumni communication preference entity.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumni individual whose communication preferences are captured in this record. Links to the alumni master record.']
  bounce_status varchar [note: 'The current email deliverability status for this alumnus. Hard bounces indicate invalid addresses and trigger automatic suppression. Soft bounces are temporary delivery failures.']
  career_news_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive communications about career services, job postings, professional development, and networking opportunities.']
  communication_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the alumnus prefers to receive communications. Used to throttle outreach volume and respect alumni preferences.']
  consent_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the alumnus submitted their communication preferences via web form. Used for fraud detection and consent verification in compliance audits.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the alumnus provided their communication preferences. Used for consent verification and audit trail purposes.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus provided or updated their communication preferences. Critical for GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance auditing.']
  consent_user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser user agent string captured when the alumnus submitted preferences via web form. Provides technical context for consent verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this preference record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Master suppression flag indicating the alumnus has requested no contact through any channel for any purpose. Overrides all other opt-in preferences. True if contact is prohibited.']
  do_not_solicit boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has specifically requested not to receive fundraising solicitations while remaining open to other types of communications. True if fundraising solicitation is prohibited.']
  double_opt_in_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has confirmed their email opt-in via a double opt-in process (confirmation link). True if confirmed. Required for GDPR-compliant email marketing.']
  double_opt_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus clicked the confirmation link in the double opt-in email. Provides audit trail for verified consent.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has explicitly opted in to receive email communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  event_invitations_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive invitations to alumni events including reunions, homecoming, regional gatherings, and networking events.']
  fundraising_appeals_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive fundraising appeals including annual giving campaigns, major gift solicitations, and planned giving information.']
  gdpr_consent_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the GDPR consent language and privacy policy that the alumnus agreed to. Required for international alumni subject to GDPR.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'The GDPR Article 6 lawful basis under which the institution processes the alumnus communication data. Typically consent or legitimate interest for alumni relations.']
  institutional_news_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive general institutional news including academic achievements, research highlights, campus updates, and alumni spotlights.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference record is the current active record for the alumnus. True if active. Supports preference versioning and historical tracking.']
  last_bounce_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent email bounce event for this alumnus. Used to track deliverability issues and trigger address verification workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this preference record was last modified in the system. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  phone_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has consented to receive phone calls from the institution for engagement, fundraising, or event invitations.']
  postal_mail_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive postal mail communications including newsletters, magazines, and fundraising appeals.']
  preference_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the current preferences expire and require re-confirmation. Used in jurisdictions requiring periodic consent renewal (e.g., GDPR soft opt-in).']
  preference_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when any preference field in this record was last modified. Used to track preference change history and recency.']
  preference_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or application from which this preference record originated (e.g., Blackbaud Raisers Edge, Slate CRM, Alumni Portal). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'The primary channel through which the alumnus prefers to be contacted. Used to route communications through the appropriate delivery system.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'The language in which the alumnus prefers to receive communications. Supports multilingual outreach for international alumni populations.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has consented to receive text messages (SMS) for event reminders, urgent updates, or brief announcements.']
  spam_complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has marked institutional emails as spam. True if a spam complaint has been received. Triggers immediate suppression to protect sender reputation.']
  spam_complaint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus marked an institutional email as spam. Used for compliance reporting and sender reputation management.']
  suppression_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the do-not-contact or do-not-solicit suppression became effective. Used to ensure compliance with suppression requests from the specified date forward.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the alumnus is suppressed from communications. Captures context such as deceased status, legal request, or complaint history.']
  unsubscribe_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or categorical reason provided by the alumnus for unsubscribing. Used for feedback analysis and communication strategy improvement.']
  unsubscribe_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus unsubscribed from communications. Used to track opt-out events and ensure immediate suppression.']
  volunteer_opportunities_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive information about volunteer opportunities including mentoring, admissions support, career advising, and board service.']

  Note: 'Captures each alumnus\'s explicit communication preferences including preferred language, preferred contact method (email, postal mail, phone, SMS), frequency preferences (weekly, monthly, annual), topic opt-ins (career news, event invitations, fundraising appeals, institutional news), and global do-not-contact flag. Tracks consent timestamps and the source of consent (web form, phone, event). Supports FERPA-compliant outreach and CAN-SPAM/GDPR compliance for international alumni.'
}

Table advancement.affinity_group {
  affinity_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the affinity group. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Alumni chapters and affinity groups organized by program (MBA Alumni, Engineering Society, etc.) require FK for event targeting, engagement metrics, and program-specific fundraising campaigns. Replace']
  chapter_president_alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier linking the chapter president to their alumnus record in the alumni master data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Affinity groups often align with academic units/cost centers for budget and fundraising tracking. Creating affinity_group.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for financial reporting.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Affinity groups are reported by fiscal period for fundraising and engagement tracking. Creating affinity_group.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  institutional_staff_liaison_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier linking the staff liaison to their employee record in the human resources system.']
  affinity_group_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the affinity group in its lifecycle.']
  annual_dues_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard annual membership dues amount charged to members of this affinity group, in USD. Null if no dues are required.']
  annual_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of events hosted by the affinity group in the current fiscal year. Key metric for chapter health assessment.']
  associated_athletic_team varchar [note: 'Name of the athletic team or sport supported by the affinity group (e.g., \'Football\', \'Women\'s Basketball\'). Applicable to athletic booster clubs.']
  charter_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the group\'s charter expires and requires renewal. Null for groups with perpetual charters.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the affinity group is headquartered or primarily active.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the affinity group leadership or for general inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting the affinity group leadership.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the primary country where the affinity group operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the affinity group record was first created in the system.']
  dues_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether membership in this affinity group requires payment of dues. True if dues are required, False if membership is free.']
  engagement_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the affinity group\'s engagement level based on activity metrics, event participation, and member involvement. Used for chapter health analysis and resource allocation.']
  founding_date date [note: 'Date when the affinity group was officially established or chartered by the institution.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic area served by the affinity group (e.g., \'San Francisco Bay Area\', \'Northeast Corridor\', \'International - Europe\'). Applicable primarily to geographic chapters.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the affinity group in systems and reports.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the affinity group, chapter, or alumni network as recognized by the institution.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the affinity group by its organizing principle: geographic chapter (regional alumni clubs), academic college chapter (school/college-based), identity-based network (cultural, gender, LGBTQ+), athletic booster club (sports support), professional network (career/industry), or special interest (hobby, cause).']
  last_event_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent event hosted or sponsored by the affinity group. Used to track chapter activity and engagement.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the affinity group record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the affinity group record was last updated in the system.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address for the affinity group (street address or P.O. Box).']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address (suite, apartment, building, etc.). Nullable.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'City for the affinity group\'s mailing address.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the affinity group\'s mailing address.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code for the affinity group\'s mailing address.']
  mailing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province for the affinity group\'s mailing address.']
  membership_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active members enrolled in the affinity group. Updated periodically based on membership roster.']
  mission_statement varchar [note: 'Official mission statement or purpose description for the affinity group, articulating its goals and values.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the affinity group. Used by advancement staff for internal documentation.']
  recognition_level varchar [note: 'Institutional recognition level awarded to the affinity group based on performance criteria such as membership growth, event quality, fundraising, and volunteer engagement.']
  social_media_handle varchar [note: 'Primary social media handle or username for the affinity group (e.g., Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook). Format may vary by platform.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the affinity group is primarily based. Applicable to domestic chapters.']
  total_funds_raised_ytd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount raised by the affinity group in the current fiscal year through fundraising activities, in USD.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL for the affinity group, if one exists. May be a dedicated site or a page on the institutional alumni portal.']

  Note: 'Defines the official alumni affinity groups, chapters, and networks recognized by the institution, and manages their membership rosters. Includes group name, type (geographic chapter, academic college chapter, identity-based network, athletic booster club, professional network), founding date, geographic region, associated college or school, active status, chapter president, and institutional staff liaison. Also captures individual membership records: member alumnus, membership start/end dates, role (member, officer, board member, chapter president), status (active, lapsed, honorary), enrollment method (self-enrolled, staff-assigned, event-triggered), and dues payment status. Supports chapter health analysis, officer succession planning, and engagement segmentation.'
}

Table advancement.group_membership {
  group_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the group membership record. Primary key for the group membership entity.']
  affinity_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the affinity group or chapter to which the alumnus belongs. Links to the affinity group master record.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who holds this group membership. Links to the alumni master record.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Group memberships are tracked by fiscal period for affinity group reporting and dues tracking. Creating group_membership.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this membership is set to automatically renew at the end of the current term. True means automatic renewal is enabled, false means manual renewal required.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'The preferred method of communication for this group membership. Indicates how the alumnus wishes to receive group-related communications and updates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this membership record was first created in the system. Represents the initial capture of the membership relationship.']
  dues_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of dues associated with this membership for the current period. May be zero for complimentary or honorary memberships.']
  dues_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the dues amount. Typically USD for U.S. institutions but may vary for international chapters.']
  dues_paid_date date [note: 'The date on which the membership dues were paid for the current period. Used to track payment timeliness and compliance.']
  dues_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of membership dues. Paid indicates full payment received, unpaid indicates no payment, waived indicates dues exempted, partial indicates incomplete payment, overdue indicates past due date, and pending indicates payment processing.']
  election_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus was elected to a leadership position within the affinity group. Applicable for elected officer roles.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this membership ended or is scheduled to end. Null for active memberships with no defined end date. For annual memberships, represents the expiration date.']
  events_attended_count varchar [note: 'Total number of affinity group events attended by the alumnus since joining. Used to measure engagement level and chapter health.']
  join_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the alumnus joined this affinity group. Self-enrolled indicates alumnus initiated, staff-assigned indicates advancement office action, event-triggered indicates automatic enrollment from event attendance, auto-assigned indicates system rule-based assignment, invitation-accepted indicates response to invitation, and application-approved indicates formal application process.']
  last_event_attended_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the alumnus attended an event hosted by this affinity group. Used to track recency of engagement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this membership record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness assessment.']
  last_renewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the alumnus renewed this membership. Tracks renewal history and engagement continuity.']
  leadership_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus completed or will complete their leadership term within the affinity group. Used for officer succession planning and historical tracking.']
  leadership_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus assumed a leadership role within the affinity group. Applicable only when membership_role indicates an officer or leadership position. Used for officer succession planning.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique membership number or identifier assigned to this alumnus within the specific affinity group. May be used on membership cards or communications.']
  membership_role varchar [note: 'The role or position the alumnus holds within the affinity group. Distinguishes between general members and leadership positions such as officers, board members, or chapter presidents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: member|officer|board_member|chapter_president|vice_president|treasurer|secretary|committee_chair|honorary_member — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the membership. Active indicates current participation, lapsed indicates expired membership, honorary indicates special recognition status, suspended indicates temporary hold, pending indicates awaiting approval, and inactive indicates permanently ended.']
  membership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the membership indicating the category or tier. Regular is standard annual membership, lifetime is permanent membership, associate is non-graduate affiliate, emeritus is retired member, student is current student affiliate, and young alumni is recent graduate category.']
  nomination_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus was nominated for membership or a leadership role within the affinity group. Used for governance and election tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this membership. May include special circumstances, historical context, or staff observations relevant to the membership relationship.']
  opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has explicitly opted in to receive communications and participate in activities for this affinity group. True means opted in, false means opted out.']
  recognition_level varchar [note: 'The recognition or tier level assigned to this membership based on engagement, giving, or service. Used for stewardship and recognition programs.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which this membership is due for renewal. Used for annual or term-based memberships to track renewal cycles.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this membership record originated. Typically Blackbaud Raisers Edge or similar advancement CRM system.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this membership record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which this membership became effective. Represents when the alumnus officially joined the affinity group.']
  term_length_months varchar [note: 'The length of the membership or leadership term in months. Used for term-based memberships and officer positions to calculate expiration and succession timing.']
  volunteer_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of volunteer hours contributed by the alumnus in their capacity as a member of this affinity group. Used to track engagement and recognize service.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording which alumni belong to which affinity groups or chapters. Captures membership start date, end date, membership role (member, officer, board member, chapter president), membership status (active, lapsed, honorary), how the alumnus joined (self-enrolled, staff-assigned, event-triggered), and dues payment status where applicable. Enables analysis of chapter health, officer succession, and engagement by affinity segment.'
}

Table advancement.engagement_activity {
  engagement_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each engagement activity record. Primary key for the engagement activity entity.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who participated in this engagement activity. Links to the alumni master record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising or engagement campaign associated with this activity. Nullable for activities not tied to a specific campaign.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Advancement offices track which CRM/donor management system (Raiser\'s Edge, Salesforce Education Cloud, Ellucian Advance) recorded each engagement activity for system-of-record attribution, data quali']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event record if this activity represents attendance at or participation in a scheduled event. Nullable for non-event activities.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Engagement activities occur in specific fiscal periods for donor relations tracking and reporting. Creating engagement_activity.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based repor']
  primary_engagement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advancement or alumni relations staff member who recorded or facilitated this engagement activity. Tracks ownership and accountability for relationship management.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumni relations or advancement program under which this activity was conducted. Examples include annual giving programs, volunteer programs, or career services initiatives.']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Engagement activities center on research (lab tours, research presentations, symposia). Advancement tracks which research awards are featured in donor cultivation for major gift proposals and stewards']
  activity_date date [note: 'The date on which the engagement activity occurred. Represents the primary business event timestamp for this interaction.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engagement activity. Tracks whether the activity was completed as planned, cancelled, or is still pending.']
  activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the engagement activity took place. Provides granular timing for activities requiring time-of-day tracking.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engagement activity. Defines the nature of the interaction between the alumnus and the institution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: event_attendance|volunteer_service|mentorship_session|career_fair_participation|webinar|campus_visit|phone_call|email_response|social_media_interaction|donation_solicitation|survey_response|networking_event|reunion_attendance|board_meeting|committee_participation — promote to reference product]']
  attendee_count varchar [note: 'Number of individuals who participated in the engagement activity. Applicable to group activities such as events, webinars, or meetings. Null for one-on-one interactions.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication or interaction channel through which the engagement activity was conducted. Distinguishes the medium of interaction from the activity type.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Direct cost incurred by the institution to conduct this engagement activity. Includes event costs, travel expenses, materials, or other direct expenditures. Used for ROI analysis and budget tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this engagement activity record was first created in the system. Audit field for data governance and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount. Supports multi-currency tracking for institutions with international operations.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the engagement activity in minutes. Applicable to activities with measurable duration such as phone calls, meetings, mentorship sessions, or events.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score assigned to this activity representing its contribution to the alumnus overall engagement level. Used for engagement scoring models and prospect qualification. Scale and methodology defined by institutional advancement strategy.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Target date by which follow-up action should be completed. Nullable if no follow-up is required or scheduled.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engagement activity requires a follow-up action or contact. Used to drive task management and ensure relationship continuity.']
  is_major_gift_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engagement activity is part of a major gift cultivation, solicitation, or stewardship effort. Used to prioritize high-value donor relationship activities.']
  is_volunteer_activity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activity represents volunteer service by the alumnus on behalf of the institution. Used to track volunteer engagement separately from other interaction types.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the engagement activity took place. May include campus building names, city names, or virtual platform identifiers.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this engagement activity record. Audit field for accountability and change management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this engagement activity record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text notes documenting the content, discussion points, outcomes, and any follow-up actions from the engagement activity. Critical for relationship continuity and institutional memory.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result or outcome of the engagement activity. Captures whether the activity achieved its intended purpose and what next steps may be required.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to this engagement activity based on strategic importance, donor capacity, or relationship stage. Guides staff time allocation and follow-up urgency.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code capturing the alumnus response or reaction to the engagement activity. Used for tracking sentiment and engagement quality across interactions.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this engagement activity record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with source systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this engagement activity record originated. Examples include Blackbaud Raisers Edge, Slate CRM, or manual entry systems.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject line or title summarizing the purpose or topic of the engagement activity. Provides quick context for the interaction.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this engagement activity record. Audit field for accountability and data quality tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every meaningful interaction between an alumnus and the institution. Captures activity type (event attendance, volunteer service, mentorship session, career fair participation, webinar, campus visit, phone call, email response, social media interaction), activity date, channel, associated program or campaign, staff member who recorded the interaction, engagement score contribution, and notes. This is the primary engagement history ledger for alumni relations and advancement qualification.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_event {
  alumni_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni event record. Primary key.']
  affinity_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.affinity_group. Business justification: alumni_event has a STRING attribute \'affinity_group\' that should be normalized to a FK relationship. An alumni event is typically organized by or for a specific affinity group/chapter. This creates pr']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Alumni events are often hosted by academic units/cost centers for budget and expense tracking. Creating alumni_event.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for event cost allocation.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Alumni events occur in specific fiscal periods for budget and expense tracking. Creating alumni_event.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Final count of attendees who participated in the event. Populated after event completion for ROI (Return on Investment) analysis and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and event planning timeline analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports international events with local pricing.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Format of event delivery. In-person requires physical venue; virtual is online-only; hybrid offers both options simultaneously.']
  early_registration_deadline date [note: 'Deadline for early-bird registration pricing. Used to incentivize advance commitments and improve planning accuracy.']
  early_registration_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Discounted registration cost for attendees who register before the early deadline, in US dollars.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the event, used in communications and registration systems.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the event, including agenda highlights, speakers, activities, and value proposition. Used in marketing materials and registration pages.']
  event_end_date date [note: 'The date the event concludes. For single-day events, this equals the start date. For multi-day events (e.g., reunion weekend), this is the final day.']
  event_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the event programming concludes, including time zone information.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the alumni event as displayed in marketing materials and communications.']
  event_start_date date [note: 'The date the event begins. For single-day events, this is the event date. For multi-day events, this is the first day.']
  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the event programming begins, including time zone information for accurate scheduling across regions.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event. Controls visibility, registration availability, and reporting inclusion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|planning|registration_open|registration_closed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|postponed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alumni event by its primary purpose and format. Determines programming, budget allocation, and success metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homecoming|reunion|regional_chapter|career_networking|commencement|donor_recognition|athletic_event|volunteer_service|lecture_series|gala|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fundraising_goal_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target dollar amount to be raised through the event, if applicable. Null for non-fundraising events.']
  guest_registration_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost for non-alumni guests (spouses, partners, friends) to attend the event, in US dollars.']
  is_fundraising_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event has a fundraising component or goal (e.g., gala, donor recognition dinner). True triggers gift tracking and campaign attribution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record. Tracks changes throughout the event lifecycle.']
  organizing_staff_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary event organizer. Used for attendee inquiries and internal coordination.']
  organizing_staff_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary advancement staff member responsible for planning and executing the event.']
  organizing_staff_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary event organizer for urgent inquiries and day-of-event coordination.']
  registration_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the event can accommodate, based on venue capacity or virtual platform limits. Null indicates unlimited capacity.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'Date when registration closes. After this date, no new registrations are accepted unless manually overridden.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'Date when registration becomes available to alumni. Controls visibility in event portals and communications.']
  requires_rsvp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendees must formally register or RSVP to attend. True for capacity-limited or catered events; false for open-access events.']
  reunion_class_year varchar [note: 'Graduation year for reunion events (e.g., 1995, 2010). Null for non-reunion events. Used to calculate milestone reunions (5th, 10th, 25th, 50th).']
  standard_registration_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base cost for an individual to attend the event, in US dollars. Zero indicates a free event.']
  target_attendance varchar [note: 'Planned or goal number of attendees for the event. Used for budgeting, venue selection, and success measurement.']
  venue_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the event venue. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  venue_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information for the venue (suite, floor, building name). Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  venue_city varchar [note: 'City where the event venue is located. Used for regional event analysis and alumni engagement mapping.']
  venue_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the venue location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international alumni engagement.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical location or facility hosting the event (e.g., Alumni Center, Grand Ballroom, Stadium Club). Null for virtual-only events.']
  venue_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the venue. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  venue_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the venue location (e.g., CA, NY, ON).']
  virtual_meeting_url varchar [note: 'Web link for accessing the virtual event. Confidential to prevent unauthorized access. Null for in-person-only events.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the online platform used for virtual or hybrid events (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Hopin). Null for in-person-only events.']

  Note: 'Master record for alumni-focused events organized or co-sponsored by the institution. Captures event name, type (homecoming, reunion, regional chapter event, career networking, commencement, donor recognition, athletic event), event date(s), location (venue name, city, state, country), virtual/hybrid flag, registration capacity, registration open/close dates, event cost, associated affinity group or college, and organizing staff. Distinct from the instruction domain\'s class schedule — this covers alumni relations programming.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_event_registration {
  alumni_event_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni event registration record. Primary key for this entity.']
  alumni_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alumni event for which the registration is made. Links to the alumni event master record.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alumnus registering for the event. Links to the alumni master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who entered the registration on behalf of the alumnus. Null if self-registered by the alumnus.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Alumni event registrations occur in specific fiscal periods for event revenue and attendance tracking. Creating alumni_event_registration.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-b']
  accessibility_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any accessibility accommodations needed by the alumnus (e.g., wheelchair access, sign language interpreter, hearing assistance). Ensures ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) compliance.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the registration was cancelled by the alumnus or staff. Null if the registration was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or categorized reason for registration cancellation (e.g., schedule conflict, illness, duplicate registration). Used for event planning improvement.']
  check_in_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel used to check in the alumnus at the event (e.g., mobile app, QR code scan, manual staff entry).']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the alumnus checked in at the event venue. Null if the alumnus did not attend. Used for attendance verification and engagement scoring.']
  confirmation_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a registration confirmation email was successfully sent to the alumnus.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this registration record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dietary_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any dietary restrictions, allergies, or preferences specified by the alumnus (e.g., vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, nut allergy). Used for catering planning.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score representing the level of engagement demonstrated by this registration (e.g., based on early registration, attendance, participation). Used for alumni relationship scoring and predictive analytics.']
  guest_count varchar [note: 'Number of additional guests accompanying the alumnus to the event. Used for capacity planning and catering.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this registration record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  name_badge_printed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a name badge has been printed for the alumnus for this event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal staff notes or comments about the registration, not visible to the alumnus. Used for operational coordination and special handling instructions.']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount paid or due for the registration, including all tickets and guests. Expressed in the institutions base currency (USD).']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when payment was received or processed for the registration.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for payment (e.g., credit card, bank transfer, check, cash, complimentary waiver). Distinct from payment channel.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment for the registration. Tracks whether payment has been received, is outstanding, or has been refunded.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'How the alumnus learned about the event (e.g., email invitation, social media, word of mouth, website). Used for marketing effectiveness analysis.']
  registration_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign code or tracking identifier associated with the registration source. Enables ROI (Return on Investment) analysis of promotional efforts.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus registered for the event. Represents the business event date of registration submission.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique registration confirmation number provided to the alumnus upon successful registration.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the registration was submitted. Enables analysis of registration channel effectiveness.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the registration. Tracks progression from initial registration through event attendance or cancellation.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the registration was submitted, including time zone information. Used for audit and sequence analysis.']
  reminder_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-event reminder email was successfully sent to the alumnus.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Additional notes or special requests submitted by the alumnus during registration (e.g., seating preferences, program participation).']
  table_assignment varchar [note: 'Assigned table number or seating location for the alumnus at the event. Used for seated events with assigned seating.']
  ticket_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of ticket selected by the alumnus (e.g., general admission, VIP, early bird, complimentary, student rate). Determines pricing and access level.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus is designated as a VIP (Very Important Person) for this event, requiring special handling or recognition.']
  waitlist_added_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus was added to the event waitlist. Null if never waitlisted.']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Numeric position of the alumnus on the event waitlist if registration status is waitlisted. Null if not on waitlist.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each alumnus\'s registration for an alumni event. Includes registration date, registration status (registered, waitlisted, cancelled, attended, no-show), ticket type, payment amount, payment status, dietary or accessibility requirements, guest count, check-in timestamp, and registration source (online portal, staff entry, mobile app). Enables attendance tracking, event ROI analysis, and engagement scoring.'
}

Table advancement.volunteer_role {
  volunteer_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volunteer role. Primary key for the volunteer role catalog.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volunteer role. Active roles are available for volunteer assignment; inactive roles are not currently recruiting.']
  application_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volunteers must submit a formal application to be considered for this role. False indicates open enrollment or staff assignment.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volunteer assignments to this role require formal approval by advancement staff or leadership. True for high-visibility or sensitive roles.']
  associated_program varchar [note: 'The specific volunteer program or initiative this role supports (e.g., Alumni Admissions Network, Career Connections Program, Annual Fund Leadership Council).']
  background_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volunteers in this role must complete a background check before service. True for roles involving minors or sensitive institutional access.']
  benefits_description varchar [note: 'Description of benefits, recognition, or perks provided to volunteers in this role (e.g., Invitation to annual volunteer recognition dinner, Professional development opportunities, Networking access).']
  capacity_target varchar [note: 'Target number of volunteers needed to fill this role across the institution. Used for recruitment planning and capacity management.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role definition was retired or superseded. Null indicates the role is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role definition became active and available for volunteer assignment. Used for historical tracking of role evolution.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic limitations for volunteer service in this role (e.g., Local area only, Regional chapters, No restriction). Used for volunteer matching and assignment.']
  impact_statement varchar [note: 'Statement describing the institutional impact and value of this volunteer role. Used in recruitment materials to inspire volunteer engagement.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when this volunteer role definition was last reviewed by advancement staff for accuracy and relevance. Used to ensure role catalog currency.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this volunteer role definition. Used for proactive role catalog maintenance.']
  owning_office varchar [note: 'The institutional office or department responsible for managing this volunteer role (e.g., Office of Alumni Relations, Office of Admissions, Career Services, Annual Giving).']
  preferred_qualifications varchar [note: 'Desired but not mandatory qualifications that would enhance volunteer effectiveness in this role (e.g., Leadership experience, Public speaking skills, Industry expertise).']
