  remote_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this volunteer role can be performed remotely or requires on-campus presence. True enables broader geographic volunteer participation.']
  reporting_relationship varchar [note: 'Description of the staff member or committee to whom volunteers in this role report (e.g., Director of Alumni Relations, Regional Volunteer Coordinator, Annual Fund Manager).']
  required_qualifications varchar [note: 'Mandatory qualifications, credentials, or prerequisites for volunteers to serve in this role (e.g., Must be alumnus/alumna, Minimum 5 years professional experience, Background check required).']
  role_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the volunteer role by functional area. Determines the type of engagement and reporting hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: admissions_ambassador|career_mentor|class_agent|reunion_committee|advisory_board|scholarship_interviewer|phonathon_caller|event_volunteer|student_mentor|fundraising_volunteer|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used for internal reference and reporting (e.g., ADM-AMB, CAR-MEN, CLS-AGT).']
  role_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the volunteer role including responsibilities, expectations, and impact. Used for volunteer recruitment and orientation materials.']
  role_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the volunteer role as presented to alumni and volunteers (e.g., Admissions Ambassador, Career Mentor, Class Agent, Reunion Committee Member).']
  role_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the role category (e.g., within Career Mentor: Industry Mentor, Mock Interview Coach, Resume Reviewer).']
  term_length_months varchar [note: 'Standard length of service term for this volunteer role in months (e.g., 12 for one-year term, 36 for three-year term). Null indicates ongoing or flexible term.']
  time_commitment_estimate varchar [note: 'Expected time commitment for the volunteer role expressed in hours per month, hours per year, or descriptive terms (e.g., 2-4 hours per month, 10 hours annually, Flexible).']
  time_commitment_hours_max decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum estimated hours per year expected for this volunteer role. Used for volunteer matching and capacity planning.']
  time_commitment_hours_min decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum estimated hours per year required for this volunteer role. Used for volunteer matching and capacity planning.']
  training_description varchar [note: 'Description of required training programs, modules, or orientation sessions volunteers must complete (e.g., FERPA training, Admissions interview protocol, Fundraising ethics).']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether volunteers must complete formal training before beginning service in this role. True for roles requiring specific knowledge or compliance training.']
  visibility_status varchar [note: 'Controls who can view and apply for this volunteer role in self-service portals. Public roles appear on public website; invitation-only roles require staff nomination.']

  Note: 'Defines the catalog of volunteer roles available to alumni within institutional programs. Captures role name, role category (admissions ambassador, career mentor, class agent, reunion committee, advisory board member, scholarship interviewer, phonathon caller), associated program or office, time commitment estimate, required qualifications, active status, and role description. Serves as the reference for volunteer assignment and tracking.'
}

Table advancement.volunteer_assignment {
  volunteer_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volunteer assignment record. Primary key for the volunteer assignment entity.']
  ada_accommodation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ada_accommodation. Business justification: Alumni volunteers serving in official institutional capacities (admissions interviewers, mentors, board members) may request ADA accommodations for participation. Required for compliance with ADA Titl']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who is assigned to this volunteer role. Links to the alumni master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Volunteer assignments often support academic units/cost centers. Creating volunteer_assignment.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for program cost allocation and reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advancement staff member who coordinates and manages this volunteer assignment. Used for accountability and communication.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Volunteer assignments are tracked by fiscal period for program reporting and cost allocation. Creating volunteer_assignment.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.principal_investigator. Business justification: Faculty PIs volunteer for advancement activities (advisory boards, fundraising events, donor cultivation). Advancement tracks faculty engagement for recognition, stewardship, and volunteer management.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.service_request. Business justification: Volunteers requiring IT support (VPN access for remote mentoring, application training, equipment loans for events) generate service requests that link to volunteer context for prioritization, cost tr']
  volunteer_role_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the volunteer role catalog entry that defines the role type, requirements, and program association.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer assignment officially ends or is scheduled to end. Nullable for open-ended assignments.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes about the volunteer assignment, including special arrangements, preferences, or contextual information.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the volunteer assignment, formatted as VA- followed by 8 digits. Used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer assignment officially begins. Used for tenure calculation and capacity planning.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volunteer assignment. Active indicates the volunteer is currently serving; completed indicates the assignment has ended successfully; on-hold indicates temporary suspension; cancelled indicates early termination; pending indicates awaiting start.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'The date when the background check was completed or cleared. Nullable if not yet completed or not required.']
  background_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check is required for this volunteer assignment (e.g., for roles involving minors or sensitive data). True if required; false otherwise.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the background check for this volunteer assignment. Not-required if no check is needed; pending if in progress; cleared if approved; flagged if issues identified.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this volunteer assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated engagement score for this volunteer assignment based on hours served, performance, and impact. Used for advancement qualification and major gift prospect identification.']
  hours_served decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of volunteer hours served by the alumnus in this assignment. Used for recognition programs and engagement scoring.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this volunteer assignment record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  performance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the volunteer\'s performance, contributions, challenges, and feedback. Used for stewardship and future assignment planning.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance assessment of the volunteer in this assignment. Used for future assignment decisions and recognition.']
  recognition_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal recognition (award, certificate, event invitation) has been awarded for this volunteer assignment. True if awarded; false otherwise.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date when formal recognition was awarded to the volunteer for this assignment. Nullable if not yet awarded.']
  recognition_level varchar [note: 'Recognition tier awarded to the volunteer based on hours served, impact, and tenure. Used for stewardship and awards programs.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer assignment was renewed or is scheduled for renewal review. Nullable if not applicable.']
  renewal_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the volunteer is eligible for renewal of this assignment upon completion. True if eligible; false otherwise.']
  termination_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the circumstances and details of the assignment termination. Used for stewardship and future engagement planning.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the volunteer assignment ended or was terminated. Used for retention analysis and program improvement.']
  time_commitment_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated or expected number of hours per month or per assignment period that the volunteer role requires. Used for capacity planning and volunteer recruitment.']
  training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the volunteer has completed required training for this assignment. True if training is complete; false otherwise.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer completed required training for this assignment. Nullable if training is not yet complete.']

  Note: 'Manages the full alumni volunteer lifecycle including the catalog of available volunteer roles and individual assignment records. Role catalog captures: role name, category (admissions ambassador, career mentor, class agent, reunion committee, advisory board member, scholarship interviewer, phonathon caller), associated program or office, time commitment estimate, required qualifications, and active status. Assignment records capture: alumnus reference, assigned role, assignment start/end dates, hours served, assignment status (active, completed, on-hold), performance notes, and recognition level (bronze, silver, gold, lifetime). Supports volunteer recruitment, capacity planning, recognition programs, and engagement scoring for advancement qualification.'
}

Table advancement.career_record {
  career_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni career record. Primary key for the career history tracking system.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic program from which the alumni graduated. Links career outcomes to specific degree programs for program-level ROI analysis and accreditation reporting.']
  accreditation_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_review. Business justification: Career outcomes data (employment rates, salary ranges, job placement timelines) are REQUIRED accreditation evidence for ABET, ACBSP, CACREP, and other specialized accreditors. Direct link needed for d']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumni individual whose career is being tracked. Links to the alumni master record in the advancement domain.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instruction.course_section. Business justification: Accreditation bodies (ABET, regional accreditors) require employment outcome reporting by specific program and course sequences. Linking career records to course sections enables program-level effecti']
  survey_response_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumni survey response that provided this career information. Links career data to broader alumni engagement and satisfaction surveys for integrated analysis.']
  career_field_alignment varchar [note: 'Assessment of how closely the position aligns with the alumni\'s degree program. Used for program effectiveness evaluation and accreditation reporting to AACSB, ABET, and other specialized accreditors.']
  career_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context about the career position, including special achievements, awards, promotions, or unique circumstances. Supports personalized alumni engagement and success story identification.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code of the degree program associated with this career record. Enables standardized career outcomes reporting by academic discipline for IPEDS and accreditation.']
  company_website varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the employer organization. Supports employer research, corporate partnership development, and validation of employer information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this career record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage tracking and audit trail requirements under FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act).']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Specific method used to collect the career information. Distinguishes between online forms, phone interviews, email surveys, LinkedIn API integration, manual data entry by staff, or batch imports. Used for data quality assessment and process improvement.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the career information. Indicates whether data was self-reported by alumni, imported from LinkedIn, collected via survey, provided by career services, verified by employer, or obtained from third-party sources. Critical for data quality assessment.']
  employer_industry_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code classifying the employer\'s primary industry sector. Used for career field analysis and accreditation reporting to AACSB and ABET.']
  employer_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or commonly recognized name of the organization employing the alumni. Supports career outcomes reporting and employer relationship tracking.']
  employer_size_category varchar [note: 'Classification of employer organization size. Startup (1-50 employees), Small (51-500), Medium (501-5000), Large (5001-50000), Enterprise (50000+). Supports employer relationship strategy and career outcomes diversity analysis.']
  employment_city varchar [note: 'City where the alumni is employed. Supports geographic distribution analysis and regional alumni engagement strategies.']
  employment_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the alumni is employed. Supports international alumni tracking and global career outcomes reporting.']
  employment_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the alumni is employed. Follows USPS state abbreviations for US locations and ISO 3166-2 for international.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement. Distinguishes between full-time, part-time, self-employed, contract, academic positions, and internships for FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) calculations and outcomes reporting.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the alumni ended employment in this position. Null for current positions. Supports career mobility and retention analysis.']
  is_current_position boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the alumni\'s current employment position. True for active employment, false for historical positions.']
  is_featured_success_story boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this career record has been selected as a featured alumni success story for marketing, recruitment, or advancement communications. Supports storytelling and donor engagement initiatives.']
  is_self_employed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the alumni is self-employed or operates their own business. Supports entrepreneurship tracking and specialized alumni engagement programs for business owners.']
  is_stem_position boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the position is classified as a STEM career. Used for STEM program outcomes reporting, federal grant compliance (NSF, NIH), and specialized accreditation (ABET).']
  job_function_category varchar [note: 'Broad functional area of the position such as Engineering, Marketing, Finance, Operations, Research, Education, Healthcare, Legal, or Technology. Supports career pathway analysis and alumni networking by professional interest.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official position title held by the alumni at the employer organization. Captures role level and functional area for career progression analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this career record was most recently updated. Tracks data currency and supports change detection for downstream analytics and reporting systems.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the career information was last verified or confirmed as accurate. Used to assess data freshness and prioritize outreach for data updates.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'URL to the alumni\'s LinkedIn profile. Enables automated career data updates via LinkedIn API integration and supports alumni professional networking initiatives.']
  position_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of this position in the alumni\'s career history. First position after graduation is 1, second is 2, etc. Supports career mobility and progression analysis.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the career record. Active records are current and verified, archived records are historical, pending-verification records await confirmation, disputed records have data quality issues, and deleted records are soft-deleted for audit purposes.']
  remote_work_status varchar [note: 'Classification of work location arrangement. Captures whether position is on-site, hybrid, or fully remote. Relevant for post-pandemic career outcomes analysis and alumni geographic engagement.']
  salary_range_band varchar [note: 'Categorical salary range for the position. Collected in bands to protect privacy while enabling ROI (Return on Investment) and career outcomes analysis for accreditation.']
  seniority_level varchar [note: 'Career level classification of the position. Tracks alumni career progression and leadership attainment for advancement engagement and program effectiveness metrics.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the alumni began employment in this position. Used to calculate time-to-employment metrics and career tenure analysis.']
  supervisor_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the alumni\'s direct supervisor or manager. Provides organizational context and supports alumni networking by identifying potential mentors or senior alumni connections.']
  years_to_position decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated number of years from degree completion to the start of this position. Used for time-to-employment metrics and career progression analysis for accreditation and program evaluation.']

  Note: 'Tracks the professional career history and current employment status of alumni. Captures employer name, employer industry (NAICS code), job title, employment type (full-time, part-time, self-employed, academic), start date, end date, city/state/country of employment, salary range band, career field alignment with degree (related/unrelated), data source (alumni self-reported, LinkedIn import, survey), and last verified date. Supports career outcomes reporting, accreditation data (AACSB, ABET), and alumni career services.'
}

Table advancement.advanced_degree {
  advanced_degree_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advanced degree record. Primary key for this entity.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who earned this advanced degree at an external institution after leaving this institution.']
  cip_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.cip_code. Business justification: Alumni advanced degree tracking for outcomes assessment and prospect research (wealth screening) requires proper CIP classification. Enables analysis of graduate school pathways by field and STEM desi']
  awarding_institution_city varchar [note: 'The city where the awarding institution is located.']
  awarding_institution_country varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the country where the awarding institution is located (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN, AUS).']
  awarding_institution_ipeds_code varchar [note: 'The six-digit IPEDS Unit ID (UNITID) that uniquely identifies the awarding institution in the federal postsecondary education database.']
  awarding_institution_name varchar [note: 'The full official name of the external institution that conferred this advanced degree to the alumnus.']
  awarding_institution_state varchar [note: 'The state, province, or region where the awarding institution is located (applicable for USA, Canada, and other federated countries).']
  awarding_institution_type varchar [note: 'The institutional control or sector classification of the awarding institution (public, private nonprofit, private for-profit, or foreign).']
  career_relevance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this advanced degree is directly relevant to the alumnus current or intended career path, used for career outcomes analysis.']
  concentration_specialization varchar [note: 'The specific concentration, specialization, track, or focus area within the broader field of study (e.g., Finance concentration within MBA, Artificial Intelligence specialization within Computer Science PhD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this advanced degree record was first created in the system (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source from which this advanced degree information was obtained (e.g., alumni survey, LinkedIn, National Student Clearinghouse, prospect research, direct alumnus report, institutional partnership).']
  degree_level varchar [note: 'The level of the advanced degree earned (masters, doctoral, professional such as JD/MD/MBA, post-doctoral, graduate certificate, or specialist degree).']
  degree_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this advanced degree record, used for tracking and reference in advancement systems.']
  degree_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the advanced degree record (completed and verified, in progress, withdrawn, deferred, verified by institution, or unverified self-reported).']
  degree_title varchar [note: 'The full official title of the degree as conferred by the awarding institution (e.g., Master of Business Administration, Doctor of Philosophy in Computer Science).']
  degree_type varchar [note: 'The specific type or abbreviation of the degree earned (e.g., MBA, PhD, JD, MD, MS, MA, EdD, DBA, MFA, MPH).']
  enrollment_start_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the alumnus began enrollment in the advanced degree program at the awarding institution.']
  field_of_study varchar [note: 'The primary academic discipline or field of study for the advanced degree (e.g., Computer Science, Business Administration, Medicine, Law, Engineering).']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'The primary source of funding for the advanced degree program (e.g., self-funded, employer-sponsored, fellowship, assistantship, scholarship, research grant).']
  graduation_date date [note: 'The specific date on which the advanced degree was officially conferred (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  graduation_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the alumnus graduated and was conferred the advanced degree.']
  honors_distinction varchar [note: 'Any honors, distinctions, or special recognitions received with the advanced degree (e.g., summa cum laude, magna cum laude, with distinction, departmental honors).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this advanced degree record was most recently updated or modified (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  major_advisor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary faculty advisor, dissertation chair, or thesis supervisor who guided the alumnus through the advanced degree program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or additional context about the advanced degree, including any special circumstances, clarifications, or supplementary information.']
  prospect_research_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this advanced degree record was obtained through prospect research activities for fundraising purposes.']
  record_created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this advanced degree record.']
  record_created_date date [note: 'The date on which this advanced degree record was first created in the system (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  record_last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that most recently modified this advanced degree record.']
  record_last_modified_date date [note: 'The date on which this advanced degree record was most recently updated or modified (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  thesis_dissertation_title varchar [note: 'The title of the thesis, dissertation, or capstone project completed as part of the advanced degree requirements (applicable primarily to masters and doctoral degrees).']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the advanced degree information was officially verified through external documentation or institutional confirmation (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the advanced degree information (e.g., official transcript, National Student Clearinghouse, institutional registrar confirmation, LinkedIn profile, alumni survey).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the advanced degree information has been verified through official transcripts or institutional confirmation, or is self-reported by the alumnus.']

  Note: 'Records post-baccalaureate and advanced degrees earned by alumni at other institutions after leaving this institution. Captures degree level (master\'s, doctoral, professional — JD, MD, MBA), degree type, awarding institution name, field of study, CIP code, graduation year, and data source. Distinct from the curriculum domain\'s degree records (which track degrees earned HERE) — this captures external educational attainment for alumni outcomes reporting and prospect research.'
}

Table advancement.mentorship_program {
  mentorship_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mentorship program. Primary key for the mentorship program entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Mentorship programs are often run by academic units/cost centers. Creating mentorship_program.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for program budget and expense tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who serves as the primary coordinator and point of contact for the mentorship program. Responsible for program administration, participant support, and outcome tracking.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Mentorship programs run in specific fiscal periods for budget and program tracking. Creating mentorship_program.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic college, school, or administrative unit that sponsors and oversees this mentorship program. May be a college of business, engineering, arts and sciences, or a central alumni relations office.']
  owning_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Mentorship programs are institutional engagement initiatives requiring org hierarchy linkage for budget ownership, program performance reporting by college/department, resource allocation, and alumni ']
  application_close_date date [note: 'The date when applications for the mentorship program close for the current cycle. After this date, no new applications are accepted until the next cycle.']
  application_open_date date [note: 'The date when applications for the mentorship program open for the current cycle. Used to manage enrollment windows and communications.']
  background_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mentors must pass a background check before participating in the program. Typically required for programs involving current students or minors. True if required, false if not required.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total budget allocated to operate the mentorship program for the current fiscal year, including staff time, marketing, events, technology platform costs, and participant support. Amount in institutional base currency.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for inquiries about the mentorship program. Typically monitored by the program coordinator or advancement office staff.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for inquiries about the mentorship program. Organizational contact information for the program office.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this mentorship program record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Requirements that alumni or students must meet to participate in the program as mentors or mentees, such as graduation year, degree level, professional experience, geographic location, or membership status.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Description of how the mentorship program is funded, such as institutional operating budget, endowment income, donor-restricted gifts, participant fees, or corporate sponsorship.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this mentorship program record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  matching_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the criteria used to match mentors and mentees, such as industry sector, career stage, geographic location, academic major, demographic factors, professional interests, and personal goals.']
  matching_methodology varchar [note: 'The method used to pair mentors with mentees. Staff-matched programs have advancement staff manually review profiles and create matches. Self-selected programs allow participants to browse and choose their own matches. Algorithm-matched programs use automated matching based on criteria such as industry, location, interests, and goals. Hybrid programs combine multiple approaches.']
  maximum_mentee_capacity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of mentees that can be enrolled in the program during a single cycle. Used for capacity planning and application management.']
  maximum_mentor_capacity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of mentors that can be enrolled in the program during a single cycle. Used for capacity planning and recruitment management.']
  orientation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants must complete an orientation session before beginning their mentorship relationship. True if orientation is mandatory, false if optional or not offered.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify the program in operational systems and reporting. Typically 4-12 characters.']
  program_cycle varchar [note: 'The operational cycle or term structure for the mentorship program. Academic year programs run September through May. Semester programs align with fall or spring terms. Rolling programs accept applications and match participants continuously throughout the year.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the mentorship programs purpose, goals, structure, and benefits. Used in marketing materials, program catalogs, and participant communications.']
  program_duration_months varchar [note: 'The standard length of a mentorship relationship in this program, measured in months. Common durations are 6, 9, or 12 months.']
  program_end_date date [note: 'The date when the mentorship program was permanently closed or archived, if applicable. Null for active or ongoing programs.']
  program_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged to participants to enroll in the mentorship program, if applicable. Zero or null if the program is offered at no cost. Amount in institutional base currency.']
  program_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the program fee amount. Typically USD for U.S. institutions.']
  program_launch_date date [note: 'The date when the mentorship program was first launched and began accepting participants. Used for historical tracking and program maturity analysis.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the mentorship program as it appears in institutional communications and marketing materials.']
  program_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special considerations, historical context, or operational notes about the mentorship program that do not fit in other structured fields.']
  program_objectives varchar [note: 'Specific learning and development objectives that the program aims to achieve for participants, such as career advancement, skill development, network expansion, or leadership growth.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the mentorship program. Active programs are currently accepting applications and matching participants. Inactive programs are not currently running but may resume. Suspended programs are temporarily paused. Planning programs are in development. Archived programs are permanently closed.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mentorship program by its primary focus area. Career mentorship supports professional development, academic mentorship supports scholarly pursuits, entrepreneurship supports startup and business ventures, diversity and inclusion supports underrepresented populations, peer mentorship connects alumni of similar experience levels, and executive mentorship connects senior leaders with emerging leaders.']
  program_website_url varchar [note: 'The web address where prospective participants can learn more about the program, view requirements, and submit applications.']
  scholarship_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial assistance or fee waivers are available for participants who cannot afford the program fee. True if scholarships are offered, false if not.']
  success_metrics varchar [note: 'Key performance indicators (KPIs) used to measure program effectiveness, such as participant satisfaction scores, match completion rates, career advancement outcomes, or engagement levels.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended participant population for the program, such as recent graduates, mid-career professionals, executive leaders, students from specific majors, or alumni from underrepresented groups.']
  time_commitment_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of hours per month or per cycle that participants should commit to the mentorship relationship. Used to set expectations and ensure program quality.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mentors must complete formal training before being matched with mentees. True if training is mandatory, false if optional or not offered.']
  virtual_participation_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mentorship relationships can be conducted entirely remotely via video conferencing, phone, and email. True if virtual participation is permitted, false if in-person meetings are required.']

  Note: 'Defines the institution\'s formal alumni mentorship programs. Captures program name, program type (career mentorship, academic mentorship, entrepreneurship, diversity and inclusion), sponsoring college or office, program cycle (academic year, semester, rolling), maximum mentee capacity, application open/close dates, matching methodology (staff-matched, self-selected, algorithm-matched), active status, and program coordinator. Serves as the catalog for mentorship enrollment and matching.'
}

Table advancement.mentorship_match {
  mentorship_match_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mentorship match record. Primary key for the mentorship match entity.']
  ada_accommodation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ada_accommodation. Business justification: Student mentees may have ADA accommodations that must be communicated to alumni mentors (communication format preferences, meeting accessibility needs). Required for program compliance and to ensure e']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instruction.course_section. Business justification: Many institutions integrate alumni mentorship into credit-bearing courses (capstone, internship, practicum courses). Linking matches to course sections enables academic credit tracking, experiential l']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or volunteer coordinator responsible for overseeing this mentorship match.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Mentorship programs operated through dedicated platforms (PeopleGrove, Graduway, Mentor Collective) require system attribution for usage analytics, matching algorithm performance, ROI measurement, and']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Mentorship matches are tracked by fiscal period for program reporting and outcomes assessment. Creating mentorship_match.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  mentor_alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus serving as the mentor in this match. Links to the alumnus master record.']
  mentor_principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.principal_investigator. Business justification: Faculty PIs serve as mentors in advancement programs (career mentoring, research mentoring). Advancement tracks faculty engagement for recognition, volunteer management, and demonstrating institutiona']
  mentorship_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal mentorship program under which this match was created.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mentee in this match. May be a current student or fellow alumnus depending on program type.']
  actual_duration_months varchar [note: 'The actual duration of the mentorship relationship in months, calculated from start to end date.']
  agreement_signed_date date [note: 'Date when the mentorship agreement was signed by both parties.']
  agreement_signed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether both mentor and mentee signed the mentorship agreement or commitment form.']
  communication_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed-upon frequency of communication between mentor and mentee.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mentorship match record was first created in the system.']
  expected_duration_months varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the mentorship relationship in months as defined by the program structure.']
  focus_area varchar [note: 'Primary focus area or theme of the mentorship relationship as defined at match creation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: career_development|academic_guidance|networking|industry_transition|leadership|entrepreneurship|work_life_balance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_alignment varchar [note: 'The industry or professional field that this mentorship match is focused on (e.g., Technology, Healthcare, Finance).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mentorship match record was last updated in the system.']
  match_date date [note: 'The date when the mentor and mentee were formally paired and the mentorship relationship began.']
  match_end_date date [note: 'The date when the mentorship relationship concluded, either through successful completion or early termination.']
  match_method varchar [note: 'The method used to create this match: algorithm-based matching, manual assignment by coordinator, self-selection by participants, or hybrid approach.']
  match_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the mentorship match, including coordinator observations, special circumstances, or program-specific details.']
  match_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the mentorship match, used for external reference and communication.']
  match_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Algorithmic or coordinator-assigned score indicating the quality or compatibility of the mentor-mentee pairing, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00.']
  match_start_date date [note: 'The official start date of the mentorship relationship, which may differ from match_date if there was a delay between pairing and first interaction.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mentorship match. Active indicates ongoing relationship; completed indicates successful conclusion; withdrawn indicates early termination; paused indicates temporary suspension.']
  mentee_goals_achieved varchar [note: 'Number of mentee-defined goals that were successfully achieved during the mentorship relationship.']
  mentee_goals_total varchar [note: 'Total number of goals defined by the mentee at the start of the mentorship relationship.']
  mentee_satisfaction_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Mentee\'s satisfaction rating for the mentorship experience, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00.']
  mentee_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the mentee is a current student or a fellow alumnus participating in peer mentorship.']
  mentor_satisfaction_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Mentor\'s satisfaction rating for the mentorship experience, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00.']
  orientation_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether both mentor and mentee completed the required program orientation before beginning their relationship.']
  orientation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when both participants completed the mentorship program orientation.']
  primary_communication_mode varchar [note: 'The primary method of communication used by the mentor and mentee for their interactions.']
  program_cycle varchar [note: 'The cohort or cycle designation for this match (e.g., Fall 2023, Spring 2024, FY2024-Q1).']
  recognition_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mentor received formal recognition or awards for their participation in this match.']
  recognition_type varchar [note: 'Type of recognition awarded to the mentor (e.g., certificate, award, public acknowledgment, points).']
  renewal_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this mentorship match is eligible for renewal or extension into another program cycle.']
  renewed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this mentorship match was renewed or extended beyond the original program cycle.']
  sessions_completed varchar [note: 'Total count of documented mentorship sessions or interactions completed between mentor and mentee.']
  sessions_expected varchar [note: 'The target number of mentorship sessions defined by the program requirements for this match.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination or withdrawal from the mentorship relationship if applicable (e.g., scheduling conflicts, relocation, goal mismatch).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a formal pairing between an alumni mentor and a student or fellow alumnus mentee within a mentorship program. Captures match date, match method, mentor alumnus reference, mentee reference (student or alumnus), program cycle, match status (active, completed, withdrawn, paused), number of sessions completed, mentee satisfaction rating, mentor satisfaction rating, and match end date. Enables program effectiveness measurement and mentor recognition.'
}

  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instruction.course_section. Business justification: Alumni lifelong learning enrollments are actual course registrations requiring integration with instruction systems for scheduling, grading, transcript recording, and revenue attribution. Essential fo']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Continuing education courses delivered via LMS platforms (Canvas, Blackboard, Moodle) or specialized systems (Destiny One, CourseStorm) require system linkage for enrollment synchronization, completio']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Lifelong learning enrollments occur in specific fiscal periods for revenue and program tracking. Creating lifelong_learning_enrollment.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-base']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Lifelong learning enrollments generate billing accounts for tuition and fees. Essential for alumni program billing integration, revenue recognition, and financial aid processing for continuing educati']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Continuing education and certificate programs are often scheduled within institutional term structures for administrative consistency, resource planning, and financial aid eligibility determination. E']
  tuition_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tuition_charge. Business justification: Direct link from lifelong learning enrollment to specific tuition charge enables precise revenue tracking, refund processing, and financial reporting for alumni continuing education programs. Critical']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the alumnus completed all requirements for the lifelong learning program. Null if the program has not been completed.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the certificate of completion was issued to the alumnus. Null if no certificate has been issued.']
  certificate_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certificate of completion or achievement was issued to the alumnus upon successful completion of the program.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or serial number assigned to the certificate issued for this enrollment. Used for verification and authentication purposes.']
  ceu_credits_earned decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of Continuing Education Unit credits earned by the alumnus upon successful completion of the program. CEUs are a standard measure of non-credit professional development.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Indicator of whether the alumnus successfully completed the lifelong learning program and met all requirements for certification or recognition.']
  contact_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of instructional contact hours associated with the lifelong learning program. Used for professional licensing and certification requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  delivery_modality varchar [note: 'Method by which the lifelong learning program is delivered. Indicates whether the program is conducted in-person, fully online, or in a hybrid format combining both.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative measure of the alumnus\'s engagement with the lifelong learning program, based on participation metrics such as login frequency, content completion, and interaction with materials.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the alumnus enrolled in the lifelong learning program. Represents the official start of the enrollment relationship.']
  enrollment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the enrollment. May include special accommodations, administrative notes, or other relevant information.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the lifelong learning enrollment. Externally visible enrollment reference number used in communications and reporting.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment. Tracks whether the alumnus is actively enrolled, has completed the program, withdrawn, or is in another state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrolled|in_progress|completed|withdrawn|incomplete|deferred|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expected_completion_date date [note: 'Anticipated date by which the alumnus is expected to complete the lifelong learning program based on the program schedule and duration.']
  grade_earned varchar [note: 'Final grade or assessment result earned by the alumnus in the lifelong learning program. May be a letter grade, pass/fail, or other institutional grading scale.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary instructor or facilitator for the lifelong learning program. May be null for self-paced or asynchronous programs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  oer_based_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lifelong learning program is based on or incorporates Open Educational Resources. Supports tracking of OER adoption and alumni engagement with open content.']
  offering_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lifelong learning offering type. Distinguishes between MOOCs (Massive Open Online Courses), certificate programs, executive education, alumni audit courses, online modules, workshops, and other professional development formats. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mooc|certificate_program|executive_education|alumni_audit|online_module|workshop|professional_development|continuing_education — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  owning_college varchar [note: 'Name of the college, school, or academic division that owns and administers the lifelong learning program (e.g., College of Business, School of Engineering, Division of Continuing Education).']
  owning_department varchar [note: 'Name of the department or unit within the college that is responsible for the lifelong learning program.']
  pass_fail_indicator varchar [note: 'Simple pass/fail indicator for programs that use binary assessment rather than letter grades.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which tuition payment was received or processed for the lifelong learning enrollment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of tuition payment for the lifelong learning enrollment. Indicates whether fees have been paid, waived, or are outstanding.']
  platform_name varchar [note: 'Name of the learning management system or platform used to deliver the lifelong learning program (e.g., Canvas, Coursera, edX, institutional LMS).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Institutional code or identifier for the lifelong learning program. Used for internal classification and reporting.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lifelong learning program or course in which the alumnus is enrolled. Examples include certificate program names, executive education course titles, or MOOC names.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the alumnus registered for the lifelong learning program. Used for marketing attribution and channel effectiveness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|phone|email|in_person|referral|event — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scholarship_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of scholarship or financial aid applied to the enrollment tuition. Null if no scholarship was applied.']
  scholarship_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a scholarship, discount, or financial aid was applied to reduce the tuition cost for this enrollment.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the lifelong learning program officially begins instruction or content delivery. May differ from enrollment date if enrollment occurs before program start.']
  tuition_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Tuition fee charged for the lifelong learning program. May be zero for complimentary alumni offerings or OER-based programs.']
  tuition_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the tuition amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the alumnus officially withdrew from the lifelong learning program. Null if the alumnus has not withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the alumnus for withdrawing from the lifelong learning program. Used for program improvement and retention analysis.']

  Note: 'Records alumni participation in continuing education, professional development, and lifelong learning offerings provided by the institution. Captures program or course name, offering type (MOOC, certificate program, executive education, alumni audit, online module, workshop), enrollment date, completion date, completion status, CEU credits earned, tuition paid (if any), delivery modality (in-person, online, hybrid), and associated college or school. Supports alumni engagement through education and tracks OER and MOOC participation.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_survey {
  alumni_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni survey campaign. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Alumni outcome surveys are required for accreditation (SACSCOC, ABET, AACSB) and IPEDS reporting, aggregated by program. Replaces denormalized academic_program_code with proper FK for program-level ou']
  accreditation_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_review. Business justification: Alumni outcomes surveys (employment rates, graduate school placement, salary data) are REQUIRED evidence for specialized accreditors (ABET, AACSB, ACEN) and regional standards on student achievement. ']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alumnus who responded to this survey. Links to the alumnus master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Alumni surveys are often administered by academic units/cost centers for accreditation and outcomes assessment. Creating alumni_survey.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for survey cost all']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Survey instruments deployed through specific platforms (Qualtrics, SurveyMonkey, Alchemer) need system tracking for response data integration, accreditation reporting workflows, and platform cost allo']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Alumni surveys are conducted in specific fiscal periods for outcomes assessment and accreditation reporting. Creating alumni_survey.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based r']
  survey_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent survey campaign under which this individual response was collected.']
  accreditation_association varchar [note: 'The accrediting body for which this survey data will be reported (e.g., AACSB, ABET, HLC, SACSCOC). Determines specific data elements required.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of survey questions completed by this respondent (e.g., 85.50 for 85.5% completion).']
  continuing_education_institution varchar [note: 'Name of the institution where the alumnus is pursuing further education, if applicable.']
  continuing_education_program varchar [note: 'The program or degree the alumnus is pursuing in continuing education.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this survey response record was first created in the system.']
  degree_level varchar [note: 'The level of degree earned by the alumnus at graduation.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the survey was delivered to the alumnus.']
  employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization employing the alumnus at time of response, if employed.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'The employment status of the alumnus at the time of survey response. Critical for NACE First Destination and IPEDS Graduate Outcomes reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: employed_full_time|employed_part_time|self_employed|continuing_education|military_service|volunteer_service|seeking_employment|not_seeking — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  graduation_term varchar [note: 'The academic term in which the alumnus graduated.']
  graduation_year varchar [note: 'The year the alumnus graduated, used for cohort analysis and longitudinal tracking.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'The industry sector of the alumnus employer (e.g., Technology, Healthcare, Finance, Education, Manufacturing).']
  instrument_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey instrument used (e.g., \'v2.1\', \'2024-Spring\'). Ensures consistency when survey questions evolve over time.']
  invitation_sent_date date [note: 'The date the survey invitation was sent to the alumnus.']
  job_location_city varchar [note: 'City where the alumnus is employed.']
  job_location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the alumnus is employed (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  job_location_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the alumnus is employed.']
  job_related_to_degree boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus current employment is related to their degree field. Used for program effectiveness assessment.']
  job_start_date date [note: 'The date the alumnus began their current employment position.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title or position held by the alumnus at time of response, if employed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this survey response record was last updated.']
  last_reminder_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent reminder communication sent to the alumnus.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this survey response, including any data quality issues, follow-up actions, or contextual information.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of reminder communications sent to the alumnus to encourage survey completion.']
  response_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus submitted their survey response.']
  response_rate_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target response rate percentage for the survey campaign (e.g., 65.00 for 65%). Used to assess campaign effectiveness.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'The method by which the response data was obtained. Some institutions supplement direct survey responses with data from other sources.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Status of this individual alumnus response indicating completion level.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the alumnus completed and submitted the survey response.']
  salary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reported salary (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  salary_range varchar [note: 'The salary range or band reported by the alumnus. Often collected in ranges rather than exact amounts to improve response rates.']
  satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Overall satisfaction rating provided by the alumnus regarding their educational experience (typically on a scale such as 1-5 or 1-10).']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the survey campaign (e.g., \'Class of 2023 First Destination Survey\', \'Alumni Career Outcomes Survey 2024\').']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey campaign.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'The category of survey being administered. Determines the question set and reporting requirements.']
  target_population varchar [note: 'Description of the intended respondent population (e.g., \'Class of 2023 Bachelor Degree Recipients\', \'MBA Graduates 2020-2023\', \'Engineering Alumni 5-Year Post-Graduation\').']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the response data was verified by advancement staff.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the response data has been verified for accuracy and completeness, particularly important for accreditation reporting.']
  would_recommend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus would recommend the institution or program to others. Key metric for Net Promoter Score (NPS) calculation.']

  Note: 'Manages alumni survey campaigns and individual response collection. Captures survey definition (name, type, target population, instrument version, delivery channel, response rate target, accreditation association) and individual alumnus responses (response date, completion status, structured answers, employment status at response time). Supports IPEDS graduate outcomes reporting, NACE First Destination Survey, AACSB/ABET accreditation data, and program-level assessment.'
}

Table advancement.survey_response {
  survey_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the survey response record. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Individual survey responses must link to programs for accreditation evidence files and program review. Enables employment rate calculations, salary analysis, and satisfaction metrics by program as req']
  alumni_survey_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the survey instrument that this response belongs to.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who submitted this survey response.']
  course_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instruction.course_section. Business justification: First-destination and alumni outcome surveys must link to specific courses for program-level assessment. Required for IPEDS graduate outcome reporting, specialized accreditation (AACSB, ACEN), and ins']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or coordinator responsible for administering this survey and managing responses.']
  academic_college varchar [note: 'College or school within the institution from which the alumnus graduated.']
  accreditation_reporting_cycle varchar [note: 'Accreditation reporting cycle or year in which this response was used (e.g., 2023-2024, Fall 2023).']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of survey questions answered by the alumnus, calculated as answered questions divided by total questions.']
  continuing_education_institution varchar [note: 'Name of the institution where the alumnus is pursuing continuing education, if applicable.']
  continuing_education_program varchar [note: 'Name or type of the continuing education program the alumnus is enrolled in.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey response record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization employing the alumnus at the time of response.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Employment status of the alumnus at the time of survey response, used for graduate outcomes reporting and accreditation.']
  first_access_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus first opened or accessed the survey.']
  graduation_year varchar [note: 'Year the alumnus graduated from the institution, used for cohort analysis and outcomes tracking.']
  incentive_offered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an incentive (gift card, prize entry, donation) was offered to encourage survey completion.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive offered to the alumnus (e.g., gift card, prize drawing entry, charitable donation).']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'Industry sector or field in which the alumnus is employed, typically aligned with NAICS or similar classification systems.']
  ipeds_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this response meets IPEDS graduate outcomes reporting criteria and was included in federal reporting.']
  job_location_city varchar [note: 'City where the alumnus is employed or working.']
  job_location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code for the job location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  job_location_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the alumnus is employed or working.']
  job_related_to_degree_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus reported that their current job is related to their degree program.']
  job_search_duration_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the alumnus spent searching for employment after graduation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position held by the alumnus at the time of response.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survey response record was last updated in the data warehouse.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of reminder communications sent to the alumnus before the response was received.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or medium through which the alumnus submitted the survey response (web portal, mobile app, email link, phone interview, postal mail, or in-person event).']
  response_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus submitted or last updated the survey response.']
  response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by survey administrators regarding this response.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the survey response, typically generated by the survey platform or advancement system.']
  response_payload_json varchar [note: 'JSON-formatted payload containing individual question responses and structured survey data for flexible storage and analysis.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the survey response indicating whether the alumnus completed, partially completed, refused, abandoned, is still in progress, or allowed the survey to expire.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the survey response was submitted or last saved, including time zone information.']
  salary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reported salary (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  salary_range varchar [note: 'Salary range reported by the alumnus, typically captured as a bracket rather than exact amount to encourage response.']
  satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Overall satisfaction rating provided by the alumnus, typically on a numeric scale (e.g., 1-5 or 1-10).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this survey response was captured (e.g., Blackbaud Raisers Edge, Qualtrics, SurveyMonkey).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this survey response in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  survey_invitation_date date [note: 'Date when the survey invitation was sent to the alumnus.']
  used_in_accreditation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this response was included in accreditation reporting (AACSB, ABET, program-level accreditation, or IPEDS).']
  would_recommend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus would recommend the institution to others, often used for Net Promoter Score (NPS) calculation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing an individual alumnus\'s response to an alumni survey. Captures survey reference, alumnus reference, response date, response channel, completion status (complete, partial, refused), individual question responses stored as structured fields or JSON payload, employment status at time of response, salary range reported, and whether the response was used in accreditation reporting. Supports IPEDS graduate outcomes, NACE First Destination Survey, and program-level accreditation data.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_award {
  alumni_award_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni award record. Primary key.']
  alumni_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumni event where the award was presented (e.g., homecoming, gala, commencement).']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who received or was nominated for this award.']
  award_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the award definition in the institutional award catalog, defining the award name, category, and criteria.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Alumni awards are often given by academic units/cost centers. Creating alumni_award.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for award cost allocation and budget tracking.']
  endowment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the endowment fund that supports this award, if funded by a named endowment.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Alumni awards are given in specific fiscal periods for recognition and budget tracking. Creating alumni_award.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  graduation_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Alumni awards often have eligibility criteria based on graduation term (e.g., 5-year, 10-year alumni awards). Award ceremonies are scheduled relative to reunion cycles tied to specific graduation term']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual or body that provided final approval for the award.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the award selection received final institutional approval (e.g., by President, Board of Trustees).']
  award_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the award type, used for reporting and segmentation of recognition programs.']
  award_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the award as it appears on certificates and in official communications (e.g., Distinguished Alumni Award, Young Alumni Achievement Award).']
  award_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or tracking number for this specific award instance, used in publications and communications.']
  award_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the award record, tracking progression from nomination through presentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nominated|under_review|selected|approved|declined|presented|revoked — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  award_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the award category (e.g., professional achievement, community service, academic excellence).']
  award_year varchar [note: 'Calendar or academic year in which the award was conferred, used for historical tracking and cohort analysis.']
  citation_text varchar [note: 'Official citation or commendation text read during the presentation ceremony and printed on the award certificate, summarizing the recipients achievements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this award record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this award record was most recently updated.']
  monetary_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash or scholarship value associated with the award, if applicable. Null for recognition-only awards.']
  nomination_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus was formally nominated for this award.']
  nomination_source varchar [note: 'Origin or channel through which the nomination was submitted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|peer|faculty|staff|committee|board|public — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nomination_statement varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative statement submitted by the nominator explaining why the alumnus should receive the award.']
  nominator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or organization who submitted the nomination.']
  nominator_relationship varchar [note: 'Description of the nominators relationship to the nominee (e.g., colleague, classmate, supervisor, community partner).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special circumstances, or administrative notes related to the award.']
  presentation_date date [note: 'Date when the award was formally presented to the recipient at a ceremony or event.']
  presentation_location varchar [note: 'Venue or location where the award presentation ceremony took place.']
  presenter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who formally presented the award to the recipient (e.g., President, Dean, Board Chair).']
  prospect_qualification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this award recipient should be flagged as a major gift prospect for advancement cultivation.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date when the award was announced in alumni publications, website, or press releases.']
  publication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the award recipient has consented to public announcement and publication of the award in alumni communications.']
  recipient_class_year varchar [note: 'Graduation year of the award recipient, used for reunion planning and cohort analysis.']
  recipient_degree varchar [note: 'Primary degree earned by the recipient from the institution (e.g., BA, BS, MBA, PhD).']
  recipient_major varchar [note: 'Primary academic major or field of study of the award recipient during their time at the institution.']
  review_committee varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the committee responsible for evaluating this award nomination.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date when the selection committee began formal review of this nomination.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date when the award was revoked, if applicable. Null for awards in good standing.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the award was revoked, if applicable. Confidential institutional record.']
  selection_date date [note: 'Date when the recipient was officially selected to receive the award by the review committee or governing body.']
  selection_rationale varchar [note: 'Internal documentation of the committees reasoning and justification for selecting this recipient.']

  Note: 'Manages institutional awards, honors, and recognition programs for alumni, including both the award catalog (name, category, selection criteria, nomination process) and recipient records (nomination date, selection date, presentation date, citation text, ceremony reference). Award categories include distinguished alumni, young alumni achievement, service award, honorary degree, and hall of fame induction. Supports recognition tracking, alumni publications, and advancement prospect qualification.'
}

Table advancement.award_recipient {
  award_recipient_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the award recipient record. Primary key for the award recipient entity.']
  alumni_award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific institutional award being conferred. Links to the award master data defining award name, criteria, and prestige level.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus receiving the award. Links to alumni master record containing biographical and engagement history.']
  presentation_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumni event at which the award was presented. Links to event master data for venue, attendance, and program details.']
  award_year varchar [note: 'Calendar or fiscal year in which the award was conferred. Used for cohort analysis and historical tracking of award recipients.']
  citation_text varchar [note: 'Official narrative citation read at the award presentation ceremony. Summarizes the recipients accomplishments and contributions that merited the award.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this award recipient record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary award amount. Typically USD for U.S. institutions but may vary for international awards.']
  engagement_score_impact varchar [note: 'Point value added to the recipients overall alumni engagement score as a result of receiving this award. Reflects increased connection to the institution.']
  featured_in_publication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the award recipient was featured in alumni magazines, newsletters, or other institutional publications. Used to track recognition and engagement activities.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who most recently modified this award recipient record. Supports audit and accountability requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this award recipient record was most recently updated. Tracks the currency of the data and supports change detection.']
  monetary_award_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar value of any monetary prize or stipend accompanying the award. Some awards are purely honorary while others include financial recognition.']
  nomination_date date [note: 'Date on which the alumnus was formally nominated for the award. Marks the beginning of the selection process.']
  nomination_source varchar [note: 'Origin or channel through which the nomination was submitted. Indicates whether the nomination came from the alumnus themselves, peers, institutional leadership, or other sources. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|peer|faculty|staff|alumni_chapter|advancement_office|president|board_of_trustees|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nomination_statement varchar [note: 'Narrative text submitted by the nominator describing why the alumnus merits the award. Includes accomplishments, impact, and alignment with award criteria.']
  nominator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who submitted the nomination. May be kept confidential depending on institutional policy.']
  nominator_relationship varchar [note: 'Description of the nominators relationship to the award recipient (e.g., classmate, colleague, faculty advisor, supervisor). Provides context for the nomination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for advancement staff to record additional context, special circumstances, follow-up actions, or other relevant information about the award recipient.']
  presentation_date date [note: 'Date on which the award was formally presented to the recipient. Typically occurs at a ceremony, gala, or commencement event.']
  presentation_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the award was presented. May include venue name, city, or virtual platform details.']
  press_release_date date [note: 'Date on which the press release announcing the award was distributed to media outlets and published on institutional channels.']
  press_release_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a press release was issued to announce the award. Used to track public relations activities and media coverage.']
  prospect_qualification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this award recipient has been identified as a major gift prospect based on the award recognition. Award recipients often have increased capacity and affinity for giving.']
