  donor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary donor or constituent who established this gift agreement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advancement officer or volunteer who was primarily responsible for securing this gift agreement.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the institutional fund or account that will receive and manage the gift proceeds.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the gift agreement, typically used in donor communications and legal documentation.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the gift agreement indicating its operational standing and enforceability.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gift agreement based on the nature and purpose of the philanthropic commitment.']
  balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the gift agreement, calculated as commitment amount minus paid amount.']
  commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value pledged by the donor under this gift agreement, representing the full commitment before any payments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp indicating when this gift agreement record was first created in the advancement system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the monetary denomination of the gift agreement amounts.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the gift agreement becomes legally binding and enforceable between the institution and the donor.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the gift agreement terms conclude or the commitment period ends. Nullable for perpetual agreements.']
  final_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the last payment installment, marking the expected fulfillment of the gift commitment.']
  first_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the initial payment installment under this gift agreement.']
  gift_purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the donor\'s intended use and purpose for the gift, including any restrictions or designations.']
  gift_valuation_date date [note: 'Date on which non-cash gifts were appraised or valued for accounting and tax reporting purposes.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the donor has requested anonymity in public recognition and reporting of this gift.']
  is_matching_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this gift agreement qualifies for corporate or foundation matching gift programs.']
  legal_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the formal legal contract or memorandum of understanding governing this gift agreement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp indicating when this gift agreement record was last updated or modified.']
  naming_opportunity varchar [note: 'Specific building, program, scholarship, or institutional asset that will bear the donor\'s name or designated honoree name.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, or internal comments about the gift agreement.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount received to date against this gift agreement, tracking fulfillment progress.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the donor will make installment payments toward fulfilling the gift commitment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary instrument or mechanism through which the donor will transfer gift payments to the institution.']
  proposal_date date [note: 'Date when the formal gift proposal was presented to the donor for consideration.']
  recognition_level varchar [note: 'Stewardship tier or recognition category assigned to this gift agreement, determining naming rights and acknowledgment benefits.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for providing impact reports and updates to the donor regarding the use and outcomes of their gift.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on the use of gift funds, aligned with accounting standards for nonprofit organizations.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the donor and institutional representative executed the gift agreement with binding signatures.']
  stewardship_plan varchar [note: 'Documented plan for ongoing donor engagement, reporting, and recognition activities associated with this gift agreement.']
  tax_deductible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the gift commitment that qualifies for charitable tax deduction under applicable tax regulations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for gift_agreement. Referenced by gift_agreement_id.'
}

Table advancement.stewardship_plan {
  stewardship_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for stewardship_plan']
  advancement_fund_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the fund or designation that this stewardship plan supports (e.g., scholarship fund, endowment, capital project).']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the fundraising campaign associated with this stewardship plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the advancement officer or gift officer responsible for managing relationships under this stewardship plan.']
  superseded_stewardship_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on stewardship_plan (superseded_stewardship_plan_id)']
  annual_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether donors in this stewardship plan receive a personalized annual impact report.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this stewardship plan was formally approved by the appropriate authority.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of the stewardship plan indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by leadership.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the individual who approved this stewardship plan for implementation.']
  benefits_summary varchar [note: 'A summary of the benefits, privileges, and recognition opportunities provided to donors under this stewardship plan.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The allocated budget for executing stewardship activities under this plan for the fiscal year.']
  contact_strategy varchar [note: 'The overall approach and methodology for donor contact and engagement defined by this stewardship plan (e.g., personalized outreach, event-based, digital-first, relationship-driven).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this stewardship plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for gift level thresholds (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD).']
  description varchar [note: 'A detailed narrative description of the stewardship plan objectives, activities, and engagement strategy.']
  donor_segment varchar [note: 'The target donor segment or constituency group that this stewardship plan is designed to engage (e.g., major donors, alumni, corporate partners, foundation supporters).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this stewardship plan expires or is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended plan.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this stewardship plan becomes active and begins to govern donor engagement activities.']
  engagement_frequency varchar [note: 'The planned frequency of donor touchpoints and stewardship activities under this plan.']
  event_invitation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether donors in this stewardship plan are invited to exclusive donor events and recognition ceremonies.']
  gift_acknowledgment_template varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the gift acknowledgment letter template used for donors in this stewardship plan.']
  gift_level_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum gift amount threshold for this stewardship plan tier. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  gift_level_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum gift amount threshold that qualifies a donor for inclusion in this stewardship plan.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this stewardship plan was most recently reviewed and assessed for effectiveness.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the individual who last modified this stewardship plan record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this stewardship plan record was last updated or modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review of this stewardship plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, comments, or observations about the stewardship plan for internal reference.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'The unique business code or identifier assigned to this stewardship plan for external reference and system integration.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable name or title of the stewardship plan used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'The category or classification of the stewardship plan based on the fundraising program it supports.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'The preferred or primary channel for donor communication under this stewardship plan.']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'A numeric score representing the relative priority or importance of this stewardship plan within the advancement portfolio (higher values indicate higher priority).']
  recognition_level varchar [note: 'The tier or level of donor recognition associated with this stewardship plan (e.g., Presidents Circle, Deans Society, Founders Club).']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency for reviewing and updating this stewardship plan to ensure continued relevance and effectiveness.']
  status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the stewardship plan indicating whether it is actively in use, under development, or retired.']
  target_donor_count varchar [note: 'The projected or target number of donors expected to be managed under this stewardship plan.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the individual who created this stewardship plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for stewardship_plan. Referenced by stewardship_plan_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_requirement {
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory requirement record. Primary key for the regulatory requirement catalog.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Regulatory requirements assign organizational accountability for compliance monitoring and reporting. Enables requirement workload distribution, organizational compliance scoring, and resource allocat']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory requirement is currently active and enforceable. True for active requirements, false for superseded or expired requirements.']
  applicability_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or criteria that determine when this requirement applies (e.g., applies to all Title IV participating institutions, applies to NCAA Division I athletics programs, applies to programs with clinical components, applies to institutions with on-campus housing).']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the organizational scope to which this requirement applies: institution-wide (all units), program-specific (certain academic programs), department-specific (specific departments), college-specific (specific colleges/schools), campus-specific (specific campus locations), student-specific (certain student populations), employee-specific (certain employee groups). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: institution-wide|program-specific|department-specific|college-specific|campus-specific|student-specific|employee-specific — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  citation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific legal or regulatory citation (e.g., 20 U.S.C. § 1232g, 34 CFR Part 99, 34 CFR 668.46, HLC Criterion 2.A.1). Provides precise statutory or regulatory section reference.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current institutional compliance status for this requirement: compliant (fully meeting obligation), non-compliant (not meeting obligation), in-progress (working toward compliance), not-applicable (requirement does not apply), under-review (compliance assessment underway), remediation-required (corrective action needed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory requirement record was first created in the compliance catalog system. Provides audit trail for record creation.']
  documentation_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of records, evidence, or documentation that must be maintained to demonstrate compliance with this requirement (e.g., training completion records, audit trails, signed attestations, published notices, meeting minutes).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this regulatory requirement became or becomes effective and enforceable. Marks the start of the compliance obligation period.']
  enforcement_mechanism varchar [note: 'Description of how this requirement is enforced and what consequences exist for non-compliance (e.g., loss of Title IV eligibility, accreditation sanctions, civil penalties, loss of NCAA membership, corrective action plans, public disclosure of violations).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this regulatory requirement expires or is superseded, if applicable. Null for ongoing requirements with no sunset provision.']
  external_reporting_system varchar [note: 'Name of the external system or portal used for submitting compliance reports or filings (e.g., IPEDS Data Collection System, NCAA Compliance Assistance, Federal Student Aid Portal, Accreditation Portal). Null if no external reporting is required.']
  implementation_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes on how the institution implements or interprets this requirement, including operational procedures, system configurations, or special considerations.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit or compliance review that assessed this requirement. Used to track audit history and compliance verification.']
  last_audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent audit or compliance review: compliant (no findings), non-compliant (findings identified), compliant-with-recommendations (compliant but improvements suggested), not-reviewed (not included in audit scope).']
  last_compliance_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the responsible officer last certified institutional compliance with this requirement. Used for attestation tracking and accountability.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who last updated this regulatory requirement record. Provides accountability for data maintenance.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when this regulatory requirement record was last updated in the compliance catalog. Used to track currency of compliance information.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory requirement record was last modified. Provides audit trail for record changes and supports change tracking.']
  next_reporting_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the next required compliance report or filing submission to the regulatory body, if applicable. Null for non-reporting requirements.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next institutional compliance review or assessment of this requirement. Used for compliance calendar planning and tracking.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Specific penalties or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance, including monetary fines, program restrictions, loss of funding, accreditation probation, or other enforcement actions.']
  record_retention_period varchar [note: 'Required duration for retaining compliance documentation and evidence (e.g., 3 years, 5 years, 7 years, permanent). Specifies minimum retention period mandated by regulation or institutional policy.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the governing or enforcement agency responsible for this requirement (e.g., U.S. Department of Education, Higher Learning Commission, SACSCOC, NCAA, Office for Civil Rights, State Board of Education).']
  regulatory_source varchar [note: 'Name of the law, regulation, or standard that mandates this requirement (e.g., Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act, Americans with Disabilities Act, Higher Learning Commission Criteria for Accreditation).']
  related_requirements varchar [note: 'List or description of other regulatory requirements that are related, dependent, or cross-referenced with this requirement. Captures interdependencies in the compliance landscape.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for submitting compliance reports or filings to the regulatory body, if applicable. Not-applicable for requirements that do not involve periodic reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|biennial|quarterly|monthly|one-time|event-driven|not-applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the regulatory requirement (e.g., FERPA-001, TITLE-IX-042, CLERY-ACT-15). Used for external reference and cross-system tracking.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the compliance obligation, including what must be done, by whom, and under what conditions. Captures the substantive content of the regulatory mandate.']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the regulatory requirement (e.g., Annual Security Report Publication, Student Privacy Rights Notification, Athletic Eligibility Certification).']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compliance obligation type: reporting (periodic submission), disclosure (public notification), certification (attestation), audit (examination), training (education mandate), policy (governance requirement), procedural (operational process), documentation (record-keeping). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reporting|disclosure|certification|audit|training|policy|procedural|documentation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  responsible_officer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual officer or position holder with primary accountability for this compliance requirement (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, Title IX Coordinator, Director of Financial Aid, Faculty Athletics Representative).']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for institutional review or reassessment of compliance with this requirement: annual (yearly), biennial (every two years), triennial (every three years), quarterly, monthly, continuous (ongoing monitoring), event-driven (triggered by specific events), as-needed (no fixed schedule). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|biennial|triennial|quarterly|monthly|continuous|event-driven|as-needed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Institutional assessment of the risk severity associated with non-compliance: critical (severe institutional impact), high (significant impact), medium (moderate impact), low (minimal impact). Used for prioritizing compliance efforts.']
  supporting_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to institutional policies, procedures, or guidelines that implement this regulatory requirement (e.g., policy number, policy title, handbook section). Links regulatory mandate to internal governance documents.']
  training_audience varchar [note: 'Description of who must complete required training (e.g., all employees, faculty only, student-athletes, Title IX coordinators, research administrators, financial aid staff). Null if no training is required.']
  training_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for completing compliance training: annual (yearly), biennial (every two years), one-time (initial training only), as-needed (when role changes or updates occur), not-applicable (no training required).']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement mandates specific training for employees, students, or other constituencies. True if training is required, false otherwise.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all federal, state, and accreditation regulatory requirements applicable to the institution. Captures requirement source (e.g., Title IV, FERPA, Clery Act, ADA, Title IX, IPEDS, NCAA), regulatory body, effective dates, applicability scope (institution-wide, program-specific, department-specific), compliance obligation type, citation reference, and enforcement mechanism. Serves as the authoritative reference for all compliance obligations tracked across the institution.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for obligation']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee designated as the compliance officer responsible for overseeing and ensuring fulfillment of this obligation.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Compliance obligations are operationalized through formal institutional policies. For example, a Title IX regulatory obligation is implemented through the institution\'s Title IX policy. The policy_ref']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source regulatory requirement that mandates this obligation (e.g., FERPA regulation, Title IX statute, Clery Act provision, NCAA bylaw, accreditation standard).']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Compliance obligations are assigned to organizational units for execution accountability. Enables obligation workload distribution, organizational compliance dashboards, and budget allocation for comp']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred to fulfill the obligation. Expressed in USD.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of staff hours spent fulfilling the obligation, captured for process improvement and future estimation accuracy.']
  audit_finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings or deficiencies identified during the most recent audit of this obligation. Zero indicates full compliance.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when the obligation was fulfilled. Null if not yet completed.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Detailed status of the obligation fulfillment process: not-started (no action taken), in-progress (work underway), submitted (delivered to governing body), approved (accepted by regulator), rejected (requires rework), partially-complete (some components done).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which corrective actions must be completed to address audit findings or compliance deficiencies. Null if no corrective action is required.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required to address audit findings or compliance gaps (true) or the obligation is in full compliance (false).']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to fulfill the obligation, including staff time, external consultant fees, technology costs, and materials. Expressed in USD.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance obligation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the compliance obligation must be fulfilled. For recurring obligations, this represents the next due date.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this compliance obligation expires or is no longer required. Null for ongoing obligations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this compliance obligation becomes active and enforceable for the institution.']
  escalation_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the due date when the obligation should be escalated to senior leadership if not yet completed (e.g., 30 days for critical obligations, 14 days for standard obligations).']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected number of staff hours required to complete the obligation, used for resource planning and workload management.']
  evidence_location varchar [note: 'File path, URL, or document management system reference where supporting documentation and evidence of compliance are stored (e.g., SharePoint folder, compliance management system record ID).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Recurrence pattern for the obligation: annual (yearly), biennial (every two years), quarterly (four times per year), monthly, event-triggered (upon specific incidents), one-time (single occurrence), continuous (ongoing monitoring). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|biennial|quarterly|monthly|event-triggered|one-time|continuous — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or accreditation body that mandates this obligation (e.g., U.S. Department of Education, HLC, SACSCOC, NCAA, AACSB, ABET, ABA, FERPA, Title IX Office).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance obligation record is currently active (true) or has been logically deleted or archived (false). Used for soft-delete pattern.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent internal or external audit of this compliance obligation. Used to track audit cycles and identify obligations due for review.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next internal or external audit of this compliance obligation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, historical notes, or clarifications about the obligation that do not fit in structured fields.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the compliance obligation, used for external reference and reporting (e.g., FERPA-001, TITLEIX-002, CLERY-003).']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the compliance obligation (e.g., Annual FERPA Audit, Title IX Training Completion, Clery Act Crime Statistics Report).']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance obligation: active (in effect), pending (scheduled but not yet due), completed (fulfilled), overdue (past deadline), waived (exempted), suspended (temporarily inactive).']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compliance obligation by activity type: reporting (regulatory filings), training (mandatory education), investigation (incident response), audit (compliance review), self-study (accreditation), filing (document submission), certification (attestation), monitoring (ongoing oversight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reporting|training|investigation|audit|self-study|filing|certification|monitoring — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of potential consequences for non-compliance, including financial penalties, loss of accreditation, loss of federal funding eligibility, sanctions, or reputational damage.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for the obligation based on regulatory impact and institutional risk: critical (federal mandate with severe penalties), high (accreditation requirement), medium (state requirement), low (internal policy).']
  requires_board_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires formal approval from the Board of Trustees or Regents before submission (true) or can be handled at the administrative level (false).']
  requires_external_audit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires validation or certification by an external auditor or third-party reviewer (true) or can be self-certified (false).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the obligation deliverable is submitted to the governing body: online-portal (e.g., IPEDS portal), email, mail (physical documents), in-person (site visit), system-integration (automated data feed), not-applicable (internal obligations).']
  training_completion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of required staff who have completed mandatory training associated with this obligation. Expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff training is required as part of fulfilling this obligation (true) or training is not applicable (false). Examples include Title IX training, FERPA training, Clery Act training.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this compliance obligation record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance obligation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this compliance obligation record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Institutional compliance obligations derived from regulatory requirements, mapped to responsible offices, compliance officers, and reporting deadlines. Tracks obligation type (reporting, training, investigation, audit, self-study), frequency (annual, biennial, event-triggered), assigned owner, due dates, escalation thresholds, and current fulfillment status. Links regulatory requirements to institutional accountability structures.'
}

Table compliance.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the institutional policy record. Primary key.']
  implementing_it_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_policy. Business justification: Institutional compliance policies (data retention, records management, acceptable use) are implemented through IT policies. Auditors trace compliance policy requirements to technical controls. For exa']
  supersedes_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous policy version that this policy replaces. Enables version lineage tracking and historical analysis.']
  acknowledgment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individuals subject to this policy must formally acknowledge receipt and understanding. Common for high-risk policies and annual compliance certifications.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which populations or organizational units are subject to this policy. Critical for determining enforcement jurisdiction and compliance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_university|undergraduate_students|graduate_students|faculty|staff|contractors|visitors — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the issuing authority formally approved this policy version. Used for version control and audit trails.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing significant events in the policy lifecycle: amendments, emergency suspensions, legal challenges, or regulatory guidance changes. Supports compliance audits and accreditation reviews.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The primary compliance or accreditation framework this policy addresses. Used to map policies to regulatory reporting requirements and audit checklists. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ferpa|title_ix|clery_act|ada|hipaa|ncaa|aacsb|abet|hlc|sacscoc — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for inquiries related to this policy. Used by students, faculty, staff, and external parties seeking clarification or reporting concerns.']
  contact_office varchar [note: 'Office or department that serves as the primary point of contact for questions, interpretations, and guidance on this policy (e.g., Office of Compliance, Title IX Office, Registrar).']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the contact office. Supports accessibility and provides alternative communication channel for policy inquiries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data governance and compliance tracking.']
  document_repository_path varchar [note: 'File path or document management system location where the authoritative version of this policy is stored (e.g., SharePoint path, DMS identifier).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this policy became or will become enforceable. May differ from approval date to allow for communication and training periods.']
  enforcement_mechanism varchar [note: 'Description of how violations of this policy are detected, investigated, and adjudicated (e.g., student conduct process, HR grievance procedure, Title IX investigation protocol).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this policy version ceases to be enforceable. Nullable for policies without a predetermined end date.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the physical or virtual locations where this policy applies. Important for multi-campus institutions and online programs.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'The governing body or office that formally adopted and authorized this policy (e.g., Board of Trustees, President, Provost, Vice President for Finance).']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of searchable terms and phrases that facilitate policy discovery and retrieval (e.g., discrimination, harassment, accommodations, privacy).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of this policy by the policy owner or governing body. Used to track compliance with review cycle requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this policy record. Used to track data freshness and trigger downstream refresh processes.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this policy. Calculated based on review cycle frequency and last review date.']
  owner varchar [note: 'The office, department, or individual responsible for maintaining, interpreting, and enforcing this policy (e.g., Office of Compliance, Title IX Coordinator, Registrar).']
  policy_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the policy domain. Determines which compliance frameworks and regulatory requirements apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic_integrity|title_ix|ada_accessibility|ferpa_privacy|research_ethics|financial_management|safety_security|human_resources|student_affairs|athletics|information_technology|facilities|procurement — promote to reference product]']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique policy identifier assigned by the institution (e.g., AA-001, FERPA-1234). Used for reference in communications, audits, and compliance documentation.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy. Active policies are enforceable; superseded policies have been replaced by newer versions; archived policies are retained for historical reference only. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|active|suspended|archived|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  public_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy is published on the public-facing institutional website or restricted to internal audiences. Transparency requirement for certain compliance policies.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The federal, state, or accreditation requirement that mandates or informs this policy (e.g., Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, FERPA 34 CFR Part 99, HLC Criterion 2.A).']
  related_policies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of policy numbers or titles that are related to or referenced by this policy. Supports cross-referencing and policy ecosystem mapping.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which this policy must be reviewed (e.g., 12, 24, 36). Determined by regulatory requirements, risk level, and institutional governance standards.']
  sanctions_description varchar [note: 'Summary of potential consequences for policy violations, ranging from warnings to expulsion, termination, or legal action. Used for transparency and due process.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the primary category (e.g., under Title IX: sexual harassment, discrimination, retaliation).']
  summary varchar [note: 'Brief executive summary of the policy purpose, scope, and key provisions. Used for policy catalogs, training materials, and quick reference guides.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the policy as approved by the governing body. Human-readable identifier used in policy manuals and institutional communications.']
  training_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which personnel must complete refresher training on this policy. Nullable if training is one-time or not required.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether institutional personnel must complete formal training on this policy. True for policies with significant compliance risk (Title IX, FERPA, research ethics).']
  url varchar [note: 'Web address where the full text of this policy is published. Enables direct access for students, faculty, staff, and external auditors.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for this policy iteration (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented with each revision to maintain version history and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Institutional policy master record covering all formally adopted policies governing academic, administrative, financial, and student affairs. Captures policy number, title, category (academic integrity, Title IX, ADA, FERPA, research ethics, financial, safety), issuing authority, approval date, effective date, review cycle, policy owner, version history, and applicability scope. Serves as the SSOT for institutional policy governance.'
}

Table compliance.policy_version {
  policy_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this specific version of an institutional policy. Primary key for the policy version record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who holds the approval authority and formally approved this policy version. Links to the executive or administrator who signed off.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent policy document that this version belongs to. Links to the master policy entity.']
  primary_policy_author_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who drafted or authored this version of the policy. Typically a compliance officer, legal counsel, or policy administrator.']
  superseded_version_policy_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous policy version that this version replaces. Null for the first version. Enables full version lineage tracking required for accreditation self-studies.']
  tertiary_policy_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee designated as the primary contact for questions or concerns about this policy version. Typically a compliance officer or policy administrator.']
  accessibility_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy document version meets ADA Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards. True when document is accessible to users with disabilities.']
  acknowledgment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees or students must formally acknowledge receipt and understanding of this policy version. True for policies requiring signed acknowledgment for compliance purposes.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Title or role of the individual or body authorized to approve this policy version (e.g., President, Board of Trustees, Provost, Vice President for Compliance). Defines governance hierarchy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the approval authority formally approved this policy version. Marks the transition from draft/review to approved status.']
  archived_date date [note: 'Date when this policy version was moved to archived status. Null for non-archived versions. Supports retention schedule compliance.']
  archived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy version has been archived and is no longer actively referenced. True for historical versions retained for audit and accreditation purposes but not displayed in active policy libraries.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of changes made in this version compared to the previous version. Describes what was added, modified, or removed and why.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this policy version record was first created in the system. Audit field for tracking record creation.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the policy document (PDF, Word, HTML, Markdown). Indicates how the policy is stored and published.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or document management system link to the full text of this policy version. Provides access to the authoritative policy document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this policy version becomes officially active and enforceable across the institution. May differ from approval date to allow for communication and training periods.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this policy version is scheduled to expire or undergo mandatory review. Null for policies without scheduled expiration. Supports periodic review requirements for accreditation.']
  legal_review_comments varchar [note: 'Confidential comments and recommendations provided by legal counsel during their review. May include risk assessments, suggested language changes, or approval conditions.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when legal counsel completed their review of this policy version. Required for compliance audit trails.']
  legal_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy version requires formal legal counsel review before approval. True for policies with legal, regulatory, or contractual implications.']
  legal_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of legal counsel review for this policy version. Tracks whether legal has approved, rejected, or conditionally approved the policy language.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this policy version record was last modified in the system. Audit field for tracking record updates.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this policy. Supports periodic review cycles required by accreditation agencies and best practices in policy governance.']
  public_comment_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy version was subject to a public comment period before finalization. True for policies affecting broad campus populations or required by shared governance.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary federal, state, or accreditation regulation that this policy addresses (e.g., FERPA, Title IX, Clery Act, ADA, SACSCOC Standard 3.3.1). Links policy to external compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ferpa|title_ix|clery_act|ada|title_iv|hipaa|ncaa|sacscoc|hlc|abet|aacsb|gasb|fasb — promote to reference product]']
  responsible_office varchar [note: 'Name of the institutional office or department responsible for administering and enforcing this policy (e.g., Office of Compliance, Office of General Counsel, Human Resources, Title IX Office).']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Standard review cycle for this policy in months (e.g., 12, 24, 36). Defines how often the policy must be reviewed for currency and compliance.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Primary business driver or trigger for creating this new version. Indicates whether the revision was driven by external compliance requirements, internal improvements, or scheduled review cycles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory_change|accreditation_requirement|legal_update|operational_improvement|periodic_review|incident_response|stakeholder_feedback — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of which institutional units, populations, or activities this policy version applies to. Defines the boundaries of policy applicability.']
  stakeholder_consultation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy version underwent formal stakeholder consultation or shared governance review before approval. True for policies requiring faculty senate, student government, or staff council input.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date when this policy version was superseded by a newer version and transitioned from active to superseded status. Null for currently active versions.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees or students are required to complete training on this policy version. True for policies with mandatory compliance training requirements (e.g., Title IX, FERPA, safety).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number for this policy revision (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Follows standard versioning conventions to indicate major and minor revisions.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this policy version. Tracks progression from draft through approval to active use and eventual supersession.']
  version_title varchar [note: 'Full title of the policy as it appears in this version. May change across versions to reflect scope or terminology updates.']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Total word count of the policy document in this version. Used for readability analysis and to track policy complexity over time.']

  Note: 'Version history for institutional policies, capturing each revision cycle including version number, change summary, author, legal review status, approval authority, approval date, effective date, superseded version reference, and reason for revision. Enables full audit trail of policy evolution required for accreditation self-studies and regulatory audits.'
}

Table compliance.title_ix_case {
  title_ix_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Title IX case record. Primary key.']
  cybersecurity_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.cybersecurity_incident. Business justification: Title IX cases increasingly involve cyber harassment, non-consensual image sharing, unauthorized access to victim accounts, or electronic stalking. OCR guidance requires institutions to address techno']
  primary_title_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the Title IX Coordinator overseeing this case.']
  primary_title_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual filing the Title IX complaint. Links to student, employee, or person record.']
  quaternary_title_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified this Title IX case record.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Title IX case management is a federal regulatory requirement under Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 (20 U.S.C. §§ 1681-1688) and implementing regulations (34 CFR Part 106). Each case is tr']
  tertiary_title_decision_maker_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the decision-maker who issued the determination regarding responsibility.']
  appeal_basis varchar [note: 'Grounds on which the appeal is based, as permitted under Title IX regulations.']
  appeal_decision_date date [note: 'Date when the appeal decision was issued.']
  appeal_filed_by varchar [note: 'Identifies which party filed the appeal (complainant, respondent, or both).']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date when an appeal was filed by either the complainant or respondent.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal has been filed by either party following the determination.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal decision by the appellate decision-maker.']
  case_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the Title IX case was officially closed, including all appeals and sanctions implementation.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the Title IX case, formatted as TIX-YYYY-#####.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the Title IX case in the grievance process workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: intake|investigation|hearing_scheduled|determination_issued|appeal_pending|closed|dismissed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the Title IX complaint based on the nature of the alleged conduct.']
  clery_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this Title IX case involves a Clery Act reportable crime.']
  complainant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the complainant\'s relationship to the institution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: student|employee|faculty|staff|visitor|contractor|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_request_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complainant requested confidentiality or that no investigation be pursued.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Title IX case record was first created in the system.']
  determination_date date [note: 'Date when the written determination regarding responsibility was issued.']
  determination_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination regarding whether the respondent is responsible for violating Title IX policy.']
  determination_rationale varchar [note: 'Written rationale explaining the basis for the determination outcome, including credibility assessments and evidence evaluation.']
  formal_complaint_filed_date date [note: 'Date when the formal Title IX complaint was filed by the complainant or signed by the Title IX Coordinator.']
  grievance_process_type varchar [note: 'Type of Title IX grievance process being followed for this case.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Date when the live hearing was conducted for the Title IX case, if applicable.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date when the alleged Title IX incident occurred.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the alleged Title IX incident as reported by the complainant.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Description of the location where the alleged Title IX incident occurred (on-campus, off-campus, or specific building/area).']
  interim_measures_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether emergency removal or administrative leave was implemented during the investigation.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Title IX investigation was completed and investigative report finalized.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal Title IX investigation commenced.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Title IX case record was last updated.']
  law_enforcement_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local law enforcement was notified of the incident.']
  ocr_complaint_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a complaint was filed with the U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights related to this case.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date when the Title IX complaint was officially reported to the institution.']
  respondent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the respondent\'s relationship to the institution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: student|employee|faculty|staff|visitor|contractor|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retaliation_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether retaliation against the complainant or witnesses was reported during or after the case.']
  sanctions_imposed varchar [note: 'Description of disciplinary sanctions or remedies imposed on the respondent if found responsible.']
  supportive_measures_provided varchar [note: 'Description of non-disciplinary, non-punitive individualized services offered to complainant and/or respondent.']

  Note: 'Tracks Title IX complaints, investigations, and resolutions as required under the U.S. Department of Education Title IX regulations. Captures complainant type, respondent type, incident date, report date, case type (sexual harassment, sexual assault, stalking, dating violence), assigned investigator, grievance process type (formal/informal), hearing panel details, determination outcome, appeal status, sanctions imposed, and case closure date. Supports mandatory Title IX coordinator reporting and OCR audit readiness.'
}

Table compliance.clery_incident {
  clery_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Clery Act reportable incident. Primary key for the clery_incident data product.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Clery Act incidents (campus crimes, safety incidents) occur in specific campus buildings. This FK links compliance.clery_incident to the facility building master, enabling geographic analysis of campu']
  csa_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Clery Act requires tracking Campus Security Authority who received/reported incident. CSA designation is employee role. Enables CSA training compliance reporting and incident audit trails required by ']
  cybersecurity_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.cybersecurity_incident. Business justification: Clery Act incidents may involve cybersecurity events: stalking via email, unauthorized access to victim records, image-based abuse distributed electronically. Campus safety and IT security must coordi']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Clery Act incident reporting is a specific federal regulatory requirement (Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act). Each incident is tracked under this regul']
  residence_hall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.residence_hall. Business justification: Clery Act requires geographic classification of crimes; residence halls are on-campus residential facilities requiring specific reporting. Compliance offices must accurately categorize incidents by bu']
  studentlife_housing_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.housing_assignment. Business justification: Clery incidents involving students in housing require tracking victim/respondent housing status for timely warnings, supportive measures, and no-contact order enforcement. Campus safety and housing mu']
  arrest_made boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an arrest was made in connection with this incident. Arrests are reported separately in the Annual Security Report (ASR) statistics for certain offense categories.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Official case number assigned by the law enforcement agency investigating the incident. Used for cross-referencing with police reports and court proceedings.']
  clery_crime_category varchar [note: 'The Clery Act crime classification for this incident as defined in the Handbook for Campus Safety and Security Reporting. Includes criminal offenses, hate crimes, and Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) offenses. Determines statistical reporting category in the Annual Security Report (ASR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: criminal_homicide|sex_offense_forcible|sex_offense_non_forcible|robbery|aggravated_assault|burglary|motor_vehicle_theft|arson|hate_crime|dating_violence|domestic_violence|stalking — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  clery_geography_classification varchar [note: 'The geographic classification of the incident location per Clery Act definitions: on-campus (including residential facilities), non-campus property (controlled by institution), public property (adjacent to campus), or not within Clery geography. Determines whether the incident must be included in Annual Security Report (ASR) statistics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incident record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  disciplinary_referral_made boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident was referred for campus disciplinary action. Disciplinary referrals are reported separately in the Annual Security Report (ASR) for liquor law violations, drug law violations, and illegal weapons possession.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the incident investigation and resolution. Tracks lifecycle from initial report through final disposition. Unfounded incidents are excluded from Clery statistics per federal guidance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_investigation|closed_arrest_made|closed_no_arrest|referred_for_prosecution|referred_for_disciplinary_action|unfounded|administratively_closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  emergency_notification_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an emergency notification was issued in response to this incident. Emergency notifications are required for significant emergencies or dangerous situations involving an immediate threat to the health or safety of students or employees.']
  hate_crime_bias_category varchar [note: 'The bias motivation category for hate crime incidents as defined by the Clery Act and FBI Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR) Hate Crime Statistics Program. Required for hate crime statistical reporting in the Annual Security Report (ASR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: race|religion|sexual_orientation|gender|gender_identity|ethnicity|national_origin|disability — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the Clery reportable incident occurred. This is the principal business event timestamp for statistical reporting and Annual Security Report (ASR) compilation.']
  incident_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the incident, including circumstances, actions taken, and outcomes. Used for internal investigation and case management. Confidential and not disclosed in public crime logs or Annual Security Report (ASR).']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned by campus security or law enforcement to uniquely identify this incident in operational systems. Used for cross-referencing with police reports and internal case management systems.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'The time of day (24-hour format HH:MM) when the incident occurred, if known. Used for temporal pattern analysis and resource allocation planning.']
  included_in_asr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incident was included in the institution\'s Annual Security Report (ASR) statistical disclosure to the U.S. Department of Education. Unfounded incidents and incidents outside Clery geography are excluded.']
  is_hate_crime boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets the definition of a hate crime under the Clery Act: a criminal offense motivated by bias based on race, religion, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, ethnicity, national origin, or disability. Hate crimes are reported separately in the Annual Security Report (ASR).']
  is_residential_facility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident occurred in an on-campus student housing facility. Residential facility incidents are reported as a separate sub-category in the Annual Security Report (ASR) per Clery Act requirements.']
  is_vawa_offense boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident is a Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) offense: dating violence, domestic violence, or stalking. VAWA offenses are reported separately in the Annual Security Report (ASR) and trigger specific victim notification and support obligations.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency that responded to or investigated the incident (e.g., campus police, local police department, county sheriff). Used for inter-agency coordination and crime log documentation.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific location where the incident occurred (e.g., building name, room number, parking lot, street address). Used for internal investigation and geographic pattern analysis.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this incident record. Used for audit trail and accountability in compliance reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incident record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking to ensure data integrity for regulatory reporting.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the incident was first reported to a Campus Security Authority (CSA) or law enforcement. Distinct from incident_date; used to track reporting lag and timely warning obligations.']
  reporting_source varchar [note: 'The source from which the incident was reported: Campus Security Authority (CSA), law enforcement, anonymous tip line, direct victim report, or third-party witness. Determines compliance with CSA reporting obligations.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the incident occurred, used for Annual Security Report (ASR) statistical compilation. Clery statistics are reported by calendar year, not academic or fiscal year.']
  timely_warning_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a timely warning was issued to the campus community in response to this incident. Timely warnings are required for Clery crimes that represent a serious or continuing threat to students and employees.']
  unfounded_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the incident was determined to be unfounded (false or baseless) through investigation by sworn or commissioned law enforcement personnel. Unfounded incidents are excluded from Clery statistics but must be documented and reported separately.']
  victim_notification_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the victim was provided with written notification of rights, resources, and support services as required by the Clery Act and VAWA. Applies to victims of dating violence, domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking.']

  Note: 'Records campus security incidents required for Clery Act Annual Security Report (ASR) disclosure. Captures incident date, location (on-campus, non-campus, public property, residential facility), Clery crime category (criminal homicide, sex offenses, robbery, aggravated assault, burglary, motor vehicle theft, arson, hate crimes, VAWA offenses), reporting source (CSA or law enforcement), disposition status, hate crime bias indicator, and geographic classification per Clery geography definitions. Feeds the annual CLERY statistical disclosure to the U.S. Department of Education.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: extended_time|note_taker|captioning|interpreter|accessible_housing|assistive_technology|flexible_schedule|modified_duties|ergonomic_equipment|accessible_parking|dietary|other — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accommodation is currently active and in effect. False for expired, withdrawn, or superseded accommodations.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: learning|mobility|visual|hearing|psychological|chronic_health|temporary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  documentation_received_date date [note: 'Date the institution received medical or professional documentation supporting the accommodation request.']
  documentation_type varchar [note: 'Type of professional documentation provided to support the accommodation request (medical evaluation, psychoeducational assessment, functional capacity evaluation).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the accommodation expires or is scheduled for renewal review. May be null for permanent accommodations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the approved accommodation becomes active and available for use by the requestor.']

  eligibility_determination_date date [note: 'Date the eligibility determination was made by the disability services coordinator or accommodation review committee.']

  interactive_process_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required interactive process between requestor and institution was completed per ADA requirements.']
  interactive_process_notes varchar [note: 'Documentation of the interactive process discussions, alternatives considered, and rationale for final accommodation decision.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accommodation record was last updated. Tracks changes throughout the accommodation lifecycle.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date the accommodation approval letter or notification was sent to the requestor and relevant implementing offices.']
  ocr_complaint_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a complaint related to this accommodation was filed with the U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which this accommodation record originated (Accommodate, Clockwork, Banner, Workday, manual entry).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which the accommodation must be renewed or will automatically expire. Triggers notification to requestor and coordinator.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accommodation requires periodic renewal with updated documentation. True for temporary or condition-specific accommodations.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the accommodation request was formally submitted to the disability services office or HR accommodation coordinator.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the accommodation request. Used for tracking and communication with requestor and implementing offices.']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual requesting accommodation. Determines applicable compliance framework and approval workflow.']

  Note: 'Master record for ADA and Section 504 disability accommodation requests and approved accommodations for students, faculty, and staff. Captures requestor type, disability category, documentation received date, eligibility determination, accommodation type (extended time, captioning, interpreter, accessible housing, assistive technology), approval status, effective term, renewal date, implementing office, and accommodation delivery confirmation. Supports ADA/Section 504 compliance and OCR audit readiness.'
}

Table compliance.ferpa_disclosure {

Business justification: FERPA disclosures must be authorized by authenticated institutional users. Audit trails require linking disclosure events to NetID for accountability and non-repudiation. OCR audits verify that only a']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the institutional official who authorized the disclosure. Links to the employee master record for accountability and audit trail.']
  health_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.health_visit. Business justification: FERPA disclosures of student health records must reference specific visits disclosed. Compliance tracking requires linking disclosure authorization to exact health visit records released. Essential fo']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this disclosure record. Used for accountability and audit trail purposes.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student whose education records were disclosed. Links to the student master record in the Student Information System (SIS).']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: FERPA disclosures are governed by specific provisions of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (20 U.S.C. § 1232g). Each disclosure must comply with specific FERPA exceptions (e.g., school off']
  academic_term_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the academic term to which the disclosed records pertain, if applicable. Used for contextualizing transcript and enrollment verification disclosures.']
  audit_review_date date [note: 'Date on which this disclosure record was reviewed during a FERPA compliance audit. Used for tracking audit coverage and compliance verification.']
  audit_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disclosure record has been reviewed as part of an internal or external FERPA compliance audit. True if reviewed, False if pending review.']
  authorizing_official_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position of the institutional official who authorized the disclosure. Used to verify legitimate educational interest and authorization authority.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the student or eligible parent provided written consent for the disclosure. Null if disclosure was made under a FERPA exception not requiring consent.']
  consent_form_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier of the signed consent form authorizing the disclosure, if applicable. Links to the consent document repository for audit verification.']
  consent_obtained_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether written consent from the student (or parent if student is a dependent minor) was obtained prior to disclosure. True if consent was obtained, False if disclosure was made under a FERPA exception.']
  court_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Name and jurisdiction of the court that issued the order or subpoena, if applicable. Used for legal compliance tracking and audit documentation.']
  court_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the court order or subpoena compelling disclosure, if applicable. Required for disclosures made under judicial order exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this disclosure record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  disclosure_date date [note: 'The date on which the student education records were disclosed to the third party. This is the principal business event timestamp for FERPA compliance reporting.']
  disclosure_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the education records were transmitted to the recipient. Used for security audit and compliance with data protection requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: electronic_secure_portal|email_encrypted|postal_mail|fax|in_person_pickup|phone_verbal|electronic_transcript_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disclosure_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the disclosure event. Used for documenting special circumstances, follow-up actions, or compliance considerations.']
  disclosure_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the disclosure event, formatted as FERPA-YYYY-NNNNNN for external reference and audit tracking.']
  disclosure_purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the legitimate educational interest, legal requirement, or other purpose for which the education records were disclosed. Required for FERPA compliance documentation.']
  disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disclosure record. Tracks the disclosure from authorization through completion and any subsequent review or dispute processes.']
  disclosure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the disclosure occurred, including time zone. Used for detailed audit trail and chronological sequencing of disclosure events.']
  emergency_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the health or safety emergency that justified disclosure without consent under the FERPA emergency exception. Required for emergency disclosures.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this disclosure record was last updated. Used for tracking changes to disclosure documentation and audit trail integrity.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The specific FERPA exception or legal authority under which the disclosure was made. Determines whether student consent was required and governs redisclosure restrictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written_consent|legitimate_educational_interest|directory_information|school_transfer|financial_aid|accreditation|judicial_order|subpoena|health_safety_emergency|state_local_authorities|audit_evaluation — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the recipient for follow-up communication and verification of disclosure receipt. Used for electronic disclosure confirmation.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or organization that received the disclosed education records. Required for FERPA audit trail and compliance verification.']
  recipient_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or institution to which the recipient is affiliated, if applicable. Used for tracking disclosures to educational institutions, employers, and government agencies.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the recipient for verification and follow-up regarding the disclosed records.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the third party to whom the education records were disclosed. Determines applicable FERPA exception category and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent|legal_guardian|school_official|other_institution|employer|financial_aid_agency|accrediting_organization|court_order|law_enforcement|health_safety_emergency|research_organization — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of education records disclosed. Used for categorizing disclosures by record sensitivity and regulatory reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transcript|enrollment_verification|financial_aid_records|disciplinary_records|health_records|academic_progress|degree_audit|course_schedule|grades|attendance — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  records_disclosed varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific education records that were disclosed, including document types, date ranges, and content categories. Required for FERPA audit trail completeness.']
  redisclosure_notification_text varchar [note: 'The exact text of the redisclosure restriction notification provided to the recipient, as required by FERPA regulations. Documents compliance with notification requirements.']
  redisclosure_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient was notified that the disclosed records may not be redisclosed without student consent. True if redisclosure restrictions apply per FERPA requirements.']
  student_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the student was notified of the disclosure, if notification was required. Used for compliance with FERPA notification requirements.']
  student_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student was notified of the disclosure. True if notification was provided, False if notification was not required or not yet completed.']

  Note: 'Tracks all FERPA-governed disclosures of student education records to third parties. Captures student identifier, disclosure date, recipient type (parent, employer, law enforcement, court order, accreditor), disclosure purpose, legal basis for disclosure (consent, legitimate educational interest, health/safety emergency, judicial order), records disclosed, authorizing official, and consent form reference. Mandatory for FERPA compliance audit trail and U.S. Department of Education reporting.'
}

Table compliance.accreditation_review {
  accreditation_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accreditation review record. Primary key for the accreditation review entity.']

  compliance_accreditation_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_standard. Business justification: Each accreditation review cycle evaluates the institution against a specific set of standards from the accrediting body. The review assesses compliance with individual standards. FK will be populated ']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: Accreditation reviews assess institutional technology infrastructure (SIS, LMS, library systems, advising platforms) for standards compliance. Accreditors require evidence that systems support learnin']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this accreditation review record in the system.']
  self_study_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the self-study coordinator, linking to the institutional human resources system.']
  student_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.student_org. Business justification: Program accreditation reviews assess student engagement and co-curricular learning.   updated_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this accreditation review record in the system.']
  accreditation_decision_date date [note: 'Official date the accrediting body issued its accreditation decision following review of the self-study and site visit reports.']
  accreditation_effective_date date [note: 'Date the accreditation status becomes effective. May differ from decision date due to administrative processing or appeal periods.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the current accreditation status expires, triggering the next review cycle. Null for denied or withdrawn status.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation standing resulting from the review: accredited (initial grant), reaffirmed (renewed), probation (conditional status with deficiencies), show-cause (at risk of loss), withdrawn (voluntarily surrendered), denied (not granted), or deferred (decision postponed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: accredited|reaffirmed|probation|show_cause|withdrawn|denied|deferred — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accreditation scope: institutional (entire institution), programmatic (specific academic program or department), or specialized (professional/discipline-specific).']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date the formal appeal of the accreditation decision was filed with the accrediting body. Null if no appeal was filed.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the institution or program filed a formal appeal of the accreditation decision (True) or not (False).']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the appeal process: upheld (original decision stands), overturned (decision reversed), modified (decision changed), or pending (under review). Null if no appeal was filed.']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'Date the appeal process was concluded and final outcome determined. Null if no appeal was filed or appeal is still pending.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit CIP code identifying the academic discipline or program area under review (format: XX.XXXX). Used for programmatic accreditation alignment.']
