  keyholder_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated IPEDS Keyholder who has authority to certify and submit data on behalf of the institution to NCES.']
  keyholder_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the IPEDS Keyholder, documenting their institutional authority and responsibility for federal reporting compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent update to this IPEDS submission record, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  lock_date date [note: 'The date on which the institution locked the submission in the IPEDS system, preventing further edits and finalizing the data for NCES review.']
  nces_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation identifier issued by NCES upon successful submission receipt, serving as proof of compliance and audit documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for institutional staff to document special circumstances, data anomalies, methodology changes, or other contextual information relevant to this submission.']
  peer_comparison_group varchar [note: 'The peer group classification assigned by NCES or selected by the institution for benchmarking purposes in IPEDS data feedback reports.']
  preparer_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the survey preparer for NCES communication and institutional coordination regarding this submission.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the institutional staff member responsible for compiling and preparing the IPEDS survey data for this submission.']
  preparer_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the survey preparer, used by NCES for clarification questions or submission issues.']
  public_disclosure_date date [note: 'The date on which NCES makes this submission data publicly available through College Navigator and IPEDS Data Center, fulfilling transparency requirements.']
  responsible_office varchar [note: 'Name of the institutional office or department responsible for this IPEDS submission, typically Institutional Research or Compliance.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the data was revised after initial submission, documenting the nature of the error or data quality issue that necessitated correction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the institutional system of record from which the IPEDS data was extracted, documenting data lineage and supporting audit requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the institution officially submitted the completed IPEDS survey component to NCES through the IPEDS Data Collection System.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The technical method used to transmit the IPEDS data to NCES, indicating whether data was manually entered, uploaded via file, or integrated via API.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the IPEDS submission, typically combining institution identifier, survey component, and academic year for external reference and tracking.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IPEDS submission indicating its progress through the institutional review, submission, and NCES acceptance workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|submitted|locked|accepted|rejected|revision_required — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the IPEDS submission was transmitted to NCES, used for audit trail and compliance verification purposes.']
  survey_component varchar [note: 'The specific IPEDS survey component being submitted. Each component collects different institutional data required by NCES for federal reporting compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Institutional Characteristics|Completions|12-month Enrollment|Fall Enrollment|Finance|Human Resources|Student Financial Aid|Graduation Rates|Outcome Measures|Admissions — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  survey_year varchar [note: 'The academic year for which the IPEDS data is being reported, typically represented as the ending year (e.g., 2024 for the 2023-2024 academic year).']
  unitid varchar [note: 'Six-digit unique identifier assigned by NCES to each postsecondary institution participating in federal student aid programs. This is the primary institutional identifier in IPEDS.']

  Note: 'Tracks annual IPEDS (Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System) survey submissions including completion, enrollment, graduation rate, finance, and human resources surveys required by NCES for federal reporting compliance'
}

Table compliance.standard_requirement_mapping {
  standard_requirement_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the standard_requirement_mapping association']
  compliance_accreditation_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the accreditation standard being mapped to regulatory requirements.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement that underlies or is satisfied by the accreditation standard.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status for this specific standard-requirement mapping. Indicates whether the institution is meeting both the standard and the underlying requirement through this mapping.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope or extent to which the accreditation standard addresses the regulatory requirement. May specify which aspects of the requirement are covered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mapping record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this mapping became effective and recognized by the institution. Used to track when the crosswalk was established or updated.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to documentation or evidence that supports this mapping, such as accreditor guidance documents, legal opinions, or institutional policy references.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when this mapping is no longer valid, typically due to changes in standards or regulations. Null if the mapping is currently active.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this mapping was most recently reviewed or validated by the compliance office. Used to ensure mappings remain current as standards and regulations evolve.']
  mapping_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for institutional notes about this specific mapping, including rationale, interpretation guidance, or special considerations.']
  mapping_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the accreditation standard relates to the regulatory requirement: direct (standard explicitly implements the regulation), indirect (standard addresses the regulation through related criteria), partial (standard covers only part of the requirement), full (standard fully satisfies the requirement).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this mapping to ensure continued accuracy and relevance.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that most recently updated this mapping record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mapping record was most recently updated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this mapping record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance mapping between accreditation standards and underlying regulatory requirements. It captures the explicit crosswalk maintained by institutions to demonstrate how accreditation standards satisfy federal and state regulatory obligations. Each record links one accreditation standard to one regulatory requirement with mapping metadata including effective dates, mapping type, coverage scope, and compliance status. This is a core compliance artifact used during self-studies, audits, and regulatory reporting.. Existence Justification: In higher education compliance operations, institutions actively maintain standard-requirement crosswalk matrices that map accreditation standards to underlying federal and state regulatory requirements. One accreditation standard frequently addresses multiple regulatory requirements (e.g., a faculty qualification standard may satisfy Title IV, state authorization, and professional accreditation requirements), and one regulatory requirement is typically addressed by multiple accreditation standards (e.g., FERPA requirements are addressed through standards on records management, student services, and IT security). These mappings are living documents maintained by compliance offices, updated during self-studies, and used for audit preparation and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table compliance.policy_implementation {
  policy_implementation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this policy-to-regulatory-requirement implementation mapping. Primary key.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the institutional policy that implements the regulatory requirement']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement being addressed by the policy']
  citation_mapping varchar [note: 'Specific sections or provisions within the policy that address the regulatory requirement. Enables precise cross-referencing for audits and accreditation reviews.']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of how well this policy satisfies the regulatory requirement: fully_compliant (meets all obligations), substantially_compliant (meets most with minor gaps), partially_compliant (significant gaps exist), non_compliant (does not meet requirement), not_applicable (requirement does not apply).']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Extent to which this policy addresses the regulatory requirement: full (completely satisfies), partial (addresses some aspects), supplemental (provides additional guidance), indirect (supports compliance indirectly).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this implementation mapping record was first created in the compliance management system.']
  gap_analysis_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from compliance reviews identifying any gaps, deficiencies, or areas where the policy does not fully address the regulatory requirement. Used for remediation planning and accreditation self-studies.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date when this policy was formally adopted or updated to address this specific regulatory requirement. Tracks when the implementation relationship became effective.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date when this specific policy-to-requirement mapping was last reviewed or audited for adequacy and compliance. Distinct from audits of the policy or requirement individually.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this implementation mapping record was most recently modified, reflecting changes to coverage scope, compliance level, or gap analysis findings.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review of this implementation mapping. Calculated based on regulatory review cycles and institutional audit schedules.']
  responsible_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or position of the compliance officer or subject matter expert responsible for reviewing and certifying this implementation mapping.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the implementation mapping review: current (recently verified), needs_review (scheduled for assessment), under_review (actively being evaluated), gap_identified (deficiency found), compliant (verified as adequate).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the implementation relationship between institutional policies and regulatory requirements. It captures how each policy addresses specific regulatory obligations, including implementation dates, coverage scope, compliance levels, and gap analysis findings. Each record links one policy to one regulatory requirement with attributes that exist only in the context of this implementation relationship.. Existence Justification: In higher education compliance operations, institutional policies routinely implement multiple regulatory requirements (e.g., a Title IX policy addresses federal Title IX regulations, Clery Act reporting, and state anti-discrimination laws), and regulatory requirements are typically addressed by multiple policies (e.g., FERPA requirements are implemented through records management policies, student conduct policies, and IT security policies). Compliance officers actively manage policy-to-regulation mappings as operational records with implementation dates, coverage assessments, gap analysis findings, and review statuses - this is required for accreditation self-studies and federal program reviews.'
}

Table compliance.accrediting_body {
  accrediting_body_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for accrediting_body']
  parent_accrediting_body_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on accrediting_body (parent_accrediting_body_id)']
  accreditation_cycle_years varchar [note: 'Standard number of years in the accreditation cycle used by this accrediting body (e.g., 5, 7, 10 years).']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Type of accreditation provided by this body: institutional (whole institution), programmatic (specific programs), specialized (specific disciplines), national, or regional.']
  acronym varchar [note: 'Standard acronym or abbreviation used to identify the accrediting body (e.g., HLC, SACSCOC, ABET).']
  annual_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accredited institutions must submit annual reports to this accrediting body.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the accrediting body headquarters is located.']
  complaint_process_url varchar [note: 'URL to the accrediting body\'s complaint and grievance process documentation.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the accrediting body.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the accrediting body.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the accrediting body is headquartered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accrediting body record was first created in the system.']
  discipline_focus varchar [note: 'Primary academic discipline or professional field focus of the accrediting body for specialized or programmatic accreditors (e.g., Engineering, Business, Nursing, Law).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the accrediting body\'s jurisdiction: regional (specific U.S. region), national (entire U.S.), or international.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accrediting body is currently active and operational.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accrediting body record was last updated in the system.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent recognition review conducted by the recognizing authority.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address for the accrediting body headquarters.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address for the accrediting body headquarters (suite, floor, building).']
  member_institution_count varchar [note: 'Total number of institutions or programs currently accredited by this accrediting body.']
  name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the accrediting body or accreditation agency.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next recognition review by the recognizing authority.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special considerations related to this accrediting body.']
  peer_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accrediting body requires peer review as part of its accreditation process.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the accrediting body headquarters.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'Date when the accrediting body was first recognized by the U.S. Department of Education or CHEA.']
  recognition_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current recognition period expires and renewal is required.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current recognition status of the accrediting body by the U.S. Department of Education or CHEA.']
  recognizing_authority varchar [note: 'The authority that recognizes this accrediting body: USDE (U.S. Department of Education), CHEA (Council for Higher Education Accreditation), both, other, or none.']
  region varchar [note: 'Specific geographic region served by the accrediting body if it is a regional accreditor (e.g., Middle States, New England, North Central, Northwest, Southern, Western).']
  self_study_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accrediting body requires institutions to complete a comprehensive self-study as part of the accreditation process.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used short name for the accrediting body.']
  site_visit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accrediting body requires on-site evaluation visits as part of the accreditation process.']
  standards_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current version of accreditation standards became effective.']
  standards_version varchar [note: 'Current version or edition of the accreditation standards used by this accrediting body.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the accrediting body headquarters is located.']
  substantive_change_policy_url varchar [note: 'URL to the accrediting body\'s substantive change policy documentation.']
  title_iv_eligibility_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accreditation by this body is required for institutions to participate in federal Title IV student financial aid programs.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the accrediting body.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for accrediting_body. Referenced by accrediting_body_id.'
}

Table compliance.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit']
  followup_audit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit (followup_audit_id)']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: audit currently has regulatory_framework as a STRING field (e.g., \'Title IV\', \'FERPA\', \'NCAA\'). Adding regulatory_requirement_id FK provides structured linkage to the regulatory_requirement master tab']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The name of the accrediting agency or regulatory body for which this audit was conducted, such as HLC, SACSCOC, ABET, or U.S. Department of Education.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The date when audit fieldwork was actually completed, which may differ from the scheduled end date.']
  actual_remediation_date date [note: 'The date when corrective actions were verified as fully implemented and audit findings were closed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The date when audit fieldwork actually commenced, which may differ from the scheduled start date due to operational factors.']
  audit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred for the audit engagement, including internal staff time and external fees, expressed in U.S. dollars.']
  audit_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of staff hours invested in conducting the audit engagement, including planning, fieldwork, and reporting.']
  audit_methodology varchar [note: 'The audit approach and techniques used, such as risk-based audit, compliance audit, process audit, or systems audit.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the audit, typically formatted as AUD-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  audit_objective varchar [note: 'The primary purpose and goals of the audit engagement, describing what the audit seeks to accomplish or verify.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'The breadth and boundaries of the audit engagement, defining what organizational units, processes, or systems are included in the examination.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit. Planned audits are scheduled but not started, in-progress audits are actively being conducted, fieldwork indicates data collection phase, reporting indicates draft preparation, issued means final report delivered, and closed indicates all follow-up completed.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on its purpose and scope. Internal audits are conducted by institutional staff, external audits by third parties, compliance audits verify regulatory adherence, financial audits examine fiscal controls, operational audits assess process efficiency, and academic audits review program quality.']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'The name of the organization or department conducting the audit, such as Internal Audit, external audit firm name, or regulatory agency.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'The determination of whether the audited area meets applicable regulatory, policy, or accreditation requirements.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'The data classification level assigned to the audit report and working papers, determining who may access the audit documentation.']
  corrective_action_plan_received_date date [note: 'The date when management\'s corrective action plan was received by the audit function.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audit record was first created in the system.']
  critical_finding_count varchar [note: 'The number of critical-severity findings identified that require immediate management attention and remediation.']
  department_audited varchar [note: 'The name of the academic or administrative department that is the subject of the audit.']
  estimated_remediation_date date [note: 'The target date by which management commits to fully implement corrective actions to address audit findings.']
  external_auditor_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fee paid to external audit firms or consultants for conducting the audit, expressed in U.S. dollars. Null for internal audits.']
  finding_count varchar [note: 'The total number of audit findings identified during the engagement, including all severity levels.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The date by which management must submit corrective action plans or follow-up responses to audit findings.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether management is required to provide follow-up responses or corrective action plans for audit findings.']
  high_finding_count varchar [note: 'The number of high-severity findings identified that represent significant control weaknesses.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audit record was most recently updated.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the principal auditor responsible for conducting and overseeing the audit engagement.']
  low_finding_count varchar [note: 'The number of low-severity findings identified that represent minor control weaknesses or opportunities for improvement.']
  medium_finding_count varchar [note: 'The number of medium-severity findings identified that represent moderate control weaknesses.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional context, observations, or special circumstances related to the audit engagement.']
  overall_opinion varchar [note: 'The auditor\'s overall conclusion regarding the adequacy and effectiveness of controls in the audited area. Satisfactory indicates effective controls, needs improvement indicates control gaps requiring attention, unsatisfactory indicates significant control deficiencies, and adverse indicates pervasive control failures.']
  population_size varchar [note: 'The total number of items or transactions in the population from which the audit sample was drawn.']
  report_distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of roles or individuals who received the final audit report, such as Board of Trustees, President, CFO, Audit Committee.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'The date when the final audit report was formally issued to stakeholders.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'The overall risk assessment assigned to the audit area based on potential impact and likelihood of control failures. Critical indicates severe institutional risk, high indicates significant risk, moderate indicates manageable risk, and low indicates minimal risk.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of items or transactions selected for detailed testing during the audit.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'The technique used to select items for testing during the audit. Statistical sampling uses probability theory, judgmental sampling relies on auditor expertise, random sampling selects items by chance, stratified sampling divides population into subgroups, and census examines all items.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'The planned date when the audit engagement is scheduled to be completed.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned date when the audit engagement is scheduled to begin.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit. Referenced by audit_id.'
}

Table compliance.certificate_template {
  certificate_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for certificate_template']
  accreditation_body_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external accreditation or regulatory body that recognizes or requires this certificate type.']
  approved_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this certificate template for active use.']
  competency_framework_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competency or learning outcomes framework associated with this certificate template.']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic department or administrative unit responsible for this certificate template.']
  identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this certificate template record.']
  last_modified_by_user_identity_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this certificate template record.']
  parent_certificate_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on certificate_template (parent_certificate_template_id)']
  print_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external print vendor or service provider used for physical certificate production.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the academic or professional program associated with this certificate template.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certificates generated from this template require administrative approval before issuance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certificate template was approved for active use.']
  background_image_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the background image or watermark used in the certificate template.']
  blockchain_verification_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certificates generated from this template are registered on a blockchain for tamper-proof verification.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or accreditation framework that this certificate template supports (e.g., Title IX, Clery Act, FERPA, NCAA, ADA, regional accreditor standards). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: title_ix|clery_act|ferpa|ncaa|ada|sacscoc|hlc|msche|wscuc|nwccu|neche — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certificate template record was first created in the system.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credential represented by this certificate template within the institutional credential framework.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of academic credit hours or continuing education units represented by the credential on this certificate template.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the certificate template purpose, usage context, and intended recipients.']
  digital_signature_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certificates generated from this template support digital signature technology for authentication.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this certificate template is no longer active for new certificate generation. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this certificate template becomes active and available for use in certificate generation.']
  expiration_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after issuance when certificates from this template expire and require renewal. Null indicates no expiration.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the institutional office, department, or official authorized to issue certificates from this template.']
  issuing_authority_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the person or office authorized to issue certificates from this template (e.g., Registrar, Dean, President).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language of the certificate template (e.g., en, es, fr).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certificate template record was most recently modified.']
  layout_format varchar [note: 'Page orientation format for the certificate template.']
  logo_image_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the institutional logo image displayed on the certificate.']
  page_size varchar [note: 'Standard page size specification for the certificate template.']
  paper_stock_specification varchar [note: 'Technical specification of the paper stock used for physical certificates including weight, finish, and security features.']
  public_display_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certificates issued from this template can be publicly displayed or shared by recipients.']
  renewal_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certificates issued from this template are eligible for renewal after expiration.']
  seal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an official institutional seal or embossment is required on certificates generated from this template.']
  security_features varchar [note: 'Description of security features embedded in the certificate template such as watermarks, holograms, QR codes, or blockchain verification.']
  signature_block_count varchar [note: 'Number of signature blocks included in the certificate template for authorized signatories.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certificate template indicating its availability for use.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the certificate template used for external reference and system integration.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the certificate template describing its purpose or program.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the certificate template by its primary purpose within the institution.']
  transcript_notation varchar [note: 'Standard text notation that appears on the academic transcript when this certificate is awarded.']
  verification_url varchar [note: 'URL where third parties can verify the authenticity of certificates issued from this template.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the certificate template to track revisions and updates.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for certificate_template. Referenced by certificate_template_id.'
}

Table hr.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the non-faculty staff employee record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the employee master data product sourced from Workday HCM.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'The employee_id of the direct supervisor or manager for this employee. Supports org hierarchy traversal, performance management workflows, and approval routing in Workday HCM.']
  annual_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'The employee\'s annualized base salary in USD. For part-time employees, this reflects the annualized equivalent of their scheduled hours. Used for budget planning, CUPA-HR benchmarking, and compensation equity analysis.']
  benefits_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible to enroll in institutional benefit plans (health, dental, vision, retirement). Determined by employment type, FTE percentage, and ACA eligibility rules.']
  citizenship_status varchar [note: 'The employee\'s citizenship or immigration status relevant to employment eligibility verification. Used for I-9 compliance, visa sponsorship tracking, and export control compliance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The financial cost center code to which the employee\'s compensation is charged. Used for budget allocation, financial reporting, and Oracle PeopleSoft Financials integration.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The institutional department or organizational unit code to which the employee is primarily assigned. Used for cost allocation, org hierarchy reporting, and IPEDS submissions.']
  disability_status varchar [note: 'The employee\'s self-reported disability status per Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act. Required for federal contractor affirmative action reporting and ADA accommodation tracking.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'The EEO-6 job category assigned to the employee for federal equal employment opportunity reporting. Categories include Executive/Administrative, Professional, Technical, Clerical, Skilled Crafts, and Service/Maintenance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive_administrative|professional|technical_paraprofessional|clerical_secretarial|skilled_crafts|service_maintenance — promote to reference product]']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee\'s employment relationship with the institution. Drives access provisioning, payroll processing, and workforce reporting.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s engagement basis with the institution. Determines benefit eligibility, FTE calculation, and IPEDS workforce reporting categories.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'The employee\'s FLSA overtime exemption status. Exempt employees are salaried and not eligible for overtime pay; non-exempt employees are hourly and eligible for overtime. Drives payroll processing rules.']
  fte_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The employee\'s FTE percentage expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.0000 = full-time, 0.5000 = half-time). Used for IPEDS HR Survey reporting, benefit eligibility determination, and workforce planning analytics.']
  gender varchar [note: 'The employee\'s self-reported gender identity. Required for IPEDS HR Survey federal reporting and EEO-6 compliance. Collected and stored in accordance with FERPA and institutional privacy policies.']
  highest_degree_earned varchar [note: 'The highest academic degree attained by the employee. Used for IPEDS HR Survey reporting, minimum qualification verification, and workforce education analytics.']
  hire_date date [note: 'The official date the employee began employment at the institution. Used for benefits eligibility determination, service anniversary calculations, and IPEDS reporting.']
  institution_employee_number varchar [note: 'The institution-assigned employee badge or personnel number used across campus systems (ERP, badge access, payroll). Serves as the human-readable cross-system identifier for the employee.']
  institutional_email varchar [note: 'The official institution-issued email address for the employee. Primary contact channel for HR communications, SSO authentication, and directory services.']
  job_profile_code varchar [note: 'The Workday HCM job profile identifier that defines the standard set of responsibilities, qualifications, and compensation grade for this position type across the institution.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The official institutional job title for the employee\'s current position as defined in the position control system. Used in directories, org charts, and CUPA-HR salary benchmarking.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'The employee\'s legal given (first) name as recorded on official government-issued identification. Used for payroll, tax reporting, and FERPA-compliant records.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'The employee\'s legal surname (family name) as recorded on official government-issued identification. Used for payroll, tax reporting, and FERPA-compliant records.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'The employee\'s legal middle name or initial as recorded on official identification. Used for disambiguation and formal legal documents.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'The employee\'s personal mobile phone number. Used for emergency notifications, two-factor authentication, and urgent HR communications.']
  original_hire_date date [note: 'The employee\'s first-ever hire date at the institution, preserved across rehires and position changes. Used for continuous service calculations, retirement vesting, and long-service recognition.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the employee is paid. Drives payroll processing schedules and period-to-date compensation calculations in Workday HCM Payroll.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'The compensation grade or band assigned to the employee\'s position, defining the salary range minimum, midpoint, and maximum. Used for salary administration and CUPA-HR benchmarking.']
  personal_email varchar [note: 'The employee\'s personal (non-institutional) email address. Used for emergency contact, offboarding communications, and benefit enrollment notifications.']
  position_number varchar [note: 'The unique position control number assigned to the budgeted position the employee occupies. Links to the position control system for headcount and budget management.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the employee prefers to be addressed by in day-to-day institutional communications, directories, and LMS systems. May differ from legal name.']
  race_ethnicity_code varchar [note: 'The employee\'s self-reported race and ethnicity classification per OMB standards. Required for IPEDS HR Survey and EEO-6 federal reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hispanic_latino|american_indian_alaska_native|asian|black_african_american|native_hawaiian_pacific_islander|white|two_or_more_races — promote to reference product]']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the employee record was first created in the Workday HCM system of record. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and onboarding workflow tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to the employee record in the Workday HCM system of record. Used for incremental ETL processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  rehire_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for rehire at the institution following separation. Set during offboarding review and used to screen future applications in Slate CRM and Workday Recruiting.']
  remote_work_type varchar [note: 'The employee\'s approved work arrangement classification indicating whether they work on-site, in a hybrid arrangement, or fully remote. Used for space planning, tax nexus compliance, and workforce analytics.']
  retirement_plan_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the retirement plan in which the employee participates (e.g., TIAA-CREF 403(b), state pension plan). Used for retirement contribution processing and ERISA compliance reporting.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date the employee\'s employment with the institution ended. Null for active employees. Used for offboarding workflows, final pay processing, and workforce attrition analytics.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The coded reason for the employee\'s separation from the institution (e.g., voluntary resignation, retirement, involuntary termination, end of contract). Used for turnover analysis and IPEDS reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary_resignation|retirement|involuntary_termination|end_of_contract|death|transfer_out — promote to reference product]']
  union_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the collective bargaining unit or union to which the employee belongs, if applicable. Drives contract-specific pay rules, leave accruals, and grievance tracking.']
  veteran_status varchar [note: 'The employee\'s self-reported protected veteran status per VEVRAA requirements. Required for VETS-4212 federal contractor reporting and affirmative action planning.']
  visa_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the employee\'s work authorization visa. Used to trigger renewal workflows and ensure continuous employment eligibility compliance.']
  visa_type varchar [note: 'The type of work authorization visa held by the employee if applicable (e.g., H-1B, J-1, O-1, TN). Null for U.S. citizens and permanent residents. Used for visa expiration tracking and compliance.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the primary physical work location or campus building assigned to the employee. Links to the Archibus facilities management system for space planning and emergency management.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'The employee\'s primary institutional work telephone number including area code. Used for directory listings and HR communications.']
  workday_worker_reference varchar [note: 'The native worker identifier assigned by Workday HCM. Used as the operational system-of-record key for cross-system integration and ETL reconciliation with Workday HCM.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Workday HCM worker classification distinguishing regular employees from contingent workers, interns, and graduate assistants. Governs payroll processing rules and benefit eligibility.']

  Note: 'Master record for all non-faculty staff employees at the institution. Captures full identity, demographic, employment classification, job title, department assignment, FTE status, FLSA classification, EEO category, hire date, termination date, and employment type (full-time, part-time, temporary, contract). Serves as the SSOT for non-faculty workforce identity sourced from Workday HCM. Excludes faculty, who are owned by the faculty domain.'
}

Table hr.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the authorized position control record within the institution. Primary key for the position data product in the HR domain. Sourced from Workday HCM Position Management.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical work location (building, campus, or site) assigned to this position. Used in Archibus space planning, facilities management, and geographic workforce distribution reporting.']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsored research grant or contract funding this position, applicable when position_type is grant_funded. Links to the Kuali Research award record for effort reporting and F&A cost allocation.']
  job_family_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job family grouping that classifies this position within the institution\'s job architecture. Supports CUPA-HR benchmarking, compensation band alignment, and career pathway analysis.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.job_profile. Business justification: Position control records must reference the institutional job classification catalog. Position has job_code (STRING) as a denormalized business key, but lacks the formal FK to job_profile. This FK est']
  position_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational department or cost center to which this position is assigned. Used for workforce planning, budget allocation, and IPEDS FTE reporting by organizational unit.']
  position_supervisory_org_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Workday HCM supervisory organization to which this position belongs. Defines the management hierarchy, reporting structure, and span of control for workforce analytics.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier of the position to which this position directly reports in the organizational hierarchy. Enables org chart construction and management chain analysis without requiring an active incumbent.']
  authorized_date date [note: 'The date on which the position was formally authorized and approved by institutional leadership or the budget office. Marks the effective start of the position\'s existence in the position control system.']
  available_for_overlap boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position allows an overlap period where both an outgoing and incoming incumbent can be simultaneously active. Used in transition planning and headcount management within Workday HCM.']
  budgeted_annual_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'The annual salary amount budgeted for this position in the institutional operating budget. Used in position control, budget encumbrance calculations, and financial planning. Expressed in USD. Distinct from the incumbent\'s actual salary.']
  chartfield_string varchar [note: 'The Oracle PeopleSoft Financials chartfield string (combination of fund, department, program, project, and account codes) used to budget and encumber salary costs for this position. Enables financial reconciliation between HR and Finance systems.']
  compensation_grade_profile varchar [note: 'The specific compensation grade profile within Workday HCM that defines the salary range steps and progression rules applicable to this position. Supports structured merit and equity review processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the position record was first created in Workday HCM Position Management. Provides audit trail for position control governance and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  cupa_hr_benchmark_code varchar [note: 'The CUPA-HR survey benchmark code assigned to this position for participation in the annual CUPA-HR Staff Salary Survey. Enables peer institution salary comparisons and market pricing for compensation administration.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'EEO-1 job category assigned to the position as required by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) for federal reporting. Categories include Executive/Senior Level Officials, First/Mid-Level Officials, Professionals, Technicians, Sales Workers, Administrative Support, Craft Workers, Operatives, Laborers/Helpers, and Service Workers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exec_senior|first_mid_officials|professionals|technicians|sales|admin_support|craft|operatives|laborers|service — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the current position attributes (title, grade, FTE, department) are effective. Supports position history tracking and point-in-time workforce reporting in Workday HCM.']
  employee_category varchar [note: 'Broad institutional classification of the position\'s workforce category for IPEDS reporting and HR analytics. Distinguishes staff, administrators, executives, technical/paraprofessional, and service/maintenance roles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: staff|administrator|executive|technical|service|professional — promote to reference product]']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the position is scheduled to end or was eliminated. Applicable to temporary, grant-funded, and seasonal positions with defined terms. Null for permanent positions with no planned end.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Designation of the position under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) indicating whether the role is exempt from overtime pay requirements (exempt) or entitled to overtime compensation (non_exempt). Critical for payroll compliance and labor law adherence.']
  frozen_date date [note: 'The date on which the position was placed in frozen status, suspending active recruitment. Used in budget freeze tracking and workforce planning analysis.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,4) [note: 'The authorized Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) value for this position, expressed as a decimal fraction of a full-time workload (e.g., 1.0000 for full-time, 0.5000 for half-time). Used in IPEDS FTE workforce reporting and institutional workforce planning.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'The primary funding source supporting this position\'s salary and benefits costs. General fund positions are supported by institutional operating budget; grant positions are funded by sponsored research awards; auxiliary positions are funded by self-supporting units; restricted and designated funds have specific use constraints.']
  headcount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the position is designated as full-time or part-time based on the authorized FTE allocation and institutional policy thresholds. Used in IPEDS headcount reporting and workforce analytics.']
  ipeds_occupation_category varchar [note: 'The IPEDS occupation category code assigned to this position for federal human resources reporting to the U.S. Department of Education. Classifies positions into IPEDS-defined staff categories for annual HR survey submission.']
  is_benefits_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for institutional employee benefits (health insurance, retirement, tuition remission, etc.) based on FTE allocation and position type. Drives benefits enrollment eligibility in Workday HCM Benefits.']
  is_critical_position boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position has been designated as critical to institutional operations or strategic mission. Critical positions receive priority in succession planning and budget protection during financial constraints.']
  is_remote_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements per institutional telework policy. Supports workforce planning, facilities space planning, and Archibus space utilization analysis.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job code from the institution\'s job catalog in Workday HCM that classifies the position\'s duties and responsibilities. Used in CUPA-HR benchmarking, IPEDS reporting, and compensation equity analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the position record was most recently updated in Workday HCM. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  max_incumbents varchar [note: 'The maximum number of employees authorized to hold this position simultaneously. Typically 1 for standard positions; greater than 1 for pooled positions supporting multiple incumbents (e.g., graduate assistant pools).']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the incumbent of this position is paid (e.g., biweekly, semimonthly, monthly). Drives payroll processing cycles in Workday HCM Payroll.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation pay grade or salary band assigned to the position within the institution\'s salary administration structure (e.g., Grade 7, Band E). Determines the minimum, midpoint, and maximum salary range applicable to the position.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the position\'s primary duties, responsibilities, required qualifications, and scope of work. Used in recruitment postings, performance management, and accreditation documentation.']
  position_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the authorized position in Workday HCM (e.g., P-00012345). Used in workforce planning, IPEDS reporting, and budget reconciliation across institutional systems.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the authorized position. Open indicates the position is vacant and available for recruitment; filled indicates an active incumbent; frozen indicates the position is budgeted but hiring is suspended; eliminated indicates the position has been abolished; pending_approval indicates the position is awaiting authorization.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the position\'s funding and employment nature. Regular positions are permanently budgeted; temporary positions are time-limited; grant-funded positions are supported by sponsored research awards; seasonal positions are cyclical; pooled positions support variable staffing needs.']
  requires_background_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position requires a pre-employment background check as a condition of hire per institutional policy or regulatory requirement (e.g., positions working with minors, financial fiduciary roles, law enforcement).']
  salary_maximum decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum annual salary defined for the position\'s pay grade or compensation band. Used in salary administration, offer approvals, and CUPA-HR benchmarking. Expressed in USD.']
  salary_midpoint decimal(12,2) [note: 'The midpoint annual salary for the position\'s pay grade or compensation band, representing the market reference point. Used in compa-ratio analysis, equity reviews, and CUPA-HR benchmarking. Expressed in USD.']
  salary_minimum decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum annual salary defined for the position\'s pay grade or compensation band. Used in salary administration, equity analysis, and CUPA-HR benchmarking. Expressed in USD.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code assigned to this position. Used in IPEDS reporting, labor market analysis, and H-1B visa sponsorship documentation for international hires.']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'The standard number of scheduled work hours per week for this position as defined in Workday HCM. Used to calculate FTE allocation, benefits eligibility thresholds under the ACA, and overtime liability under FLSA.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official working title of the position as defined in Workday HCM and used in institutional communications, org charts, and CUPA-HR benchmarking submissions.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the collective bargaining unit or union affiliation applicable to this position, if any. Determines applicable labor contract terms, grievance procedures, and wage scales. Null for non-unionized positions.']

  Note: 'Authorized position control record representing a budgeted staff role within the institution. Tracks position number, title, department, FTE allocation, funding source, FLSA classification, pay grade, position type (regular, temporary, grant-funded), and open/filled/frozen status. Supports institutional workforce planning and IPEDS FTE reporting. Sourced from Workday HCM Position Management.'
}

Table hr.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the job profile record in the institutional catalog. Primary key for the hr.job_profile data product.']
  job_family_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.job_family']
  workday_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Native system identifier for this job profile record in Workday HCM, used for ETL reconciliation, system-of-record traceability, and API integration with downstream HR analytics platforms.']
  ada_essential_functions_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the essential physical and cognitive functions of this job profile have been formally documented in compliance with ADA requirements, supporting reasonable accommodation determinations and interactive process management.']
  background_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-employment background check is required for all incumbents hired into this job profile. Supports compliance with institutional policy, Title IX, and Clery Act obligations for positions with student contact.']
  bargaining_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific collective bargaining unit or union contract applicable to this job profile when union_eligible is true (e.g., \'AFSCME\', \'AAUP\', \'SEIU\'). Used for contract administration and labor cost reporting.']
  benefits_eligibility_class varchar [note: 'Benefits eligibility classification assigned to this job profile determining which institutional benefit plans (health, dental, retirement, tuition waiver) are available to incumbents. Drives ACA compliance tracking and benefits cost modeling.']
  certifications_required varchar [note: 'Free-text description of mandatory professional licenses or certifications required for this job profile (e.g., \'CPA\', \'PMP\', \'PE License\', \'RN License\'). Used in applicant screening and compliance verification.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'CIP code aligned to the functional area or academic discipline most closely associated with this job profile, enabling linkage between workforce data and academic program structures. Used for IPEDS reporting and institutional research analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was first created in the institutional data catalog. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  critical_job boolean [note: 'Flags this job profile as mission-critical to institutional operations, triggering enhanced succession planning, targeted retention strategies, and priority position-fill timelines in workforce planning.']
  cupa_hr_survey_code varchar [note: 'CUPA-HR benchmark survey position code mapped to this job profile, enabling direct salary benchmarking against national higher-education compensation survey data. Critical for compensation equity analysis and market pricing.']
  driving_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incumbents in this job profile are required to operate institutional or personal vehicles as part of their duties, triggering MVR (Motor Vehicle Record) check requirements and institutional auto liability coverage.']
  eeo1_category varchar [note: 'EEO-1 occupational category assigned to this job profile as required by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission for annual EEO-1 Component 1 workforce demographic reporting. Drives federal compliance reporting and diversity analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Executive/Senior Officials|First/Mid Officials|Professionals|Technicians|Sales Workers|Administrative Support|Craft Workers|Operatives|Laborers|Service Workers — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this job profile version became effective and available for use in position creation and hiring actions. Supports point-in-time compensation analysis and audit trail requirements.']
  employee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement associated with this job profile indicating whether positions are regular (continuing), temporary, seasonal, or casual. Affects benefits eligibility, position control, and FTE workforce reporting.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the job profile under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) indicating whether incumbents are exempt from overtime pay requirements. Critical for payroll compliance, overtime liability management, and U.S. Department of Labor regulatory reporting.']
  fte_capacity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard FTE value associated with this job profile (typically 1.00 for full-time, 0.50 for half-time). Used in position control, workforce planning, and IPEDS FTE headcount calculations.']
  inactive_date date [note: 'Date on which this job profile was deactivated or deprecated in the institutional catalog. Null for currently active profiles. Used for historical workforce reporting and position control audits.']
  ipeds_occupational_category varchar [note: 'IPEDS staff occupational category assigned to this job profile for annual IPEDS Human Resources (HR) survey submission to the U.S. Department of Education. Categories include Instructional Staff, Research Staff, Public Service Staff, Management, Business/Financial Operations, etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Instructional|Research|Public Service|Management|Business/Financial|Computer/Mathematical|Architecture/Engineering|Life/Physical/Social Science|Community Service|Legal|Arts/Design|Healthcare|Service|Sales|Office/Admin|Natural Resources|Construction|Maintenance|Production|Transportation — promote to reference product]']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the job profile within Workday HCM and is referenced in position control, offer letters, and CUPA-HR survey submissions. Serves as the business-facing identifier across HR systems.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Full detailed narrative of duties, responsibilities, and essential functions for this job profile. Serves as the authoritative document for ADA essential functions determination, FLSA classification justification, and accreditation staff qualification documentation.']
  job_family_group varchar [note: 'Higher-level cluster that aggregates multiple job families into a strategic workforce segment (e.g., \'Administrative and Professional\', \'Technical and Skilled Trades\', \'Executive Leadership\'). Supports institutional workforce analytics and IPEDS reporting.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Granular career level designation within a job family indicating seniority and scope (e.g., \'Level 1\', \'Level 2\', \'Senior\', \'Principal\', \'Lead\'). Drives pay grade assignment and career progression frameworks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Entry|Associate|Mid|Senior|Principal|Lead|Expert|Fellow — promote to reference product]']
  job_summary varchar [note: 'Concise narrative description of the primary purpose and scope of the job profile, typically 2-4 sentences. Used in job postings, offer letters, and accreditation documentation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official human-readable title of the job profile as it appears on offer letters, organizational charts, and institutional directories (e.g., \'Senior Financial Analyst\', \'Director of Admissions\').']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this job profile was last formally reviewed and validated by HR Compensation or the Classification and Compensation team. Supports cyclical job evaluation programs and accreditation documentation.']
  management_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical management tier assigned to the job profile indicating span of control and organizational authority. Used for succession planning, compensation banding, and organizational design analytics.']
  min_education_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum academic credential required for appointment to this job profile. Used in applicant screening, position posting compliance, and accreditation documentation for staff qualification standards.']
  min_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for appointment to this job profile. Used in applicant screening, job posting, and compensation equity analysis.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Institutional pay grade or salary band code assigned to this job profile (e.g., \'P3\', \'M5\', \'E2\'). Determines the applicable salary range minimum, midpoint, and maximum for compensation administration and NACUBO benchmarking.']
  pay_grade_max_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Maximum annual base salary in US dollars defined for this pay grade and job profile. Caps compensation for incumbents and triggers reclassification review when employees approach or exceed this threshold.']
  pay_grade_midpoint_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Midpoint annual base salary in US dollars for this pay grade, representing the market reference point for a fully competent incumbent. Used in compa-ratio calculations and compensation equity reviews.']
  pay_grade_min_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Minimum annual base salary in US dollars defined for this pay grade and job profile. Used in offer generation, equity analysis, and CUPA-HR compensation benchmarking to ensure pay equity compliance.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile in the institutional catalog. Active profiles are available for position creation and hiring. Inactive or Deprecated profiles are retained for historical position and compensation records but cannot be used for new positions.']
  retirement_plan_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incumbents in this job profile are eligible to participate in institutional retirement plans (e.g., TIAA, state pension). Supports benefits administration and ERISA compliance reporting.']
  sox_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is designated as a SOX-sensitive role with access to financial systems or controls, requiring enhanced internal audit oversight and segregation of duties controls. Applicable to institutions subject to SOX or analogous GASB/FASB internal control frameworks.']
  stem_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is classified as a STEM-designated role, relevant for OPT STEM extension eligibility for international employees and for institutional STEM workforce diversity reporting.']
  union_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions filled under this job profile are eligible for collective bargaining unit membership. Drives labor relations tracking, union dues processing, and contract compliance monitoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was most recently modified in the institutional data catalog. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Designated work arrangement type for this job profile indicating whether the role is performed on-site at an institutional facility, fully remote, or in a hybrid arrangement. Supports workforce planning and facilities space utilization analysis.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'Standard work shift designation associated with this job profile indicating the expected hours of operation. Relevant for facilities, public safety, and healthcare-adjacent roles within the institution.']

  Note: 'Institutional catalog of standardized job classifications defining role families, job codes, FLSA exemption status, EEO-1 category, pay grade range, minimum qualifications, and CIP-aligned functional area. Used for compensation benchmarking against CUPA-HR survey data and for position control alignment. Sourced from Workday HCM Job Profile.'
}

Table hr.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the organizational unit record in the institutional hierarchy. Primary key for the org_unit data product sourced from Workday HCM Supervisory Organization.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial cost center associated with this organizational unit. Supports budget alignment, financial reporting rollups, and cross-system reconciliation between Workday HCM and Oracle PeopleSoft Financials.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the responsible manager or unit head for this organizational unit. Used for supervisory hierarchy navigation, approval routing, and workforce planning. Sourced from Workday HCM position management.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediate parent organizational unit in the institutional hierarchy tree. Null for the root-level institution node. Enables recursive parent-child traversal for workforce rollup reporting and supervisory hierarchy navigation.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the primary specialized or programmatic accreditation body governing this organizational unit\'s academic programs. Examples: \'ABET\', \'AACSB\', \'LCME\', \'ABA\', \'HLC\'. Null for non-academic or non-accredited units. Used for accreditation compliance tracking and self-study documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ABET|AACSB|LCME|ABA|HLC|SACSCOC|MSCHE|NWCCU|NECHE|WSCUC|ACEN|CCNE — promote to reference product]']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the organizational unit with its primary accreditation body. Accredited units are in good standing; candidate units are pursuing initial accreditation; probation units have conditions to resolve; not_accredited units are unaccredited; exempt units are not subject to specialized accreditation requirements.']
  banner_org_code varchar [note: 'Ellucian Banner SIS organization code mapped to this Workday HCM supervisory organization. Used for cross-system reconciliation between HR and student information systems, particularly for academic department alignment in course scheduling and faculty assignment.']
  building_code varchar [note: 'Archibus building code for the primary building where this organizational unit\'s administrative offices are located. Used for facilities management, space planning, and emergency contact routing.']
  cip_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Department of Education Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) code associated with the academic unit\'s primary discipline. Used for IPEDS completions reporting, program accreditation alignment, and STEM designation tracking. Applicable to academic units only; null for administrative and auxiliary units.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the Databricks Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and SCD Type 2 history management.']
  dissolved_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the organizational unit was formally dissolved, merged, or reorganized out of existence by institutional governance. Null for active units. Retained for accreditation audit trails and IPEDS historical reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the organizational unit ceased or will cease to be operationally effective. Null for currently active units with no planned end date. Used for historical reporting, accreditation documentation, and SCD management in the Silver layer.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the organizational unit became or becomes operationally effective. Used for time-based workforce reporting, budget period alignment, and IPEDS organizational structure snapshots. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking in the Silver layer.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Official institutional email address for the organizational unit used for external communications, directory listings, and system notifications. Typically a shared mailbox (e.g., engineering@university.edu).']
  established_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the organizational unit was formally established or chartered by institutional governance. Distinct from effective_start_date which tracks system activation. Used for accreditation history, institutional research, and anniversary reporting.']
  fte_actual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current actual Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount filled within this organizational unit based on active position assignments in Workday HCM. Compared against fte_budget for workforce planning variance reporting and IPEDS Human Resources Survey submissions.']
  fte_budget decimal(10,2) [note: 'Authorized Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount budget allocated to this organizational unit for workforce planning and position control purposes. Used in CUPA-HR benchmarking, institutional workforce planning, and budget variance analysis. Expressed as a decimal to accommodate fractional FTE allocations.']
  gl_fund_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger fund code from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials that classifies the primary funding source for this organizational unit (e.g., general fund, restricted fund, auxiliary fund, endowment fund). Supports NACUBO-compliant financial reporting and budget alignment.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Integer representing the depth level of this unit within the institutional hierarchy tree, where the root institution node is level 1. Used for hierarchy-aware reporting, rollup aggregation logic, and organizational structure analytics.']
  idc_rate_type varchar [note: 'Facilities and Administrative (F&A) indirect cost rate type applicable to this organizational unit for sponsored research and grant administration. Determines the IDC rate applied to direct costs on federal awards administered through Kuali Research. Governed by OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.']
  ipeds_unit_code varchar [note: 'Federal IPEDS unit identifier assigned by the U.S. Department of Education for postsecondary institution reporting. Required for Title IV financial aid administration and federal compliance reporting. Applicable primarily at the institution and campus levels.']
  is_academic_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is classified as an academic unit that delivers instruction, awards credentials, or conducts academic programs. Used to filter units for IPEDS academic reporting, accreditation submissions, and curriculum governance workflows.']
  is_degree_granting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit has authority to award academic degrees or credentials. Used for accreditation scope determination, IPEDS completions reporting, and program review governance.']
  is_research_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit actively administers sponsored research grants, contracts, or IRB protocols through Kuali Research. Used for Facilities and Administrative (F&A) cost allocation, indirect cost rate (IDC) reporting, and research compliance oversight.']
  mission_statement varchar [note: 'Formal mission statement of the organizational unit as approved by institutional governance. Required for programmatic accreditation submissions (AACSB, ABET, LCME) and institutional self-study documentation.']
  next_accreditation_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date of the next accreditation review, site visit, or self-study submission for this organizational unit. Used for compliance planning, institutional research scheduling, and accreditation project management.']
  org_hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized full path string representing the unit\'s position in the institutional hierarchy from root to current node, using unit codes delimited by forward slashes. Example: \'INST/DIV-ACAD/COLL-ENG/DEPT-CS\'. Enables efficient hierarchy traversal and rollup queries in the Databricks Silver layer without recursive CTEs.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit. Active units are operational and accept workforce assignments and budget allocations. Inactive units are suspended but retained for historical reporting. Pending units are approved but not yet operational. Dissolved units have been formally closed and are retained for audit and accreditation history.']
  peoplesoft_deptid varchar [note: 'Oracle PeopleSoft Financials department identifier (DEPTID) mapped to this organizational unit. Enables financial reporting rollups, budget alignment, and cross-system reconciliation between Workday HCM workforce data and PeopleSoft GL transactions.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the organizational unit\'s main administrative office. Used for institutional directory, emergency communications, and vendor/partner contact routing.']
  position_count_authorized varchar [note: 'Total number of authorized positions (filled and vacant) approved for this organizational unit through the institutional position control process. Supports workforce planning, budget justification, and headcount reporting.']
  position_count_filled varchar [note: 'Number of currently filled (occupied) positions within this organizational unit as tracked in Workday HCM. Used for vacancy analysis, workforce planning, and IPEDS Human Resources Survey reporting.']
  primary_campus_location varchar [note: 'Name or code of the primary campus or physical location where this organizational unit is headquartered. Used for facilities planning, space allocation in Archibus, and multi-campus reporting. Examples: \'Main Campus\', \'North Campus\', \'Downtown Center\', \'Online\'.']
  room_number varchar [note: 'Room number of the primary administrative office for this organizational unit within the designated building. Used for facilities management, directory services, and space utilization reporting in Archibus.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the organizational unit within institutional systems. Used as the business key in Banner, Workday, and PeopleSoft integrations. Examples: \'COLL-ENG\', \'DEPT-CS\', \'DIV-ACAD\'.']
  unit_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the organizational unit\'s mission, scope, and primary functions. Used for institutional directory, accreditation self-study documentation, and onboarding materials.']
  unit_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the organizational unit within the institutional structure. Determines the unit\'s position in the parent-child tree and governs reporting rollup behavior. Institution is the root; divisions and colleges are mid-tier; departments, programs, offices, and centers are leaf-level units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: institution|division|college|school|department|program|office|center — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the organizational unit as recognized in institutional records, accreditation filings, and IPEDS reporting. Examples: \'College of Engineering\', \'Department of Computer Science\', \'Office of Financial Aid\'.']
  unit_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or shortened display name for the organizational unit used in dashboards, reports, and space-constrained interfaces. Examples: \'Eng College\', \'CS Dept\', \'Fin Aid Office\'.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit by its primary institutional function. Academic units deliver instruction and award degrees; administrative units provide institutional support services; auxiliary units are self-supporting enterprises (e.g., housing, dining); research centers conduct sponsored and non-sponsored research; athletics units manage NCAA-governed programs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was most recently modified in the Databricks Silver layer data product. Used for incremental ETL processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official public-facing website URL for the organizational unit. Used for institutional directory, accreditation documentation, and alumni/prospective student communications.']
  workday_org_reference varchar [note: 'Native Workday HCM Supervisory Organization identifier for this unit. Serves as the source system key for ETL reconciliation, API integration, and cross-system lineage tracking between Workday HCM and the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Institutional organizational hierarchy entity representing departments, divisions, colleges, schools, and administrative units in a parent-child tree structure. Tracks unit name, unit code, parent unit, cost center reference, unit type (academic, administrative, auxiliary, research center), effective dates, responsible manager, and active/inactive status. Supports workforce reporting rollups by organizational level, budget alignment, supervisory hierarchy navigation, and IPEDS organizational structure reporting. Sourced from Workday HCM Supervisory Organization.'
}

Table hr.staffing_event {
  staffing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each staffing event record in the Workday HCM business process audit trail. Primary key for the staffing_event data product.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile (standardized job definition including title, family, and level) assigned to the employee after this staffing event. Drives compensation grade and CUPA-HR job classification alignment.']
