  tertiary_hr_recruiter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HR recruiter assigned to manage this applicant\'s candidacy. Used for recruiter workload analysis, time-to-fill accountability, and sourcing effectiveness reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the applicant. Used for background check initiation, offer letter mailing, and OFCCP geographic compliance analysis.']
  application_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the applicant record was first created in the Workday HCM Recruiting system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp and the anchor for time-to-fill calculations.']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the applicant submitted their application for the position. Used as the start point for time-to-fill and time-to-hire calculations.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the applicant\'s progression through the recruiting workflow for the associated requisition. Drives pipeline reporting and time-to-fill analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: applied|screening|interview|offer|hired|rejected|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the pre-employment background check for the applicant. Required for compliance with institutional hiring policy and federal contractor obligations.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of residence for the applicant. Used in geographic sourcing analytics and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the applicant\'s country of residence. Supports international applicant tracking and work authorization screening.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'Name of the applicant\'s current or most recent employer. Used for background screening, reference checks, and competitive sourcing intelligence.']
  current_job_title varchar [note: 'The applicant\'s current or most recent job title. Used for qualification assessment, salary benchmarking, and CUPA-HR position classification alignment.']
  desired_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'The annual salary expectation stated by the applicant during the application process. Used for early-stage compensation fit screening and offer negotiation planning.']
  disability_status varchar [note: 'Applicant\'s self-identified disability status per Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act. Required for federal contractor OFCCP compliance reporting and disability inclusion benchmarking (7% utilization goal).']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for the final disposition of the applicant (e.g., \'Not Qualified\', \'Position Filled\', \'Candidate Withdrew\', \'Failed Background Check\'). Required for OFCCP Internet Applicant recordkeeping and adverse impact analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_qualified|position_filled|candidate_withdrew|failed_background_check|better_qualified_candidate|salary_mismatch|no_show — promote to reference product]']
  eeo_gender varchar [note: 'Applicant\'s self-identified gender as collected on the voluntary EEO self-identification form. Required for OFCCP and EEOC compliance reporting and diversity hiring analytics. Collected separately from the application and stored with restricted access.']
  eeo_race_ethnicity varchar [note: 'Applicant\'s self-identified race and ethnicity category per EEOC EEO-1 classification (e.g., Hispanic or Latino, White, Black or African American, Asian, etc.). Required for OFCCP compliance and institutional diversity reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hispanic_latino|white|black_african_american|asian|native_hawaiian_pacific_islander|american_indian_alaska_native|two_or_more_races|decline_to_state — promote to reference product]']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the applicant used for all recruiting communications, interview scheduling, and offer delivery. Sourced from Workday HCM candidate profile.']
  expected_start_date date [note: 'The anticipated first day of employment for the applicant upon offer acceptance. Used for onboarding planning, position vacancy tracking, and FTE workforce forecasting.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the applicant as provided during the application process. Used for identity verification, correspondence, and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  highest_education_level varchar [note: 'The highest academic degree or credential attained by the applicant as self-reported on the application. Used for minimum qualification screening and workforce education analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctoral|professional|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  interview_stage varchar [note: 'The most recent or current interview stage reached by the applicant in the selection process. Supports pipeline funnel analysis and interview-to-offer conversion rate reporting.']
  is_internal_applicant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant is a current employee of the institution applying for a different position (internal transfer or promotion). Distinguishes internal mobility from external hiring for workforce planning analytics.']
  is_rehire_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant is a former employee who has been designated as eligible for rehire by the institution. Sourced from Workday HCM separation records. Prevents inadvertent rehire of ineligible former employees.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal family name (surname) of the applicant as provided during the application process. Used for identity verification, correspondence, and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the applicant record was most recently modified in the Workday HCM Recruiting system. Used for incremental data extraction (ETL) and audit trail maintenance.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the applicant. Supports full legal name construction for background check and onboarding documentation.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'The date on which the applicant formally accepted the employment offer. Used to calculate offer acceptance rate and time-to-fill KPIs.']
  offer_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The annual base salary amount included in the formal offer extended to the applicant. Populated when application_status reaches \'offer\'. Used for compensation equity analysis and CUPA-HR benchmarking.']
  offer_date date [note: 'The date on which a formal employment offer was extended to the applicant. Used to calculate offer-to-acceptance cycle time and time-to-fill metrics.']
  offer_response varchar [note: 'The applicant\'s response to the formal employment offer. Supports offer acceptance rate analytics and compensation negotiation pattern analysis.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the applicant. Used for interview scheduling and recruiter outreach. Stored in E.164 international format.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the applicant\'s residence. Supports geographic labor market analysis and commute-distance sourcing metrics.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the applicant prefers to be addressed by, which may differ from their legal name. Supports inclusive hiring practices and respectful candidate communication.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The recruitment channel through which the applicant was sourced (e.g., job board, employee referral, direct application, staffing agency). Critical for sourcing effectiveness analytics and cost-per-hire reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|employee_referral|direct|agency|career_fair|social_media|internal_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  source_detail varchar [note: 'Specific source name within the source channel (e.g., \'Indeed\', \'LinkedIn\', \'HigherEdJobs\', \'Chronicle of Higher Education\', employee name for referrals). Enables granular sourcing ROI analysis.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of residence for the applicant. Used in geographic sourcing analytics, relocation eligibility assessment, and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  veteran_status varchar [note: 'Applicant\'s self-identified veteran status per VEVRAA (Vietnam Era Veterans\' Readjustment Assistance Act) categories. Required for federal contractor OFCCP compliance reporting and veteran hiring benchmarks.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant is legally authorized to work in the country of the position without employer sponsorship. Used for I-9 eligibility screening and visa sponsorship decision tracking.']
  workday_candidate_reference varchar [note: 'The native candidate identifier assigned by Workday HCM Recruiting. Used to cross-reference records back to the system of record for reconciliation and integration purposes.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of relevant professional work experience as self-reported by the applicant. Used for minimum qualification screening and compensation band placement during offer stage.']

  Note: 'Master record for individuals who have applied for non-faculty staff positions at the institution. Captures applicant identity, contact information, source channel (job board, employee referral, direct), EEO self-identification data, veteran status, disability status, and application history per requisition including application status (applied, screening, interview, offer, hired, rejected, withdrawn), disposition reason, offer details, and final hiring decision. Distinct from the admissions domain applicant (prospective students). Sourced from Workday HCM Recruiting. Supports OFCCP compliance reporting, diversity hiring analytics, and time-to-fill metrics.'
}

Table hr.performance_review {
  performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each performance review record in the Workday HCM Talent and Performance module. Primary key for the performance_review data product.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational department to which the employee belongs at the time of the review, used for departmental performance reporting.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position held by the employee at the time of the review, supporting position control and workforce planning analysis.']
  primary_performance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff employee who is the subject of this performance review. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or supervisor who authored and submitted the performance review. Typically the direct supervisor in the Workday HCM reporting hierarchy.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'FK to hr.training_record']
  calibrated_overall_rating varchar [note: 'The overall performance rating after calibration review, which may differ from the manager\'s initial rating. Represents the final agreed-upon rating used for merit decisions and talent planning.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current state of the rating calibration process for this review. Calibration sessions ensure consistency and fairness of ratings across departments and divisions before final approval.']
  competency_rating varchar [note: 'Aggregate rating reflecting the employee\'s demonstration of institutional core competencies (e.g., collaboration, communication, service excellence, DEI commitment) as evaluated during the review period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the performance review record was first created in the Workday HCM system and ingested into the Silver Layer of the Databricks Lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  development_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal individual development plan (IDP) is required as an outcome of this performance review, typically triggered by a needs_improvement or unsatisfactory rating.']
  due_date date [note: 'The institutional deadline by which the performance review must be completed and submitted in Workday HCM. Used for compliance tracking and HR reporting on timely completion rates.']
  employee_acknowledged_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee electronically acknowledged receipt and review of their performance evaluation in Workday HCM. Required for FERPA-adjacent employee record integrity and HR audit trails.']
  employee_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the employee in their self-assessment, capturing their own perspective on accomplishments, challenges, and development goals. Confidential HR record.']
  final_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the performance review received final HR or senior leadership approval, marking the review as officially closed and eligible to inform merit increase decisions.']
  fte_at_review decimal(4,2) [note: 'The employee\'s Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) value at the time of the performance review. Supports workforce planning analytics and ensures part-time employees are evaluated proportionally.']
  goal_achievement_rating varchar [note: 'Rating reflecting the degree to which the employee achieved their individual performance goals set at the beginning of the review period. Distinct from competency ratings; focuses on measurable outcomes and KPI attainment.']
  goals_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual performance goals formally documented and evaluated in this review cycle. Supports workforce analytics on goal-setting practices and alignment with institutional KPIs.']
  goals_met_count varchar [note: 'The number of individual performance goals that the employee fully achieved during the review period. Used in conjunction with goals_count to assess goal attainment rates.']
  leadership_rating varchar [note: 'Rating specific to leadership behaviors and people management competencies, applicable to employees in supervisory or managerial roles. Marked not_applicable for individual contributors.']
  merit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for a merit-based salary increase based on their performance review outcome. Eligibility may be restricted by budget, employment type, or rating threshold policies.']
  merit_increase_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage salary increase recommended or approved based on the performance review outcome. Confidential compensation data used in salary administration and CUPA-HR benchmarking analysis.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'The summary performance rating assigned to the employee for the review period, drawn from the institution\'s standardized rating scale in Workday HCM. Directly informs merit increase eligibility and talent development planning.']
  overall_rating_numeric decimal(3,2) [note: 'Numeric representation of the overall performance rating on a standardized scale (e.g., 1.00–5.00) as configured in Workday HCM. Enables quantitative analytics, benchmarking, and merit matrix calculations.']
  pip_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) has been initiated as a result of this review. A PIP is a structured corrective action document for employees rated unsatisfactory.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative comments provided by the reviewing manager summarizing the employee\'s performance, accomplishments, and areas for development during the review period. Confidential HR record.']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the performance evaluation period covered by this review. Together with review_period_start_date, defines the full evaluation window.']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the performance evaluation period covered by this review. Defines the beginning of the window during which the employee\'s performance is assessed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the performance review in Workday HCM. Tracks progression from initial draft through employee acknowledgment, manager submission, HR approval, and final completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|employee_acknowledged|manager_submitted|hr_approved|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the performance review cycle. Annual reviews support merit decisions; probationary reviews assess new hires within their introductory period; mid-year reviews provide interim feedback; project and off-cycle reviews address specific circumstances.']
  self_assessment_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee completed a self-assessment as part of the performance review process in Workday HCM. Self-assessments are a standard component of annual and mid-year review cycles.']
  self_assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee submitted their self-assessment in Workday HCM. Used to track employee engagement in the review process and measure timeliness.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the manager formally submitted the completed performance review in Workday HCM. Used to measure on-time submission compliance against the due date.']
  talent_segment varchar [note: 'Confidential talent classification assigned during calibration, used for succession planning and targeted development investment. Supports the institution\'s talent development and retention strategy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to the performance review record in the source system or the Silver Layer. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture.']
  workday_review_reference varchar [note: 'The native business identifier assigned by Workday HCM to this performance review event. Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation with the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Annual or mid-cycle performance evaluation and professional development record for a staff employee. Captures review period, review type (annual, probationary, mid-year), overall rating, goal achievement rating, competency ratings, reviewer identity, employee self-assessment flag, calibration status, and final approval date. Also tracks training and professional development completions including training type (mandatory compliance, professional development, safety, DEI), delivery method, completion date, credit hours, pass/fail status, certificate issued flag, and expiration date for recurring compliance trainings (Title IX, FERPA, HIPAA, cybersecurity). Sourced from Workday HCM Talent and Performance. Supports merit increase decisions, talent development planning, and compliance training verification.'
}

Table hr.grievance {
  grievance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the employee grievance or disciplinary action record. Primary key for the grievance data product.']
  grievance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who filed the grievance or is the subject of the disciplinary action. Links to the HR employee master record.']
  grievance_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR staff member, compliance officer, or external investigator assigned to conduct the formal investigation of the grievance.']
  grievance_respondent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee named as the respondent or subject of the grievance (e.g., the accused party in a harassment or misconduct complaint). May differ from the filing employee.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational department in which the grievance or disciplinary matter originated. Used for workforce analytics and compliance reporting.']
  ada_accommodation_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance involves a dispute related to a requested or denied Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) reasonable accommodation.']
  appeal_decision_date date [note: 'The date on which the appeal review body issued its final decision. Null if appeal is pending or not filed.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'The date on which a formal appeal of the grievance resolution was submitted. Null if no appeal was filed.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed by the grievant or respondent following the initial resolution. Tracks whether the case has been escalated through institutional or arbitration channels.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the grievance case was fully closed, including completion of any corrective actions, appeals, or follow-up requirements. Distinct from resolution_date when appeals extend the case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the grievance record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  disciplinary_action_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of disciplinary action imposed on the respondent employee as a result of the grievance investigation findings. Distinct from resolution_type which captures the broader case outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  eeoc_charge_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievant has filed or expressed intent to file a formal charge with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) in connection with this matter. Triggers legal hold and counsel notification.']
  eeoc_charge_number varchar [note: 'The official charge number assigned by the EEOC if a formal charge has been filed. Used to cross-reference internal HR records with external regulatory proceedings.']
  external_counsel_engaged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external legal counsel has been engaged to advise on or represent the institution in connection with this grievance matter.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the grievance or complaint was formally filed by the employee or initiated by HR. Serves as the principal business event date for timeline and statute-of-limitations tracking.']
  filing_method varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the grievance was submitted (e.g., written form, online HR portal, email, verbal report). Relevant for procedural compliance and documentation standards.']
  finding_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the investigation findings, documenting key facts established, evidence reviewed, and the basis for the finding determination. Restricted to authorized HR and legal personnel.']
  fmla_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance involves a dispute related to Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) rights, including denial of leave, interference, or retaliation for taking FMLA leave.']
  grievance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the grievance record. Tracks progression from initial filing through investigation, resolution, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filed|under_investigation|pending_review|resolved|closed|appealed|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  grievance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the grievance or disciplinary matter. Drives routing, investigation procedures, and regulatory reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: harassment|discrimination|retaliation|policy_violation|workplace_conduct|labor_relations|wage_dispute|other — promote to reference product]']
  hr_case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric case reference number assigned by the HR department or case management system for tracking and correspondence. Format: GRV-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  incident_date date [note: 'The date on which the underlying incident, policy violation, or conduct giving rise to the grievance allegedly occurred. May predate the filing date.']
  investigation_close_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal investigation was concluded and findings were documented. Used to calculate investigation duration and compliance with regulatory timelines.']
  investigation_finding varchar [note: 'The formal outcome determination of the investigation indicating whether the allegations were substantiated, unsubstantiated, or inconclusive. Drives resolution and disciplinary action decisions.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal investigation into the grievance was initiated. Used to measure investigation duration and compliance with institutional response-time policies.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance was filed anonymously. Anonymous filings may limit the scope of investigation and available remedies.']
  is_union_grievance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance is filed under a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) and involves union representation. Triggers specific procedural timelines and arbitration rights under the NLRA.']
  legal_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a legal hold has been placed on records associated with this grievance due to pending or anticipated litigation, EEOC proceedings, or regulatory inquiry. Prevents record destruction.']
  mediation_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the mediation process if mediation was conducted. Indicates whether the parties reached a settlement agreement through mediation.']
  mediation_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal mediation was requested by either party as an alternative dispute resolution mechanism prior to or in lieu of a full investigation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Assigned urgency level of the grievance case, used to prioritize investigator workload and escalation timelines. Critical cases may involve imminent safety or legal risk.']
  protected_class_basis varchar [note: 'The protected class characteristic(s) alleged as the basis for discrimination or harassment in the grievance. Applicable for EEO-related grievances. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: race|color|national_origin|sex|religion|age|disability|pregnancy|genetic_information|veteran_status|not_applicable|other — promote to reference product]']
  record_retention_date date [note: 'The date after which the grievance record may be eligible for destruction per institutional records retention policy and applicable regulatory requirements. Calculated from closure date plus required retention period.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the grievance was formally resolved and a resolution determination was communicated to the involved parties.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'The type of resolution or corrective action taken as a result of the grievance investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_action|verbal_warning|written_warning|suspension|termination|mediation|settlement|training_required|policy_change|other — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary settlement amount paid to the grievant as part of the resolution, if applicable. Null if no monetary settlement was reached. Reported in USD.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular categorization within the grievance type (e.g., \'sexual harassment\' under harassment, \'racial discrimination\' under discrimination, \'FMLA retaliation\' under retaliation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  suspension_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of suspension imposed on the respondent employee as part of the disciplinary action. Applicable only when disciplinary_action_type includes a suspension.']
  union_contract_article varchar [note: 'The specific article or section of the collective bargaining agreement (CBA) cited in the union grievance. Applicable only when is_union_grievance is true.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the grievance record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  workday_case_reference varchar [note: 'The source system case identifier from Workday HCM Employee Relations module. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail between the data lakehouse and the system of record.']

  Note: 'Formal employee grievance or disciplinary action record tracking complaints, investigations, and resolutions related to workplace conduct, policy violations, harassment, discrimination, or labor relations. Captures grievance type, filing date, parties involved, investigation status, findings, resolution type, appeal status, and closure date. Supports HR compliance, Title VII, ADA, and labor relations reporting. Distinct from Title IX cases owned by the compliance domain.'
}

Table hr.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the time entry record. Primary key.']
  adjusted_time_entry_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on time_entry (adjusted_time_entry_id)']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research grant or sponsored project funding this time entry. Required for grant-funded employee time tracking and effort reporting.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational department where the work was performed. Used for departmental labor reporting and workforce analytics.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pay period in which this time entry will be processed for payment. Links time entries to payroll cycles.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific payroll run in which this time entry was processed for payment. Links time entries to payroll results.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position under which the time was worked. Links to the position master record.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who recorded this time entry. Links to the employee master record.']
  tertiary_time_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved this time entry. Provides audit trail for approval authority.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the time entry. Tracks whether the supervisor has reviewed and approved the time for payroll processing.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the supervisor approved the time entry. Indicates readiness for payroll processing.']
  chartfield_string varchar [note: 'Complete financial accounting string combining fund, department, account, program, and project codes. Used for detailed labor cost allocation in the general ledger.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the employee clocked in or started work. Critical for FLSA compliance and overtime calculation.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the employee clocked out or ended work. Used to calculate total hours worked.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting code identifying the cost center to which this time should be charged. Used for labor cost allocation and budgetary reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  earning_code varchar [note: 'Classification code indicating the type of time worked. Examples include regular hours, overtime, shift differential, on-call, holiday pay, or compensatory time. Drives pay rate calculation in payroll.']
  earning_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the earning code. Provides context for the type of time worked.']
  employee_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or explanations provided by the employee regarding this time entry. May include justification for unusual hours or work circumstances.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the time entry in the approval and payroll workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval to payment.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the time entry was created. Distinguishes between clock-in/out, manual entry, system import, or adjustment/correction entries.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee position as exempt or non-exempt from FLSA overtime requirements. Determines overtime eligibility and timekeeping requirements.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Financial fund code identifying the funding source for this labor. Critical for grant-funded positions and restricted fund compliance.']
  hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours worked during this time entry period. Calculated from clock-in/out timestamps or manually entered. Used for payroll calculation.']
  is_overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this time entry is eligible for overtime compensation. Typically true for non-exempt employees working over 40 hours per week.']
  on_call_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours the employee was on-call and available for work. May be compensated at a different rate than active work hours.']
  overtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked beyond the standard workweek threshold (typically 40 hours). Subject to overtime premium pay under FLSA for non-exempt employees.']
  payroll_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator showing whether this time entry has been processed through payroll and included in a paycheck. Prevents duplicate payment processing.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked at the standard pay rate. Typically up to 40 hours per week for non-exempt employees under FLSA.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the supervisor when rejecting a time entry. Helps employee understand what corrections are needed.']
  shift_differential_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked during shifts that qualify for differential pay (e.g., evening, night, weekend shifts). Eligible for additional compensation per institutional policy.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'Date when the employee submitted the time entry for supervisor approval. Used to track timeliness of time entry submission.']
  supervisor_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or feedback provided by the supervisor during the approval process. May include approval rationale or correction instructions.']
  time_entry_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the time entry. May be used for employee and supervisor reference.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the work was performed. Used for pay period assignment and labor distribution reporting.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical location where the work was performed. Used for labor distribution, facilities management, and multi-campus reporting.']
  work_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the work location. Provides context for where the employee performed work.']
  workday_time_entry_reference varchar [note: 'External unique identifier from Workday HCM Time Tracking system. Used for source system reconciliation and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of employee time worked including clock-in/clock-out timestamps, hours worked by earning code (regular, overtime, shift differential, on-call), work location, cost center allocation, supervisor approval status, and pay period assignment. Critical for non-exempt/hourly staff FLSA compliance, overtime calculation, and payroll input. Sourced from Workday HCM Time Tracking.'
}

Table hr.onboarding_task {
  onboarding_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the onboarding task record. Primary key for the onboarding task entity.']
  enterprise_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.enterprise_application. Business justification: IT onboarding tasks in higher-ed explicitly provision access to specific enterprise applications (Banner, Workday, Canvas, email). Links HR onboarding workflows to target systems for automated provisi']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department the new hire is joining. Used for department-specific onboarding tasks and reporting.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position the employee is being onboarded into. Links to the position control record.']
  prerequisite_onboarding_task_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on onboarding_task (prerequisite_onboarding_task_id)']
  primary_onboarding_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee undergoing onboarding. Links to the employee master record.']
  quaternary_onboarding_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved the task completion. Applicable only when requires_approval is true.']
  quinary_onboarding_waived_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HR or management employee who authorized the waiver. Required for audit trail.']
  staffing_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent staffing event that triggered this onboarding task. Links to the hire or rehire event.']
  tertiary_onboarding_completed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who marked the task as completed. May differ from assigned_to_employee_id in cases of delegation or escalation.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Level or type of access being granted (e.g., Read-Only, Standard User, Administrator, Faculty, Staff). Used for role-based access control (RBAC).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the task completion was approved. Marks final closure for tasks requiring approval.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the task was assigned to the responsible party. Marks the start of the task lifecycle.']
  assigned_to_role varchar [note: 'Functional role responsible for the task. Used for routing and escalation when specific employee assignment is not yet determined. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_hire|hr_specialist|it_administrator|hiring_manager|payroll_specialist|benefits_coordinator|facilities_manager|training_coordinator — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  certificate_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a completion certificate was issued for the training. Required for certain compliance trainings.']
  completed_date date [note: 'Date when the task was marked as completed. Used for compliance reporting and onboarding cycle time analysis.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory framework or policy that mandates this task (e.g., IRCA, FERPA, Institutional Background Check Policy, Title IX Training Requirement).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding task record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  document_received_date date [note: 'Date when the required document was received and verified. Used for compliance reporting and audit trail.']
  document_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required document has been received and verified. Critical for compliance tasks.']
  document_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task requires submission or verification of a document (e.g., I-9 supporting documents, degree verification, certifications).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document required for task completion (e.g., I-9 List A Document, Degree Certificate, Professional License, Policy Acknowledgment Form).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the onboarding task. Critical for compliance tasks that must be completed by the first day of work or within regulatory timeframes.']
  is_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task is required for regulatory compliance (e.g., I-9 verification per Immigration Reform and Control Act, background checks per institutional policy).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task is required for all new hires or optional. Mandatory tasks block onboarding completion if not finished.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task requires managerial or HR approval before being marked as complete.']
  started_date date [note: 'Date when work on the task began. Indicates transition from assigned to in-progress status.']
  system_access_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task involves provisioning access to institutional systems (e.g., Banner, Workday, Canvas, email, network).']
  task_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for completing the task. May include links to forms, systems, or resources needed.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the onboarding task (e.g., Complete I-9 Form, Background Check Verification, System Access Provisioning).']
  task_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about task progress, issues encountered, or special circumstances. Used for communication between HR, IT, and hiring managers.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the onboarding task. Used for tracking and reference in communications.']
  task_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the task. Critical tasks (e.g., I-9, background check) must be completed before start date; others may have flexible timelines.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the onboarding task. Tracks progress from assignment through completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|pending_approval|completed|waived|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Category of the onboarding task. Classifies tasks by functional area to support workflow routing and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliance|administrative|system_access|orientation|training|documentation|benefits|payroll|equipment|facility_access — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the required training was completed. Used for compliance reporting and certification tracking.']
  training_module_name varchar [note: 'Name of the training module or orientation session required (e.g., FERPA Compliance Training, Title IX Awareness, Campus Safety Orientation).']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task involves completion of a training module or orientation session (e.g., FERPA training, Title IX training, safety orientation).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding task record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  waived_date date [note: 'Date when the task waiver was approved. Used for compliance reporting and audit trail.']
  waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task was waived for this employee (e.g., rehire who already completed background check, transfer from another department).']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the task was waived. Required when waived_flag is true for audit and compliance purposes.']
  workday_task_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Workday HCM system. Used for integration and reconciliation with source system.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual onboarding tasks and milestones for new hires including I-9 verification, background check completion, system access provisioning, orientation attendance, and policy acknowledgments'
}

Table hr.training_record {
  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training record. Primary key.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or organizational unit to which the employee belonged at the time of training.']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who completed or is assigned the training.']
  renewal_training_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_record (renewal_training_record_id)']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the training program or course catalog entry.']
  attempts_count varchar [note: 'Number of attempts the employee made to complete or pass the training.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date the training certificate was issued to the employee.']
  certificate_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether a certificate of completion was issued to the employee.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or serial number of the issued training certificate.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee successfully completed the training program.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory framework or policy that mandates this training such as Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA), Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), or Title IX.']
  compliance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this training is required for regulatory or institutional compliance.']
  contact_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of instructional contact hours for the training program.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the employee to complete this training program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of continuing education or professional development credit hours awarded for completing the training.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 4217 currency code for the training cost.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The format or modality through which the training was delivered.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the employee must complete the training to remain compliant.']
  employee_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or feedback provided by the employee about the training experience.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the employee was enrolled or assigned to the training program.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the training certification or completion expires and requires renewal.']
  ferpa_training_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this training covers Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) student data privacy requirements.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funding for the training such as departmental budget, grant, or professional development fund.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the instructor or facilitator who delivered the training.']
  passed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the employee passed the training assessment.']
  passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to successfully pass the training program.']
  safety_training_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this training covers workplace safety or Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) requirements.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or grade achieved by the employee on the training assessment.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the employee began the training program or course.']
  title_ix_training_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this training covers Title IX sexual harassment and discrimination prevention.']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the training content such as Safety, Compliance, Leadership, Technical Skills, or Diversity and Inclusion.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training was conducted.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or vendor that provided the training program.']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record for this employee.']
  training_title varchar [note: 'The full title or name of the training program or course.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training program by requirement type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training record was last modified in the system.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the employee was granted a waiver exempting them from completing this training.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for granting a training waiver.']
  workday_training_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Workday HCM for this training record.']

  Note: 'Records employee completion of required and elective training programs including safety training, harassment prevention, FERPA training, and professional development courses with certification tracking'
}

Table hr.accrual_plan {
  accrual_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for accrual_plan']
  superseded_accrual_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on accrual_plan (superseded_accrual_plan_id)']
  accrual_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which leave time is accrued under this plan (e.g., monthly, per pay period).']
  accrual_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate at which leave time accrues per accrual period, expressed in hours or days depending on unit of measure.']
  accrual_start_event varchar [note: 'Event that triggers the start of accrual under this plan (e.g., hire date, eligibility date).']
  accrual_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for accrued leave time (hours or days).']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required before taking leave under this plan. Zero if no advance notice required.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supervisor or HR approval is required before leave under this plan can be taken.']
  carryover_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused accrued leave can be carried over to the next accrual period or fiscal year.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accrual plan record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the accrual plan including purpose, rules, and any special conditions.']
  documentation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documentation (e.g., medical certificate) is required for leave requests under this plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual plan is no longer active. Null for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual plan becomes active and available for employee assignment.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the employee eligibility requirements for this accrual plan (e.g., full-time staff, faculty with tenure).']
  employee_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the employee group or classification to which this accrual plan applies (e.g., staff, faculty, administrative).']
  fiscal_year_reset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accrual balances reset at the start of each fiscal year.']
  integration_code varchar [note: 'External system integration code used to synchronize this accrual plan with payroll or HRIS systems (e.g., Workday HCM).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this accrual plan record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accrual plan record was last updated.']
  maximum_accrual_balance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of leave time that can be accrued under this plan. Null if no cap applies.']
  maximum_carryover_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of accrued leave that can be carried over to the next period if carryover is allowed. Null if no limit or carryover not allowed.']
  minimum_usage_increment decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum increment in which leave can be taken (e.g., 0.25 for quarter-hour increments, 1.0 for full-day increments).']
  negative_balance_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees can take leave in advance of accrual, resulting in a negative balance.']
  payout_on_termination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused accrued leave is paid out to the employee upon termination of employment.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the accrual plan used in external communications and system integrations.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the accrual plan (e.g., \'Standard Staff PTO\', \'Faculty Sabbatical Leave\').']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave or time-off that this accrual plan governs.']
  policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the institutional policy document or section that governs this accrual plan.']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to prorate accruals for part-time employees or mid-period hires.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual plan indicating whether it is available for assignment to employees.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the union or collective bargaining unit associated with this accrual plan, if applicable. Null for non-union plans.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must be employed before they begin accruing leave under this plan. Zero if accrual starts immediately.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this accrual plan record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for accrual_plan. Referenced by accrual_plan_id.'
}

Table hr.benefit_program {
  benefit_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_program']
  superseded_benefit_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_program (superseded_benefit_program_id)']
  aca_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for ACA Form 1095-C reporting to indicate the type of coverage offered under this benefit program.']
  annual_maximum_benefit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of benefits payable per employee per plan year under this benefit program.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or third-party administrator providing the benefit program.']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number assigned by the insurance carrier for this benefit program.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of costs the employee pays after meeting the deductible under this benefit program.']
  compliance_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit program requires regulatory compliance reporting such as ACA 1095 forms or ERISA annual reports.']
  copay_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employee pays per service or visit under this benefit program.']
  coverage_level_options varchar [note: 'Available coverage tiers for the benefit program such as employee-only, employee-plus-spouse, employee-plus-children, or family.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit program record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount the employee must pay before the benefit program begins coverage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit program is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for open-ended programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit program becomes active and available for employee enrollment.']
  eligibility_rule_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the eligibility criteria employees must meet to enroll in this benefit program.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employee contributes per pay period for this benefit program.']
  employee_contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of premium or salary the employee contributes for this benefit program.']
  employee_contribution_type varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for employee contributions to the benefit program.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employer contributes per employee per pay period for this benefit program.']
  employer_contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of premium or salary the employer contributes for this benefit program.']
  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method by which employees enroll in the benefit program.']
  erisa_plan_number varchar [note: 'Three-digit plan number assigned for ERISA reporting and compliance purposes.']
  is_cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit program qualifies for COBRA continuation coverage upon termination of employment.']
  is_fmla_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit program is protected under FMLA during employee leave.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enrollment in this benefit program is mandatory for eligible employees.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit program record was last updated in the system.']
  lifetime_maximum_benefit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of benefits payable per employee over their lifetime under this benefit program.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this benefit program record.']
  open_enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Ending date of the annual open enrollment period for this benefit program.']
  open_enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Beginning date of the annual open enrollment period for this benefit program.']
  out_of_pocket_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the employee will pay out-of-pocket per plan year before the benefit program covers 100% of costs.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or document repository location for the official benefit plan document.']
  plan_year_end_date date [note: 'Ending date of the benefit plan year cycle.']
  plan_year_start_date date [note: 'Beginning date of the benefit plan year cycle used for enrollment periods and benefit calculations.']
  program_administrator_contact varchar [note: 'Email address or phone number for the benefit program administrator.']
  program_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal or external administrator responsible for managing this benefit program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the benefit program used in HR systems and employee communications.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the benefit program as displayed to employees and administrators.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit program indicating availability for enrollment and administration.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the benefit program by primary benefit category.']
  qualifying_life_event_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees can make enrollment changes outside open enrollment due to qualifying life events.']
  summary_plan_description_url varchar [note: 'Web address or document repository location for the employee-facing summary plan description.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must wait after hire date or eligibility date before they can enroll in the benefit program.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for benefit_program. Referenced by benefit_program_id.'
}

Table hr.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for compensation_plan']
  superseded_compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compensation_plan (superseded_compensation_plan_id)']
  annual_leave_accrual_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate at which annual leave hours accrue per pay period for employees on this compensation plan.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level or role required to approve hiring or salary changes for positions on this compensation plan.']
  bargaining_unit varchar [note: 'Specific bargaining unit within a union that this compensation plan covers. Null for non-union plans.']
  base_salary_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum base salary or wage amount for this compensation plan in institutional currency.']
  base_salary_midpoint decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target midpoint salary or wage amount for this compensation plan representing market competitive rate.']
  base_salary_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum base salary or wage amount for this compensation plan in institutional currency.']
  benefits_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees on this compensation plan are eligible for institutional benefits programs.']
  budget_pool_code varchar [note: 'Financial budget pool or cost center code associated with funding for this compensation plan.']
  compa_ratio_maximum decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable compa-ratio (actual salary divided by midpoint) for employees on this plan.']
  compa_ratio_minimum decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable compa-ratio (actual salary divided by midpoint) for employees on this plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this compensation plan.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the compensation plan including purpose, scope, and key features.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended plan.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan becomes active and available for employee assignment.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'FLSA classification indicating whether employees on this plan are exempt from overtime regulations.']
  fte_standard decimal(5,4) [note: 'Standard FTE value for positions on this compensation plan, typically 1.0000 for full-time and fractional for part-time.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational job family grouping for positions assigned to this compensation plan.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this compensation plan was last reviewed for market competitiveness and internal equity.']
  market_percentile_target varchar [note: 'Target market percentile for compensation positioning (e.g., 50 for median, 75 for upper quartile).']
  market_reference_source varchar [note: 'External market survey or benchmark source used to establish compensation ranges for this plan.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compensation plan record was last modified in the system.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this compensation plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional administrative notes or special instructions related to this compensation plan.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees on this compensation plan are eligible to receive overtime pay.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard payment cycle frequency for employees assigned to this compensation plan.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Institutional pay grade or band classification associated with this compensation plan.']
  performance_bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees on this compensation plan are eligible for performance-based bonus compensation.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Broad employee category this compensation plan applies to, aligned with institutional workforce segmentation.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the compensation plan used in HR systems and reporting.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the compensation plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compensation plan structure indicating the primary payment method.']
  retirement_plan_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees on this compensation plan are eligible to participate in institutional retirement plans.']
  sick_leave_accrual_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate at which sick leave hours accrue per pay period for employees on this compensation plan.']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of work hours per week for employees on this compensation plan.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compensation plan indicating whether it is available for assignment.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name or code of the labor union associated with this compensation plan, if applicable. Null for non-union plans.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for compensation_plan. Referenced by compensation_plan_id.'
}

Table hr.pay_grade {
  pay_grade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pay_grade']
  parent_pay_grade_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pay_grade (parent_pay_grade_id)']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level required to approve hiring or salary actions for positions in this pay grade.']
  benchmark_job_title varchar [note: 'Representative job title used for external market benchmarking and CUPA-HR survey matching.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this pay grade are eligible for performance-based bonuses or incentive compensation.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'FLSA classification determining overtime eligibility and compensation rules.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the pay grade (e.g., \'PG01\', \'E5\', \'M3\'). Used as the business identifier in HR systems and employee communications.']
  compa_ratio_maximum decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable compa-ratio (actual salary divided by midpoint) for positions in this grade. Typically 1.00 to 1.20.']
  compa_ratio_minimum decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable compa-ratio (actual salary divided by midpoint) for positions in this grade. Typically 0.80 to 1.00.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay grade record was first created in the system.']
  cupa_hr_category_code varchar [note: 'CUPA-HR standard occupational category code for benchmarking and survey participation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary amounts (e.g., \'USD\', \'CAD\', \'GBP\'). Supports multi-currency institutions.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pay grade including typical job responsibilities, required qualifications, and scope of authority.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this pay grade is no longer effective. Null for currently active pay grades.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this pay grade becomes effective and can be assigned to positions.']
  employee_category varchar [note: 'Broad employee category this pay grade applies to, used for workforce segmentation and CUPA-HR reporting.']
  grade_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level or rank of this pay grade within the institutional compensation structure. Lower numbers typically indicate entry-level positions; higher numbers indicate senior positions.']
  hourly_rate_maximum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly rate for this pay grade in USD. Applicable for non-exempt hourly positions.']
  hourly_rate_minimum decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum hourly rate for this pay grade in USD. Applicable for non-exempt hourly positions.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Job family or occupational group this pay grade belongs to (e.g., \'Finance & Accounting\', \'Information Technology\', \'Student Services\').']
  last_market_review_date date [note: 'Date when this pay grade was last reviewed against external market data and CUPA-HR benchmarks.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay grade record was last updated.']
  market_reference_percentile varchar [note: 'Target market percentile for midpoint salary (e.g., 50 for median, 75 for 75th percentile). Used for competitive positioning against CUPA-HR benchmarks.']
  maximum_annual_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary amount for this pay grade in USD. Represents the ceiling of the compensation range.']
  midpoint_annual_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Midpoint annual salary amount for this pay grade in USD. Represents the target market rate for fully competent performance.']
  minimum_annual_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary amount for this pay grade in USD. Represents the floor of the compensation range.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational attainment typically required for positions in this pay grade.']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience typically required for positions in this pay grade.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the pay grade (e.g., \'Administrative Support I\', \'Senior Faculty\', \'Executive Leadership\').']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next market review and potential adjustment of this pay grade.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special considerations related to this pay grade.']
  number_of_steps varchar [note: 'Total number of steps or increments within this pay grade. Used in step-based compensation structures.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this pay grade are eligible for overtime compensation under FLSA regulations.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard pay frequency for positions in this grade (e.g., monthly for salaried staff, biweekly for hourly).']
  range_spread_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage spread between minimum and maximum salary ((max - min) / min * 100). Indicates the width of the compensation range.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pay grade. Active grades can be assigned to positions; inactive grades are retained for historical reference only.']
  step_increment_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard dollar amount for step increases within this pay grade. Applicable for step-based progression systems.']
  step_increment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard percentage increase for step progression within this pay grade. Applicable for percentage-based progression systems.']
  union_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this pay grade are covered by collective bargaining agreements.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pay_grade. Referenced by pay_grade_id.'
}

Table hr.payroll_run {
  payroll_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_run']
  adjustment_payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_run (adjustment_payroll_run_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The user identifier of the person who approved this payroll run.']
  gl_posting_batch_id varchar [note: 'The batch identifier used when posting this payroll run to the general ledger system.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary department or organizational unit associated with this payroll run.']
  original_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payroll run if this is a correction or reversal run.']
  payroll_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll calendar that defines the schedule for this payroll run.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payroll run was approved for payment processing.']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payroll calculations were completed for this run.']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which payment is issued to employees for this payroll run.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The company or legal entity code for which this payroll run is processed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payroll run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll run.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'The total number of employees included in this payroll run.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period within the fiscal year for this payroll run.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this payroll run is processed.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payroll run record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this payroll run for audit and documentation purposes.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which employees are paid in this payroll run.']
  pay_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pay group or employee population included in this payroll run.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the pay period covered by this payroll run.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the pay period covered by this payroll run.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The primary payment method used for disbursing funds in this payroll run.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payroll run was posted to the general ledger.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payroll run is a reversal of a previous run.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the payroll run, typically formatted as PR-YYYY-####.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run indicating the purpose and processing category.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll run in the processing workflow.']
  total_deductions decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of all deductions (taxes, benefits, garnishments) for this payroll run.']
  total_employer_benefits decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of employer-paid benefits contributions for this payroll run.']
  total_employer_taxes decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of employer-paid taxes (FICA, FUTA, SUTA) for this payroll run.']
  total_gross_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross pay amount for all employees in this payroll run before any deductions.']
  total_net_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total net pay amount for all employees in this payroll run after all deductions.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_run. Referenced by payroll_run_id.'
}

Table hr.job_family {
  job_family_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for job_family']
  competency_framework_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competency framework or skill model associated with this job family, used for talent development and succession planning.']
  parent_job_family_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent job family if this job family is part of a hierarchical classification structure.']
  benefits_tier varchar [note: 'Default benefits tier or package level for positions within this job family.']
  career_ladder_type varchar [note: 'Type of career progression path associated with this job family (e.g., individual contributor, management track, technical specialist).']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level functional category to which this job family belongs. Used for organizational reporting and workforce segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: administrative|academic_support|facilities|finance|human_resources|information_technology|student_services|research|advancement|athletics|legal|communications — promote to reference product]']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the job family in human-readable format. Used in HR systems, reports, and position postings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job family record was first created in the system.']
  cupa_hr_category varchar [note: 'CUPA-HR job family classification code used for benchmarking and salary surveys in higher education.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the job family, including the types of roles, responsibilities, and functional areas covered.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'EEO-1 reporting category for positions in this job family, used for federal compliance reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job family definition was retired or superseded. Null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job family definition became effective and available for use in position management.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Default FLSA classification for positions within this job family, indicating whether roles are typically exempt or non-exempt from overtime regulations.']
  ipeds_category varchar [note: 'IPEDS occupational category for positions in this job family, used for federal higher education reporting.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Typical minimum education level required for entry-level positions within this job family.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the job family (e.g., Information Technology, Facilities Management, Student Services).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this job family for HR administrators.']
  occupational_group varchar [note: 'Standard occupational group classification aligned with federal or industry standards (e.g., SOC code group, EEO-1 category).']
  performance_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard performance review cycle for positions in this job family.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this job family are generally eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  salary_grade_max varchar [note: 'Maximum salary grade applicable to positions within this job family, used for compensation planning.']
  salary_grade_min varchar [note: 'Minimum salary grade applicable to positions within this job family, used for compensation planning.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value used to control the display order of job families in reports and user interfaces.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job family. Active families are in use; inactive families are no longer used for new positions.']
  succession_planning_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for succession planning activities for this job family, indicating strategic importance to the institution.']
  typical_experience_years varchar [note: 'Typical number of years of relevant experience expected for mid-level positions in this job family.']
  union_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this job family are typically eligible for union representation or collective bargaining.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job family record was last modified.']
  workforce_planning_category varchar [note: 'Workforce planning classification indicating expected growth, stability, or decline in demand for this job family.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for job_family. Referenced by job_family_id.'
}

Table hr.pay_period {
  pay_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pay_period']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the payroll administrator who finalized this pay period.']
  payroll_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll calendar that defines the schedule and rules for this pay period.']
  prior_pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pay_period (prior_pay_period_id)']
  approval_deadline_date date [note: 'Final deadline date by which all supervisory approvals must be completed for timesheets and payroll adjustments.']
  benefits_eligibility_date date [note: 'Date used to determine benefits eligibility and accruals for employees paid during this period.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which this pay period occurs, used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  check_date date [note: 'Date when paychecks or direct deposits are issued to employees for this pay period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pay period, including any special circumstances, adjustments, or processing notes.']
  employee_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the employee group or classification to which this pay period applies (e.g., staff, faculty, student workers, administrators).']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period when time and attendance tracking ends.']
  finalized_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period was finalized and locked from further changes.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Fiscal month (1-12) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1-4) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this pay period belongs, used for budgeting and financial reporting alignment.']
